## Newsletter 70: Avoid These 3 YouTube Strategies At ALL Costs

**URL:** https://creatorhooks.com/avoid-these-3-youtube-strategies-at-all-costs/

```
Avoid These 3 YouTube Strategies At ALL Costs 
July 14, 2025 by admin 


I’ve asked some of the best creators on YouTube, “How do you write such great titles?”
And they’ve all said the same thing: “I just model what works.”
So this email will show you 5 videos worth modeling to help you write great titles and save time.
P.S. If you want to save more time and use a title generator that uses all the titles from this newsletter, check out ​​Creator Hooks Pro​​.
Fat to Fit


🖼️ Thumbnail 2:
5 ‘healthy’ habits that kept me fat
663K views + 2 months ago

Title: 5 ‘healthy’ habits that kept me fat
Framework: 5 ‘(Desirable)’ habits that kept me (Undesirable state)
​Hook score​: +596
Why this works:
List – Lists make videos feel more tangible (you know exactly what the video will be about) and they add some curiosity.
Curiosity – It’s counterintuitive that “healthy” habits would keep her fat.
Negativity – This is about a pain point: staying fat.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience about good habits that kept you in a bad state.
Examples of this framework in action:

5 ‘confidence’ tips that kept me insecure
5 ‘communication’ skills that ruined my marriage

3 Second Stop


🖼️ Thumbnail 3:
How to STOP a DOG ATTACK in 3 Seconds -
GUARANTEED!

Title: How to STOP a DOG ATTACK in 3 Seconds – GUARANTEED!
Framework: How to STOP a (Problem) in (Short time frame) – GUARANTEED!
​Hook score​: +999
Why this works:
Negativity – This uses fear to grab your attention.
Time Frame – The time frame of “in 3 Seconds” makes this feel more tangible and increases the desire to watch this video since it’s so short.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience how to stop something bad in a short time frame.
Examples of this framework in action:

How to UNCLOG a Drain in 3 Seconds – GUARANTEED!
How to STOP a Panic Attack in 10 Seconds



Write better YouTube titles
Grow your channel
Save time

​Check out Creator Hooks Pro here​.
P.S. Here’s a big win from a Creator Hooks Pro customer:
“This title was from the title generator and it’s performing extremely well: “The ONE Shopping Secret Home Depot Hides That Changes EVERYTHING!” When I saw this suggestion, I knew it would be a banger.”
– Matt Outlaw, 731 Woodworks, 790K Subscribers


🖼️ Thumbnail 4:
Views Watch time (hours)

293.5K © 35.9K @

202.2K more than usual 22.6K more than usual

© This video © Typical performance

‘Subscribers

+2.7K

10

Cabinet Conundrum


🖼️ Thumbnail 5:


Title: Avoid These 3 Cabinets At ALL Costs
Framework: Avoid These 3 (Entities) At ALL Costs
​Hook score​: +1414
Why this works:
Negativity – This warning does a great job of grabbing attention.
List – Lists make videos feel more tangible (you know exactly what the video will be about) and they add some curiosity.
Curiosity – This makes you wonder what these 3 cabinets are.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience to avoid a list of things at all costs.
Examples of this framework in action:

Avoid These 3 Squat Variations At ALL Costs
Avoid These 3 Popular European Travel Destinations At ALL Costs

Pitch Please


🖼️ Thumbnail 6:
QPITCHGRIPS IN2MINUTES

169K vi

Title: The Quick Guide to Baseball Pitch Grips
Framework: The Quick Guide to (Entities)
​Hook score​: +1448
Why this works:
Desire – This creator’s audience is into baseball pitch grips.
Speed – People love speed and “Quick Guide” promises that.
The time frame of “in 2 minutes” in the thumbnail also adds to the speed and desire.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience about the quick guide to something they’re into.
Examples of this framework in action:

The Quick Guide to Every Seattle Neighborhood
The Quick Guide to Cooking the Perfect Steak

yesssss


🖼️ Thumbnail 7:
ava Think!
=a \ ]

7 Signs You Are WAY Above Average
(Shocking Money Stats!)

352K v

Title: 7 Signs You Are WAY Above Average (Shocking Money Stats!)
Framework: 7 Signs You Are WAY Above Average (Shocking [Niche] Stats!)
​Hook score​: +1996
Why this works:
List – Lists make videos feel more tangible (you know exactly what the video will be about) and they add some curiosity.
Status – Everybody wants to know how they compare against other people.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience about signs they’re above average.
Examples of this framework in action:

7 Signs You Are WAY Above Average (Shocking Golf Stats!)
7 Signs Your Instagram Is WAY Above Average

Flop of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 8:
How to Grade Your Dog's Behavior: FromF to:
A

36K views + 8 months ago


Title: How to Grade Your Dog’s Behavior: From F to A
Hook score: -87
Why this flopped: People love benchmarks and seeing how they compare, so why did this flop?
My guess is that people don’t want to grade their dog’s behavior, they want to skip that step and just have a better-behaved dog.
The thumbnail hints at that, but the title doesn’t.
It might’ve done better if it were, “How to Turn Your Dog’s Behavior From F to A”.
Or, to be even more direct, we could try, “How to Turn Your Crazy Dog Into a Calm, Obedient Dog”.
The lesson: write your title about the end goal the audience will get, not about the difficult journey they have to go on.
Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
Hope you enjoyed this week’s edition and if you know a fellow creator who needs help writing better titles, please send them to creatorhooks.com.
– Jake
(@jthomas__ on Twitter)
P.S. Read previous editions here.
P.P.S. If you want to write better titles and grow your channel, check out Creator Hooks Pro.
Read last week’s edition »

## Newsletter 71: this is the only skill you need to grow on youtube

**URL:** https://creatorhooks.com/this-is-the-only-skill-you-need-to-grow-on-youtube/

```
this is *the only* skill you need to grow on youtube 
July 7, 2025July 7, 2025 by admin 


I’ve asked some of the best creators on YouTube, “How do you write such great titles?”
And they’ve all said the same thing: “I just model what works.”
So this email will show you 5 videos worth modeling to help you write great titles and save time.
P.S. If you want to save more time and use a title generator that uses all the titles from this newsletter, check out ​​Creator Hooks Pro​​.
Say what?


🖼️ Thumbnail 2:
Listening is *the only* skill you need to
become fluent (here's why)

325K views « 2 months ago


Title: Listening is *the only* skill you need to become fluent (here’s why)
Framework: [Activity] is *the only* skill you need to [Achieve goal] (here’s why)
​Hook score​: +327
Why this works:
Curiosity – The constraint that listening is the only skill you need to become fluent builds curiosity because it’s counterintuitive. Shouldn’t speaking and other skills be necessary, too?
Desire – This creator’s audience would love to become fluent. Plus, the fact that listening is the only skill you need to become fluent increases the audience’s desire to learn about it.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience about the only skill they need to achieve a goal.
Examples of this framework in action:

Sleep is *the only* habit you need to master to get healthy (here’s why)
This is *the only* skill you need to blow up on Instagram

Dollar Dwelling


🖼️ Thumbnail 3:
| Renovated This $1 House (Start To Finish)

3.6M views - 1 month ago


Title: I Renovated This $1 House (Start to Finish)
Framework: I [Activity] This $1 [Entity] (Start to Finish)
​Hook score​: +589
Why this works:
Desire – This leverages desire two ways:

This creator’s audience loves renovations.
Since the house was only $1, some people feel like they could do this, too.

Curiosity – $1 house builds so much curiosity because it makes you ask all sorts of questions, like:

How in the world did she get a $1 house?
How bad of shape is a $1 house?
What does it look like now?

A $1 house is kind of an oxymoron because it just doesn’t seem possible.
How you can use this framework: Do an activity your audience wants to do and start with the absolute bare minimum.
Examples of this framework in action:

I Turned $1K Into A $1,000,000 Business (Start to Finish)
I Saved A Dying Marriage (Exactly How I Did It)



Write better YouTube titles
Grow your channel
Save time

​Check out Creator Hooks Pro here​.
P.S. Here’s a big win from a Creator Hooks Pro customer:
“I got this title directly from Creator Hooks Pro’s title generator. It’s a 1 of 10 and it’s brought in 5 clients worth over $25k in less than 3 weeks”
– Jeff Hampton, Hampton Law, 360K Subscribers


🖼️ Thumbnail 4:
LAWYER: How Police Get Around :
Your Right to Remain Silent

341K views * 3 weeks ago

Driver Delusion


🖼️ Thumbnail 5:
YOU ARE WRONG

NOT HERE:

This #1 Golf coach PROVES You're HITTING
driver all wrong!!

52 2 months

Title: This #1 Golf coach PROVES You’re HITTING driver all wrong!!
Framework: This (Authority Figure) PROVES You’re (Activity) all wrong!!
​Hook score​: +635
Why this works:
Authority – #1 Golf coach brings attention, trust and credibility to this video.
Negativity – This is all about a pain point: hitting your driver all wrong.
Credibility – This might be a little weak, but “PROVES” sounds more credible and believable than just “explains why” or something.
Curiosity – This opens a loop and makes you want to know how this coach proves you’re hitting your driver all wrong.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience about how an authority figure proves they’re doing something wrong.
Examples of this framework in action:

This #1 Sales Trainer PROVES You’re CLOSING deals all wrong!!
This #1 Strength Coach PROVES You’re BENCH PRESSING all wrong

CIA Secrets


🖼️ Thumbnail 6:
YOUR PHON!
IS

Ex-CIA Officer Reveals 5 Signs Your Phone's
Been Hacked

Title: Ex-CIA Officer Reveals 5 Signs Your Phone’s Been Hacked
Framework: (Authority Figure) Reveals 5 Signs (Problem has happened)
​Hook score​: +895
Why this works:
Authority – “Ex-CIA Officer” brings authority and trust to this video.
List – Lists make videos feel more tangible (you know exactly what the video will be about) and they add some curiosity.
Negativity – This leverages the fear of your phone getting hacked to grab your attention.
How you can use this framework: Have an authority figure reveal signs that something bad has happened.
Examples of this framework in action:

Ex-Navy SEAL Reveals 5 Signs You Lack Mental Toughness
Ex-Nat Geo Photographer Reveals 5 Reasons Why Your Photos Suck

Cheat Mode On


🖼️ Thumbnail 7:
3 Sound Design Secrets That Feel Like
CHEATING

Title: 3 Sound Design Secrets That Feel Like CHEATING
Framework: 3 (Activity or Niche) Secrets That Feel Like CHEATING
​Hook score​: +1440
Why this works:
List – Lists make videos feel more tangible (you know exactly what the video will be about) and they add some curiosity.
Desire – This creator’s audience wants to improve their sound design.
Curiosity – “Secrets” is a great, simple way to build curiosity.
Negativity – “CHEATING” brings some controversy here that grabs the audience’s attention.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience about secrets that feel like cheating.
Examples of this framework in action:

3 Piano Practice Secrets That Feel Like CHEATING
3 Backyard Garden Secrets That Feel Like CHEATING

Flop of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 8:
Is a Backup Gun Necessary for Self- :
Defense?

7K views - 3 weeks ago

Title: Is a Backup Gun Necessary for Self-Defense?
Hook score: -93
Why this flopped: Here’s a strategy that I’ve heard a lot of people recommend: Take questions your audience has asked and just make videos answering them.
Sometimes that’s a good strategy, but sometimes it leads to very specific videos that don’t resonate with a large part of your audience.
That’s what this video feels like to me.
But even with this very specific idea, I think the title could still be better.
Titles that are bold opinions often do better on YouTube than questions like this.
This video might have done better if the title was more opinionated, like: “Here’s Why You Don’t Need A Backup Gun For Self-Defense”
Or it could’ve taken more of a personal opinion and story angle: “Here’s Why I Never Carry A Backup Gun Anymore”
Or, if the topic of backup guns just isn’t cutting it for the audience, we could make it more broad.
Something like, “This Popular Self-Defense Hack Is A Waste Of Time & Money”.
Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
Hope you enjoyed this week’s edition and if you know a fellow creator who needs help writing better titles, please send them to creatorhooks.com.
– Jake
(@jthomas__ on Twitter)
P.S. Read previous editions here.
P.P.S. If you want to write better titles and grow your channel, check out Creator Hooks Pro.
Read last week’s edition »

## Newsletter 73: Hate Writing Clickbaity YouTube Titles THIS is for YOU

**URL:** https://creatorhooks.com/hate-writing-clickbaity-youtube-titles-this-is-for-you/

```
Hate Writing Clickbaity YouTube Titles? THIS is for YOU 
June 30, 2025 by admin 


I’ve asked some of the best creators on YouTube, “How do you write such great titles?”
And they’ve all said the same thing: “I just model what works.”
So this email will show you 5 videos worth modeling to help you write great titles and save time.
P.S. If you want to save more time and use a title generator that uses all the titles from this newsletter, check out ​​Creator Hooks Pro​​.
Easy Way Out


🖼️ Thumbnail 2:
>»

Hate Talking On Camera for Content?
THIS is for YOU

1471 s + 3 month

Title: Hate Talking On Camera for Content? THIS is for YOU
Framework: Hate (Beneficial, but difficult action)? THIS is for YOU
​Hook score​: +359
Why this works:
Negativity – This starts with a pain point to grab the right audience’s attention.
Curiosity – This opens a loop and makes you wonder what “THIS” is.
How you can use this framework: Ask your audience if they hate something that they have to or should do, then say your video is for them.
Examples of this framework in action:

Hate Cleaning Your Toilets? THIS is for YOU
Hate “Productivity Hacks”? THIS is for YOU

Helpful Habits


🖼️ Thumbnail 3:
30 Habits That (Quietly) Changed My
Life Forever

260K views + 3 weeks ago

Title: 30 Habits That (Quietly) Changed My Life Forever
Framework: 30 Habits That (Quietly) [Achieved Goal]
​Hook score​: +1092
Why this works:
List – Lists make videos feel more tangible (you know exactly what the video will be about) and they add some curiosity.
Desire – This creator’s audience would love to change their life.
Curiosity – “(Quietly)” kinda makes this feel like you’re getting in on a secret.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience about the habits that achieved a big goal in a quiet way.
Examples of this framework in action:

10 Habits That (Quietly) Changed My YouTube Channel Forever
5 Habits That (Quietly) Changed My Golf Game Forever



Write better YouTube titles
Grow your channel
Save time

​Check out Creator Hooks Pro here​.
“Creator Hooks Pro has helped me generate millions of views on my channel since I started using it by giving me amazing title ideas I would not have been able to come up with on my own. It’s like having a team of title experts at your fingertips.”
– Matt Outlaw, 731 Woodworks, 667K Subscribers


🖼️ Thumbnail 4:
DeWALT Just Changed
Woodworking FOREVER (genius...

709K views + 1 month ago

2 Bad


🖼️ Thumbnail 5:
tendo is ALREADY BANNING
h2

Title: Nintendo is ALREADY BANNING Switch 2
Framework: (Entity) is ALREADY (Negative Action) (Entity)
​Hook score​: +1734
Why this works:
Negativity – People love drama like this.
Curiosity – The word “Already” makes this feel like the ban at this time came as a surprise and makes you wonder why they’re banning it so soon.
News – People love news like this.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience about some news that is “already” happening. Bonus points if it’s negative.
Examples of this framework in action:

Tesla is ALREADY Crushing Waymo
Celtics Are ALREADY Regretting Blowing Up Their Roster

Video Proof


🖼️ Thumbnail 6:
Do Snap Swivels REALLY Hurt Lure :
Action? (Side-by-Side Look...

530K views + 2 months ago

Title: Do Snap Swivels REALLY Hurt Lure Action? (Side-by-Side Look Underwater)
Framework: Do (Entities) REALLY Hurt (Desired outcome)? ([Proof of evidence])
​Hook score​: +2138
Why this works:
Curiosity – This builds curiosity by asking a question, and the “REALLY” enhances that curiosity.
Negativity – This is about avoiding a problem (hurting your lure action).
Credibility – By promising a side-by-side underwater look, this video feels more credible and believable.
How you can use this framework: Ask if something really causes a problem, and then promise some element of proof.
Examples of this framework in action:

Do Credit Checks REALLY Lower Your Credit Score? (FICO Score Tracking)
Does Leaving Your Phone Charging REALLY Damage the Battery? (30 Day Test)

Oh, really?


🖼️ Thumbnail 7:
yes, really
so]

Every Font is Free

632K views - 3 months ago

Title: Every Font is Free
Framework: Every (Entity) is Free
​Hook score​: +3755
Why this works:
Desire – This creator’s audience would love for every front to be free.
Curiosity – This is counterintuitive because many fonts are not free.
This combo of desire + curiosity makes this video feel too good to be true, but there’s a sliver of hope, so you’ve gotta watch it if you’re a font guy or gal.
How you can use this framework: Make a bold, counterintuitive statement that feels too good to be true.
Examples of this framework in action:

Every Marketing Tool is Free
Every Plane Ticket is Free

Flop of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 8:
NOT What) =.
Expected :

The 1 Bass Spawning Behavior |
DIDN'T Expect (Dock Cam Footage)

6.6K views « 1 month ago

Title: The 1 Bass Spawning Behavior I DIDN’T Expect (Dock Cam Footage)
Hook score: -71
Why this flopped: This creator’s audience has one goal: catch more bass.
My guess is that although bass spawning behavior can be related to catching bass, it’s a few steps away.
It takes a bit of mental work for the audience to realize that this video can help them with their main goal.
And since most people are mindlessly scrolling on YouTube, any amount of mental work is often too much work, so they scroll on by and choose another video.
A super simple change might be just: “The 1 Bass Behavior I DIDN’T Expect (Dock Cam Footage)” (just remove the word “spawning.”)
You could also try a more dramatic title with a broader angle: “Underwater Video Changes Everything I Thought I Knew About Bass”
TLDR: Know why people watch your videos and have your titles reflect that, and make your titles super simple to read and understand.
Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
Hope you enjoyed this week’s edition and if you know a fellow creator who needs help writing better titles, please send them to creatorhooks.com.
– Jake
(@jthomas__ on Twitter)
P.S. Read previous editions here.
P.P.S. If you want to write better titles and grow your channel, check out Creator Hooks Pro.
Read last week’s edition »

## Newsletter 75: The One Choice That Will Determine Your YouTube Channels Success

**URL:** https://creatorhooks.com/the-one-choice-that-will-determine-your-youtube-channels-success/

```
The One Choice That Will Determine Your YouTube Channel’s Success 
June 23, 2025 by admin 


I’ve asked some of the best creators on YouTube, “How do you write such great titles?”
And they’ve all said the same thing: “I just model what works.”
So this email will show you 5 videos worth modeling to help you write great titles and save time.
P.S. If you want to save more time and use a title generator that uses all the titles from this newsletter, check out ​​Creator Hooks Pro​​.
Gossip Girls


🖼️ Thumbnail 2:
“This Is What | Think Of Pete Hegseth” -
Jocko Willink

Title: “This Is What I Think Of Pete Hegseth” – Jocko Willink
Framework: “This Is What I Think Of (Trending or polarizing subject)” – (Authority Figure)
​Hook score​: +669
Why this works:
Authority – People are interested in what authority figures like Jocko Willink have to say.
Timeliness – People love polarizing and/or trending topics, like Pete Hegseth in this case.
Curiosity – This does a great job of opening a loop and making you wonder what he thinks, so you have to click and find out.
How you can use this framework: This one is simple — just share what an authority figure in your space thinks about a trending or polarizing topic.
Examples of this framework in action:

“This Is What I Think The Future of AI Is” – Sam Altman
“This Is What I Think Of SGA” – Michael Jordan

Pre Practice


🖼️ Thumbnail 3:
Don't start practicing art before you've done
THIS

104K views + 2 months ago

Title: Don’t start practicing art before you’ve done THIS
Framework: Don’t start (Activity) before you’ve done THIS
​Hook score​: +1618
Why this works:
Negativity – Warning someone to not do something does a great job at grabbing their attention.
Curiosity – This opens a loop and makes you want to know what “THIS” is.
Urgency – This makes you feel like you need to watch this now.
How you can use this framework: Warn your audience to not do something they want to do until they’ve done “this”.
Examples of this framework in action:

Don’t start meditating before you’ve done THIS
Don’t start stretching your hips before you’ve done THIS



Write better YouTube titles
Grow your channel
Save time

​Check out Creator Hooks Pro here​.
P.S. Here’s a big win from a Creator Hooks Pro customer:
“I got this title directly from Creator Hooks Pro’s title generator. It’s a 1 of 10 and it’s brought in 5 clients worth over $25k in less than 3 weeks”
– Jeff Hampton, Hampton Law, 360K Subscribers


🖼️ Thumbnail 4:
LAWYER: How Police Get Around :
Your Right to Remain Silent

341K views * 3 weeks ago

no pressure but…


🖼️ Thumbnail 5:
Paudiology
Moncy

The One Choice That Will Determine :
Your Next 5 Years

702K views + 1 month ago

Title: The One Choice That Will Determine Your Next 5 Years
Framework: The One (Action) That Will Determine (Future)
​Hook score​: +4094
Why this works:
Curiosity – This builds curiosity two ways:

It opens a loop and makes you wonder what the choice is.
Since it’s about just one choice, this constraint raises the stakes of that one choice and makes it more interesting.

Time Frame – The time frame of “Your Next 5 Years” also raises the stakes of this one choice.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience about one thing that will make a big difference for their future.
Examples of this framework in action:

The One Choice That Will Determine Your Whole Career
The One Choice That Will Determine America’s Next 10 Years

Perfect Powder


🖼️ Thumbnail 6:
The SECRET Powder That Makes
Plants Grow Faster & Repels Pests!

+ 2 month:

Title: The SECRET Powder That Makes Plants Grow Faster & Repels Pests!
Framework: The SECRET (Entity) That (Achieves goal) & (Removes problem)!
​Hook score​: +4451
Why this works:
Curiosity – Using the word “Secret” is a simple way to build curiosity and make the audience feel like they’re getting in on a secret.
Desire – This audience would love for their plants to grow faster.
Negativity – They would also love to repel pests.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience about a secret thing that helps them achieve a goal and avoid a problem.
Examples of this framework in action:

The SECRET Study Hack That Improves Retention & Stops Procrastination!
The SECRET Golf Drill That Adds Distance & Decreases Your Slice!

Healthy Addictions


🖼️ Thumbnail 7:
GET ADDICTED TO STUDYING
LIKE SCROLLING REELS

G3

Getting ADDICTED to STUDYING is
Easy, Actually
716K views + 2 months ago

Title: Getting ADDICTED to STUDYING is Easy, Actually
Framework: Getting ADDICTED to (Beneficial, but hard activity) is Easy, Actually
​Hook score​: +17518
Why this works:
Desire – This creator’s audience wants to study more. The confident tone of this title makes you feel like this video can help you.
Curiosity – Many people struggle with studying, so saying it’s “Easy, Actually,” is counterintuitive.
Controversy – Saying getting “ADDICTED” to studying is more bold, polarizing, and controversial than just, “studying more,” so it grabs more attention.
Contrast – The contrast between “ADDICTED” (negative connotation) and “STUDYING” (positive connotation) makes this title more interesting.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience that getting addicted to something beneficial, but is sometimes hard to do, is easy, actually.
Examples of this framework in action:

Getting ADDICTED to Practicing Piano is Easy, Actually
Getting ADDICTED to Eating Healthy is Easy, Actually

Flop of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 8:
The Battle Between Elon, Trump &
‘Tne Ler - Navel Rew seme

26K views * 3 weeks ago

Title: The Battle Between Elon, Trump & The Left – Naval Ravikant
Hook score: -82
Why this flopped: People love drama, so the idea of this sounds great, but I think part of the reason it flopped because it’s too complicated.
Specifically, I think there are too many nouns.
Elon, Trump, The Left, Naval Ravikant — these four nouns make this title hard to digest.
One thing they could do is take a stand and write the title about just one of the sides: “Why Trump Is Dominating The Left – Naval Ravikant”
(I’m not saying that’s true or what this video is about — just using this as an example.)
You could also argue that since this video has so few views compared to the rest of the channel, people aren’t interested in this battle.
Part of this video is about power, and since this channel is about improving yourself, that could be an interesting focus for the title.
The title could be something like, “Why Leaders Today Have More Power Than Any Other Man In History.”
TLDR: Take a bold stance, or change the subject of the title if your video is struggling.
Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
Hope you enjoyed this week’s edition and if you know a fellow creator who needs help writing better titles, please send them to creatorhooks.com.
– Jake
(@jthomas__ on Twitter)
P.S. Read previous editions here.
P.P.S. If you want to write better titles and grow your channel, check out Creator Hooks Pro.
Read last week’s edition »

## Newsletter 76: The One Choice That Will Determine Your YouTube Channels Success

**URL:** https://creatorhooks.com/the-one-choice-that-will-determine-your-youtube-channels-success/

```
The One Choice That Will Determine Your YouTube Channel’s Success 
June 23, 2025 by admin 


I’ve asked some of the best creators on YouTube, “How do you write such great titles?”
And they’ve all said the same thing: “I just model what works.”
So this email will show you 5 videos worth modeling to help you write great titles and save time.
P.S. If you want to save more time and use a title generator that uses all the titles from this newsletter, check out ​​Creator Hooks Pro​​.
Gossip Girls


🖼️ Thumbnail 2:
“This Is What | Think Of Pete Hegseth” -
Jocko Willink

Title: “This Is What I Think Of Pete Hegseth” – Jocko Willink
Framework: “This Is What I Think Of (Trending or polarizing subject)” – (Authority Figure)
​Hook score​: +669
Why this works:
Authority – People are interested in what authority figures like Jocko Willink have to say.
Timeliness – People love polarizing and/or trending topics, like Pete Hegseth in this case.
Curiosity – This does a great job of opening a loop and making you wonder what he thinks, so you have to click and find out.
How you can use this framework: This one is simple — just share what an authority figure in your space thinks about a trending or polarizing topic.
Examples of this framework in action:

“This Is What I Think The Future of AI Is” – Sam Altman
“This Is What I Think Of SGA” – Michael Jordan

Pre Practice


🖼️ Thumbnail 3:
Don't start practicing art before you've done
THIS

104K views + 2 months ago

Title: Don’t start practicing art before you’ve done THIS
Framework: Don’t start (Activity) before you’ve done THIS
​Hook score​: +1618
Why this works:
Negativity – Warning someone to not do something does a great job at grabbing their attention.
Curiosity – This opens a loop and makes you want to know what “THIS” is.
Urgency – This makes you feel like you need to watch this now.
How you can use this framework: Warn your audience to not do something they want to do until they’ve done “this”.
Examples of this framework in action:

Don’t start meditating before you’ve done THIS
Don’t start stretching your hips before you’ve done THIS



Write better YouTube titles
Grow your channel
Save time

​Check out Creator Hooks Pro here​.
P.S. Here’s a big win from a Creator Hooks Pro customer:
“I got this title directly from Creator Hooks Pro’s title generator. It’s a 1 of 10 and it’s brought in 5 clients worth over $25k in less than 3 weeks”
– Jeff Hampton, Hampton Law, 360K Subscribers


🖼️ Thumbnail 4:
LAWYER: How Police Get Around :
Your Right to Remain Silent

341K views * 3 weeks ago

no pressure but…


🖼️ Thumbnail 5:
Paudiology
Moncy

The One Choice That Will Determine :
Your Next 5 Years

702K views + 1 month ago

Title: The One Choice That Will Determine Your Next 5 Years
Framework: The One (Action) That Will Determine (Future)
​Hook score​: +4094
Why this works:
Curiosity – This builds curiosity two ways:

It opens a loop and makes you wonder what the choice is.
Since it’s about just one choice, this constraint raises the stakes of that one choice and makes it more interesting.

Time Frame – The time frame of “Your Next 5 Years” also raises the stakes of this one choice.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience about one thing that will make a big difference for their future.
Examples of this framework in action:

The One Choice That Will Determine Your Whole Career
The One Choice That Will Determine America’s Next 10 Years

Perfect Powder


🖼️ Thumbnail 6:
The SECRET Powder That Makes
Plants Grow Faster & Repels Pests!

+ 2 month:

Title: The SECRET Powder That Makes Plants Grow Faster & Repels Pests!
Framework: The SECRET (Entity) That (Achieves goal) & (Removes problem)!
​Hook score​: +4451
Why this works:
Curiosity – Using the word “Secret” is a simple way to build curiosity and make the audience feel like they’re getting in on a secret.
Desire – This audience would love for their plants to grow faster.
Negativity – They would also love to repel pests.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience about a secret thing that helps them achieve a goal and avoid a problem.
Examples of this framework in action:

The SECRET Study Hack That Improves Retention & Stops Procrastination!
The SECRET Golf Drill That Adds Distance & Decreases Your Slice!

Healthy Addictions


🖼️ Thumbnail 7:
GET ADDICTED TO STUDYING
LIKE SCROLLING REELS

G3

Getting ADDICTED to STUDYING is
Easy, Actually
716K views + 2 months ago

Title: Getting ADDICTED to STUDYING is Easy, Actually
Framework: Getting ADDICTED to (Beneficial, but hard activity) is Easy, Actually
​Hook score​: +17518
Why this works:
Desire – This creator’s audience wants to study more. The confident tone of this title makes you feel like this video can help you.
Curiosity – Many people struggle with studying, so saying it’s “Easy, Actually,” is counterintuitive.
Controversy – Saying getting “ADDICTED” to studying is more bold, polarizing, and controversial than just, “studying more,” so it grabs more attention.
Contrast – The contrast between “ADDICTED” (negative connotation) and “STUDYING” (positive connotation) makes this title more interesting.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience that getting addicted to something beneficial, but is sometimes hard to do, is easy, actually.
Examples of this framework in action:

Getting ADDICTED to Practicing Piano is Easy, Actually
Getting ADDICTED to Eating Healthy is Easy, Actually

Flop of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 8:
The Battle Between Elon, Trump &
‘Tne Ler - Navel Rew seme

26K views * 3 weeks ago

Title: The Battle Between Elon, Trump & The Left – Naval Ravikant
Hook score: -82
Why this flopped: People love drama, so the idea of this sounds great, but I think part of the reason it flopped because it’s too complicated.
Specifically, I think there are too many nouns.
Elon, Trump, The Left, Naval Ravikant — these four nouns make this title hard to digest.
One thing they could do is take a stand and write the title about just one of the sides: “Why Trump Is Dominating The Left – Naval Ravikant”
(I’m not saying that’s true or what this video is about — just using this as an example.)
You could also argue that since this video has so few views compared to the rest of the channel, people aren’t interested in this battle.
Part of this video is about power, and since this channel is about improving yourself, that could be an interesting focus for the title.
The title could be something like, “Why Leaders Today Have More Power Than Any Other Man In History.”
TLDR: Take a bold stance, or change the subject of the title if your video is struggling.
Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
Hope you enjoyed this week’s edition and if you know a fellow creator who needs help writing better titles, please send them to creatorhooks.com.
– Jake
(@jthomas__ on Twitter)
P.S. Read previous editions here.
P.P.S. If you want to write better titles and grow your channel, check out Creator Hooks Pro.
Read last week’s edition »

## Newsletter 77: You Dont Want 100K Subs  You Want To Grow A Real Business

**URL:** https://creatorhooks.com/you-dont-want-100k-subs-you-want-to-grow-a-real-business/

```
You Don’t Want 100K Subs — You Want To Grow A Real Business 
June 16, 2025 by admin 


I’ve asked some of the best creators on YouTube, “How do you write such great titles?”
And they’ve all said the same thing: “I just model what works.”
So this email will show you 5 videos worth modeling to help you write great titles and save time.
P.S. If you want to save more time and use a title generator that uses all the titles from this newsletter, check out ​​Creator Hooks Pro​​.
Texas Terror


🖼️ Thumbnail 2:
We Chased Driverless Trucks In
Texas. What We Saw Will Scare You.

eks at

Title: We Chased Driverless Trucks In Texas. What We Saw Will Scare You.
Framework: We (Investigated new technology). What We Saw Will Scare You.
​Hook score​: +424
Why this works:
Credibility – This isn’t just an armchair opinion — they actually went and gathered firsthand information, which makes this video feel more credible.
Negativity – Scary things do a great job of grabbing people’s attention and interest.
Curiosity – This opens a loop and makes you wonder what they saw.
How you can use this framework: Go get firsthand information about a new technology and build curiosity by only telling the audience which emotion they’ll feel about it.
Examples of this framework in action:

We Tested AI Video Generators. What We Discovered Will Scare You.
We Tried Viral TikTok Beauty Products. What We Found Is Horrifying.

Inflection Point


🖼️ Thumbnail 3:
WTF Happened In 1971?
946K views « 1 month ago

Title: WTF Happened In 1971?
Framework: WTF Happened In (Specific time)?
​Hook score​: +1859
Why this works:
Curiosity – This builds curiosity by asking a big, bold question.
The thumbnail complements this perfectly by showing that something big happened in 1971.
How you can use this framework: Ask what happened at a specific point or time, and show a big change in a graph.
Examples of this framework in action:

WTF Happened In My Latest Video?
WTF Happened To New York City?



Write better YouTube titles
Grow your channel
Save time

​Check out Creator Hooks Pro here​.
P.S. Here’s a big win from a Creator Hooks Pro customer:
“I got this title directly from Creator Hooks Pro’s title generator. It’s a 1 of 10 and it’s brought in 5 clients worth over $25k in less than 3 weeks”
– Jeff Hampton, Hampton Law, 360K Subscribers


🖼️ Thumbnail 4:
LAWYER: How Police Get Around :
Your Right to Remain Silent

341K views * 3 weeks ago

The Truth Hurts


🖼️ Thumbnail 5:
You Don't Want Love—You Want to
Be Picked So You Feel Worthy

Title: You Don’t Want Love—You Want to Be Picked So You Feel Worthy
Framework: You Don’t Want (Desirable entity)—You Want (True desire)
​Hook score​: +1945
Why this works:
Curiosity – “You Don’t Want Love” is counterintuitive to people who assume they want love.
Negativity – Telling people what they don’t want does a great job of grabbing attention.
Desire – This resonates with the audience and reveals to them what they truly desire.
Note: This format is only effective if you know your audience’s hopes, desires, and fears. The best creators know their audiences better than their audience knows themselves.
How you can use this framework: Make a counterintuitive statement telling the audience they don’t want what they think they want, and tell them what they actually want instead.
Examples of this framework in action:

You Don’t Want To Be Rich—You Want To Be Wealthy
You Don’t Want A Clean House—You Want to Feel In Control

Burrito Ballin


🖼️ Thumbnail 6:
amaket

| Left The U.S. For India And Built A
$23M Burrito Business

6.2M views - 1 month ago

Title: I Left The U.S. For India And Built A $23M Burrito Business
Framework: I Left (Advantageous place) For (Seemingly less advantageous place) And (Achieved big goal)
​Hook score​: +2444
Why this works:
Curiosity – This builds curiosity three ways:

It’s counterintuitive because you think he’d have a better chance at building a $23M business in the U.S. than in India.
It’s counterintuitive that a burrito business can be so big.
And maybe I’m just an ignorant Florida man, but I don’t associate burritos with India, so that’s also counterintuitive.

Desire – This creator’s audience would love to build a $23M business (bonus points if it involves burritos).
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience how you, or someone else, left an advantageous position for a less advantageous position, but achieved something amazing.
Examples of this framework in action:

I Ditched My $5,000 Canon For A Polaroid And Took Better Photos
I Stopped Counting Calories And Lost 20 Pounds

Melody Memory


🖼️ Thumbnail 7:
50 Recognizable songs you don't :

know the name of

2.2M views + 1 month ago &

Title: 50 Recognizable songs you don’t know the name of
Framework: 50 Recognizable (Entities) (Counterintuitive statement)
​Hook score​: +3562
Why this works:
List – Lists make videos feel more tangible (you know exactly what the video will be about) and they add some curiosity.
Curiosity – The contrast between “recognizable songs” and “you don’t know the name of” builds curiosity.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience about a list of things they’d recognize, but don’t truly know.
Examples of this framework in action:

20 Recognizable actors you don’t know the name of
10 Psychological Hacks You See Every Day But Don’t Realize It

Flop of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 8:
What It Really Takes To Transform
Vehicles Into Homes

16K views + 2 months ago

Title: What It Really Takes To Transform Vehicles Into Homes
Hook score: -99
Why this flopped: One of the golden rules of marketing is to talk about benefits, not features.
This title feels like it’s doing the opposite — talking about features, not benefits.
Talking about “what it really takes” isn’t as exciting as what you actually get: a sweet home made out of a vehicle.
If it were me, that’s what I’d focus my title on. Something like, “Turning A Van Into Your Home Will Change Your Life Forever”. This is focused more on what you get (change your life forever) than what it takes.
Here’s the lesson: think about the outcome your audience will get from your video, and make the title about that.
Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
Hope you enjoyed this week’s edition and if you know a fellow creator who needs help writing better titles, please send them to creatorhooks.com.
– Jake
(@jthomas__ on Twitter)
P.S. Read previous editions here.
P.P.S. If you want to write better titles and grow your channel, check out Creator Hooks Pro.
Read last week’s edition »

## Newsletter 79: 5 Habits of Top 1 YouTubers

**URL:** https://creatorhooks.com/5-habits-of-top-1-youtubers/

```
5 Habits of Top 1% YouTubers 
June 9, 2025 by admin 


I’ve asked some of the best creators on YouTube, “How do you write such great titles?”
And they’ve all said the same thing: “I just model what works.”
So this email will show you 5 videos worth modeling to help you write great titles and save time.
P.S. If you want to save more time and use a title generator that uses all the titles from this newsletter, check out ​​Creator Hooks Pro​​.
Career Commitment


🖼️ Thumbnail 2:
JOB HOPPING IS DEAD! (YOU SHOULD :
HAVE BEEN LOYAL!)

Title: JOB HOPPING IS DEAD! (YOU SHOULD HAVE BEEN LOYAL!)
Framework: [Common Action] IS DEAD! (YOU SHOULD HAVE BEEN [Alternative, better action])
​Hook score​: +388
Why this works:
Negativity – This is full of polarizing drama and controversy, which does a great job at grabbing people’s attention.
Even though this isn’t a style I’d personally use, this does show how drama and bold statements can do well on YouTube.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience that something is dead and they should have made a better decision.
Examples of this framework in action:

THE DOLLAR IS DEAD! (YOU SHOULD HAVE BOUGHT BITCOIN!)
TIKTOK IS DEAD! (YOU NEED TO BE ON YOUTUBE!)

Winning Women


🖼️ Thumbnail 3:


Title: 7 Habits of Top 1% Women
Framework: 7 Habits of Top 1% (Specific audience)
​Hook score​: +557
Why this works:
List – Lists make videos feel more tangible (you know exactly what the video will be about) and they add some curiosity.
Authority – The top 1% have authority and credibility when it comes to successful habits.
Desire – This creator’s audience wants to be a top 1% woman.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience about the habits of the top 1% in your niche.
Examples of this framework in action:

7 Habits of Top 1% Golfers
7 Habits of Top 1% Guitarists



Write better YouTube titles
Grow your channel
Save time

​Check out Creator Hooks Pro here​.
“Creator Hooks Pro has helped me generate millions of views on my channel since I started using it by giving me amazing title ideas I would not have been able to come up with on my own. It’s like having a team of title experts at your fingertips.”
– Matt Outlaw, 731 Woodworks, 667K Subscribers


🖼️ Thumbnail 4:
DeWALT Just Changed
Woodworking FOREVER (genius...

709K views + 1 month ago

Hacker’s Handbook


🖼️ Thumbnail 5:
Passwords]

How Hackers Steal Passwords: 5
Attack Methods Explained

854K

1 mont!

Title: How Hackers Steal Passwords: 5 Attack Methods Explained
Framework: How (Bad guys) (Negative action): 5 (Negative action) Methods Explained
​Hook score​: +2196
Why this works:
Negativity – This is all about controversy and the fear of hackers, getting your password stolen, and attack methods.
List – Lists make videos feel more tangible (you know exactly what the video will be about) and they add some curiosity.
Authority – This isn’t just about how your password gets stolen, it’s about how Hackers do it, which brings some credibility and authority.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience how the bad guys do something, and explain the specific methods they use.
Examples of this framework in action:

How Credit Card Companies Trap You: 5 Debt Tactics Explained
How Bots Spread Misinformation: 5 Deception Strategies Explained

Level Up


🖼️ Thumbnail 6:
WHERE ARE YOU?
upper middle
middle class,

lower class

ACCOUNTANT EXPLAINS: The 6 Levels :
of Wealth In America

360K views - 1 month ago

Title: ACCOUNTANT EXPLAINS: The 6 Levels of Wealth In America
Framework: (Authority Figure) EXPLAINS: The 6 Levels of (Entity)
​Hook score​: +2564
Why this works:
Authority Figure – “ACCOUNTANT EXPLAINS” brings trust and credibility to this video.
Curiosity – Things like levels and rankings are interesting and build curiosity.
Status – This caters to people’s self-interest because it’s all about where you rank compared to others.
How you can use this framework: Have an authority figure explain the levels of something in your niche.
Examples of this framework in action:

PERSONAL TRAINER EXPLAINS: The 6 Levels Of Fitness
TAMPA REALTOR EXPLAINS: The 6 Levels of Neighborhoods In Hillsborough County

Shell Shocked


🖼️ Thumbnail 7:
How To RAISE A CONFIDENT CHILD! :
Without Forcing THEM Out Of Their...

164K views + 2 months ago

Title: How To RAISE A CONFIDENT CHILD! Without Forcing THEM Out Of Their SHELL
Framework: How To (Achieve goal)! Without (Unwanted action)
​Hook score​: +11492
Why this works:
Desire – This creator’s audience would love to raise a confident child.
Refute Objection – Many people might think they have to force their child out of their shell in order to get them to become confident, but this title refutes that objection.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience how to do something without something they assume they have to do, but don’t want to.
Examples of this framework in action:

How To TRAVEL THE WORLD! Without Spending a Fortune
How To RENOVATE YOUR KITCHEN! Without Demoing Anything

Flop of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 8:
What You NEED To Know About
FORGIVENESS In Your 20s

25K views + 2 months ago

Title: What You NEED To Know About FORGIVENESS In Your 20s
Hook score: -90
Why this flopped: I like this title, but if I had to guess, the audience just isn’t into forgiveness.
But here’s the thing: sometimes your audience doesn’t care about your video topic, even though it might be exactly what they need to hear.
In this case, you can try to write the title about the outcome or pain, rather than the actual video subject.
Here are some examples:
“If your damaged soul is holding you back, you need to watch this”
“Once I learned this, I stopped beating myself up”
“The Small Mindset Shift That Changed My Life Forever”
Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
Hope you enjoyed this week’s edition and if you know a fellow creator who needs help writing better titles, please send them to creatorhooks.com.
– Jake
(@jthomas__ on Twitter)
P.S. Read previous editions here.
P.P.S. If you want to write better titles and grow your channel, check out Creator Hooks Pro.
Read last week’s edition »

## Newsletter 81: Youre Used To Winning So Why Are You Struggling With YouTube

**URL:** https://creatorhooks.com/youre-used-to-winning-so-why-are-you-struggling-with-youtube/

```
You’re Used To Winning. So Why Are You Struggling With YouTube? 
June 2, 2025 by admin 


I’ve asked some of the best creators on YouTube, “How do you write such great titles?”
And they’ve all said the same thing: “I just model what works.”
So this email will show you 5 videos worth modeling to help you write great titles and save time.
P.S. If you want to save more time and use a title generator that uses all the titles from this newsletter, check out ​​Creator Hooks Pro​​.
Super Sabrina


🖼️ Thumbnail 2:
most popular song each monthinthe
2020s

431K views + 1 month ago

Title: most popular song each month in the 2020s
Framework: most popular (Entity) (In time frame)
​Hook score​: +420
Why this works:
Interest – People love rankings like this. They’re interesting and they build curiosity.
Time Frame – The time frames of “each month” and “in the 2020s” makes this video more tangible and interesting.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience about the most popular things in a certain time frame.
Examples of this framework in action:

most popular TikTok trends each month in the 2020s
the best NBA team each year in the last 50 years

Too Real


🖼️ Thumbnail 3:


Title: Have Humanoid Robots Gone Too Far?
Framework: Have (Polarizing, progressive entity) Gone Too Far?
​Hook score​: +446
Why this works:
Negativity – People love the controversy of things going too far.
Curiosity – This builds curiosity by asking a question.
Timeliness – Humanoid robots are trending more and more as technology improves.
How you can use this framework: Ask if a trending, polarizing topic has gone too far.
Examples of this framework in action:

Have Betting Apps Gone Too Far?
Have Energy Drinks Gone Too Far?



Write better YouTube titles
Grow your channel
Save time

​Check out Creator Hooks Pro here​.
“Creator Hooks Pro gave me inspiration for the title of one of my latest videos. It’s a 1 of 10 and already over 100K views in 4 days!”
– Ilana, Life Well Cruised 377K Subscribers


🖼️ Thumbnail 4:
ADIN AD CRUIGE HACK
POPULAR CRUISE AAG."
PROHIBITED?!

7 Things Cruisers Get WRONG :
About Banned Items on a Cruise

104K views + 4 days ago

Superior Steak


🖼️ Thumbnail 5:
NOT, PRIME

This Cut Of Beef Is Better And Cheaper :
Than Prime Rib!

250K views - 1 month ago

Title: This Cut Of Beef Is Better And Cheaper Than Prime Rib!
Framework: This (Entity) Is (More desirable) Than (Best current option)!
​Hook score​: +946
Why this works:
Desire – This creator’s audience loves prime rib and something better and cheaper than prime rib sounds awesome.
Curiosity – This builds curiosity because it doesn’t tell you what the cut of beef is, so you have to click to find out.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience that something is better than what they currently like, but don’t specify what it is to build curiosity.
Examples of this framework in action:

This Country Is Prettier And Cheaper Than Italy!
This Camera is Better and Cheaper than the Sony A7!

Ouch


🖼️ Thumbnail 6:
You Were the Smart Kid. So Why Do You Feel So :
Lost Now?

Title: You Were the Smart Kid. So Why Do You Feel So Lost Now?
Framework: You Were the (Accomplished past state). So Why Do You Feel So (Negative emotion) Now?
​Hook score​: +841
Why this works:
Negativity – This calls out a pain point that grabs people’s attention and interest.
Specific Audience – This calls out a very specific audience with “You Were the Smart Kid,” to grab the right audience’s attention and make them feel like this video was made for them.
Curiosity – This builds curiosity by asking a question.
How you can use this framework: Call out the right audience by mentioning a very specific situation, then ask why they’re experiencing a specific pain point.
Examples of this framework in action:

You Were the Athletic Kid. So Why Are You So Out Of Shape Now?
You Used To Be Happy. So Why Are You So Down Now?

Invisible Items


🖼️ Thumbnail 7:
Here's How To Make Your Assets
Invisible From Creditors

110K views + 4 weeks ago

Title: Here’s How To Make Your Assets Invisible From Creditors
Framework: Here’s How To Make Your (Possession) Invisible From (People who want to take advantage of possession)
​Hook score​: +1863
Why this works:
Desire – This creator’s audience wants to make their assets invisible from creditors.
Negativity – This video is about avoiding a problem.
I also think a big part of this video’s success is that the word “invisible” is such a great word. It feels secretive, powerful and cool.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience how to do something to avoid a problem, and use powerful, descriptive language.
Examples of this framework in action:

Here’s How To Make Your Crypto Wallet Invisible From Scammers
Here’s How To Make Your Anxiety Invisible On Dates

Flop of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 8:
f NOLL
Sap Oi

Make This Iconic Sandwich At Home!

6.8K views - 3 months ago

Title: Make This Iconic Sandwich At Home!
Hook score: -75
Why this flopped: If you want to write great titles, you need to know your audience. You need to know their fears, desires, obstacles, and problems.
And keeping that in mind, I think one of the reasons this video flopped is because making this iconic sandwich at home just isn’t a big problem for them.
It might’ve been better to make the focus of the title about a different problem, like price or time:
“Make This Iconic Sandwich For Under $5!”
“Make This Iconic Sandwich In 5 Minutes!”
There’s also the possibility that this creator’s audience is into big hunks of meats, not sandwiches, so maybe this video was doomed from the start.
But that’s why we do thorough research, right?
Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
Hope you enjoyed this week’s edition and if you know a fellow creator who needs help writing better titles, please send them to creatorhooks.com.
– Jake
(@jthomas__ on Twitter)
P.S. Read previous editions here.
P.P.S. If you want to write better titles and grow your channel, check out Creator Hooks Pro.
Read last week’s edition »

```
You’re Used To Winning. So Why Are You Struggling With YouTube? 
June 2, 2025 by admin 


I’ve asked some of the best creators on YouTube, “How do you write such great titles?”
And they’ve all said the same thing: “I just model what works.”
So this email will show you 5 videos worth modeling to help you write great titles and save time.
P.S. If you want to save more time and use a title generator that uses all the titles from this newsletter, check out ​​Creator Hooks Pro​​.
Super Sabrina


🖼️ Thumbnail 2:
most popular song each monthinthe
2020s

431K views + 1 month ago

Title: most popular song each month in the 2020s
Framework: most popular (Entity) (In time frame)
​Hook score​: +420
Why this works:
Interest – People love rankings like this. They’re interesting and they build curiosity.
Time Frame – The time frames of “each month” and “in the 2020s” makes this video more tangible and interesting.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience about the most popular things in a certain time frame.
Examples of this framework in action:

most popular TikTok trends each month in the 2020s
the best NBA team each year in the last 50 years

Too Real


🖼️ Thumbnail 3:


Title: Have Humanoid Robots Gone Too Far?
Framework: Have (Polarizing, progressive entity) Gone Too Far?
​Hook score​: +446
Why this works:
Negativity – People love the controversy of things going too far.
Curiosity – This builds curiosity by asking a question.
Timeliness – Humanoid robots are trending more and more as technology improves.
How you can use this framework: Ask if a trending, polarizing topic has gone too far.
Examples of this framework in action:

Have Betting Apps Gone Too Far?
Have Energy Drinks Gone Too Far?



Write better YouTube titles
Grow your channel
Save time

​Check out Creator Hooks Pro here​.
“Creator Hooks Pro gave me inspiration for the title of one of my latest videos. It’s a 1 of 10 and already over 100K views in 4 days!”
– Ilana, Life Well Cruised 377K Subscribers


🖼️ Thumbnail 4:
ADIN AD CRUIGE HACK
POPULAR CRUISE AAG."
PROHIBITED?!

7 Things Cruisers Get WRONG :
About Banned Items on a Cruise

104K views + 4 days ago

Superior Steak


🖼️ Thumbnail 5:
NOT, PRIME

This Cut Of Beef Is Better And Cheaper :
Than Prime Rib!

250K views - 1 month ago

Title: This Cut Of Beef Is Better And Cheaper Than Prime Rib!
Framework: This (Entity) Is (More desirable) Than (Best current option)!
​Hook score​: +946
Why this works:
Desire – This creator’s audience loves prime rib and something better and cheaper than prime rib sounds awesome.
Curiosity – This builds curiosity because it doesn’t tell you what the cut of beef is, so you have to click to find out.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience that something is better than what they currently like, but don’t specify what it is to build curiosity.
Examples of this framework in action:

This Country Is Prettier And Cheaper Than Italy!
This Camera is Better and Cheaper than the Sony A7!

Ouch


🖼️ Thumbnail 6:
You Were the Smart Kid. So Why Do You Feel So :
Lost Now?

Title: You Were the Smart Kid. So Why Do You Feel So Lost Now?
Framework: You Were the (Accomplished past state). So Why Do You Feel So (Negative emotion) Now?
​Hook score​: +841
Why this works:
Negativity – This calls out a pain point that grabs people’s attention and interest.
Specific Audience – This calls out a very specific audience with “You Were the Smart Kid,” to grab the right audience’s attention and make them feel like this video was made for them.
Curiosity – This builds curiosity by asking a question.
How you can use this framework: Call out the right audience by mentioning a very specific situation, then ask why they’re experiencing a specific pain point.
Examples of this framework in action:

You Were the Athletic Kid. So Why Are You So Out Of Shape Now?
You Used To Be Happy. So Why Are You So Down Now?

Invisible Items


🖼️ Thumbnail 7:
Here's How To Make Your Assets
Invisible From Creditors

110K views + 4 weeks ago

Title: Here’s How To Make Your Assets Invisible From Creditors
Framework: Here’s How To Make Your (Possession) Invisible From (People who want to take advantage of possession)
​Hook score​: +1863
Why this works:
Desire – This creator’s audience wants to make their assets invisible from creditors.
Negativity – This video is about avoiding a problem.
I also think a big part of this video’s success is that the word “invisible” is such a great word. It feels secretive, powerful and cool.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience how to do something to avoid a problem, and use powerful, descriptive language.
Examples of this framework in action:

Here’s How To Make Your Crypto Wallet Invisible From Scammers
Here’s How To Make Your Anxiety Invisible On Dates

Flop of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 8:
f NOLL
Sap Oi

Make This Iconic Sandwich At Home!

6.8K views - 3 months ago

Title: Make This Iconic Sandwich At Home!
Hook score: -75
Why this flopped: If you want to write great titles, you need to know your audience. You need to know their fears, desires, obstacles, and problems.
And keeping that in mind, I think one of the reasons this video flopped is because making this iconic sandwich at home just isn’t a big problem for them.
It might’ve been better to make the focus of the title about a different problem, like price or time:
“Make This Iconic Sandwich For Under $5!”
“Make This Iconic Sandwich In 5 Minutes!”
There’s also the possibility that this creator’s audience is into big hunks of meats, not sandwiches, so maybe this video was doomed from the start.
But that’s why we do thorough research, right?
Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
Hope you enjoyed this week’s edition and if you know a fellow creator who needs help writing better titles, please send them to creatorhooks.com.
– Jake
(@jthomas__ on Twitter)
P.S. Read previous editions here.
P.P.S. If you want to write better titles and grow your channel, check out Creator Hooks Pro.

## Newsletter 83: If youre sick of your channel not growing you need to read this

**URL:** https://creatorhooks.com/if-youre-sick-of-your-channel-not-growing-you-need-to-read-this/

```
If you’re sick of your channel not growing, you need to read this. 
May 26, 2025 by admin 


I’ve asked some of the best creators on YouTube, “How do you write such great titles?”
And they’ve all said the same thing: “I just model what works.”
So this email will show you 5 videos worth modeling to help you write great titles and save time.
P.S. If you want to save more time and use a title generator that uses all the titles from this newsletter, check out ​​Creator Hooks Pro​​.
Ancient Mistakes


🖼️ Thumbnail 2:
N TLEOK LIKE} THIS}

3 FASHION MISTAKES THAT ARE
MAKING YOU LOOK OLDER THAN YO...

79K views « 1 month ago

Title: 3 FASHION MISTAKES THAT ARE MAKING YOU LOOK OLDER THAN YOU ARE
Framework: 3 (Niche) Mistakes That Are (Causing problem)
​Hook score​: +467
Why this works:
List – Lists make videos feel more tangible (you know exactly what the video will be about) and they add some curiosity.
Negativity – This uses negativity two ways:

“Mistakes” grabs your attention and interest.
It calls out a pain point — looking older than you are.

How you can use this framework: Tell your audience about the mistakes they’re making that are causing a problem.
Examples of this framework in action:

3 YouTube Mistakes That Are Making Your Channel Feel Shadow Banned
3 Baking Mistakes That Are Making Your Bread Dry

Real Estate Ranked


🖼️ Thumbnail 3:
San Antonio's BEST & WORST !
Suburbs Ranked

13K views + 2 weeks ago

Title: San Antonio’s BEST & WORST Suburbs Ranked
Framework: (Entity’s) BEST & WORST (Entities) Ranked
​Hook score​: +520
Why this works:
Desire – This creator’s audience wants to hear about the best San Antonio suburbs.
Negativity – The “worst” San Antonio suburbs grabs attention because this audience definitely does not want to move there.
Curiosity – Everybody likes to see how things rank and compare against each other.
How you can use this framework: Rank the best and worst things in your niche.
Examples of this framework in action:

The BEST & WORST Remote Job Sites Ranked
The Best & Worst Planets Ranked



Write better YouTube titles
Grow your channel
Save time

​Check out Creator Hooks Pro here​.
P.S. Here’s a big win from a Creator Hooks Pro customer:
“I got this title directly from Creator Hooks Pro’s title generator. It’s a 1 of 10 and it’s brought in 5 clients worth over $25k in less than 3 weeks.”
– Jeff Hampton, Hampton Law, 360K Subscribers


🖼️ Thumbnail 4:
LAWYER: How Police Get Around :
Your Right to Remain Silent

341K views * 3 weeks ago

Delicious DIY


🖼️ Thumbnail 5:
$0.90 PROTEIN BARS

Title: STOP buying protein bars. Make these instead.
Framework: STOP (Action) (Entity). (Action) these instead.
​Hook score​: +625
Why this works:
Negativity – The big “STOP” in all caps does a great job of grabbing your attention.
Curiosity – This opens a loop and makes you wonder what “these” are. The first part is also a little counterintuitive — why would you stop buying protein bars?
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience to stop doing something they’re currently doing and try an alternative instead.
Examples of this framework in action:

STOP planting tomatoes. Plant these instead.
STOP practicing the piano. Try this instead.

Checking in


🖼️ Thumbnail 6:
How much progress have we made on
climate change?

Title: How much progress have we made on climate change?
Framework: How much progress have we made on (Big problem)?
​Hook score​: +850
Why this works:
Curiosity – This builds curiosity by asking a question many people are probably wondering in the back of their minds.
Negativity – Climate change is a global problem that many people are aware of and interested in.
How you can use this framework: Ask a question checking in on how a big issue is doing.
Examples of this framework in action:

How much progress have we made on homelessness?
How much progress have we made on cancer treatment?

Love Language


🖼️ Thumbnail 7:
aay)
SHE'S \\/ (0716 FOR YOU '°2”

If you are craving love, God wants you
to hear this.

112K views + 1 month ago

Title: If you are craving love, God wants you to hear this.
Framework: If you are craving (Entity), (Authority figure) wants you to hear this.
​Hook score​: +1272
Why this works:
Negativity – This calls out people experiencing a specific pain.
Curiosity – This opens a loop and makes you wonder what it is God wants you to hear.
Authority – I’m not sure if this is just relevant to this niche and this video, but if God wants you to hear something, that’s a pretty powerful sign you need to hear it.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience that if they’re craving something, or experiencing a certain pain point, they need to hear this.
Examples of this framework in action:

If your business is stuck, Hormozi wants you to hear this.
If you are craving more hip mobility, you need to watch this.

Flop of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 8:
Can we Ber
AND ast,

STYLISH?!)

50 AND FABULOUS: COSY COMFY
STYLE FOR WOMEN
5.7K views - 2 months ago

Title: 50 AND FABULOUS: COSY COMFY STYLE FOR WOMEN
Hook score: -64
Why this flopped: The best titles are clear and simple.
This title doesn’t really fit that.
“50 AND FABULOUS” is too clever and vague, and dilutes the main idea of the title: “COSY COMFY STYLE FOR WOMEN.”
Here are some other options we could try:
We could try calling out a pain point and dropping in the current year:
“How To Dress Stylish In 2025 (Without Being Uncomfortable)”
We could add a little curiosity by making it about a secret:
“The Secret To Dressing Stylish AND Comfy”
Or we could use a similar format to the outlier above this section:
“If you want to dress stylish and cosy, you need to watch this.”
Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
Hope you enjoyed this week’s edition and if you know a fellow creator who needs help writing better titles, please send them to creatorhooks.com.
– Jake
(@jthomas__ on Twitter)
P.S. Read previous editions here.
P.P.S. If you want to write better titles and grow your channel, check out Creator Hooks Pro.

## Newsletter 85: Dont Make Your Next YouTube Video Until You Read This

**URL:** https://creatorhooks.com/dont-make-your-next-youtube-video-until-you-read-this/

```
Don’t Make Your Next YouTube Video Until You Read This 
May 19, 2025 by admin 


I’ve asked some of the best creators on YouTube, “How do you write such great titles?”
And they’ve all said the same thing: “I just model what works.”
So this email will show you 5 videos worth modeling to help you write great titles and save time.
P.S. If you want to save more time and use a title generator that uses all the titles from this newsletter, check out ​​Creator Hooks Pro​​.
Explain Everything


🖼️ Thumbnail 2:
6266

COMPRESSION REVERB DELAY

@660

SATURATION CHORUS FLaNser LIE

Every Major Audio Effect Explainedin8 :
Minutes!

104K views + 2 months ago

Title: Every Major Audio Effect Explained in 8 Minutes!
Framework: Every Major (Entity) Explained in (Short Time Frame)!
​Hook score​: +760
Why this works:
Desire – This creator’s audience is into audio effects.
Time Frame – The time frame of 8 minutes does 3 things:

Makes this video feel more tangible and interesting.
Adds to the desire because it’s relatively short.
Adds some curiosity because there’s contrast between “Every Major Audio Effect Explained” (which feels like a lot) and “in 8 Minutes” (which feels like a short amount of time).

Epic – The word “Every” makes this video feel epic and complete.
How you can use this framework: Explain every element of something in your niche in a short time frame.
Examples of this framework in action:

Every Thunderbolts Character Explained in 8 Minutes!
Every Squat Variation Explained in 8 Minutes!

The New King


🖼️ Thumbnail 3:
=
BIGGEST-SNAKE
EVER FOUNL™’,

We Found a Snake BIGGER Than
Titanoboa!

176K views + 2 months ago

Title: We Found a Snake BIGGER Than Titanoboa!
Framework: We Found a (Entity) (Extreme comparative adjective) Than (Entity previously considered most extreme)!
​Hook score​: +781
Why this works:
Epic – People love epic things, and finding a snake bigger than what was previously thought to be the biggest definitely qualifies as extreme epic.
News – People love news and this feels like news.
Curiosity – This makes you wonder what the snake is and how big it is. Plus, it’s a little counterintuitive because this audience knows that there are no snakes bigger than Titanoboa.
How you can use this framework: Share the news that you found or did something more extreme than the previous record.
Examples of this framework in action:

We Found a Pepper SPICIER Than Carolina Reaper!
We Found a Mountain TALLER Than Everest!

Free - | wasn't ready

241K views * 4 months ago

Canned Caution


🖼️ Thumbnail 6:
Don't Eat Canned Sardines Until You :
Watch This

271K views * 2 months ago

Title: Don’t Eat Canned Sardines Until You Watch This
Framework: Don’t (Activity) Until You Watch This
​Hook score​: +2426
Why this works:
Negativity – Warnings like this do a great job of grabbing the audience’s attention.
Curiosity – This is a major cliffhanger and makes you wonder what’s wrong with canned sardines.
Urgency – If you eat sardines, there’s some urgency here to watch this before you eat your next can.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience not to do something until they watch your video.
Examples of this framework in action:

Don’t Post on Instagram Until You Watch This
Don’t Move To North Carolina Until You Watch This

Magic Method


🖼️ Thumbnail 7:
The Reading Method That Changed My :
Language Learning

131K views * 3 months ago

Title: The Reading Method That Changed My Language Learning
Framework: The (Action) Method That Changed My (Desired skill)
​Hook score​: +2640
Why this works:
Desire – This creator’s audience would love to change their language learning.
Curiosity – This builds curiosity two ways:

“Changed my Language Learning” is open-ended and makes you wonder what changed.
It makes you wonder what this method is.

How you can use this framework: Tell your audience about the method that changed your journey.
Examples of this framework in action:

The Listening Method That Changed My Marriage
The Grip Technique That Changed My Driver Swing

World Changer


🖼️ Thumbnail 8:
DA VINCI

AHEAD OF HIS TIME

Leonardo da Vinci: The Renaissance
Genius Who Changed the World

Title: Leonardo da Vinci: The Renaissance Genius Who Changed the World
Framework: (Person): The (Specific description) Who (Epic action)
​Hook score​: +5226
Why this works:
Epic – Phrases like “Renaissance Genius” and “Changed the World” make this video feel exciting and epic.
How you can use this framework: Share the story of someone or an interview, and hype the person up by giving them an epic label.
Editor’s note: If the person you’re making the video about is not that well-known, I’d probably just model the second half of this title (like the second example below).
Examples of this framework in action:

Mahatma Gandhi: The Peaceful Revolutionary Who Changed the World
The Investing Genius Who Predicted Every Recession

Flop of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 9:
My MASSIVE Reptile Enclosure has 23:
Animals Preparing for a STORM!

months ago

Title: My MASSIVE Reptile Enclosure has 23 Animals Preparing for a STORM!
Hook score: -74
Why this flopped: The #1 rule of writing YouTube titles: be clear, not clever.
This title violates that rule.
This video is about “the coolest jungle enclosure you’ve ever seen” (according to the intro).
Making the title about animals preparing for a storm is just trying to be too clever.
If it were me, I’d make the title about how this is the coolest reptile enclosure you’ve ever seen. And I’d probably use the word “reptile” and not “jungle,” because reptiles are the reason people watch this channel.
Now, you could ask if making a video about jungle enclosures was even a good idea in the first place, considering people watch this channel to learn about reptiles, but that’s a conversation for another email.
Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
Hope you enjoyed this week’s edition and if you know a fellow creator who needs help writing better titles, please send them to creatorhooks.com.
– Jake
(@jthomas__ on Twitter)

## Newsletter 86: Dont Make Your Next YouTube Video Until You Read This

**URL:** https://creatorhooks.com/dont-make-your-next-youtube-video-until-you-read-this/

```
Don’t Make Your Next YouTube Video Until You Read This 
May 19, 2025 by admin 


I’ve asked some of the best creators on YouTube, “How do you write such great titles?”
And they’ve all said the same thing: “I just model what works.”
So this email will show you 5 videos worth modeling to help you write great titles and save time.
P.S. If you want to save more time and use a title generator that uses all the titles from this newsletter, check out ​​Creator Hooks Pro​​.
Explain Everything


🖼️ Thumbnail 2:
6266

COMPRESSION REVERB DELAY

@660

SATURATION CHORUS FLaNser LIE

Every Major Audio Effect Explainedin8 :
Minutes!

104K views + 2 months ago

Title: Every Major Audio Effect Explained in 8 Minutes!
Framework: Every Major (Entity) Explained in (Short Time Frame)!
​Hook score​: +760
Why this works:
Desire – This creator’s audience is into audio effects.
Time Frame – The time frame of 8 minutes does 3 things:

Makes this video feel more tangible and interesting.
Adds to the desire because it’s relatively short.
Adds some curiosity because there’s contrast between “Every Major Audio Effect Explained” (which feels like a lot) and “in 8 Minutes” (which feels like a short amount of time).

Epic – The word “Every” makes this video feel epic and complete.
How you can use this framework: Explain every element of something in your niche in a short time frame.
Examples of this framework in action:

Every Thunderbolts Character Explained in 8 Minutes!
Every Squat Variation Explained in 8 Minutes!

The New King

Titanoboa!

​Hook score​: +781

News – People love news and this feels like news.
Curiosity – This makes you wonder what the snake is and how big it is. Plus, it’s a little counterintuitive because this audience knows that there are no snakes bigger than Titanoboa.
How you can use this framework: Share the news that you found or did something more extreme than the previous record.
Examples of this framework in action:

We Found a Pepper SPICIER Than Carolina Reaper!
We Found a Mountain TALLER Than Everest!
