## Newsletter 121: The future of YouTube  2025 trends you NEED to know

**URL:** https://creatorhooks.com/the-future-of-youtube-2025-trends-you-need-to-know/

```
The future of YouTube | 2025 trends you NEED to know 
January 28, 2025January 13, 2025 by admin 


I’ve asked some of the best creators on YouTube, “How do you write such great titles?”
And they’ve all said the same thing: “I just model what works.”
So this email will show you 5 videos worth modeling to help you write great titles and save time.
P.S. If you want to save more time and use a title generator that uses all the titles from this newsletter, check out ​​Creator Hooks Pro​​.
Healthy Baby


🖼️ Thumbnail 2:
| EARLY SIGNS

of

15 Signs Your Pregnancy Is Going Well in
The First Trimester

144K views « 2 months ago

Title: 15 Signs Your Pregnancy Is Going Well in The First Trimester
Framework: 15 Signs Your (Journey or Entity) Is Going Well (In the beginning)
​Hook score​: +913
Why this works:
List – Lists make videos feel more tangible (you know exactly what the video will be about) and they add some curiosity.
Desire – Pregnant women would love to see signs their pregnancy is going well.
New – This one might be a bit of a stretch, but in the first trimester pregnancy is new, exciting, and nerve-wracking, so it’s calling out a specific audience. Plus, later in pregnancy there’s often a clear sign the baby is doing well that you don’t have in the first trimester: the baby kicking.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience about the signs a new thing is going well.
Examples of this framework in action:

15 Signs Your New YouTube Channel Is Going Well
10 Signs A Relationship Looks Promising After The First Date

The Future Is Here


🖼️ Thumbnail 3:
The future of social media | 2025
trends you NEED to know

162K views + 1 month ago

Title: The future of social media | 2025 trends you NEED to know
Framework: The future of (Niche) | (Upcoming or current year) trends you NEED to know
​Hook score​: +1080
Why this works:
Curiosity & Desire – Everybody wants to know what the future holds, but nobody actually knows, so the future is such a great topic because its full of curiosity and desire.
Timeliness – This video was published at the end of 2024, when 2025 was on everybody’s mind.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience about the future of your industry and current trends they need to know.
Examples of this framework in action:

The future of interior design | 2025 trends you NEED to know
The future of gardening | 2025 trends you NEED to know

Stop Writing The Same Boring Titles

Get 30 title ideas in 30 seconds
Beat blank page syndrome
Grow your channel

​Get Creator Hooks Pro here.
P.S. Here’s what one Creator Hooks Pro customer said about Creator Hooks Pro:


🖼️ Thumbnail 4:
Josh Howard
@RealJoshHoward

Enjoying it? We can't live without it! Client video
with your titles is taking off to the moon right now
after a changes?

Views

40.8K ©

16.1K more than usual

@ This video Typical perforrr

Pastor Problems


🖼️ Thumbnail 5:
If You Hear a Pastor SKIP This :
Verse... RUN!! (Here's What They...

548K views + 3 weeks ago

Title: If You Hear a Pastor SKIP This Verse… RUN!! (Here’s What They Are Hiding)
Framework: If You Hear a (Authority Figure) (Avoid Entity)… RUN!! (Here’s What They Are Hiding)
​Hook score​: +2403
Why this works:
Curiosity – This opens a loop and makes you want to know what “this verse” is.
Negativity – This has lots of drama and conspiracy, which people love.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience that if an authority figure they trust is skipping something, they should run.
Examples of this framework in action:

If Your Financial Advisor SKIPS This Step… RUN!! (Here’s What They Are Hiding)
If Your Lawyer IGNORES This Law… RUN! (Here’s What They Are Hiding)

Daily Drawing


🖼️ Thumbnail 6:
One Trick to Draw Everyday with Ease 3

113K views «2 montns ag0

Title: One Trick to Draw Everyday with Ease
Framework: One Trick to (Desirable, but sometimes difficult activity) Everyday with Ease
​Hook score​: +3150
Why this works:
Desire – This creator’s audience would love to easily draw every day.
Curiosity – This opens a loop and makes you want to know what the “one trick” is.
Daily – There’s something about doing an activity every day that’s exciting. It’s tangible and aspirational, so talking about doing things every day can be great to use in titles.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience about one trick to do something they want to do, but is sometimes difficult to stick with, every day with ease.
Examples of this framework in action:

One Trick to Eat Healthy Everyday with Ease
One Trick to Meditate Everyday with Ease

Less is More


🖼️ Thumbnail 7:
Why Studying Less will Give You an UNFAIR
ADVANTAGE.

‘month

Title: Why Studying Less will Give You an UNFAIR ADVANTAGE
Framework: Why (Counterintuitive Statement)
​Hook score​: +3443
Why this works:
Curiosity – This is counterintuitive. How can studying less give you an advantage?
Desire – This leverages desire in two ways:

Everybody would love to study less.
Everybody wants an unfair advantage.

How you can use this framework: Tell your audience how doing something counterintuitive and desirable will give them an unfair advantage.
Examples of this framework in action:

Why Eating MORE Can Help You Lose Weight
Why Running Slower Will Make You FASTER

Flop of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 8:
A Mother Forgives and "ADOPTS"
Her Son's MURDERER... (True Story)

2.3K views + 1 month ago

Title: A Mother Forgives and “ADOPTS” Her Son’s MURDERER… (True Story)
Hook score: -95
Why this flopped: Here are some of the most-viewed titles on this channel:

If You Hear a Pastor SKIP This Verse… RUN!! (Here’s What They Are Hiding)
They “REMOVED” This Verse From The Bible!! (WHY??)
Pastors Want This Video BANNED!! (You’ll See Why)

Two things are clear from these titles:

The videos are about Christianity
The videos are educational

This title doesn’t meet either of those criteria.
It’s a story and doesn’t say anything about Christianity, which is probably a big part of why it didn’t get as many views as most of the other videos on this channel.
Two things he could do:
First, make it clear that this video is about a Christian to attract the right audience:
“Christian Woman Forgives and “ADOPTS” Her Son’s MURDERER… (True Story)”
(Note that we’re using a keyword not to let the algorithm know what this video is about, but to let the audience know.)
The other option is to spin it in an educational way:
“The Bible Verse That Let This Woman Forgive and “ADOPT” Her Son’s MURDERER”
To avoid a flop like this on your channel, know why people watch your channel and deliver on that premise in each video.
Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
Hope you enjoyed this week’s edition and if you know a fellow creator who needs help writing better titles, please send them to creatorhooks.com.
– Jake
(@jthomas__ on Twitter)
P.S. Read previous editions here.
P.P.S. If you want to write better titles and grow your channel, check out Creator Hooks Pro.
```

## Newsletter 123: The 5 YouTube Skills That Will Separate Winners From Losers in 2025

**URL:** https://creatorhooks.com/the-5-youtube-skills-that-will-separate-winners-from-losers-in-2025/

```
The 5 YouTube Skills That Will Separate Winners From Losers in 2025 
January 6, 2025 by admin 


I’ve asked some of the best creators on YouTube, “How do you write such great titles?”
And they’ve all said the same thing: “I just model what works.”
So this email will show you 5 videos worth modeling to help you write great titles and save time.
P.S. If you want to save more time and use a title generator that uses all the titles from this newsletter, check out ​​Creator Hooks Pro​​.
Parental Pointers


🖼️ Thumbnail 2:
Psychoanalyst's Advice For Young Parents |
Erica Komisar

102K views * 2 months ago

Title: Psychoanalyst’s Advice For Young Parents | Erica Komisar
Framework: (Authority Figure’s) Advice For (Noobs)
​Hook score​: +301
Why this works:
Authority – “Psychoanalyst” brings authority and credibility to this title.
Beginners – Calling out beginners, or, in this case, “Young Parents,” is great for grabbing attention because they’re the most eager to learn.
Desire – Young or new parents often appreciate the advice of an authority figure.
How you can use this framework: Share the advice of an authority figure for beginners or new people in your niche.
Examples of this framework in action:

Physical Therapist’s Advice For New Runners
Realtor’s Advice For New Home Buyers In Miami

Aloe Accidents


🖼️ Thumbnail 3:
P

| Stopped Killing Aloe Vera Once | Knew This

400K views + 2 months ago

Title: I Stopped Killing Aloe Vera Once I Knew This
Framework: I Stopped (Unwanted Accident) Once I Knew This
​Hook score​: +645
Why this works:
Negativity – This is all about stopping an accidental problem.
Curiosity – This opens a loop and makes you want to know what “this” is.
Desire – This creator’s audience would love to stop killing their aloe vera.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience how to stop accidentally doing something once they learn “this”.
Examples of this framework in action:

I Stopped Gaining Weight Once I Knew This
I Stopped Slicing My Driver Swing Once I Knew This

Stop Writing The Same Boring Titles

Get 30 title ideas in 30 seconds
Beat blank page syndrome
Grow your channel

​Get Creator Hooks Pro here.
P.S. Here’s what one Creator Hooks Pro customer said about Creator Hooks Pro:


🖼️ Thumbnail 4:
Josh Howard
@RealJoshHoward

Enjoying it? We can't live without it! Client video
with your titles is taking off to the moon right now
after a changes?

Views

40.8K ©

16.1K more than usual

@ This video Typical perforrr

Future-Proof Your Life


🖼️ Thumbnail 5:
19:32

The 8 Al Skills That Will Separate Winners
From Losers in 2025

582K views * 4 weeks ago

Title: The 8 AI Skills That Will Separate Winners From Losers in 2025
Framework: The (Number) (Niche) Skills That Will Separate Winners From Losers in (Current Year)
​Hook score​: +795
Why this works:
List – Lists make videos feel more tangible (you know exactly what the video will be about) and they add some curiosity.
Status – This is about status: are you a winner or loser?
Timeliness – This video was published at the end of 2024 when 2025 was on everybody’s mind.
Desire – This creator’s audience loves AI and wants to get better at using it.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience about the skills that will separate the winners from the losers this year.
Examples of this framework in action:

The 8 Soft Skills That Will Separate Winners From Losers in 2025
The 5 YouTube Skills That Will Separate Winners From Losers in 2025

Uh oh…


🖼️ Thumbnail 6:
PSYCHO STALKER,

Psychopathic Stalker Goes Before the Wrong
Judge

1.2M views » 2 months ago

Title: Psychopathic Stalker Goes Before the Wrong Judge
Framework: (Polarizing Entity) (Action) the Wrong (Entity)
​Hook score​: +1478
Why this works:
Curiosity – This title does a great job of starting a story to grab your attention. It’s a cliffhanger that uses tension and almost forces you to click because you’ve got to see what happens.
Negativity – “Psychopathic Stalker” and “Wrong Judge” bring some drama and conflict to get you interested in this video.
How you can use this framework: Use a cliffhanger to build tension around a story involving dramatic characters.
Examples of this framework in action:

Naive YouTuber Travels to the Wrong Country
Cocky Bully Picks on the Wrong Kid

Life Changing Notebook


🖼️ Thumbnail 7:
Replace your phones

A notebook to replace doomscrolling

416K views « 1 month ago

Title: A notebook to replace doomscrolling
Framework: A (Object) to replace (Problem)
​Hook score​: +8320
Why this works:
Negativity – “doomscrolling” is a problem that many people in this audience resonate with.
Desire – This creator’s audience would love to stop doomscrolling by using something more wholesome, like this notebook.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience about a simple product that fixes or replaces a problem.
Examples of this framework in action:

A keyboard to stop distractions
A drink to replace stress eating

Flop of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 8:
"OUR DAILY
DISASTER"

Judge DiSanto Calls This Court Case Her
“Daily Disaster”

3.5K views * 2 weeks ago

Title: Judge DiSanto Calls This Court Case Her “Daily Disaster”
Hook score: -92
Why this flopped: People make this mistake all the time: using proper names at the wrong time.
Unless the person’s name is a household name, like Elon Musk or LeBron James, it’s often better to use their position, like judge, NBA player, etc.
This is because if people don’t immediately recognize the name, that may cause them to not be interested in the video. On the other hand, everybody will immediately recognize “judge.”
On top of that, it seems like the audience isn’t really into this angle.
Here are a few other angles:
“This Court Case Ruined This Judge’s Life”
This adds a bit more drama and may be too hyperbolic, but people do love drama.
“Drunk Defendant Accidentally Makes His Case A Lot Worse”
Again adding more drama here, but it’s also a bit more interesting because it gives more detail about the defendant, as opposed to the vague, “daily disaster”.
If your initial angle isn’t working, switch it up and try something new, don’t just make tiny little changes.
Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
Hope you enjoyed this week’s edition and if you know a fellow creator who needs help writing better titles, please send them to creatorhooks.com.
– Jake
(@jthomas__ on Twitter)

P.P.S. If you want to write better titles and grow your channel, check out Creator Hooks Pro.
```

## Newsletter 124: The 5 YouTube Skills That Will Separate Winners From Losers in 2025

**URL:** https://creatorhooks.com/the-5-youtube-skills-that-will-separate-winners-from-losers-in-2025/

```
The 5 YouTube Skills That Will Separate Winners From Losers in 2025 
January 6, 2025 by admin 


I’ve asked some of the best creators on YouTube, “How do you write such great titles?”
And they’ve all said the same thing: “I just model what works.”
So this email will show you 5 videos worth modeling to help you write great titles and save time.
P.S. If you want to save more time and use a title generator that uses all the titles from this newsletter, check out ​​Creator Hooks Pro​​.
Parental Pointers


🖼️ Thumbnail 2:
Psychoanalyst's Advice For Young Parents |
Erica Komisar

102K views * 2 months ago

Title: Psychoanalyst’s Advice For Young Parents | Erica Komisar
Framework: (Authority Figure’s) Advice For (Noobs)
​Hook score​: +301
Why this works:
Authority – “Psychoanalyst” brings authority and credibility to this title.
Beginners – Calling out beginners, or, in this case, “Young Parents,” is great for grabbing attention because they’re the most eager to learn.
Desire – Young or new parents often appreciate the advice of an authority figure.
How you can use this framework: Share the advice of an authority figure for beginners or new people in your niche.
Examples of this framework in action:

Physical Therapist’s Advice For New Runners
Realtor’s Advice For New Home Buyers In Miami

Aloe Accidents


🖼️ Thumbnail 3:
P

| Stopped Killing Aloe Vera Once | Knew This

400K views + 2 months ago

Title: I Stopped Killing Aloe Vera Once I Knew This
Framework: I Stopped (Unwanted Accident) Once I Knew This
​Hook score​: +645
Why this works:
Negativity – This is all about stopping an accidental problem.
Curiosity – This opens a loop and makes you want to know what “this” is.
Desire – This creator’s audience would love to stop killing their aloe vera.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience how to stop accidentally doing something once they learn “this”.
Examples of this framework in action:

I Stopped Gaining Weight Once I Knew This
I Stopped Slicing My Driver Swing Once I Knew This

Stop Writing The Same Boring Titles

Get 30 title ideas in 30 seconds
Beat blank page syndrome
Grow your channel

​Get Creator Hooks Pro here.
P.S. Here’s what one Creator Hooks Pro customer said about Creator Hooks Pro:


🖼️ Thumbnail 4:
Josh Howard
@RealJoshHoward

Enjoying it? We can't live without it! Client video
with your titles is taking off to the moon right now
after a changes?

Views

40.8K ©

16.1K more than usual

@ This video Typical perforrr

Future-Proof Your Life


🖼️ Thumbnail 5:
19:32

The 8 Al Skills That Will Separate Winners
From Losers in 2025

582K views * 4 weeks ago

Title: The 8 AI Skills That Will Separate Winners From Losers in 2025
Framework: The (Number) (Niche) Skills That Will Separate Winners From Losers in (Current Year)
​Hook score​: +795
Why this works:
List – Lists make videos feel more tangible (you know exactly what the video will be about) and they add some curiosity.
Status – This is about status: are you a winner or loser?
Timeliness – This video was published at the end of 2024 when 2025 was on everybody’s mind.
Desire – This creator’s audience loves AI and wants to get better at using it.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience about the skills that will separate the winners from the losers this year.
Examples of this framework in action:

The 8 Soft Skills That Will Separate Winners From Losers in 2025
The 5 YouTube Skills That Will Separate Winners From Losers in 2025

Uh oh…


🖼️ Thumbnail 6:
PSYCHO STALKER,

Psychopathic Stalker Goes Before the Wrong
Judge

1.2M views » 2 months ago

Title: Psychopathic Stalker Goes Before the Wrong Judge
Framework: (Polarizing Entity) (Action) the Wrong (Entity)
​Hook score​: +1478
Why this works:
Curiosity – This title does a great job of starting a story to grab your attention. It’s a cliffhanger that uses tension and almost forces you to click because you’ve got to see what happens.
Negativity – “Psychopathic Stalker” and “Wrong Judge” bring some drama and conflict to get you interested in this video.
How you can use this framework: Use a cliffhanger to build tension around a story involving dramatic characters.
Examples of this framework in action:

Naive YouTuber Travels to the Wrong Country
Cocky Bully Picks on the Wrong Kid

Life Changing Notebook


🖼️ Thumbnail 7:
Replace your phones

A notebook to replace doomscrolling

416K views « 1 month ago

Title: A notebook to replace doomscrolling
Framework: A (Object) to replace (Problem)
​Hook score​: +8320
Why this works:
Negativity – “doomscrolling” is a problem that many people in this audience resonate with.
Desire – This creator’s audience would love to stop doomscrolling by using something more wholesome, like this notebook.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience about a simple product that fixes or replaces a problem.
Examples of this framework in action:

A keyboard to stop distractions
A drink to replace stress eating

Flop of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 8:
"OUR DAILY
DISASTER"

Judge DiSanto Calls This Court Case Her
“Daily Disaster”

3.5K views * 2 weeks ago

Title: Judge DiSanto Calls This Court Case Her “Daily Disaster”
Hook score: -92
Why this flopped: People make this mistake all the time: using proper names at the wrong time.
Unless the person’s name is a household name, like Elon Musk or LeBron James, it’s often better to use their position, like judge, NBA player, etc.
This is because if people don’t immediately recognize the name, that may cause them to not be interested in the video. On the other hand, everybody will immediately recognize “judge.”
On top of that, it seems like the audience isn’t really into this angle.
Here are a few other angles:
“This Court Case Ruined This Judge’s Life”
This adds a bit more drama and may be too hyperbolic, but people do love drama.
“Drunk Defendant Accidentally Makes His Case A Lot Worse”
Again adding more drama here, but it’s also a bit more interesting because it gives more detail about the defendant, as opposed to the vague, “daily disaster”.
If your initial angle isn’t working, switch it up and try something new, don’t just make tiny little changes.
Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
Hope you enjoyed this week’s edition and if you know a fellow creator who needs help writing better titles, please send them to creatorhooks.com.
```

## Newsletter 125: YouTube Trends That Died In 2024

**URL:** https://creatorhooks.com/youtube-trends-that-died-in-2024/

```
YouTube Trends That Died In 2024 
January 28, 2025December 30, 2024 by admin 


I’ve asked some of the best creators on YouTube, “How do you write such great titles?”
And they’ve all said the same thing: “I just model what works.”
So this email will show you 5 videos worth modeling to help you write great titles and save time.
P.S. If you want to save more time and use a title generator that uses all the titles from this newsletter, check out ​​​Creator Hooks Pro​​​.
Hopefully Heaven


🖼️ Thumbnail 2:
"going to
heaven"

ut Ye ie i

STOP talking about "going to heaven"

156K views + 1 month ago

Title: STOP talking about “going to heaven”
Framework: STOP talking about “(Common Goal or Desire)”
​Hook score​: +350
Why this works:
Negativity – Telling someone to stop doing something they think is good or is common is a great way to grab their attention.
Curiosity – This is counterintuitive because many people in this creator’s audience think the goal of Christianity is to go to Heaven. The thumbnail text also challenges that assumption.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience to stop talking about something they think is good or is common.
Examples of this framework in action:

STOP talking about “going viral”
STOP talking about “being productive”

The Afterlife


🖼️ Thumbnail 3:
il
AlBeats

Career Advice For A World After Al

463K views » 2 months ago

Title: Career Advice For A World After AI
Framework: (Niche) Advice For A World After (World Changing Event or Technology)
​Hook score​: +387
Why this works:
Desire – This creator’s audience wants career advice.
Timeliness – This is about a current issue: AI disrupting the job market.
Negativity – Calling out problems, like AI disrupting the job market, is a great way to grab your audience’s attention and interest.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience about advice for a world after a big world-changing event or technology.
Examples of this framework in action:

Business Advice For A World After AI
Graphic Design Advice For A World After AI



Write better YouTube titles
Grow your channel
Save time

​Check out Creator Hooks Pro here​.
P.S. Here’s a big win from a Creator Hooks Pro customer:
“I got this title directly from Creator Hooks Pro’s title generator. It’s a 1 of 10 and it’s brought in 5 clients worth over $25k in less than 3 weeks”
– Jeff Hampton, Hampton Law, 360K Subscribers


🖼️ Thumbnail 4:
LAWYER: How Police Get Around :
Your Right to Remain Silent

341K views * 3 weeks ago

Fluency Signs


🖼️ Thumbnail 5:
IF YOU CAN LAUGH,

You are absolutely fluent in English if you
can understand these

222K views » 8 days ago

Title: You are absolutely fluent in English if you can understand these
Framework: You are absolutely (State of achievement) if you can (Action)
​Hook score​: +465
Why this works:
Desire – This creator’s audience wants to be fluent.
Curiosity – This opens a loop and makes you wonder what “these” are.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience that if they can do something, they’ve achieved a goal.
Examples of this framework in action:

You’re in the top 1% if you can lift these weights
You are absolutely a genius if you can solve these problems

Brain Training


🖼️ Thumbnail 6:
hisw? To Train Yoursel* To Become A Genfus =

278K views * 1 month ago

Title: How To Train Yourself To Become A Genius
Framework: How To Train Yourself To (Achieve Goal)
​Hook score​: +505
Why this works:
Desire – Almost everybody wants to become a genius, but this is a more fun, new, tangible way of saying it.
How you can use this framework: Teach your audience how to train themselves to reach a big goal.
Examples of this framework in action:

How To Train Yourself To Become Fluent In Any Language
How To Train Yourself To Be More Charming

RIP Tech


🖼️ Thumbnail 7:
Tech that Died in 2024

525K views + 5 days ago

Title: Tech that Died in 2024
Framework: (Entities) that Died in (Year that just ended)
​Hook score​: +1868
Why this works:
Negativity – This isn’t about the best tech of 2024. It’s about tech that died, and people love drama like this.
Timeliness – This video was published at the end of 2024, so it’s relevant right now.
Curiosity – This makes you wonder what this tech is.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience about the things that died in the year that just ended.
Examples of this framework in action:

Interior Design Trends that Died in 2024
Jobs that Died in 2024

Flop of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 8:
It's Time To Take Your Dreams Seriously -
The Alchemist by Paulo Coelho

10K views + 2 hs ago

Title: It’s Time To Take Your Dreams Seriously – The Alchemist by Paulo Coelho
Hook score: -88
Why this flopped: This title isn’t punchy or tangible enough. “It’s Time To Take Your Dreams Seriously” is just a little too fluffy.
Here are some of the other titles she tried:

It’s Time To Take Your Dreams Seriously
Dreams Showdown: Following Your Heart vs Playing It Safe
Take your dreams seriously before it’s too late.

These are all about a similar angle: taking your dreams seriously.
If your video isn’t doing as well as you hoped, it’s often best to totally switch up the angle.
Here are some options:
“Quit Putting Your Dreams On Pause”
Similar message, but it’s a warning that brings a bit more drama.
“Stop Letting Life Pass You By”
Similar to the “quit” title above, but this is a slightly different way to tap into the audience’s biggest fears and frustrations.
“Read This Book To Make 2025 Your Best Year Ever”
New angle that talks about the benefits of this book and brings some timeliness into it.
Lesson: if a video didn’t do well, come up with titles from new angles that bring more curiosity, fear, or desire.
Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
Hope you enjoyed this week’s edition and if you know a fellow creator who needs help writing better titles, please send them to creatorhooks.com.
– Jake
(@jthomas__ on Twitter)
P.S. Read previous editions here.
P.P.S. If you want to write better titles and grow your channel, check out Creator Hooks Pro.
```

```
Title: It’s Time To Take Your Dreams Seriously – The Alchemist by Paulo Coelho
Hook score: -88
Why this flopped: This title isn’t punchy or tangible enough. “It’s Time To Take Your Dreams Seriously” is just a little too fluffy.
Here are some of the other titles she tried:

It’s Time To Take Your Dreams Seriously
Dreams Showdown: Following Your Heart vs Playing It Safe
Take your dreams seriously before it’s too late.

These are all about a similar angle: taking your dreams seriously.
If your video isn’t doing as well as you hoped, it’s often best to totally switch up the angle.
Here are some options:
“Quit Putting Your Dreams On Pause”
Similar message, but it’s a warning that brings a bit more drama.
“Stop Letting Life Pass You By”
Similar to the “quit” title above, but this is a slightly different way to tap into the audience’s biggest fears and frustrations.
“Read This Book To Make 2025 Your Best Year Ever”
New angle that talks about the benefits of this book and brings some timeliness into it.
Lesson: if a video didn’t do well, come up with titles from new angles that bring more curiosity, fear, or desire.
Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
Hope you enjoyed this week’s edition and if you know a fellow creator who needs help writing better titles, please send them to creatorhooks.com.
– Jake
(@jthomas__ on Twitter)
P.S. Read previous editions here.
P.P.S. If you want to write better titles and grow your channel, check out Creator Hooks Pro.
Read last week’s edition »
```

## Newsletter 127: The 5 YouTube Secrets Every Creator Needs to Learn

**URL:** https://creatorhooks.com/the-5-youtube-secrets-every-creator-needs-to-learn/

```
The 5 YouTube Secrets Every Creator Needs to Learn 
December 23, 2024December 23, 2024 by admin 


I’ve asked some of the best creators on YouTube, “How do you write such great titles?”
And they’ve all said the same thing: “I just model what works.”
So this email will show you 5 videos worth modeling to help you write great titles and save time.
P.S. If you want to save more time and use a title generator that uses all the titles from this newsletter, check out ​​Creator Hooks Pro​​.
The Secret Sauce(s)


🖼️ Thumbnail 2:
The 5 Seuses Every Chet Needs to
Learn

1.8M views + 2 weeks ago

Title: The 5 Sauces Every Chef Needs to Learn
Framework: The (Number) (Entities) Every (Person Who Does Activity) Needs to (Activity)
​Hook score​: +346
Why this works:
List – Lists make videos feel more tangible (you know exactly what the video will be about) and they add some curiosity.
Desire – This creator’s audience wants to learn about delicious sauces.
Curiosity – The way this is written opens a loop and makes you wonder what these sauces are.
“Every” – The word “every” makes this list feel more epic, interesting, and relevant for, well, every chef.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience about the things everyone in your niche needs to do.
Examples of this framework in action:

The 5 Countries Every Traveler Needs to Visit
The 5 Books Every Entrepreneur Needs to Read

Legendary Licks


🖼️ Thumbnail 3:
WRAPPING?.

te

Why Did LEGENDARY Guitarists Do
This?
229K views » 2 months ago

Title: Why Did LEGENDARY Guitarists Do This?
Framework: Why Did LEGENDARY (Professionals) Do This?
​Hook score​: +714
Why this works:
Authority – This video has credibility because it’s about what the legendary guitarists did.
Curiosity – This builds curiosity by asking a question.
How you can use this framework: Ask why the legends in your niche did something, and in the thumbnail show something weird or unexpected.
Examples of this framework in action:

Why Did LEGENDARY Painters Do This?
Why Did LEGENDARY Army Generals Do This?



Write better YouTube titles
Grow your channel
Save time

​Check out Creator Hooks Pro here​.


🖼️ Thumbnail 4:
“My team and | have tried every tool out there for
writing better titles — and Creator Hooks Pro is
the one we come back to time and time again. We
use it for every video."

™ Jay Clouse
Jay Clouse, 110K Subscribers

Cyber Secrets


🖼️ Thumbnail 5:
Don’t do- THIS

7 Cybersecurity Tips NOBODY Tells
You Coutt aire FASY te de}

987K views » 3 months ago

Title: 7 Cybersecurity Tips NOBODY Tells You (but are EASY to do)
Framework: 7 [Niche or Activity] Tips NOBODY Tells You (but are EASY to do)
​Hook score​: +1006
Why this works:
List – Lists make videos feel more tangible (you know exactly what the video will be about) and they add some curiosity.
Curiosity – “NOBODY Tells You” makes you feel like you’re getting in on a secret.
Desire – This creator’s audience wants cybersecurity tips and the “(but are EASY to do)” bit at the end makes these tips even more desirable.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience about the tips nobody tells them about, but are easy to do.
Examples of this framework in action:

7 Gardening Tips NOBODY Tells You (but are EASY to do)
7 Sewing Tips NOBODY Tells You (but are EASY to do)

Anything But These


🖼️ Thumbnail 6:
The Dog Breeds | HATE To Work
With As A Dog Trainer!

597K views * 1 month ago

Title: The Dog Breeds I HATE To Work With As A Dog Trainer!
Framework: The (Entities) I HATE To (Activity) As A (Professional)!
​Hook score​: +1432
Why this works:
Authority – The fact that he’s a dog trainer makes his opinion and this video more credible.
Curiosity – This makes you wonder what dog breeds he’s talking about (and what dog breeds you shouldn’t get — or if your dog is on this list).
Negativity – People love juicy drama and negativity like this.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience about the things you hate as a professional.
Examples of this framework in action:

The Car Brands I HATE To Work On As A Mechanic!
Exercises I NEVER Recommend As A Physical Therapist

Sweet Sweet Sweaters


🖼️ Thumbnail 7:
5 MUST-TRY Crochet Sweater
Petters fer 869 immers!

274K views + 2 months ago

Title: 5 MUST-TRY Crochet Sweater Patterns for Beginners!
Framework: 5 MUST-TRY (Activities) for Beginners!
​Hook score​: +1560
Why this works:
List – Lists make videos feel more tangible (you know exactly what the video will be about) and they add some curiosity.
Desire – This creator’s audience is into crochet sweater patterns.
Beginners – Calling out beginners in your titles is so powerful because they’re the biggest, most eager-to-learn part of any audience.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience about the must-try things for beginners.
Examples of this framework in action:

5 MUST-TRY Piano Songs for Beginners!
5 MUST-SEE Animes If You’re Just Getting Into It

A/B Test of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 8:
A: Cybersecurity for FREELANCERS and SMBs
(7 Tips they DON'T Teach You)

B: 7 Cybersecurity Tips For Freelancers

A:1.53% CTR B:1.86%CTR_ Difference: 22%

Many people overcomplicate their titles and thumbnails.
Here’s an example where simplifying your title to make it more punchy and emotional and focusing on attracting a single group (only freelancers — not freelancers and SMBs), improved the CTR.
If you want to see 100+ other test results like this, you can ​​​get the 103 YouTube AB tests ebook here​​​.
And if you want to run your own A/B tests, I used ​​​thumbnailtest.com​​​ to run this test (affiliate link).
Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
Hope you enjoyed this week’s edition and if you know a fellow creator who needs help writing better titles, please send them to creatorhooks.com.
– Jake
(@jthomas__ on Twitter)
P.S. Read previous editions here.
P.P.S. If you want to write better titles and grow your channel, check out Creator Hooks Pro.
```

## Newsletter 129: These types of videos ALWAYS fail

**URL:** https://creatorhooks.com/these-types-of-videos-always-fail/

```
These types of videos ALWAYS fail 
December 16, 2024 by admin 


I’ve asked some of the best creators on YouTube, “How do you write such great titles?”
And they’ve all said the same thing: “I just model what works.”
So this email will show you 5 videos worth modeling to help you write great titles and save time.
P.S. If you want to save more time and use a title generator that uses all the titles from this newsletter, check out ​Creator Hooks Pro​.
Cool Crops


🖼️ Thumbnail 2:
20 Veggies You Can Plant In
Decamiser RIGA T NOW!

168K views * 2 weeks ago

Title: 20 Veggies You Can Plant In December RIGHT NOW!
Framework: 20 (Entities) You Can (Activity) In (Current Time) RIGHT NOW!
​Hook score​: +335
Why this works:
List – Lists make videos feel more tangible (you know exactly what the video will be about) and they add some curiosity.
Desire – This creator’s audience wants to plant veggies now. But they might be thinking winter is not a good time to plant veggies, so this topic is even more exciting.
Timeliness – This video was published at the end of November and is all about what to do right now.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience about things they can do this month right now.
Examples of this framework in action:

10 Stocks I’m Investing In For December RIGHT NOW!
10 Best Countries To Visit In December RIGHT NOW!

Mindset Magic


🖼️ Thumbnail 3:
BLESSED
7

ADHD Is a Curse... Until You Learn
This

625K views * 2 months ago

Title: ADHD Is a Curse… Until You Learn This
Framework: (Problem) Is (Negative Description)… Until You Learn This
​Hook score​: +385
Why this works:
Curiosity – This opens a loop and makes you wonder what “This” is.
Negativity – Calling out ADHD and saying it’s a curse grabs people’s attention.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience that something they struggle with is a bad or a curse until they learn “this”.
Examples of this framework in action:

Winter Camping Is Horrible… Until You Learn This
Applying For Jobs Is Hard… Until You Learn This

Stop Writing The Same Boring Titles

Get 30 title ideas in 30 seconds
Beat blank page syndrome
Grow your channel

​Get Creator Hooks Pro here.
P.S. Here’s what one Creator Hooks Pro customer said about Creator Hooks Pro:


🖼️ Thumbnail 4:
Josh Howard
@RealJoshHoward

Enjoying it? We can't live without it! Client video
with your titles is taking off to the moon right now
after a changes?

Views

40.8K ©

16.1K more than usual

@ This video Typical perforrr

Watch Out


🖼️ Thumbnail 5:
These wees oi wormer A WEYS
cheat on their men

328K views * 2 months ago

Title: These types of women ALWAYS cheat on their men
Framework: These types of (Entities) ALWAYS (Negative Action)
​Hook score​: +915
Why this works:
Curiosity – This title makes you wonder what types of women these are.
Negativity – Nobody wants to be cheated on, so this leverages fear, pain, and insecurity to grab your attention and get you to click.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience about the types of something that always do something negative.
Examples of this framework in action:

These types of Instagram posts ALWAYS fail
These types of home renovations NEVER turn out good

The Big Bucks


🖼️ Thumbnail 6:
Cae /

3 Reasons Why Your Net Worth
exploces Aitter IGGIK

213K views » 2 weeks ago

Title: 3 Reasons Why Your Net Worth Explodes After 100K
Framework: 3 Reasons Why Your (Possession) (Desirable Change) After (Big Milestone)
​Hook score​: +1027
Why this works:
List – Lists make videos feel more tangible (you know exactly what the video will be about) and they add curiosity.
Desire – This speaks to two exciting desires for this creator’s audience: saving $100K and exploding their net worth.
How you can use this framework: Get your audience excited and tell them that they’ll start making even more progress towards their goal once they hit a certain milestone.
Examples of this framework in action:

3 Reasons Why Your Sub Count Explodes After 10K
3 Reasons Why Testosterone Explodes When You Hit 15% Body Fat

Danger Dudes


🖼️ Thumbnail 7:
THE THREE
MOST
DANGEROUS

PEOPLE

The THREE Most Dangerous People
We Will Eincounter

1.2M views + 2 months ago

Title: The THREE Most Dangerous People We Will Encounter
Framework: The THREE Most Dangerous (Entities) We Will Encounter
​Hook score​: +4730
Why this works:
List – Lists make videos feel more tangible (you know exactly what the video will be about) and they add curiosity.
Curiosity – This makes you wonder what or who these dangerous people are.
Negativity – This leverages fear to grab your attention and get you to click.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience about the three most dangerous (or otherwise scary or negative) things they’ll encounter.
Examples of this framework in action:

The THREE Most Dangerous Foods People Actually Eat
The THREE Most Dangerous Beliefs Christians Have

Flop of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 8:
6 things your (introvert) women
needs you to understand about...

15K views + 1 month ago

Title: 6 things your (introvert) woman needs you to understand about dating her
Hook score: -82
Why this flopped: The most successful videos on this channel are about benefits or fears for the audience:

“These types of women ALWAYS cheat on their men” 329K views
“How to turn any woman on (without touching her)” 294K views
“Giving her distance is your superpower” 120K views

“6 things your (introvert) woman needs you to understand about dating her” is kind of about the audience, but more about their woman. And although the audience cares about their woman, they care about themselves more.
Not to mention, the fact that this is about dating introverted women narrows the audience a bit (which isn’t necessarily bad), but the audience needs to think for a second — is my woman an introvert?
If I were to rewrite this title, I’d try to make it more about the audience:
“If You’re Dating An Introvert You Need To Know THIS”
or
“6 Things You NEED To Know If You’re Dating An Introvert”
Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
Hope you enjoyed this week’s edition and if you know a fellow creator who needs help writing better titles, please send them to creatorhooks.com.
– Jake
(@jthomas__ on Twitter)
P.S. Read previous editions here.
P.P.S. If you want to write better titles and grow your channel, check out Creator Hooks Pro.
```

## Newsletter 130: These types of videos ALWAYS fail

**URL:** https://creatorhooks.com/these-types-of-videos-always-fail/

```
These types of videos ALWAYS fail 
December 16, 2024 by admin 


I’ve asked some of the best creators on YouTube, “How do you write such great titles?”
And they’ve all said the same thing: “I just model what works.”
So this email will show you 5 videos worth modeling to help you write great titles and save time.
P.S. If you want to save more time and use a title generator that uses all the titles from this newsletter, check out ​Creator Hooks Pro​.
Cool Crops


🖼️ Thumbnail 2:
20 Veggies You Can Plant In
Decamiser RIGA T NOW!

168K views * 2 weeks ago

Title: 20 Veggies You Can Plant In December RIGHT NOW!
Framework: 20 (Entities) You Can (Activity) In (Current Time) RIGHT NOW!
​Hook score​: +335
Why this works:
List – Lists make videos feel more tangible (you know exactly what the video will be about) and they add some curiosity.
Desire – This creator’s audience wants to plant veggies now. But they might be thinking winter is not a good time to plant veggies, so this topic is even more exciting.
Timeliness – This video was published at the end of November and is all about what to do right now.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience about things they can do this month right now.
Examples of this framework in action:

10 Stocks I’m Investing In For December RIGHT NOW!
10 Best Countries To Visit In December RIGHT NOW!

Mindset Magic


🖼️ Thumbnail 3:
BLESSED
7

ADHD Is a Curse... Until You Learn
This

625K views * 2 months ago

Title: ADHD Is a Curse… Until You Learn This
Framework: (Problem) Is (Negative Description)… Until You Learn This
​Hook score​: +385
Why this works:
Curiosity – This opens a loop and makes you wonder what “This” is.
Negativity – Calling out ADHD and saying it’s a curse grabs people’s attention.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience that something they struggle with is a bad or a curse until they learn “this”.
Examples of this framework in action:

Winter Camping Is Horrible… Until You Learn This
Applying For Jobs Is Hard… Until You Learn This

Stop Writing The Same Boring Titles

Get 30 title ideas in 30 seconds
Beat blank page syndrome
Grow your channel

​Get Creator Hooks Pro here.
P.S. Here’s what one Creator Hooks Pro customer said about Creator Hooks Pro:


🖼️ Thumbnail 4:
Josh Howard
@RealJoshHoward

Enjoying it? We can't live without it! Client video
with your titles is taking off to the moon right now
after a changes?

Views

40.8K ©

16.1K more than usual

@ This video Typical perforrr

Watch Out


🖼️ Thumbnail 5:
These wees oi wormer A WEYS
cheat on their men

328K views * 2 months ago

Title: These types of women ALWAYS cheat on their men
Framework: These types of (Entities) ALWAYS (Negative Action)
​Hook score​: +915
Why this works:
Curiosity – This title makes you wonder what types of women these are.
Negativity – Nobody wants to be cheated on, so this leverages fear, pain, and insecurity to grab your attention and get you to click.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience about the types of something that always do something negative.
Examples of this framework in action:

These types of Instagram posts ALWAYS fail
These types of home renovations NEVER turn out good

The Big Bucks


🖼️ Thumbnail 6:
Cae /

3 Reasons Why Your Net Worth
exploces Aitter IGGIK

213K views » 2 weeks ago

Title: 3 Reasons Why Your Net Worth Explodes After 100K
Framework: 3 Reasons Why Your (Possession) (Desirable Change) After (Big Milestone)
​Hook score​: +1027
Why this works:
List – Lists make videos feel more tangible (you know exactly what the video will be about) and they add curiosity.
Desire – This speaks to two exciting desires for this creator’s audience: saving $100K and exploding their net worth.
How you can use this framework: Get your audience excited and tell them that they’ll start making even more progress towards their goal once they hit a certain milestone.
Examples of this framework in action:

3 Reasons Why Your Sub Count Explodes After 10K
3 Reasons Why Testosterone Explodes When You Hit 15% Body Fat

Danger Dudes


🖼️ Thumbnail 7:
THE THREE
MOST
DANGEROUS

PEOPLE

The THREE Most Dangerous People
We Will Eincounter

1.2M views + 2 months ago

Title: The THREE Most Dangerous People We Will Encounter
Framework: The THREE Most Dangerous (Entities) We Will Encounter
​Hook score​: +4730
Why this works:
List – Lists make videos feel more tangible (you know exactly what the video will be about) and they add curiosity.
Curiosity – This makes you wonder what or who these dangerous people are.
Negativity – This leverages fear to grab your attention and get you to click.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience about the three most dangerous (or otherwise scary or negative) things they’ll encounter.
Examples of this framework in action:

The THREE Most Dangerous Foods People Actually Eat
The THREE Most Dangerous Beliefs Christians Have

Flop of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 8:
6 things your (introvert) women
needs you to understand about...

15K views + 1 month ago

Title: 6 things your (introvert) woman needs you to understand about dating her
Hook score: -82
Why this flopped: The most successful videos on this channel are about benefits or fears for the audience:

“These types of women ALWAYS cheat on their men” 329K views
“How to turn any woman on (without touching her)” 294K views
“Giving her distance is your superpower” 120K views

“6 things your (introvert) woman needs you to understand about dating her” is kind of about the audience, but more about their woman. And although the audience cares about their woman, they care about themselves more.
Not to mention, the fact that this is about dating introverted women narrows the audience a bit (which isn’t necessarily bad), but the audience needs to think for a second — is my woman an introvert?
If I were to rewrite this title, I’d try to make it more about the audience:
“If You’re Dating An Introvert You Need To Know THIS”
or
“6 Things You NEED To Know If You’re Dating An Introvert”
Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
Hope you enjoyed this week’s edition and if you know a fellow creator who needs help writing better titles, please send them to creatorhooks.com.
– Jake
(@jthomas__ on Twitter)
```

## Newsletter 131: Something is about to happen with YouTube Are You Ready

**URL:** https://creatorhooks.com/something-is-about-to-happen-with-youtube-are-you-ready/

```
Something is about to happen with YouTube (Are You Ready?) 
December 13, 2024December 9, 2024 by admin 


I’ve asked some of the best creators on YouTube, “How do you write such great titles?”
And they’ve all said the same thing: “I just model what works.”
So this email will show you 5 videos worth modeling to help you write great titles and save time.
P.S. If you want to save more time and use a title generator that uses all the titles from this newsletter, check out ​Creator Hooks Pro​.
It’s Going Down


🖼️ Thumbnail 2:
Something is About to Happen in America
(Are You Ready?)

124K views + 6 days ago

Title: Something is About to Happen in America (Are You Ready?)
Framework: Something is About to Happen in [Niche, place or industry] (Are You Ready?)
​Hook score​: +563
Why this works:
Curiosity – This builds curiosity 3 ways:

It’s about the future and the future is a naturally curiosity-filled topic.
It’s secretive and makes you want to know what’s going to happen.
It finishes with a question.

Negativity – This title hints at fear and doom and people love to consume that content.
Timeliness – This is about a current event, which almost adds some urgency to this title.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience that something is about to happen in their niche.
Examples of this framework in action:

Something is About to Happen in Real Estate (Are You Ready?)
Something is About to Change in AI (Are You Ready?)

Stop Writing The Same Boring Titles

Get 30 title ideas in 30 seconds
Beat blank page syndrome
Grow your channel

​Get Creator Hooks Pro here.
P.S. Here’s what one Creator Hooks Pro customer said about Creator Hooks Pro:


🖼️ Thumbnail 3:
Josh Howard
@RealJoshHoward

Enjoying it? We can't live without it! Client video
with your titles is taking off to the moon right now
after a changes?

Views

40.8K ©

16.1K more than usual

@ This video Typical perforrr

Speedy Investments


🖼️ Thumbnail 4:
The CHEAPEST Porsche Models You Can
AFFORD! (that will go UP in value!)

148K views + 1 month ago

Title: The CHEAPEST Porsche Models You Can AFFORD! (that will go UP in value!)
Framework: The CHEAPEST [Desirable, Expensive Entities] You Can AFFORD! (that will go UP in value!)
​Hook score​: +695
Why this works:
Desire – This creator’s audience would love to afford a Porsche, and the fact that they’ll go up in value adds to that desire.
Large Potential Audience – Since this is about the “cheapest” Porsches, it attracts a large potential audience.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience about cheap versions of things that are normally too expensive for them.
Examples of this framework in action:

The CHEAPEST Superfoods You Can AFFORD! (that will SAVE you money!)
The CHEAPEST Exotic Vacation You Can Actually Afford

That’s my kitchen…


🖼️ Thumbnail 5:
10 Mistakes That Make Your Kitchen Look
CHEAP

472K views * 1 month ago

Title: 10 Mistakes That Make Your Kitchen Look CHEAP
Framework: 10 Mistakes That (Cause Unwanted Result)
​Hook score​: +1279
Why this works:
List – Lists make videos feel more tangible (you know exactly what the video will be about) and they add some curiosity.
Negativity – Nobody wants to make mistakes and nobody wants a cheap-looking kitchen, so people have to click on this video to avoid those things.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience about mistakes that cause an unwanted result.
Examples of this framework in action:

10 Mistakes That Make Your Website Look CHEAP
10 Mistakes That Make Your Car Look CHEAP

Suspended Style


🖼️ Thumbnail 6:
Why Men Stopped Wearing Suspenders

818K views + 1 month ago

Title: Why Men Stopped Wearing Suspenders
Framework: Why (Group) Stopped (Popular Thing)
​Hook score​: +2042
Why this works:
Curiosity – Stopping doing something popular or common is a great way to build curiosity because it’s counterintuitive. Why would people stop doing something popular or common?
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience why a group stopped doing something common or popular.
Examples of this framework in action:

Why Men Stopped Smoking Pipes
Why Nobody Speaks Latin Anymore

Always Do This


🖼️ Thumbnail 7:
7 key things to do AFTER uploading your
YouTube video

217K views * 2 months ago

Title: 7 key things to do AFTER uploading your YouTube video
Framework: (Number) key things to do AFTER (Important Activity)
​Hook score​: +3135
Why this works:
List – Lists make videos feel more tangible (you know exactly what the video will be about) and they add some curiosity.
Desire – This creator’s audience wants to grow their channel and wants to know what to do after uploading videos.
Curiosity – Since uploading a video is so important for us creators, this builds a lot of curiosity.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience about key things to do after they do something important.
Examples of this framework in action:

7 key things to do AFTER you workout
5 key things to do AFTER you put in an offer on a house

Flop of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 8:
BEFORE

How To Clean Your Engine Bay For CHEAP!

5.3K views * 1 month ago

Title: How To Clean Your Engine Bay For CHEAP!
Hook score: -87
Why this flopped: I don’t know about you, but I’ve never thought, “Oh, I can’t wait to learn about how to clean my engine bay!”
Now this could be an SEO play and this video may get lots of views over time.
But also, sometimes you have to make a video about an unsexy topic.
Maybe a sponsor requested it, a small part of your core audience begs for it, or it promotes a product you sell.
So let’s brainstorm ways to make this title more clickable.
We could make it more tangible, yet still add curiosity:
“The $10 Cleaning Hack Every Car Lover Needs To Do”
We could add some FOMO:
“The Most Powerful Car Cleaning Step You’re Probably Not Doing”
Or, if we’re sticking with the SEO game plan, we could be a little more specific about the benefits of this video:
“How To Clean Your Engine Bay For $10 In 10 Minutes”
Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
Hope you enjoyed this week’s edition and if you know a fellow creator who needs help writing better titles, please send them to creatorhooks.com.
– Jake
(@jthomas__ on Twitter)
P.S. Read previous editions here.
P.P.S. If you want to write better titles and grow your channel, check out Creator Hooks Pro.
Read last week’s edition »
```

```
So let’s brainstorm ways to make this title more clickable.
We could make it more tangible, yet still add curiosity:
“The $10 Cleaning Hack Every Car Lover Needs To Do”
We could add some FOMO:
“The Most Powerful Car Cleaning Step You’re Probably Not Doing”
Or, if we’re sticking with the SEO game plan, we could be a little more specific about the benefits of this video:
“How To Clean Your Engine Bay For $10 In 10 Minutes”
Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
Hope you enjoyed this week’s edition and if you know a fellow creator who needs help writing better titles, please send them to creatorhooks.com.
– Jake
(@jthomas__ on Twitter)
P.S. Read previous editions here.
P.P.S. If you want to write better titles and grow your channel, check out Creator Hooks Pro.
```

## Newsletter 133: My 5 Greatest YouTube Titles of All Time

**URL:** https://creatorhooks.com/my-5-greatest-youtube-titles-of-all-time/

```
My 5 Greatest YouTube Titles of All Time 
December 2, 2024 by admin 


I’ve asked some of the best creators on YouTube, “How do you write such great titles?”
And they’ve all said the same thing: “I just model what works.”
So this email will show you 5 videos worth modeling to help you write great titles and save time.
The Secret of Strength


🖼️ Thumbnail 2:
Jesus Explained the Secret to Being
Resilient and Strong in Life

187K views + 1 month ago

Title: Jesus Explained the Secret to Being Resilient and Strong in Life
Framework: (Authority Figure) Explained the Secret To (Goal)
​Hook score​: +311
Why this works:
Authority – Jesus is an authority figure that this audience looks up to.
Desire – This channel’s audience would love to be more resilient and strong in life.
Curiosity – People love secrets and positioning your video as revealing a secret is a great way to build curiosity.
How you can use this framework: Reveal a secret to achieving a goal explained by an authority figure in your niche.
Examples of this framework in action:

Elon Musk Explained the Secret to Thinking BIG In Business
Taylor Swift Explains the Secret to Building A Cult Following

Get 30 Title Ideas in 30 Seconds

Beat blank page syndrome
Quit writing the same boring titles
Grow your channel​

Get Creator Hooks Pro here
“Creator Hooks Pro’s title generator helped with this title and this one video has made $3,000 in Adsense alone.” 
– Gjeebs, 209K subscribers


🖼️ Thumbnail 3:
Tesla Gave Me Full Self Driving (FSD) For
Free - | wasn't ready

241K views * 4 months ago

Hidden History


🖼️ Thumbnail 4:
Astonishing Historical Events that Aren't
Taught in Schools

1M views - 3m

Title: Astonishing Historical Events that Aren’t Taught in Schools
Framework: (Epic Entities) that Aren’t Taught in Schools
​Hook score​: +342
Why this works:
Epic – “Astonishing Historial Events” gives this video an epic feeling.
Curiosity – “that Aren’t Taught in Schools” brings some secrecy and FOMO. This makes you wonder what the events are and why they aren’t taught in schools.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience about epic things that aren’t taught in schools.
Examples of this framework in action:

Genius Study Tricks that Aren’t Taught in Schools
Millionaire Money Secrets that Aren’t Taught in Schools

Greatest Hits


🖼️ Thumbnail 5:
ily 10 Greatest Air Fryer Recipes of All Tine =

889K views - 1 month ago

Title: My 10 Greatest Air Fryer Recipes of All Time
Framework: My 10 Greatest (Entities) of All Time
​Hook score​: +474
Why this works:
List – Lists make videos feel more tangible (you know exactly what the video will be about) and they add some curiosity.
Epic – “Greatest of All Time” gives this video an epic feeling.
Desire – This creator’s audience loves air fryer recipes.
How you can use this framework: Give your audience a list of your “Greatest of All Time” things.
Examples of this framework in action:

My 10 Greatest Cleaning Hacks of All Time
My 10 Greatest Productivity Secrets of All Time

Expert Opinion


🖼️ Thumbnail 6:
Firearms Expert Reacts To S.T.A.L.K.E.R.
Franchise Guns

335K views * 1 month ago

Title: Firearms Expert Reacts To S.T.A.L.K.E.R. Franchise Guns
Framework: (Niche) Expert Reacts To (Trending Entity)
​Hook score​: +511
Why this works:
Authority – People love hearing expert opinions on what they’re working on or are interested in.
Timeliness – This video was published right around the time another S.T.A.L.K.E.R. game was released.
How you can use this framework: Get an authority figure’s reaction to something in your niche that’s currently trending.
Examples of this framework in action:

Copywriting Expert Reacts To AI Sales Pages
Personal Trainer Reacts To Horrible New Year’s Resolutions

Tight Tush


🖼️ Thumbnail 7:
FIRM BUTT
& LEGS

WITH 3 SIMPLE yy ip
EXERCISES :
{A SPineCare i 9:35 |

3 Simple Moves to Firm Your Butt and Legs
383K views * 3 months ago

Title: 3 Simple Moves to Firm Your Butt and Legs
Framework: 3 Simple (Activities) to (Achieve Goal)
​Hook score​: +1157
Why this works:
List – Lists make videos feel more tangible (you know exactly what the video will be about) and they add some curiosity.
Desire – This leverages desire two ways:

This creator’s audience wants to firm their butt and legs.
People love short lists of simple things.

How you can use this framework: Tell your audience about 3 simple things they can do to achieve a goal.
Examples of this framework in action:

3 Simple Hacks to Grow your LinkedIn
3 Simple Tricks to Save Money on Flights in 2025

A/B Test of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 8:
A: 10 Curious NYC Secrets Hidden in Plain
Sight!

B: 10 New York City Secrets Most Tourists Miss

4%
3%
2%
1%

0%

A: 3.61% CTR B:4.00%CTR_ Difference: 11%

Explanation: "Most Tourists Miss" in Title B adds
curiosity and FOMO.


Many people try too hard when trying to build curiosity in their titles.
They use too many words and exclamation marks when in reality they’re often better off using simpler, but more emotional titles.
In this example, we swapped out a cliche (“hidden in plain sight”) with some FOMO (“Most Tourists Miss”).
We also simplified it by removing the word “Curious” in the beginning of the title, which made it a little easier to read.
If you want to see 100+ other test results like this, you can ​​​get the 103 YouTube AB tests ebook here​​​.
And if you want to run your own A/B tests, I used ​​​thumbnailtest.com​​​ to run this test (affiliate link).
Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
Hope you enjoyed this week’s edition and if you know a fellow creator who needs help writing better titles, please send them to creatorhooks.com.
– Jake
(@jthomas__ on Twitter)
P.S. Read previous editions here.
P.P.S. If you want to write better titles and grow your channel, check out Creator Hooks Pro.
```

## Newsletter 135: 1 Rule For Winning On YouTube

**URL:** https://creatorhooks.com/1-rule-for-winning-on-youtube/

```
#1 Rule For Winning On YouTube 
November 25, 2024 by admin 


I’ve asked some of the best creators on YouTube, “How do you write such great titles?”
And they’ve all said the same thing: “I just model what works.”
So this email will show you 5 videos worth modeling to help you write great titles and save time.
P.S. If you want to save more time and use a title generator that uses all the titles from this newsletter, check out ​Creator Hooks Pro​.
The One and Only


🖼️ Thumbnail 2:
The ONLY CAMERA you will ever need...
359K views + 2 months ago

Title: The ONLY CAMERA you will ever need…
Framework: The ONLY (Entity) you will ever need…
​Hook score​: +446
Why this works:
Desire – This creator’s audience loves cameras.
Curiosity – This builds curiosity two ways:

This opens a loop and makes you wonder what the camera is and why you need it.
“ONLY” adds a constraint which makes this more interesting.

How you can use this framework: Tell your audience about the only thing they’ll ever need.
Examples of this framework in action:

The ONLY COUNTRY you need to visit…
The ONLY PRODUCTIVITY HACK you will ever need…

“Yes, this is deer, who’s calling?”


🖼️ Thumbnail 3:
#1 Rule For Calling Deer

187K views + 1 moi

Title: #1 Rule For Calling Deer
Framework: #1 Rule For (Activity or Goal)
​Hook score​: +575
Why this works:
Desire – This creator’s audience wants to know how to call deer.
Curiosity – This opens a loop and makes you wonder what the #1 rule is.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience about the #1 rule for doing something they want to do.
Examples of this framework in action:

#1 Rule For Driving The Ball Farther
#1 Rule For Losing Weight

Generate Better Ideas Faster
The best ideas are created by taking topics that have proven to work on your channel and remaking them different ways.
We just updated Creator Hooks Pro so you can now see your proven topics in the generator and generate ideas with just 1 click:


🖼️ Thumbnail 4:
Hey Jake, what's your next video about?

stoicism personal development success productivity motivation

+4 Generate title ideas

If you want to give it a try, ​you can sign up for Creator Hooks Pro here​.
P.S. Here’s a big win from one of our customers:
“Creator Hooks Pro gave me inspiration for the title of one of my latest videos. It’s a 1 of 10 and already over 100K views in 4 days!”
– Ilana Weigensberg Schattauer, Life Well Cruised, 348K subscribers


🖼️ Thumbnail 5:
EE Ee
POPULAR CRUISE HACK

PROHIBITED?!

"4

7 Things Cruisers Get WRONG :
About Banned Items on a Cruise

104K views + 4 days ago


Speedy Supplements


🖼️ Thumbnail 6:
How This Supplement Is Changing the Sport
of Running

489K views + 2 months ago

Title: How This Supplement Is Changing the Sport of Running
Framework: How This (Controversial Entity) Is Changing (Industry)
​Hook score​: +780
Why this works:
Curiosity – This title opens two loops:

“This Supplement” is open-ended and makes you want to know what the supplement is.
“Changing” is also open-ended. What’s changing about it?

Negativity – There’s a hint of controversy here since typically people don’t like supplements changing their sport, and the thumbnail text in red supports the thought that this is a negative change.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience that something is changing your industry, but build curiosity by not specifying exactly what it is or how it’s changing it.
Examples of this framework in action:

How This Instrument Is Changing the Music Industry
How This New Tool Is Changing the Crafting Industry

Bringing the Drama


🖼️ Thumbnail 7:
When Tennis turns into WAR! (Federer vs
Medvedev)

298K views * 2 months ago

Title: When Tennis turns into WAR! (Federer vs Medvedev)
Framework: When [Entity] turns into WAR! ([Authority Figure] vs [Authority Figure])
​Hook score​: +889
Why this works:
Epic – Describing a tennis match as turning into “WAR” makes this feel way more epic and exciting.
Negativity – This title brings a lot of conflict, which people love.
Authority – Federer and Medvedev grab attention here because they’re both popular tennis players.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience about a battle between two big names in your industry and describe it as something turning into WAR.
Examples of this framework in action:

When Camera Releases turn into WAR! (Canon vs Sony)
When F1 turns into WAR!

Daily Dirt Destroyers


🖼️ Thumbnail 8:
10 things people wit> Clean Homes ce DAIY ¢
| Clean Home Habits

187K views + 1 month ago

Title: 10 things people with Clean Homes do DAILY | Clean Home Habits
Framework: 10 things people with (Desirable Possession) do DAILY | (Desirable Possession) Habits
​Hook score​: +2926
Why this works:
List – Lists make videos feel more tangible (you know exactly what the video will be about) and they add some curiosity.
Desire – This creator’s audience wants a clean home.
Authority – This leverages the authority of “people with Clean Homes”.
Daily – Doing something daily makes it feel more tangible and interesting.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience about 10 things people who have what they want do daily.
Examples of this framework in action:

10 things people with 6 Packs do DAILY | Clean Eating Habits
5 things billionaires do DAILY

A/B Test of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 9:
A: How to Make NFTs That Sell

B: How To Make & Sell Your First NFT
Collection

CTR
5%

4%

3%

A:3.96% CTR  B:4.84% CTR _ Difference: 22%

Explanation: In this example, | called out beginners
by making Title B about "your first" NFT collection.

Calling out beginners in your title is one of the easiest ways to get more views.
Beginners are the biggest, hungriest, most eager-to-learn part of any audience.
And when you make them feel like your video was made just for them, they’re more likely to click.
You can call out beginners two ways:

You can be obvious and use the word “beginners”.
You can imply your video is for beginners, like how title B used the words, “Your First”.

If you want to see 100+ other test results like this, you can ​​get the 103 YouTube AB tests ebook here​​.
And if you want to run your own A/B tests, I used ​​thumbnailtest.com​​ to run this test (affiliate link).
Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
Hope you enjoyed this week’s edition and if you know a fellow creator who needs help writing better titles, please send them to creatorhooks.com.
```

## Newsletter 136: 1 Rule For Winning On YouTube

**URL:** https://creatorhooks.com/1-rule-for-winning-on-youtube/

```
#1 Rule For Winning On YouTube 
November 25, 2024 by admin 


I’ve asked some of the best creators on YouTube, “How do you write such great titles?”
And they’ve all said the same thing: “I just model what works.”
So this email will show you 5 videos worth modeling to help you write great titles and save time.
P.S. If you want to save more time and use a title generator that uses all the titles from this newsletter, check out ​Creator Hooks Pro​.
The One and Only


🖼️ Thumbnail 2:
The ONLY CAMERA you will ever need...
359K views + 2 months ago

Title: The ONLY CAMERA you will ever need…
Framework: The ONLY (Entity) you will ever need…
​Hook score​: +446
Why this works:
Desire – This creator’s audience loves cameras.
Curiosity – This builds curiosity two ways:

This opens a loop and makes you wonder what the camera is and why you need it.
“ONLY” adds a constraint which makes this more interesting.

How you can use this framework: Tell your audience about the only thing they’ll ever need.
Examples of this framework in action:

The ONLY COUNTRY you need to visit…
The ONLY PRODUCTIVITY HACK you will ever need…

“Yes, this is deer, who’s calling?”


🖼️ Thumbnail 3:
#1 Rule For Calling Deer

187K views + 1 moi

Title: #1 Rule For Calling Deer
Framework: #1 Rule For (Activity or Goal)
​Hook score​: +575
Why this works:
Desire – This creator’s audience wants to know how to call deer.
Curiosity – This opens a loop and makes you wonder what the #1 rule is.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience about the #1 rule for doing something they want to do.
Examples of this framework in action:

#1 Rule For Driving The Ball Farther
#1 Rule For Losing Weight

Generate Better Ideas Faster
The best ideas are created by taking topics that have proven to work on your channel and remaking them different ways.
We just updated Creator Hooks Pro so you can now see your proven topics in the generator and generate ideas with just 1 click:


🖼️ Thumbnail 4:
Hey Jake, what's your next video about?

stoicism personal development success productivity motivation

+4 Generate title ideas

If you want to give it a try, ​you can sign up for Creator Hooks Pro here​.
P.S. Here’s a big win from one of our customers:
“Creator Hooks Pro gave me inspiration for the title of one of my latest videos. It’s a 1 of 10 and already over 100K views in 4 days!”
– Ilana Weigensberg Schattauer, Life Well Cruised, 348K subscribers


🖼️ Thumbnail 5:
EE Ee
POPULAR CRUISE HACK

PROHIBITED?!

"4

7 Things Cruisers Get WRONG :
About Banned Items on a Cruise

104K views + 4 days ago


Speedy Supplements


🖼️ Thumbnail 6:
How This Supplement Is Changing the Sport
of Running

489K views + 2 months ago

Title: How This Supplement Is Changing the Sport of Running
Framework: How This (Controversial Entity) Is Changing (Industry)
​Hook score​: +780
Why this works:
Curiosity – This title opens two loops:

“This Supplement” is open-ended and makes you want to know what the supplement is.
“Changing” is also open-ended. What’s changing about it?

Negativity – There’s a hint of controversy here since typically people don’t like supplements changing their sport, and the thumbnail text in red supports the thought that this is a negative change.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience that something is changing your industry, but build curiosity by not specifying exactly what it is or how it’s changing it.
Examples of this framework in action:

How This Instrument Is Changing the Music Industry
How This New Tool Is Changing the Crafting Industry

Bringing the Drama


🖼️ Thumbnail 7:
When Tennis turns into WAR! (Federer vs
Medvedev)

298K views * 2 months ago

Title: When Tennis turns into WAR! (Federer vs Medvedev)
Framework: When [Entity] turns into WAR! ([Authority Figure] vs [Authority Figure])
​Hook score​: +889
Why this works:
Epic – Describing a tennis match as turning into “WAR” makes this feel way more epic and exciting.
Negativity – This title brings a lot of conflict, which people love.
Authority – Federer and Medvedev grab attention here because they’re both popular tennis players.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience about a battle between two big names in your industry and describe it as something turning into WAR.
Examples of this framework in action:

When Camera Releases turn into WAR! (Canon vs Sony)
When F1 turns into WAR!

Daily Dirt Destroyers


🖼️ Thumbnail 8:
10 things people wit> Clean Homes ce DAIY ¢
| Clean Home Habits

187K views + 1 month ago

Title: 10 things people with Clean Homes do DAILY | Clean Home Habits
Framework: 10 things people with (Desirable Possession) do DAILY | (Desirable Possession) Habits
​Hook score​: +2926
Why this works:
List – Lists make videos feel more tangible (you know exactly what the video will be about) and they add some curiosity.
Desire – This creator’s audience wants a clean home.
Authority – This leverages the authority of “people with Clean Homes”.
Daily – Doing something daily makes it feel more tangible and interesting.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience about 10 things people who have what they want do daily.
Examples of this framework in action:

10 things people with 6 Packs do DAILY | Clean Eating Habits
5 things billionaires do DAILY

A/B Test of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 9:
A: How to Make NFTs That Sell

B: How To Make & Sell Your First NFT
Collection

CTR
5%

4%

3%

A:3.96% CTR  B:4.84% CTR _ Difference: 22%

Explanation: In this example, | called out beginners
by making Title B about "your first" NFT collection.

Calling out beginners in your title is one of the easiest ways to get more views.
Beginners are the biggest, hungriest, most eager-to-learn part of any audience.
And when you make them feel like your video was made just for them, they’re more likely to click.
You can call out beginners two ways:

You can be obvious and use the word “beginners”.
You can imply your video is for beginners, like how title B used the words, “Your First”.

If you want to see 100+ other test results like this, you can ​​get the 103 YouTube AB tests ebook here​​.
And if you want to run your own A/B tests, I used ​​thumbnailtest.com​​ to run this test (affiliate link).
Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
Hope you enjoyed this week’s edition and if you know a fellow creator who needs help writing better titles, please send them to creatorhooks.com.
– Jake
(@jthomas__ on Twitter)

P.P.S. If you want to write better titles and grow your channel, check out Creator Hooks Pro.
```
