## Newsletter 214: why your YouTube channel isnt growing

**URL:** https://creatorhooks.com/why-your-youtube-channel-isnt-growing/

```
why your YouTube channel isn’t growing… 
February 26, 2024 by admin 


I’ve asked some of the best creators on YouTube, “How do you write such great titles?”
And they’ve all said the same thing: “I just model what works.”
So this email will show you 5 videos worth modeling to help you write great titles and save time.
Btw, if you want to save more time and use a title generator that uses all the titles ever featured in Creator Hooks, check out ​Creator Hooks Pro​.
Snoozin Susan


🖼️ Thumbnail 2:
stop

doing this

710m de)

71500

why you are so tired

2.2M views + 2 months ago

Title: why you are so tired
Framework: Why You Are So (Big Problem)
​Hook score​: +520
Why this works:
Negativity – This is one of the simplest titles you’ll see featured in this newsletter, but it works because it addresses such a big, common problem. And the thumbnail works well with this title because it’s a warning and shows a common thing so many people do.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience why they’re dealing with such a big, common problem, and show a big mistake they’re making in the thumbnail.
Examples of this framework in action:

Why You Are So Poor
Why You Are So Lonely

Canva Cash


🖼️ Thumbnail 3:
$3,995

PER WEEK
=

| Made $3,995/Week With Basic Canva Clip
Art - here's how...

667K views + 1 month ago

Title: I made $3,995/Week with Basic Canva Clip Art – Here’s How
Framework: I (Achieved Specific Goal) with (Simple Tool) – Here’s How
​Hook score​: +607
Why this works:
Desire – This creator’s audience would love to earn $3,995 a week. This title also uses the “a lot for a little” concept: earning $3,995/week is a lot, but “Basic Canva Clip Art” is a little.
Curiosity – This title builds curiosity two ways:
1) There’s contrast between $3,995 and “Basic Canva Clip Art” (the “a lot for a little” concept builds both desire and curiosity).
2) It opens a loop because “Here’s How” invites you in and makes you want to know how.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience how you achieved a big, specific goal with a small or simple tool or effort.
Examples of this framework in action:

I Lost 20 Pounds By Only Walking – Here’s How
I Learned To Code In 8 Weeks By Playing Video Games — Here’s How

Get Your Personal YouTube Coach


🖼️ Thumbnail 4:
Hi, I'm your personal Al coach

How about “5 Easy Tips to increase Your

YouTube Views Today”? It is more specific and er)
has a clearer call to action.

vidIQ just launched their AI Personal YouTube coach to help you grow your channel.
It can help you:

Come up with video ideas
Write better titles
Write your video script
Write your tags and description
And more

If you wanna give it a try, you can get a 30-day trial for just $1 here:
​Try vidIQ for $1​.
​(Affiliate link)
Tax Time


🖼️ Thumbnail 5:
URGENT: DO NOT FILE YOUR TAX RETURN
YET (wait until Jan. 29th)

1M views + 1 month ago

Title: URGENT: Do Not File Your Tax Return Yet (wait until Jan. 29th)
Framework: URGENT: Do Not [Activity With Upcoming Deadline] Yet (wait until [Specific Date])
​Hook score​: +625
Why this works:
Negativity – This title is a warning that works great to grab people’s attention and get them to click.
Curiosity – This opens a loop and makes you wonder why you should wait until Jan. 29th.
Timeliness – The timing of this video was perfect since it was published two weeks before the January 29th deadline and at a time when people start to think about filing taxes.
How you can use this framework: If your audience is about to do something too early, warn them and tell them they should wait until a specific time.
Examples of this framework in action:

URGENT: Do Not Buy Apple Vision Pro Yet (wait until March 19th)
URGENT: Do Not Quit Your Job Yet (wait for this to happen first)

First Class Fancy


🖼️ Thumbnail 6:
Flying First Class to Every Continent in 7
days

9.8M views + 3 months ago

Title: Flying First Class to Every Continent in 7 days
Framework: (Fancy Activity) Every (Entity) in (Short Time Frame)
​Hook score​: +702
Why this works:
Epic – This is a fancy, epic challenge.
Desire – This creator’s audience would love to fly first class to every continent.
Time Frame – “in 7 days” makes this video more tangible, challenging, and interesting.
How you can use this framework: Do an epic challenge in a short time frame.
Examples of this framework in action:

Eating at Every NYC Michelin-Starred Restaurant In 7 Days
We Drove Every Supercar In 7 Days

Thank You Next


🖼️ Thumbnail 7:
@ Apple Glasses

Apple Glasses Release Date and Price -
FORGET VISION PRO! Wait for APPLE...

475K views + 1 month

Title: Apple Glasses Release Date and Price – FORGET VISION PRO! Wait for APPLE GLASSES!!
Framework: (Upcoming Trending Product) – FORGET (Currently Trending Product)! Wait for (Upcoming Trending Product)!!
​Hook score​: +749
Why this works:
I don’t love “Apple Glasses Release Date and Price” in the beginning of this title — it feels like generic keyword stuffing to me. However, there are a few things I like:
Curiosity – “Forget Vision Pro” is counterintuitive because the Vision Pro is the coolest thing out right now, should you really forget about it?
Timeliness – This video is riding the wave of the Apple Vision Pro’s popular release.
Desire – This creator’s audience loves cool tech like the Vision Pro and Apple Glasses.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience to forget the newest trend and wait for the next one that’ll be even better.
Examples of this framework in action:

Best Investment of 2024 – Forget the Stock Market! Invest In This Instead
Forget the 2025 Mustang – Wait for the 2026 Mustang Raptor

Flop of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 8:
15 Genius Printify Hacks In Under 10
Minutes

20K views + 2 months ago

Title: 15 Genius Printify Hacks In Under 10 Minutes
Hook score: -85
Why this flopped: I actually like this title, but two things could be better:
First, the thumbnail text just repeats the title.
This is wasting an opportunity to try another angle to get the viewer to click.
If it were me, I’d show one of the tips and tricks, or an outcome or benefit from one of them.
The second issue is that it seems like it might be too specific.
The video featured in this newsletter earlier was about Canva tips, but Canva is so much more popular than Printify.
Here’s a screenshot from Google Trends comparing the search volume of Canva vs. Printify:


🖼️ Thumbnail 9:
canva rintif .
° @ printity + Add comparison

Search term Search term
United States v Past 12 months ¥ Allcategories ¥ Web Search ¥
Interest over time @ 4

Average Feb 26, 2023 Sep 10, 2023

This creator has had a few successful videos about Printify, but they all seem to be search-based videos published a few years ago, while this has more of a browse-friendly title.
If it were me, I’d either double down on search (something like, “How To Make Money With Printify In 2024”) or be more broad.
I’d do this by talking about making money in general, instead of talking specifically about one tool, like Printify (ex: “10 Genius Side Hustle Ideas In 10 Minutes”).
Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
Hope you enjoyed this week’s edition and if you know a fellow creator who needs help writing better titles, please send them to creatorhooks.com.
– Jake
(@jthomas__ on Twitter)
P.S. Read previous editions here.
P.P.S. If you want to see all of the videos from Creator Hooks in one place, check out Creator Hooks Pro.
```

## Newsletter 215: Surviving The Biggest YouTube Algorithm Change

**URL:** https://creatorhooks.com/surviving-the-biggest-youtube-algorithm-change/

```
Surviving The Biggest YouTube Algorithm Change… 
February 19, 2024 by admin 


I’ve asked some of the best creators on YouTube, “How do you write such great titles?”
And they’ve all said the same thing: “I just model what works.”
So this email will show you 5 videos worth modeling to help you write great titles and save time.
Btw, if you want to save more time and use a title generator that uses all the titles ever featured in Creator Hooks, check out ​Creator Hooks Pro​.
Minecraft Monstrosities


🖼️ Thumbnail 2:
Herobrine

We Survived The Scariest Minecraft Seed...

1.9M views + 4 weeks ago

Title: We Survived The Scariest Minecraft Seed…
Framework: We Survived The Scariest (Thing)…
​Hook score​: +305
Why this works:
Negativity – Surviving something scary brings fear and drama to this title, which people love.
Epic & Extreme – “We Survived The Scariest Minecraft Seed…” is so much more interesting than, “We Survived A Scary Minecraft Seed…”
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience how you survived the scariest thing.
Examples of this framework in action:

We Survived The Scariest Cruise…
Our Garden Survived The Worst Aphid Outbreak…

R2B2


🖼️ Thumbnail 3:
You have a B2 Level in French if you know
these Words | Advanced French Vocabulary

273K views + 1 month ago

Title: You have a B2 Level in French If You Know These Words – Advanced French Vocabulary
Framework: You Have A (Achievement Level) In (Niche) If You (Accomplishment)
​Hook score​: +315
Why this works:
Curiosity – This title opens a loop because it makes you want to know what “These Words” are. It also issues a challenge that makes you want to know what level you are.
Desire – People love learning about themselves and this title leverages that well here. It kinda feels like a smarter version of those “Which Hogwarts house would you be in?” quizzes that everybody loved.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience that they’re at a certain level if they know something.
Examples of this framework in action:

You are an Elite Level Athlete if You Can Lift These Weights
You are a Master Salesman If You Can Overcome These Objections

Get Your Personal YouTube Coach


🖼️ Thumbnail 4:
Hi, I'm your personal Al coach

How about “5 Easy Tips to increase Your

YouTube Views Today”? It is more specific and er)
has a clearer call to action.

vidIQ just launched their AI Personal YouTube coach to help you grow your channel.
It can help you:

Come up with video ideas
Write better titles
Write your video script
Write your tags and description
And more

If you wanna give it a try, you can get a 30-day trial for just $1 here:
​Try vidIQ for $1​.
​(Affiliate link)
Kitty Upgrade


🖼️ Thumbnail 5:
Best 3 FREE Image Upscalers in 2024 +
Comparison!

405K views * 1 month ago

Title: Best 3 Free Image Upscalers in 2024 + Comparison
Framework: Best 3 Free (Tools) In (Current Year)
​Hook score​: +338
Why this works:
List – Lists make videos feel more tangible (you know exactly what the video will be about) and they add some curiosity.
Desire – This creator’s audience loves image upscalers and everybody loves free things. (Plus, free things attract a bigger audience.)
Timeliness – This video was published on December 30th, when everybody was thinking about 2024.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience about the best free tools in your niche this year.
Examples of this framework in action:

Best 3 Free iPhone Games In 2024
Best 3 Free First Date Ideas In 2024

Real Vision


🖼️ Thumbnail 6:
“REALITY [nh

Using Apple Vision Pro: What It's Actually
Like!

25M views + 2 weeks ago

Title: Using Apple Vision Pro: What It’s Actually Like!
Framework: Using (New Product): What It’s Actually Like!
​Hook score​: +550
Why this works:
Curiosity – The contrast of ad vs. reality in the thumbnail is so good here and it makes you wonder what this crazy new product is actually like. Plus, the word “actually” makes you feel like you’re getting in on a secret.
Timeliness – Everybody is currently talking about the Apple Vision Pro and this creator did a great job riding that wave.
Desire – This creator’s audience loves new tech and this is right up their alley.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience what a trending or heavily advertised product is really like, and use the “Ad vs. Reality” thumbnail.
Examples of this framework in action:

Using a Traeger Grill: What It’s Actually Like!
I Bought Kneesovertoesguys’s course: What It’s Actually Like!

Dun Dun Dun


🖼️ Thumbnail 7:
IT'S BEGUN! The Car Market CRASH of 2024

1.1M views + 4 weeks ago

Title: It’s Begun! The Car Market CRASH of 2024
Framework: It’s Begun! The (Negative Event) of (Current Year)
​Hook score​: +1788
Why this works:
Timeliness – “It’s Begun” makes this feel like something just started, and people love news like this. Also, dropping “2024” in the title with this being published in January of 2024 helps reinforce that feeling.
Negativity – “The Car Market CRASH” brings drama and fear to this title.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience about a negative event in your niche that’s just begun.
Examples of this framework in action:

It’s Begun! The Food Shortage of 2024
It’s Begun! The AI Takeover of 2024

Flop of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 8:
How to Speak French like a Lady From
Versailles ? She's teaching me!

46K views - 4 months ago

Title: How to Speak French like a Lady From Versailles ? She’s teaching me!
Hook score: -70
Why this flopped: I don’t speak French and I don’t care to speak like a Lady from Versailles, so maybe I don’t get it… but this title feels way too niche compared to the rest of the videos on this channel about learning French.
If it were me, I’d probably skip this topic and focus on more broadly appealing topics, like tips and mistakes of speaking current-day French.
But since she already made this video, we could try another angle to write the title.
Since the thumbnail is about the right way to cuss, we could follow that angle for the title and write something like:

How To Curse Like A Versailles Queen
Forbidden Words You Couldn’t Say In 18th Century France

(I don’t particularly love these, I’d need to work them some more, but this is just an example of how you can go from broad and boring to specific and spicy.)
Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
Hope you enjoyed this week’s edition and if you know a fellow creator who needs help writing better titles, please send them to creatorhooks.com.
– Jake
(@jthomas__ on Twitter)
P.S. Read previous editions here.
P.P.S. If you want to see all of the videos from Creator Hooks in one place, check out Creator Hooks Pro.
```

```
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience about a negative event in your niche that’s just begun.
Examples of this framework in action:

It’s Begun! The Food Shortage of 2024
It’s Begun! The AI Takeover of 2024

Flop of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 8:
How to Speak French like a Lady From
Versailles ? She's teaching me!

46K views - 4 months ago

Title: How to Speak French like a Lady From Versailles ? She’s teaching me!
Hook score: -70
Why this flopped: I don’t speak French and I don’t care to speak like a Lady from Versailles, so maybe I don’t get it… but this title feels way too niche compared to the rest of the videos on this channel about learning French.
If it were me, I’d probably skip this topic and focus on more broadly appealing topics, like tips and mistakes of speaking current-day French.
But since she already made this video, we could try another angle to write the title.
Since the thumbnail is about the right way to cuss, we could follow that angle for the title and write something like:

How To Curse Like A Versailles Queen
Forbidden Words You Couldn’t Say In 18th Century France

(I don’t particularly love these, I’d need to work them some more, but this is just an example of how you can go from broad and boring to specific and spicy.)
Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
Hope you enjoyed this week’s edition and if you know a fellow creator who needs help writing better titles, please send them to creatorhooks.com.
– Jake
(@jthomas__ on Twitter)
P.S. Read previous editions here.
P.P.S. If you want to see all of the videos from Creator Hooks in one place, check out Creator Hooks Pro.
```

## Newsletter 217: If You Want To Grow Your YouTube Channel In 2024 Try This

**URL:** https://creatorhooks.com/if-you-want-to-grow-your-youtube-channel-in-2024-try-this/

```
If You Want To Grow Your YouTube Channel In 2024, Try This 
February 12, 2024 by admin 


I’ve asked some of the best creators on YouTube, “How do you write such great titles?”
And they’ve all said the same thing: “I just model what works.”
So this email will show you 5 videos worth modeling to help you write great titles and save time.
Btw, if you want to save more time and use a title generator that uses all the titles ever featured in Creator Hooks, check out ​Creator Hooks Pro​.
Call 911


🖼️ Thumbnail 2:
| BOUGHT A WRECKED PORSCHE 911 GT3 &
REBUILT IT IN 24 HOURS

7.4M views + 1 month ago

Title: I Bought a Wrecked Porsche 911 GT3 & Rebuilt it in 24 Hours
Framework: I Bought a (Ruined Fancy Object) & Rebuilt it in 24 Hours
​Hook score​: +314
Why this works:
Negativity – Starting with a wrecked Porsche brings some drama and conflict here.
Time Frame – Rebuilding the car in 24 hours makes this video more tangible, challenging, and interesting.
Curiosity – There’s contrast between “wrecked” and “Porsche 911 GT3” because this is a nice, expensive car. This packaging also opens a loop because you see how bad of shape it’s in in the thumbnail, but it leaves you wanting to know what it will look like in the end.
How you can use this framework: Buy something nice that’s been wrecked and repair it in a short time frame.
Examples of this framework in action:

I Bought a Ruined Rembrandt & Touched It Up In 24 Hours
I Bought a Broken Macintosh Classic & Rebuilt it in 24 Hours

Trash to Treasure


🖼️ Thumbnail 3:
|
11 Things
Not To Trash

11 MONEY-SAVING HOUSEHOLD TRICKS -
Don't throw these out!

549K views + 2 months ago

Title: 11 Money-Saving Household Tricks – Don’t throw these out!
Framework: 11 (Precious Resource)-Saving (Niche) Tricks – (Warning)
​Hook score​: +712
Why this works:
List – Lists make videos feel more tangible (you know exactly what the video will be about) and they add some curiosity.
Desire – This creator’s audience wants tricks to save money.
Negativity – This title finishes off with a warning which makes people even more likely to click because they don’t want to accidentally throw something out that they shouldn’t.
Curiosity – “Don’t throw these out!” builds opens a loop because you don’t know what “these” are.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience about tricks that can help them save a precious resource (money, time, etc) and finish it off with a vague warning that gives them even more of a reason to click.
Examples of this framework in action:

11 Money-Saving Cooking Tricks – Never Buy These!
11 Time-Saving Travel Tricks – Don’t Book On These Websites!

Get Your Personal YouTube Coach


🖼️ Thumbnail 4:
Hi, I'm your personal Al coach

How about “5 Easy Tips to increase Your

YouTube Views Today”? It is more specific and er)
has a clearer call to action.

vidIQ just launched their AI Personal YouTube coach to help you grow your channel.
It can help you:

Come up with video ideas
Write better titles
Write your video script
Write your tags and description
And more

If you wanna give it a try, you can get a 30-day trial for just $1 here:
​Try vidIQ for $1​.
​(Affiliate link)
Seventh Heaven


🖼️ Thumbnail 5:
HOW TO
GET IN THE

OF YOUR LIFE

o

Seven Steps To Get In The Best Shape Of
Your Life In 2024 | Mind Pump 2227

117K views + 1 month ago

Title: Seven Steps To Get In The Best Shape Of Your Life In 2024 | Mind Pump 2227
Framework: Seven Steps To (Achieve Big Goal) In (Upcoming Year)
​Hook score​: +835
Why this works:
List – Lists make videos feel more tangible (you know exactly what the video will be about) and they add some curiosity.
Desire – This creator’s audience wants to get in the best shape of their life. Note that this video isn’t just about getting in great shape, but specifically “the best shape of your life”, which sounds so much cooler and tangible than just great shape.
Timeliness – This video was published at the end of 2023 when 2024 was on everybody’s mind. The New Year is a time when people start to think about getting in shape the most, which also helped this video get so many views.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience about the seven steps to achieve a big goal in the upcoming year. Bonus points if it’s a big goal that many people in your audience are currently thinking about.
Examples of this framework in action:

Seven Steps To Make The Most Money In Your Life In 2024
Seven Steps To Have The Strongest Faith Of Your Life In 2024

Tiger’s Tips


🖼️ Thumbnail 6:
IS GENIUS!

My Lesson With Tiger Woods EX-Coach Is
Going To BLOW YOUR MIND!

608K views « 1 month ago

Title: My Lesson With Tiger Woods EX-Coach Is Going To BLOW YOUR MIND!
Framework: My Lesson With (Authority Figure’s) EX-Coach Is Going To Blow Your Mind!
​Hook score​: +862
Why this works:
Authority – You automatically trust and desire the information in this video because it’s from the greatest golfer of all time’s old coach.
Desire – This creator’s audience wants to get their mind blown with helpful golf tips.
Curiosity – The thumbnail text opens a loop because it makes you wonder what he’s teaching him that’s so genius, so you have to click.
How you can use this framework: Share what you learned from one of the best in your industry’s former coach or mentor.
Examples of this framework in action:

My Lesson With Taylor Swift’s EX-Singing Coach Is Going To Blow Your Mind!
This Lesson From Warren Buffett’s Original Mentor Can Make You RICH

Mysterious Men


🖼️ Thumbnail 7:
If You Want To Know How a Man Feels About
You, Try THIS!

69K views + 1 month ago

Title: If You Want To Know How a Man Feels About You, Try THIS!
Framework: If You Want To Know (Information), Try THIS!
​Hook score​: +1177
Why this works:
Desire – This creator’s audience wants to know how men feel about them.
Curiosity – “Try THIS” opens a loop because it makes you want to know what “THIS” is, so you have to click.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience that if they want to know something, “try this!”
Examples of this framework in action:

If You Want To Know Your Athletic Genetic Potential, Try THIS!
If You Want To Know If Seattle Is For You, Try THIS!

Flop of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 8:
8:46

This Chipping Technique Is So EASY And It
Really Works!

27K views + 5 months ago

Title: This Chipping Technique Is So EASY And It Really Works!
Hook score: -76
Why this flopped: This title and thumbnail aren’t that bad, but I think they could be better.
Here are a few things I’d change:
“It Really Works” is too vague and weak. What does it work for? Give me a juicy benefit that I’ll get using this chipping technique. Or show me that it really works, don’t tell me.
Also, I don’t love using the word “And” in titles because it dilutes the focus and power of the title.
Here are a few other options for a title:

“The Perfect Chipping Technique To Never Land In The Bunker”
“This Weird Chipping Tip Will Change Your Golf Game Forever”
“If You’re Sick of Chipping Into The Bunker, Try THIS”

(Note that the original title has a positive angle, but some of the other options I suggested try curiosity or negativity. If one emotion doesn’t work very well, it can often be worth trying other emotions.)
Another issue is that the title and thumbnail text are too similar: “simple” in the thumbnail and “easy” in the title pretty much reaffirm the same point.
If it were me I’d either try a different benefit in the thumbnail text or try to add some curiosity or negativity.
Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
Hope you enjoyed this week’s edition and if you know a fellow creator who needs help writing better titles, please send them to creatorhooks.com.
– Jake
(@jthomas__ on Twitter)
P.S. Read previous editions here.
P.P.S. If you want to see all of the videos from Creator Hooks in one place, check out Creator Hooks Pro.
```

## Newsletter 219: YouTube Does NOT Like What This Channel Is Doing

**URL:** https://creatorhooks.com/youtube-does-not-like-what-this-channel-is-doing/

```
YouTube Does NOT Like What This Channel Is Doing… 
February 5, 2024 by admin 


I’ve asked some of the best creators on YouTube, “How do you write such great titles?”
And they’ve all said the same thing: “I just model what works.”
So this email will show you 5 videos worth modeling to help you write great titles and save time.
Btw, if you want to save more time and use a title generator that uses all the titles ever featured in Creator Hooks, check out ​Creator Hooks Pro​.
This Is Love


🖼️ Thumbnail 2:
Yeah, The NFL Does NOT Like What The
Green Bay Packers Are Doing.. | News...

333K views + 3 weeks ago

Title: Yeah, The NFL Does NOT Like What The Green Bay Packers Are Doing.. | News (Jordan Love, Jayden Reed)
Framework: Yeah, (Group) Does NOT Like What (Individual) Are Doing…
​Hook score​: +317
Why this works:
Negativity – This title has a feeling of rebelliousness and sticking it to the man that people love.
Curiosity – The title opens a loop and makes you want to know what the Packers are doing. The thumbnail also opens a loop and makes you want to know what’s so genius.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience the story of somebody sticking it to the established authority, and how the established authority won’t like it.
Examples of this framework in action:

Yeah, YouTube Does NOT Like What Elon Musk Is Doing…
The Government HATES What This New Tax Company Is Doing For Small Business Owners…

It’s Magic


🖼️ Thumbnail 3:
Watching Her Paint Will Change How You
See Art!

183K views * 2

Title: Watching Her Paint Will Change How You See Art!
Framework: Watching Her (Activity) Will Change How You See (Entity)!
​Hook score​: +433
Why this works:
Curiosity – This opens a loop because it makes you wonder what it is about how she paints that will make such an impact on you.
Epic – “Change How You See Art” is so powerful because it’s epic and vague (which also adds to the curiosity).
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience that watching somebody do something will change how they see your niche.
Examples of this framework in action:

Watching Her Cook Will Change How You See Dinner!
Watching Him Play Will Change How You See Music!

The Top Seed


🖼️ Thumbnail 5:
Ranking The BEST Seed Companies In 2024?
(#1 Shocked Me) | Value, Germination +...

69K views + 1 month ago

Title: Ranking the BEST Seed Companies in 2024? (#1 Shocked Me)
Framework: Ranking the BEST [Products or Product Providers] in 2024? (#1 Shocked Me)
​Hook score​: +1096
Why this works:
Desire – People love rankings (and this creator’s audience loves s.
Timeliness – This video uses “2024” in the title and was published in late December, right before the new year, when everybody was thinking about 2024.
Curiosity – (#1 Shocked Me) builds curiosity because it makes you wonder what #1 is. I don’t love this type of curiosity building — it feels a bit forced and clickbaity, and reminds me of 2014 BuzzFeed articles — but I feel like I’ve been seeing this a bit more recently.
How you can use this framework: Rank the best product providers in your industry for the upcoming or current year.
Examples of this framework in action:

Ranking the BEST Running Shoe Brands in 2024? (#1 Shocked Me)
Ranking the BEST Airlines in 2024? (#1 Shocked Me)

Chunky Monkey


🖼️ Thumbnail 6:
Make in a Day Crochet Chunky Blanket ! So
FASY @@

169K views * 1 month ago

Title: Make in a Day Crochet Chunky Blanket ! So EASY 🤩 🧶
Framework: Make in a Day (Object) ! So EASY [Relevant Emojis]
​Hook score​: +1192
Why this works:
I wouldn’t necessarily model this title exactly how it is because I don’t like how it’s worded (although “make in a day crochet” videos are popular in this niche), but there are two things we can learn from it that make this title so effective:
Desire – This creator’s audience wants to learn how to make a chunky blanket and this desire is heightened even more because it’s “So EASY”.
Time Frame – The time frame of “Make in a Day” makes this video feel more tangible and easy, and these two also increase the desire.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience how to achieve a goal the easy way and in a short time.
Examples of this framework in action:

Learn Spanish in a Day! So EASY 💃
How To Make Your First Website In 1 Day! So EASY

Stoic Suggestions


🖼️ Thumbnail 7:
Marcus Aurelius’ Advice if You're Tired of
Life

1.1M views + 2 months ago

Title: Marcus Aurelius’ Advice if you’re tired of life
Framework: (Authority Figure)’s Advice if you’re (Experiencing a Problem)
​Hook score​: +1929
Why this works:
Authority & Desire – This creator’s audience respects Marcus Aurelius and wants to hear his opinion.
Negativity – This title calls out people in pain. If this was about “Marcus Aurelius’ Advice if you’re happy as a clam,” it probably wouldn’t do as well because those people wouldn’t be looking for help.
How you can use this framework: Share the advice of an authority figure for people in your niche who are in pain.
Examples of this framework in action:

Steve Jobs’ Advice if you hate your job
Zendaya’s Advice if you’re sick of your acne

Flop of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 8:
Timothy Denevi on Hunter S Thompson and
the Art of Journalism

7.8K views * 2 months ago

Title: Timothy Denevi on Hunter S Thompson and the Art of Journalism
Hook score: -96
Why this flopped: This video was published right before the video above that got 1.1M views — so why did this only get 7.8K?
First off, the title is pretty meh.
In the Marcus Aurelius video, it makes sense to drop his name because he’s so famous.
But Timothy Denevi? I don’t know much about this niche, so maybe I’m unaware, but I don’t think his name brings that much attention.
Even Hunter S Thompson might not have enough name recognition to justify using his name in the title.
And “the Art of Journalism” is a little bland.
It might do better to add some drama, fear, curiosity, or desire into this title. Something like:

The Moment Journalism Changed History
This Is The Only True Shortcut In Life

Ok, bland title aside — there’s a big strategy question we need to ask ourselves:
Should full podcast episodes be on the same channel as talking head videos, video essays, and podcast clips?
It seems like the best channels have one format/video length. MrBeast is all 15-20 minute entertainment videos, Diary of a CEO is all 2-hour long interview videos.
If I ran this channel, I’d probably have a separate channel for interviews like this one.
Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
Hope you enjoyed this week’s edition and if you know a fellow creator who needs help writing better titles, please send them to creatorhooks.com.
– Jake
(@jthomas__ on Twitter)
P.S. Read previous editions here.
```

## Newsletter 220: YouTube Does NOT Like What This Channel Is Doing

**URL:** https://creatorhooks.com/youtube-does-not-like-what-this-channel-is-doing/

```
YouTube Does NOT Like What This Channel Is Doing… 
February 5, 2024 by admin 


I’ve asked some of the best creators on YouTube, “How do you write such great titles?”
And they’ve all said the same thing: “I just model what works.”
So this email will show you 5 videos worth modeling to help you write great titles and save time.
Btw, if you want to save more time and use a title generator that uses all the titles ever featured in Creator Hooks, check out ​Creator Hooks Pro​.
This Is Love


🖼️ Thumbnail 2:
Yeah, The NFL Does NOT Like What The
Green Bay Packers Are Doing.. | News...

333K views + 3 weeks ago

Title: Yeah, The NFL Does NOT Like What The Green Bay Packers Are Doing.. | News (Jordan Love, Jayden Reed)
Framework: Yeah, (Group) Does NOT Like What (Individual) Are Doing…
​Hook score​: +317
Why this works:
Negativity – This title has a feeling of rebelliousness and sticking it to the man that people love.
Curiosity – The title opens a loop and makes you want to know what the Packers are doing. The thumbnail also opens a loop and makes you want to know what’s so genius.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience the story of somebody sticking it to the established authority, and how the established authority won’t like it.
Examples of this framework in action:

Yeah, YouTube Does NOT Like What Elon Musk Is Doing…
The Government HATES What This New Tax Company Is Doing For Small Business Owners…

It’s Magic

See Art!

Framework: Watching Her (Activity) Will Change How You See (Entity)!

Why this works:
Curiosity – This opens a loop because it makes you wonder what it is about how she paints that will make such an impact on you.
Epic – “Change How You See Art” is so powerful because it’s epic and vague (which also adds to the curiosity).
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience that watching somebody do something will change how they see your niche.
Examples of this framework in action:

Watching Her Cook Will Change How You See Dinner!
Watching Him Play Will Change How You See Music!

If I ran this channel, I’d probably have a separate channel for interviews like this one.
```

## Newsletter 221: The YouTube Tip That Changed My Life

**URL:** https://creatorhooks.com/the-youtube-tip-that-changed-my-life/

```
The YouTube Tip That Changed My Life 
January 29, 2024 by admin 


I’ve asked some of the best creators on YouTube, “How do you write such great titles?”
And they’ve all said the same thing: “I just model what works.”
So this email will show you 5 videos worth modeling to help you write great titles and save time.
Btw, if you want to save more time and use a title generator that uses all the titles ever featured in Creator Hooks, check out ​Creator Hooks Pro​.
Not In Here


🖼️ Thumbnail 2:
The Only Instrumental Banned On American
Radio

542K views * 2 months ago

Title: The Only Instrumental Banned On American Radio
Framework: The Only (Thing) Banned On (Place)
​Hook score​: +419
Why this works:
Negativity – “Banned” brings drama and controversy to this title and grabs people’s attention.
Curiosity – This opens a loop and makes you wonder what this instrumental is.
Epic – The word “Only” makes this instrumental feel more special and epic.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience about the only thing banned in a place.
Examples of this framework in action:

The Only Menu Item Banned At McDonald’s
The Only Car Banned From US Roads

Priestly Paradox


🖼️ Thumbnail 3:
» This ,
Contradicted “je é ;
Everything: Shame!

oJ

aS
mS.

The Bible Verse That Spiritually Throat-
Punched Me.

231K views + 2 weeks ago

Title: The Bible Verse That Spiritually Throat-Punched Me.
Framework: The (Object) That (Drastic Action) Me.
​Hook score​: +522
Why this works:
Negativity – “Throat-Punched” and the thumbnail text, “Contradicted Everything” brings some drama here. Plus, “throat-punched” is such visual language that it increases this dramatic feeling.
Curiosity – This opens a loop and makes you wonder what this bible verse is. There’s also contrast between “bible verse” and “throat-punched me”, which builds even more curiosity.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience about something that dramatically surprised you.
Examples of this framework in action:

The Steve Jobs Quote That Changed My Business Forever
The DaVinci Technique That Artistically Throat-Punched Me.

Get Your Personal YouTube Coach


🖼️ Thumbnail 4:
Hi, I'm your personal Al coach

How about “5 Easy Tips to increase Your

YouTube Views Today”? It is more specific and er)
has a clearer call to action.

vidIQ just launched their AI Personal YouTube coach to help you grow your channel.
It can help you:

Come up with video ideas
Write better titles
Write your video script
Write your tags and description
And more

If you wanna give it a try, you can get a 30-day trial for just $1 here:
​Try vidIQ for $1​.
​(Affiliate link)
Fake Fotos


🖼️ Thumbnail 5:
Photographers Who Got Caught Cheating

467K views + 2 months ago

Title: Photographers Who Got Caught Cheating
Framework: (Group of People) Who Got Caught Cheating
​Hook score​: +527
Why this works:
Negativity – People love drama and this title promises a lot of it.
Curiosity – The thumbnail makes you wonder what’s circled and what somebody did to cheat in this photo, so you’ve got to click to find out.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience about people in your niche who got caught cheating, and in the thumbnail show a specific, but not quite clear thing someone did to cheat.
Examples of this framework in action:

NBA Players Who Got Caught Cheating
YouTubers Who Got Caught Cheating

Winter Whoopsies


🖼️ Thumbnail 6:
My
7 Mistakes Beginners Make Camping in
Cold/Winter Weather

105K views » 2 months ago

Title: 7 Mistakes Beginners Make Camping in Cold/Winter Weather
Framework: 7 Mistakes Beginners Make (Activity) in (Hard and/or Current Circumstances)
​Hook score​: +577
Why this works:
List – Lists make videos feel more tangible (you know exactly what the video will be about) and they add some curiosity.
Negativity – This title leverages people’s fear of making mistakes. Plus, it’s about a difficult time to camp — “7 Mistakes Beginners Make Camping In Beautiful Weather” probably wouldn’t do as well.
Beginners – Calling out beginners works so well because beginners are the biggest subset of any audience and they’re usually the most eager to learn.
Timeliness – This video was published at the beginning of winter when camping in cold/winter weather was on everyone’s mind.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience about mistakes beginners make when doing a tough activity when it’s trending most.
Examples of this framework in action:

7 Mistakes Beginners Make Eating Healthy During The Holidays
7 Mistakes Beginners Make Filing Their Taxes

Empty Air


🖼️ Thumbnail 7:
STOP Throwing Away Aerosol Spray Cans
With No AIR! How To Recharge, Save And F...

1.4M views + 2 months ago

Title: STOP Throwing Away Aerosol Spray Cans With No AIR! How To Recharge, Save And Fix It! DIY
Framework: STOP (Common Thing)! How To (Achieve Goal)
​Hook score​: +2153
Why this works:
Negativity – This title starts with a warning against doing something most people do.
Curiosity – It’s counterintuitive to stop throwing away empty aerosol spray cans. They don’t have any air, so of course you should throw them away, right?
Desire – This title finishes by helping the audience get something they want.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience to stop doing something that they think makes sense to do, and offer them a better solution to get what they want.
Examples of this framework in action:

STOP Looking Up Flights on Google! How To Get Cheap Plane Tickets
STOP Trying To Lift More Weight! How To Get Stronger & More Athletic

Flop of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 8:
What | Found in One of the Smallest
Countries in the World

4.7K views + 2 months ago

Title: What I Found in One of the Smallest Countries in the World
Hook score: -85
Why this flopped: There are a few things this creator could’ve done to make this packaging better:
The biggest opportunity is to make the thumbnail and title work better together.
The title sparks a question (“What did he find?”), but it feels like the thumbnail answers it with the image of the big furry bull/yak thing.
In the “Photographers Who Got Caught Cheating” thumbnail above, there’s a little circle that the creator is pointing to that makes you say, “What’s that?”
This creator could’ve done something similar.
Instead of having the bull/yak, there could’ve been an arrow pointing to the city with text like, “So Weird” or something to make you wonder what he found.
The title also could’ve been a little punchier. I don’t like using “one of the” in titles.
Extreme things work well on YouTube and “one of the” waters that down.
Instead, I try to make something the most extreme in its category.
In this title, instead of “One of the Smallest Countries in the World”, I would’ve said “Europe’s Smallest Country”.
If that doesn’t make sense or isn’t true, then I’d either try to put it in a different category or try a different angle.
Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
Hope you enjoyed this week’s edition and if you know a fellow creator who needs help writing better titles, please send them to creatorhooks.com.
– Jake
(@jthomas__ on Twitter)
P.S. Read previous editions here.
P.P.S. If you want to see all of the videos from Creator Hooks in one place, check out Creator Hooks Pro.
```

## Newsletter 222: The YouTube Tip That Changed My Life

**URL:** https://creatorhooks.com/the-youtube-tip-that-changed-my-life/

```
The YouTube Tip That Changed My Life 
January 29, 2024 by admin 


I’ve asked some of the best creators on YouTube, “How do you write such great titles?”
And they’ve all said the same thing: “I just model what works.”
So this email will show you 5 videos worth modeling to help you write great titles and save time.
Btw, if you want to save more time and use a title generator that uses all the titles ever featured in Creator Hooks, check out ​Creator Hooks Pro​.
Not In Here


🖼️ Thumbnail 2:
The Only Instrumental Banned On American
Radio

542K views * 2 months ago

Title: The Only Instrumental Banned On American Radio
Framework: The Only (Thing) Banned On (Place)
​Hook score​: +419
Why this works:
Negativity – “Banned” brings drama and controversy to this title and grabs people’s attention.
Curiosity – This opens a loop and makes you wonder what this instrumental is.
Epic – The word “Only” makes this instrumental feel more special and epic.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience about the only thing banned in a place.
Examples of this framework in action:

The Only Menu Item Banned At McDonald’s
The Only Car Banned From US Roads

Priestly Paradox


🖼️ Thumbnail 3:
» This ,
Contradicted “je é ;
Everything: Shame!

oJ

aS
mS.

The Bible Verse That Spiritually Throat-
Punched Me.

231K views + 2 weeks ago

Title: The Bible Verse That Spiritually Throat-Punched Me.
Framework: The (Object) That (Drastic Action) Me.
​Hook score​: +522
Why this works:
Negativity – “Throat-Punched” and the thumbnail text, “Contradicted Everything” brings some drama here. Plus, “throat-punched” is such visual language that it increases this dramatic feeling.
Curiosity – This opens a loop and makes you wonder what this bible verse is. There’s also contrast between “bible verse” and “throat-punched me”, which builds even more curiosity.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience about something that dramatically surprised you.
Examples of this framework in action:

The Steve Jobs Quote That Changed My Business Forever
The DaVinci Technique That Artistically Throat-Punched Me.

Get Your Personal YouTube Coach


🖼️ Thumbnail 4:
Hi, I'm your personal Al coach

How about “5 Easy Tips to increase Your

YouTube Views Today”? It is more specific and er)
has a clearer call to action.

vidIQ just launched their AI Personal YouTube coach to help you grow your channel.
It can help you:

Come up with video ideas
Write better titles
Write your video script
Write your tags and description
And more

If you wanna give it a try, you can get a 30-day trial for just $1 here:
​Try vidIQ for $1​.
​(Affiliate link)
Fake Fotos


🖼️ Thumbnail 5:
Photographers Who Got Caught Cheating

467K views + 2 months ago

Title: Photographers Who Got Caught Cheating
Framework: (Group of People) Who Got Caught Cheating
​Hook score​: +527
Why this works:
Negativity – People love drama and this title promises a lot of it.
Curiosity – The thumbnail makes you wonder what’s circled and what somebody did to cheat in this photo, so you’ve got to click to find out.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience about people in your niche who got caught cheating, and in the thumbnail show a specific, but not quite clear thing someone did to cheat.
Examples of this framework in action:

NBA Players Who Got Caught Cheating
YouTubers Who Got Caught Cheating

Winter Whoopsies


🖼️ Thumbnail 6:
My
7 Mistakes Beginners Make Camping in
Cold/Winter Weather

105K views » 2 months ago

Title: 7 Mistakes Beginners Make Camping in Cold/Winter Weather
Framework: 7 Mistakes Beginners Make (Activity) in (Hard and/or Current Circumstances)
​Hook score​: +577
Why this works:
List – Lists make videos feel more tangible (you know exactly what the video will be about) and they add some curiosity.
Negativity – This title leverages people’s fear of making mistakes. Plus, it’s about a difficult time to camp — “7 Mistakes Beginners Make Camping In Beautiful Weather” probably wouldn’t do as well.
Beginners – Calling out beginners works so well because beginners are the biggest subset of any audience and they’re usually the most eager to learn.
Timeliness – This video was published at the beginning of winter when camping in cold/winter weather was on everyone’s mind.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience about mistakes beginners make when doing a tough activity when it’s trending most.
Examples of this framework in action:

7 Mistakes Beginners Make Eating Healthy During The Holidays
7 Mistakes Beginners Make Filing Their Taxes

Empty Air


🖼️ Thumbnail 7:
STOP Throwing Away Aerosol Spray Cans
With No AIR! How To Recharge, Save And F...

1.4M views + 2 months ago

Title: STOP Throwing Away Aerosol Spray Cans With No AIR! How To Recharge, Save And Fix It! DIY
Framework: STOP (Common Thing)! How To (Achieve Goal)
​Hook score​: +2153
Why this works:
Negativity – This title starts with a warning against doing something most people do.
Curiosity – It’s counterintuitive to stop throwing away empty aerosol spray cans. They don’t have any air, so of course you should throw them away, right?
Desire – This title finishes by helping the audience get something they want.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience to stop doing something that they think makes sense to do, and offer them a better solution to get what they want.
Examples of this framework in action:

STOP Looking Up Flights on Google! How To Get Cheap Plane Tickets
STOP Trying To Lift More Weight! How To Get Stronger & More Athletic

Flop of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 8:
What | Found in One of the Smallest
Countries in the World

4.7K views + 2 months ago

Title: What I Found in One of the Smallest Countries in the World
Hook score: -85
Why this flopped: There are a few things this creator could’ve done to make this packaging better:
The biggest opportunity is to make the thumbnail and title work better together.
The title sparks a question (“What did he find?”), but it feels like the thumbnail answers it with the image of the big furry bull/yak thing.
In the “Photographers Who Got Caught Cheating” thumbnail above, there’s a little circle that the creator is pointing to that makes you say, “What’s that?”
This creator could’ve done something similar.
Instead of having the bull/yak, there could’ve been an arrow pointing to the city with text like, “So Weird” or something to make you wonder what he found.
The title also could’ve been a little punchier. I don’t like using “one of the” in titles.
Extreme things work well on YouTube and “one of the” waters that down.
Instead, I try to make something the most extreme in its category.
In this title, instead of “One of the Smallest Countries in the World”, I would’ve said “Europe’s Smallest Country”.
If that doesn’t make sense or isn’t true, then I’d either try to put it in a different category or try a different angle.
Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
Hope you enjoyed this week’s edition and if you know a fellow creator who needs help writing better titles, please send them to creatorhooks.com.
– Jake
(@jthomas__ on Twitter)
P.S. Read previous editions here.
P.P.S. If you want to see all of the videos from Creator Hooks in one place, check out Creator Hooks Pro.
```

## Newsletter 223: STOP Doing This To Get More YouTube Subscribers

**URL:** https://creatorhooks.com/stop-doing-this-to-get-more-youtube-subscribers/

```
STOP Doing This To Get More YouTube Subscribers! 
January 22, 2024 by admin 


I’ve asked some of the best creators on YouTube, “How do you write such great titles?”
And they’ve all said the same thing: “I just model what works.”
So this email will show you 5 videos worth modeling to help you write great titles and save time.
Btw, if you want to save more time and use a title generator that uses all the titles ever featured in Creator Hooks, check out ​Creator Hooks Pro​.
Back Breaker


🖼️ Thumbnail 2:
BAD IDEA

STOP Doing These Exercises For Low Back
Pain!

786K views + 2 months ago

Title: STOP Doing These Exercises For Low Back Pain!
Framework: STOP Doing These (Solutions) For (Problem)!
​Hook score​: +367
Why this works:
Curiosity – This is counterintuitive because the thumbnail shows something that many think will help their back pain, but the title and thumbnail text say to stop doing it. You can also think of this as challenging your audience’s assumptions.
Negativity – This leverages fear and is a warning, which grabs people’s attention. It also talks about a major pain point lots of people have: low back pain.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience to stop doing something they assume will help them with their problems, but is actually bad for them.
Examples of this framework in action:

STOP Doing This To Get More YouTube Subscribers!
STOP Using Treats To Train Your Dog!

Surprise Surprise


🖼️ Thumbnail 3:
90 Year Olds Share Advice For Their Younger
Self

432K views + 2 months ago

Title: 90 Year Olds Share Advice for Their Younger Self
Framework: (Experienced People) Share Advice For Their (Past Selves)
​Hook score​: +410
Why this works:
Curiosity – The thumbnail text builds curiosity because many people assume getting married is good, but this old guy says don’t do it, so it’s counterintuitive. Plus, he’s with a woman who you would assume is his wife, which builds even more curiosity.
Negativity – The thumbnail text is a warning and people love warnings.
Credibility – This has credibility because 90-year-olds have lots of life experience. Plus, sharing advice for their “younger selves”.
Desire – Everybody loves to hear advice from old people to their younger selves.
How you can use this framework: Ask experienced people in your niche for advice they’d give to their past selves, and in the thumbnail text share an unexpected snippet.
Examples of this framework in action:

NBA Players Share Advice for Their Younger Self
Moms Share Advice for Their Pre-Children Self

Get Your Personal YouTube Coach


🖼️ Thumbnail 4:
Hi, I'm your personal Al coach

How about “5 Easy Tips to increase Your

YouTube Views Today”? It is more specific and er)
has a clearer call to action.

vidIQ just launched their AI Personal YouTube coach to help you grow your channel.
It can help you:

Come up with video ideas
Write better titles
Write your video script
Write your tags and description
And more

If you wanna give it a try, you can get a 30-day trial for just $1 here:
​Try vidIQ for $1​.
​(Affiliate link)
In A Galaxy Far, Far Away


🖼️ Thumbnail 5:
First 10 Things To Do - Samsung Galaxy ONE
UI6 (tips and tricks )

137K views » 1 month ago

Title: First 10 Things To Do – Samsung Galaxy ONE UI6 ( tips and tricks )
Framework: First 10 Things To Do – [New Product] ( tips and tricks )
​Hook score​: +850
Why this works:
List – Lists make videos feel more tangible (you know exactly what the video will be about) and they add some curiosity.
Desire – This creator’s audience wants tips and tricks for their new Galaxy ONE.
Beginners – Calling out the “first” things to do works so well because people who just got a new phone are excited to see all it can do.
How you can use this framework: If you make review videos, this is a fun twist to mix it up — talk about the first things your audience should do, not just the top 10 features.
Examples of this framework in action:

First 5 Things To Do when you get a New Job ( tips and tricks )
First 10 things to do after you buy your first house ( tips and tricks )

Lunch Lady Special


🖼️ Thumbnail 6:
| Cooked 100 Years of School Lunch

15M views +» 3 months ago

Title: I Cooked 100 Years of School Lunch
Framework: I (Activity) 100 Years of (Object)
​Hook score​: +2500
Why this works:
Curiosity – The contrast of the old and new lunches builds curiosity.
Time Frame – 100 Years of lunches makes this video more tangible, and therefore, more interesting.
Epic – 100 Years makes this video feel big and epic.
How you can use this framework: Take your audience through the last 100 years of stuff in your niche.
Examples of this framework in action:

I Played 50 Years of Video Games
I Took 100 Years of Photographs

Uno Reverse


🖼️ Thumbnail 7:
THIS ISN'T
LUXURY WY

Luxury Fashion Is For Broke People

2.3M views + 2 months ago

Title: Luxury Fashion Is For Broke People
Framework: (Extreme Thing) Is For (Opposite Extreme)
​Hook score​: +40740
Why this works:
Curiosity – This title builds curiosity two ways:

There’s contrast between “luxury fashion” and “broke people”.
It’s counterintuitive that luxury fashion is for broke people.

Negativity – There’s a bit of drama and controversy here by saying luxury fashion is for broke people.
How you can use this framework: Say that something on one end of the spectrum is actually for people on the other end of the spectrum.
Examples of this framework in action:

A High Paying Job Will Keep You Poor
Fat Loss Diets Keep You Fat

Flop of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 8:
Why People Struggle to Say ‘I Love Y.

23K views « 4 months ago

Title: Why People Struggle to Say ‘I Love You’
Hook score: -84
Why this flopped: When people ask me what makes a good YouTube title, I always say it’s about these 3 emotions:

Curiosity
Fear
Desire

You know what’s not on this list? Sadness.
I’ve broken down almost 1,000 YouTube titles in this newsletter over the years and sadness has probably appeared in less than 5 titles (whereas the other 3 emotions have appeared in 400-600 titles).
If I were to rewrite this packaging, I’d probably leave the title, but try to swap out sadness for drama or fear in the thumbnail text.
Something like, “It’s my biggest regret”.
Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
Hope you enjoyed this week’s edition and if you know a fellow creator who needs help writing better titles, please send them to creatorhooks.com.
– Jake
(@jthomas__ on Twitter)
P.S. Read previous editions here.
P.P.S. If you want to see all of the videos from Creator Hooks in one place, check out Creator Hooks Pro.
```

## Newsletter 225: New YouTube Algorithm is PERFECT but

**URL:** https://creatorhooks.com/new-youtube-algorithm-is-perfect-but/

```
New YouTube Algorithm is PERFECT, but… 
January 15, 2024 by admin 


I’ve asked some of the best creators on YouTube, “How do you write such great titles?”
And they’ve all said the same thing: “I just model what works.”
So this email will show you 5 videos worth modeling to help you write great titles and save time.
Btw, if you want to save more time and use a title generator that uses all the titles ever featured in Creator Hooks, check out ​Creator Hooks Pro​.
A big But


🖼️ Thumbnail 2:
236K views + 8 days ago

Title: NEW: Kuronami Skin Bundle is PERFECT, but…
Framework: NEW: (Product) is PERFECT, but…
​Hook score​: +357
Why this works:
Curiosity – This title opens a loop because it makes you want to know what’s wrong. Plus, the contrast between “PERFECT” and “but…” adds to the curiosity.
Negativity – The title hints that there’s some drama or something wrong with this new bundle, and the thumbnail lets you know there’s definitely something wrong.
Timeliness – This video was published right around the time this new bundle was released. Plus, people love new things and this title made it very obvious this is about something new by starting with, “NEW:”.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience how a new product is perfect, except for one thing.
Examples of this framework in action:

NEW: Cybertruck is PERFECT, but…
NEW: Fender Jazzmaster is PERFECT, but…

You Know What’s Cool? A Billion Dollars


🖼️ Thumbnail 3:
Rockefeller: The World's First Billionaire

2.4M views + 1 month ago

Title: Rockefeller: The World’s First Billionaire
Framework: (Person): The World’s First (Milestone Achiever)
​Hook score​: +375
Why this works:
Extreme – “The World’s Second Billionaire” is a lot less cool than the world’s first. Extreme things, like the biggest, fastest, cheapest, or first, make for much more interesting topics.
Epic – “World’s First” and “Billionaire” make this video feel epic.
Authority – A lot of people know Rockefeller’s name, which helps grab extra attention.
How you can use this framework: Tell the dramatic story of the world’s first big milestone achiever in your niche.
Examples of this framework in action:

Roger Bannister: The World’s First 4 Minute Miler
King Sargon: The World’s First Emperor

Get Your Personal YouTube Coach


🖼️ Thumbnail 4:
Hi, I'm your personal Al coach

How about “5 Easy Tips to increase Your

YouTube Views Today”? It is more specific and er)
has a clearer call to action.

vidIQ just launched their AI Personal YouTube coach to help you grow your channel.
It can help you:

Come up with video ideas
Write better titles
Write your video script
Write your tags and description
And more

If you wanna give it a try, you can get a 30-day trial for just $1 here:
​Try vidIQ for $1​.
​(Affiliate link)
Slumbnail


🖼️ Thumbnail 5:
| Spent a Day in Haiti's Most Dangerous Slum

2.9M views + 1 month ago

Title: I Spent a Day in Haiti’s Most Dangerous Slum
Framework: I Spent a Day in (Entity’s) Most Dangerous (Place)
​Hook score​: +412
Why this works:
Negativity – This title leverages fear to get people to click.
Extreme – “Most Dangerous Slum” is a lot cooler and more epic than, say, “A Pretty Dangerous Slum”.
Time Frame – Spending a day here makes this video feel more tangible, which makes it more interesting.
How you can use this framework: Spend a specific amount of time in a dangerous place.
Examples of this framework in action:

I Spent a Day in America’s Most Dangerous School
I Had Lunch in America’s Most Disgusting Restaurant

The Truth Comes Out


🖼️ Thumbnail 6:
How Do Portland Residents Feel About
Living in Portland Oregon?

2K views * 6 days ago

Title: How Do Portland Residents Feel About Living in Portland Oregon?
Framework: How Do (Group of People) Feel About (Their Current Choice or Activity)?
​Hook score​: +416
Why this works:
Curiosity – This builds curiosity by asking a question.
Desire – This works well because this creator’s audience is considering moving to Portland.
Credibility – This has credibility because it’s about how real residents feel about living in Portland, not visitors or a realtor trying to sell you on living in Portland.
How you can use this framework: Ask people who are currently doing the thing your audience wants to do how they feel about it.
Examples of this framework in action:

How Do Married Men Feel About Marriage?
How Do Successful YouTubers Feel About Being A YouTuber?

Spicy Spicy


🖼️ Thumbnail 7:
(You've BEEN /

Why I Don't Own A House as a Multi-
Millionaire...

1M views + 1 month ago

Title: Why I Don’t Own A House as a Multi-Millionaire…
Framework: Why I Don’t (Expected Action) as a (Person Who Would Do Expected Action)…
​Hook score​: +751
Why this works:
Curiosity – This builds curiosity because it’s counterintuitive. You would expect a multi-millionaire to own a house.
Negativity – The thumbnail piles on the drama and fear and the rent vs. buy argument is a controversial one that a lot of people have strong opinions about.
Credibility – Since this creator is a multi-millionaire he brings more credibility to this argument.
How you can use this framework: Explain why you don’t do something even though a person of your status is expected to.
Examples of this framework in action:

Why I Don’t Own A Boat as a Professional Fisherman…
Why I Don’t Cook My Own Food as a Michelin Star Chef…

Flop of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 8:
fa 6A

REPLAY

h

We SWIFTPLAY

10:04

9 Features Riot NEEDS to add to Valorant in
2024

18K views « 12 days ago

Title: 9 Features Riot NEEDS to add to Valorant in 2024
Hook score: -82
Why this flopped: I don’t play Valorant, so this might just be me, but this thumbnail doesn’t scream “this is a cool Valorant video.”
It’s kinda like keywords in titles. You don’t always need them for the algorithm, but you do need at least some sort of keywords (and in this case, imagery) to let your target audience know your video is about something they’re interested in.
If it were me, I’d probably show one or two of the features they need to add, but in a more Valoranty way.
In addition to the sus thumbnail, the title could be better.
Now if this video did well I’d be telling you how great it was because it was a list and it had “2024” in the title.
But this video flopped, so something went wrong, and if they don’t wanna change the thumbnail, here are a few ways to rewrite the title:

They could focus on just one of the features in the title and say, “The BIGGEST Thing Valorant needs to add in 2024”
Apparently Valorant lied about adding replay, so you could frame the title around that: “Valorant Lied About This, But It Needs To Change In 2024”
The creator said Valorant is getting a little bit stale, so that could be another angle he could take: “Valorant Is Getting Stale — Here’s What Needs To Change In 2024”

Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
Hope you enjoyed this week’s edition and if you know a fellow creator who needs help writing better titles, please send them to creatorhooks.com.
– Jake
(@jthomas__ on Twitter)
P.S. Read previous editions here.
P.P.S. If you want to see all of the videos from Creator Hooks in one place, check out Creator Hooks Pro.
```

## Newsletter 227: 5 Life-Changing Psychology Hacks For YouTubers

**URL:** https://creatorhooks.com/5-life-changing-psychology-hacks-for-youtubers/

```
5 Life-Changing Psychology Hacks For YouTubers 
January 8, 2024 by admin 


I’ve asked some of the best creators on YouTube, “How do you write such great titles?”
And they’ve all said the same thing: “I just model what works.”
So this email will show you 5 videos worth modeling to help you write great titles and save time.
Btw, if you want to save more time and use a title generator that uses all the titles ever featured in Creator Hooks, check out ​Creator Hooks Pro​.
Call The Po-Pos


🖼️ Thumbnail 2:
!
REAL LIFE RECRUITER

THE MOST
JOB
APPLICATION

I'VE EVER SEEN.

15:44

The most illegal job application I've ever
seen (RED FLAG Employer!)

104K views * 1 month ago

Title: The most illegal job application I’ve ever seen (RED FLAG Employer!)
Framework: The most illegal [Thing] I’ve ever seen (RED FLAG [Entity]!)
​Hook score​: +318
Why this works:
Negativity – “illegal” and “RED FLAG” both promise a lot of drama in this video.
Curiosity – This title is a cliffhanger and makes you want to know what was so illegal about this job application, so you’ve gotta click to find out.
Epic – “The most” and “I’ve ever seen” add to the drama and excitement of this video.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience about the most illegal thing you’ve ever seen in your niche.
Examples of this framework in action:

The most ILLEGAL Minecraft hacks I’ve ever seen!
The most illegal house upgrades I’ve ever seen (RED FLAG Investment!)

Sir Don’t Mix-a-Lot


🖼️ Thumbnail 3:
WTAMING = 9
‘eae
RETINOL

Skincare Ingredients You CAN'T Mix?! |
Doctorly Investigates

638K views » 3 months ago

Title: Skincare Ingredients You CAN’T Mix?! | Doctorly Investigates
Framework: (Tools) You CAN’T Mix?!
​Hook score​: +401
Why this works:
Negativity – This is all about warning your audience about things they shouldn’t do.
Curiosity – This opens a loop and makes you want to know what ingredients they’re talking about.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience about tools they cannot mix, and in the thumbnail include things they’re likely already mixing.
Examples of this framework in action:

Investment Strategies You CAN’T Mix?!
Delicious Food Ingredients You CAN’T Mix?!

Get Your Personal YouTube Coach


🖼️ Thumbnail 4:
Hi, I'm your personal Al coach

How about “5 Easy Tips to increase Your

YouTube Views Today”? It is more specific and er)
has a clearer call to action.

vidIQ just launched their AI Personal YouTube coach to help you grow your channel.
It can help you:

Come up with video ideas
Write better titles
Write your video script
Write your tags and description
And more

If you wanna give it a try, you can get a 30-day trial for just $1 here:
​Try vidIQ for $1​.
​(Affiliate link)
12 Steps To Love


🖼️ Thumbnail 5:
Make anyone OBSESSED with you in these
12 STEPS

301K views » 1 month ago

Title: Make anyone OBSESSED with you in these 12 STEPS
Framework: (Achieve Deep Desire) in these (Number) STEPS
​Hook score​: +406
Why this works:
Desire – This title speaks to a deep desire this audience has. It’s not just “Make anyone like you” it’s “Make anyone OBSESSED with you”.
List – Lists make videos feel more tangible (you know exactly what the video will be about) and they add some curiosity.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience a specific number of steps to achieve a deep desire they have.
Examples of this framework in action:

Speak Spanish like a NATIVE in these 5 STEPS
Land your DREAM Job with these 12 STEPS

Gee Whiz


🖼️ Thumbnail 6:
Are these words really BAD? | Arabic101

295K views * 1 month ago

Title: Are these words really BAD? | Arabic101
Framework: Are these (Entities) really BAD?
​Hook score​: +465
Why this works:
Negativity – This is about “BAD” words, and people love bad, controversial, or taboo things.
Curiosity – This title builds curiosity by framing it as a question.
How you can use this framework: Ask if something in your niche is actually “BAD”.
Examples of this framework in action:

Are these cities really DANGEROUS?
Are these exercises actually BAD?

Sensitive Sally


🖼️ Thumbnail 7:
10 Life-changing SELF CARE HABITS for the
Highly Sensitive Person

429K views + 1 month ago

Title: 10 Life-changing SELF CARE HABITS for the Highly Sensitive Person
Framework: 10 Life-changing (Goal or Niche) HABITS for the (Underdog)
​Hook score​: +841
Why this works:
List – Lists make videos feel more tangible (you know exactly what the video will be about) and they add some curiosity.
Desire – This creator’s audience wants to change their life and they want to learn about self-care habits.
Specific Audience – By calling out highly sensitive people, this title makes highly sensitive people more likely to click. And this works so well because highly sensitive people probably are more likely to watch a video about life-changing habits than, say, perfectly content people, or highly confident people.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience about 10 life-changing habits for a specific group of people who are open to or looking for help.
Examples of this framework in action:

10 Life-changing MONEY HABITS for Overspenders
10 Life-saving travel habits for solo travelers

Flop of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 8:
ECRUITER!

HOW TO LAND

How To Make More Money In Your Career!

12K views + 1 month ago

Title: How To Make More Money In Your Career!
Hook score: -70
Why this flopped: In the intro, this creator says, “I came across a question recently — somebody was asking how they can earn more than $70,000 per year.”
It reminds me of one of my new favorite quotes: “Don’t listen to a customer, listen to your customers.”
In this scenario, it means that if one person has a question, it might not be worth making a whole video about because it could be too small of an audience, but if a lot of people ask you the same question, then listen up because it may be a big question that many people have.
I’m sure many people in this creator’s audience want to make more money, but I feel like $70,000 — the number from this specific question — just isn’t exciting enough to put in the thumbnail and intro.
If it were me, I’d probably put $100,000 in the thumbnail because that feels like a big goal more people have and are really excited about hitting.
Even the creator said in the intro “$70,000 today isn’t worth what it was even a year ago,” so he knows this number isn’t all that enticing.
Ok, enough of me ranting about the thumbnail text. How can we make the title more exciting?
Here are a few options:

We could make it more specific: “How To Make Over $100,000/Year In Your Career”
We could make it more timely: “How To Make More Money In Your Career In 2024”
We could make it more tangible: “How To Make More Money In Your Career In 5 Simple Steps”
We could speak more to the audience’s desires: “How To Land Your Dream Job (While Making OVER $100,000)”
We could add more curiosity and authority: “Job Recruiter’s #1 Secret To Negotiate A Higher Salary”

Deciding which one of these titles (or another) to go with really depends on your audience and the traffic source you’re targeting.
Plus, you can see why it’s so important to come up with your title before you write your script because each of these titles is a different angle and, ideally, you’d reaffirm your title in the intro so the audience knows this video is right for them and they’ll keep watching.
Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
Hope you enjoyed this week’s edition and if you know a fellow creator who needs help writing better titles, please send them to creatorhooks.com.
– Jake
(@jthomas__ on Twitter)
P.S. Read previous editions here.
P.P.S. If you want to see all of the videos from Creator Hooks in one place, check out Creator Hooks Pro.
```

## Newsletter 228: 5 Life-Changing Psychology Hacks For YouTubers

**URL:** https://creatorhooks.com/5-life-changing-psychology-hacks-for-youtubers/

```
5 Life-Changing Psychology Hacks For YouTubers 
January 8, 2024 by admin 


I’ve asked some of the best creators on YouTube, “How do you write such great titles?”
And they’ve all said the same thing: “I just model what works.”
So this email will show you 5 videos worth modeling to help you write great titles and save time.
Btw, if you want to save more time and use a title generator that uses all the titles ever featured in Creator Hooks, check out ​Creator Hooks Pro​.
Call The Po-Pos


🖼️ Thumbnail 2:
!
REAL LIFE RECRUITER

THE MOST
JOB
APPLICATION

I'VE EVER SEEN.

15:44

The most illegal job application I've ever
seen (RED FLAG Employer!)

104K views * 1 month ago

Title: The most illegal job application I’ve ever seen (RED FLAG Employer!)
Framework: The most illegal [Thing] I’ve ever seen (RED FLAG [Entity]!)
​Hook score​: +318
Why this works:
Negativity – “illegal” and “RED FLAG” both promise a lot of drama in this video.
Curiosity – This title is a cliffhanger and makes you want to know what was so illegal about this job application, so you’ve gotta click to find out.
Epic – “The most” and “I’ve ever seen” add to the drama and excitement of this video.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience about the most illegal thing you’ve ever seen in your niche.
Examples of this framework in action:

The most ILLEGAL Minecraft hacks I’ve ever seen!
The most illegal house upgrades I’ve ever seen (RED FLAG Investment!)

Sir Don’t Mix-a-Lot


🖼️ Thumbnail 3:
WTAMING = 9
‘eae
RETINOL

Skincare Ingredients You CAN'T Mix?! |
Doctorly Investigates

638K views » 3 months ago

Title: Skincare Ingredients You CAN’T Mix?! | Doctorly Investigates
Framework: (Tools) You CAN’T Mix?!
​Hook score​: +401
Why this works:
Negativity – This is all about warning your audience about things they shouldn’t do.
Curiosity – This opens a loop and makes you want to know what ingredients they’re talking about.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience about tools they cannot mix, and in the thumbnail include things they’re likely already mixing.
Examples of this framework in action:

Investment Strategies You CAN’T Mix?!
Delicious Food Ingredients You CAN’T Mix?!

Get Your Personal YouTube Coach


🖼️ Thumbnail 4:
Hi, I'm your personal Al coach

How about “5 Easy Tips to increase Your

YouTube Views Today”? It is more specific and er)
has a clearer call to action.

vidIQ just launched their AI Personal YouTube coach to help you grow your channel.

And more

If you wanna give it a try, you can get a 30-day trial for just $1 here:
​Try vidIQ for $1​.
​(Affiliate link)
12 Steps To Love


🖼️ Thumbnail 5:
Make anyone OBSESSED with you in these
12 STEPS

301K views » 1 month ago

Title: Make anyone OBSESSED with you in these 12 STEPS
Framework: (Achieve Deep Desire) in these (Number) STEPS
​Hook score​: +406
Why this works:
Desire – This title speaks to a deep desire this audience has. It’s not just “Make anyone like you” it’s “Make anyone OBSESSED with you”.
List – Lists make videos feel more tangible (you know exactly what the video will be about) and they add some curiosity.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience a specific number of steps to achieve a deep desire they have.
Examples of this framework in action:

Speak Spanish like a NATIVE in these 5 STEPS
Land your DREAM Job with these 12 STEPS

Gee Whiz


🖼️ Thumbnail 6:
Are these words really BAD? | Arabic101

295K views * 1 month ago

Title: Are these words really BAD? | Arabic101
Framework: Are these (Entities) really BAD?
​Hook score​: +465
Why this works:
Negativity – This is about “BAD” words, and people love bad, controversial, or taboo things.
Curiosity – This title builds curiosity by framing it as a question.
How you can use this framework: Ask if something in your niche is actually “BAD”.
Examples of this framework in action:

Are these cities really DANGEROUS?
Are these exercises actually BAD?

Sensitive Sally


🖼️ Thumbnail 7:
10 Life-changing SELF CARE HABITS for the
Highly Sensitive Person

429K views + 1 month ago

Title: 10 Life-changing SELF CARE HABITS for the Highly Sensitive Person
Framework: 10 Life-changing (Goal or Niche) HABITS for the (Underdog)
​Hook score​: +841
Why this works:
List – Lists make videos feel more tangible (you know exactly what the video will be about) and they add some curiosity.
Desire – This creator’s audience wants to change their life and they want to learn about self-care habits.
Specific Audience – By calling out highly sensitive people, this title makes highly sensitive people more likely to click. And this works so well because highly sensitive people probably are more likely to watch a video about life-changing habits than, say, perfectly content people, or highly confident people.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience about 10 life-changing habits for a specific group of people who are open to or looking for help.
Examples of this framework in action:

10 Life-changing MONEY HABITS for Overspenders
10 Life-saving travel habits for solo travelers

Flop of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 8:
ECRUITER!

HOW TO LAND

How To Make More Money In Your Career!

12K views + 1 month ago

Title: How To Make More Money In Your Career!
Hook score: -70
Why this flopped: In the intro, this creator says, “I came across a question recently — somebody was asking how they can earn more than $70,000 per year.”
It reminds me of one of my new favorite quotes: “Don’t listen to a customer, listen to your customers.”
In this scenario, it means that if one person has a question, it might not be worth making a whole video about because it could be too small of an audience, but if a lot of people ask you the same question, then listen up because it may be a big question that many people have.
I’m sure many people in this creator’s audience want to make more money, but I feel like $70,000 — the number from this specific question — just isn’t exciting enough to put in the thumbnail and intro.
If it were me, I’d probably put $100,000 in the thumbnail because that feels like a big goal more people have and are really excited about hitting.
Even the creator said in the intro “$70,000 today isn’t worth what it was even a year ago,” so he knows this number isn’t all that enticing.
Ok, enough of me ranting about the thumbnail text. How can we make the title more exciting?
Here are a few options:

We could make it more specific: “How To Make Over $100,000/Year In Your Career”
We could make it more timely: “How To Make More Money In Your Career In 2024”
We could make it more tangible: “How To Make More Money In Your Career In 5 Simple Steps”
We could speak more to the audience’s desires: “How To Land Your Dream Job (While Making OVER $100,000)”
We could add more curiosity and authority: “Job Recruiter’s #1 Secret To Negotiate A Higher Salary”

Deciding which one of these titles (or another) to go with really depends on your audience and the traffic source you’re targeting.
Plus, you can see why it’s so important to come up with your title before you write your script because each of these titles is a different angle and, ideally, you’d reaffirm your title in the intro so the audience knows this video is right for them and they’ll keep watching.
Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
Hope you enjoyed this week’s edition and if you know a fellow creator who needs help writing better titles, please send them to creatorhooks.com.
– Jake
(@jthomas__ on Twitter)
```
