## Newsletter 259: The TRUTH about YouTube that the PROS know

**URL:** https://creatorhooks.com/the-truth-about-youtube-that-the-pros-know/

```
The TRUTH about YouTube that the PROS know 
September 18, 2023 by admin 


I’ve asked some of the best creators on YouTube, “How do you write such great titles?”
And they’ve all said the same thing: “I just model what works.”
So this email will show you 5 videos worth modeling to help you write great titles and save time.
Btw, if you want to save more time and see all of the titles and thumbnails from Creator Hooks in one place, check out ​Creator Hooks Pro​.
Under The Hood


🖼️ Thumbnail 2:
We Put a $10,000 Engine in our $500 Civic

6.3M views + 1 month ago -

Title: We Put a $10,000 Engine in our $500 Civic
Framework: We Put a (Expensive Thing) in our (Cheap Thing)
​Hook score​: +333
Why this works:
Curiosity – The contrast between $10,000 engine and $500 Civic, and how counterintuitive it is to do that builds a ton of curiosity and interest.
How you can use this framework: Pair something expensive with something cheap for a weird, funny adventure.
Examples of this framework in action:

I Put a $1,000 Fishing Reel on a $20 Rod
Professional Golfer Tries Cheap Golf Clubs

Coming To Charleston


🖼️ Thumbnail 3:
What | Wish | Knew BEFORE Moving to
Charleston, SC from Ohio

3.7K views + 2 months ago

Title: What I Wish I Knew BEFORE Moving to Charleston, SC from Ohio
Framework: What I Wish I Knew BEFORE (Action)
​Hook score​: +379
Why this works:
Curiosity – This title opens a loop and makes you wonder what he wishes he knew.
Negativity – There’s a hint of regret or warning in this title. If you’re considering moving to Charleston, this grabs your attention.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience what you wish you knew before making a big change.
Examples of this framework in action:

What I Wish I Knew BEFORE Becoming a Software Engineer
What I Wish I Knew BEFORE Getting A New Kitchen

Get Your Personal YouTube Coach


🖼️ Thumbnail 4:
Hi, I'm your personal Al coach

How about “5 Easy Tips to increase Your

YouTube Views Today”? It is more specific and er)
has a clearer call to action.

vidIQ just launched their AI Personal YouTube coach to help you grow your channel.
It can help you:

Come up with video ideas
Write better titles
Write your video script
Write your tags and description
And more

If you wanna give it a try, you can get a 30-day trial for just $1 here:
Try vidIQ for $1.
​(Affiliate link)
Show Me The Money


🖼️ Thumbnail 5:
10 Expensive Looking Color Combinations |
Fashion Over 40

134K views » 2 months ago * 26 VPH

Title: 10 Expensive Looking Color Combinations | Fashion Over 40
Framework: 10 (Desirable Entities)
​Hook score​: +403
Why this works:
List – Lists make videos feel more tangible (you know exactly what the video will be about) and they add some curiosity.
Desire – This is a proven topic for fashion channels — this creator’s audience wants to look expensive (without spending the money to be expensive). But broadly speaking, getting a benefit for cheap is a trend that’s proven across nearly every industry.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience about 10 things that will help them get what they want for cheap.
Examples of this framework in action:

10 Best Budget-Friendly Cities To Visit In 2024
10 Hacks To Make Your Home Look Expensive

Shhh


🖼️ Thumbnail 6:
The TRUTH about shooting at ISO 100 that
the PROS know.

742K views + 1 month ago « + 66 VPH

Title: The TRUTH about shooting at ISO 100 that the PROS know.
Framework: The TRUTH about (Activity) that the PROS know.
​Hook score​: +698
Why this works:
Curiosity – This builds curiosity two ways: 1) It opens a loop and makes you wonder what the truth is. 2) It makes you feel like you’re getting in on a secret and finally learning the truth.
Authority – This leverages the authority of the pros to get people more interested in this video.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience the truth about something in your niche that only the pros know.
Examples of this framework in action:

The TRUTH about Flipping Houses that the PROS know.
The TRUTH about Playing Guitar that the PROS know.

Drama In The Kitchen


🖼️ Thumbnail 7:
INSIDER

Business | BIG BUSINESS

11 Of The Most Faked Foods In The World |
Big Business | Insider Business

6.9M views + 2 weeks ago + 4.68K VPH

Title: 11 Of The Most Faked Foods In The World | Big Business | Insider Business
Framework: 11 Of The Most Faked (Items) In The World
​Hook score​: +1224
Why this works:
List – Lists make videos feel more tangible (you know exactly what the video will be about) and they add some curiosity.
Faked Foods – This brings a bit of drama and curiosity (two of the most powerful click triggers).
Epic – “In The World” makes this video feel more epic and fun.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience about faked or knockoff items in your niche.
Examples of this framework in action:

11 Of The Most Faked Fashion Items In The World
11 Of The Most Faked Paintings In The World

Flop of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 8:
How to photograph the NORTHERN LIGHTS.

26K views » 4 months ago

Title: How to photograph the NORTHERN LIGHTS.
Hook score: -76
Why this flopped: This video flopped because it has a small potential audience. Unless you’re searching for this, you’re probably not going to watch this video.
On the other hand, many photographers use ISO 100, so “The TRUTH about shooting at ISO 100 that the PROS know”, featured earlier in this email, can be watched and enjoyed by many photographers.
If you want to make videos that get lots of views, you need to have a relatively large potential audience.
In addition to this problem, this title is boring.
Here are two options for how he could’ve spiced it up:

Beginner’s Guide To Photographing the NORTHERN LIGHTS.
How to photograph the NORTHERN LIGHTS In 2023.

Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
Hope you enjoyed this week’s edition and if you know a fellow creator who needs help with writing better titles, please send them to creatorhooks.com.
– Jake
(@jthomas__ on Twitter)
P.S. Read previous editions here.
P.P.S. If you want to see all of the videos from Creator Hooks in one place, check out Creator Hooks Pro.
Read last week’s edition »

​Hook score​: +1224
Why this works:
List – Lists make videos feel more tangible (you know exactly what the video will be about) and they add some curiosity.
Faked Foods – This brings a bit of drama and curiosity (two of the most powerful click triggers).
Epic – “In The World” makes this video feel more epic and fun.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience about faked or knockoff items in your niche.
Examples of this framework in action:

11 Of The Most Faked Fashion Items In The World
11 Of The Most Faked Paintings In The World

Flop of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 8:
How to photograph the NORTHERN LIGHTS.

26K views » 4 months ago

Title: How to photograph the NORTHERN LIGHTS.
Hook score: -76
Why this flopped: This video flopped because it has a small potential audience. Unless you’re searching for this, you’re probably not going to watch this video.
On the other hand, many photographers use ISO 100, so “The TRUTH about shooting at ISO 100 that the PROS know”, featured earlier in this email, can be watched and enjoyed by many photographers.
If you want to make videos that get lots of views, you need to have a relatively large potential audience.
In addition to this problem, this title is boring.
Here are two options for how he could’ve spiced it up:

Beginner’s Guide To Photographing the NORTHERN LIGHTS.
How to photograph the NORTHERN LIGHTS In 2023.

Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
Hope you enjoyed this week’s edition and if you know a fellow creator who needs help with writing better titles, please send them to creatorhooks.com.
– Jake
(@jthomas__ on Twitter)
P.S. Read previous editions here.
P.P.S. If you want to see all of the videos from Creator Hooks in one place, check out Creator Hooks Pro.

## Newsletter 261: I Created an Anonymous YouTube Channel to Prove its not Luck

**URL:** https://creatorhooks.com/i-created-an-anonymous-youtube-channel-to-prove-its-not-luck/

```
I Created an Anonymous YouTube Channel to “Prove its not Luck” 
September 11, 2023 by admin 


I’ve asked some of the best creators on YouTube, “How do you write such great titles?”
And they’ve all said the same thing: “I just model what works.”
So this email will show you 5 videos worth modeling to help you write great titles and save time.
Btw, if you want to save more time and see all of the titles and thumbnails from Creator Hooks in one place, check out ​Creator Hooks Pro​.
Spaghetti Remix


🖼️ Thumbnail 2:
Italian Chef Reacts to Filipino Chef Cooking
Spaghetti 3 Different Ways

166K views + 1 month ago + 108 VPH

Title: Italian Chef Reacts to Filipino Chef Cooking Spaghetti 3 Different Ways
Framework: (Person) Reacts to (Adjacent Person Doing Contrasting Activity)
​Hook score​: +337
Why this works:
Curiosity – The contrast of Filipino chef cooking an Italian dish builds curiosity and makes this video more interesting.
Authority – Since this creator is an Italian Chef, he has lots of authority when it comes to cooking spaghetti, so you trust his opinion and want to watch this video more.
How you can use this framework: If you’re an expert, react to somebody who’s not an expert in your field trying something you’re good at.
Examples of this framework in action:

Rapper Reacts to Country Artist Singing Over Hip Hop Beat
Movie Director Reacts to Popular YouTube Videos

No Name No Problem


🖼️ Thumbnail 3:
| Created an Anonymous Rapper to "Prove its
not Luck"

1.5M views + 1 month ago » 496 VPH + 541 VPH

Title: I Created an Anonymous Rapper to “Prove its not Luck”
Framework: I Created an Anonymous (Entity) to “Prove its not Luck”
​Hook score​: +372
Why this works:
Curiosity – This title builds so much curiosity because it starts a story and makes you want to know what happened.
Credibility – Creating an “anonymous” rapper makes you feel like this will be a legitimate challenge that leads to a (hopefully) replicable result.
Refute Objection – A lot of people who want to be music artists probably think it’s luck that other people have made it, so this title tackles that objection head on.
How you can use this framework: Create an anonymous project and show your audience how it’s done.
Examples of this framework in action:

I Created an Anonymous YouTube Channel to “Prove its not Luck”
I Started A Real Estate Company From Scratch to “Prove its not Luck”

Get Your Personal YouTube Coach


🖼️ Thumbnail 4:
Hi, I'm your personal Al coach

How about “5 Easy Tips to increase Your

YouTube Views Today”? It is more specific and er)
has a clearer call to action.

vidIQ just launched their AI Personal YouTube coach to help you grow your channel.
It can help you:

Come up with video ideas
Write better titles
Write your video script
Write your tags and description
And more

If you wanna give it a try, you can get a 30-day trial for just $1 here:
Try vidIQ for $1.
​(Affiliate link)
In The Zone


🖼️ Thumbnail 5:
low

M9 Heart Rate
-& SeTraining oa

| Tried Zone 2 Training for 3 Months. This
Happened

1.1M views + 2 months ago - 773 VPH + 731 VPH

Title: I Tried Zone 2 Training for 3 Months. This Happened
Framework: I Tried (Trend) for 3 Months. This Happened
​Hook score​: +509
Why this works:
Curiosity – This title opens a loop and makes you want to know what happened.
Timeliness – Zone 2 Training is trending right now, so this video rides that wave.
Time Frame – Trying something for a specific time frame makes the video more interesting because it’s more tangible and you know exactly what this video is about. The time frame also helps with this next point.
Credibility – Since he actually did zone 2 training for 3 months it makes this video more believable.
How you can use this framework: Try a trending thing for a time and share what happened.
Examples of this framework in action:

I Tried Decluttering for 3 Months. This Happened
I Posted a YouTube Short Every Day for 30 Days. This Happened

If It’s Free It’s For Me


🖼️ Thumbnail 6:
10 Free Al Animation Tools: Bring Images to
Life
501K views * 1 month ago + 302 VPH -

Title: 10 Free AI Animation Tools: Bring Images to Life
Framework: 10 Free (Trend) Tools: (Action)
​Hook score​: +514
Why this works:
List – Lists make videos feel more tangible (you know exactly what the video will be about) and they add some curiosity.
Desire – Not only is this creator’s interested in AI tools, but everybody loves free tools.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience about a list of free tools they can use.
Examples of this framework in action:

10 Free Fitness Tools: Get Shredded Fast
10 Free Lightroom Presets: Take Beatiful Pictures Fast

Grass Isn’t Always Greener


🖼️ Thumbnail 7:
Ganadailsn’
. nada lane

Struggles of African Immigrant Working in
Canada

478K views + 12 days ago

Title: Struggles of African Immigrant Working in Canada
Framework: Struggles of (Specific Situation)
​Hook score​: +39833
Why this works:
This video was a massive breakout from a new channel and I’m not *exactly* sure why it did so well, but I have a few theories:
Negativity – People love negativity and drama. Talking about struggles and how a country isn’t worth it definitely fits that.
Confirmation – A lot of people in the comments are essentially saying “Yes, I know! This has been my experience too.” When you confirm what lots of people are thinking, but may not be openly talked about, they’ll want to watch and comment.
Specific Audience – When you go too specific on YouTube it’s easy to have too small of a potential audience. But people living in Canada, Africans, and immigrants seem to rally around this video saying this is what they’re feeling. If you (and the YouTube algorithm) can find a specific audience that has strong feelings about your content, you can win big.
How you can use this framework: Talk about the struggles of a specific group of people.
Examples of this framework in action:

Struggles of New Moms After Giving Birth
Struggles of Christians In A Sinful Society

P.S. Big thanks to this creator, Iram, who reads Creator Hooks and sent this to me!
Flop of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 8:
7 Generative Fill Techniques You Need To Try

6.5K views + 2 months ago

Title: 7 Generative Fill Techniques You Need To Try
Hook score: -96
Why this flopped: I don’t think there was one major reason this video flopped, but a combination of several reasons:

This video is for Photoshop users. Photoshop is a paid tool, while Midjourney, ChatGPT, and many other AI tools he mentions in his channel are free to use. It’s possible his audience is more into free tools than paid tools.
The title is a little bland. He could spice it up by saying this video is for beginners, it’ll help people make images fast, they don’t have to have any graphic design experience, etc.
The thumbnail is kinda boring. It’s just the Photoshop beta logo, no really cool showing of what it can do. (Yes, it technically shows what generative fill can do, but in a boring and nonobvious way.) Plus, his face is not in the thumbnail. This is important because when you look at his channel, this thumbnail doesn’t really look like it fits the rest of his channel, and he appears to have a pretty strong brand (so putting his face in the thumbnail would probably help).



🖼️ Thumbnail 9:
Face Swap Midjourney Images With Two 10 Free Al Animation Tools: Bring Images to
Clicks (Free) Life

21K views + 1 month ago 489K views * 1 month ago

ssosss

7 Generative Fill Techniques You Need To Try | Used Al Text to Video to Create a Faceless
6.5K views + 2 months ago YouTube Channel

30K views » 3 months ago

Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
Hope you enjoyed this week’s edition and if you know a fellow creator who needs help with writing better titles, please send them to creatorhooks.com.
– Jake
(@jthomas__ on Twitter)
P.S. Read previous editions here.
P.P.S. If you want to see all of the videos from Creator Hooks in one place, check out Creator Hooks Pro.

## Newsletter 263: The YouTube Algorithm Is About To Be VERY Different

**URL:** https://creatorhooks.com/the-youtube-algorithm-is-about-to-be-very-different/

```
The YouTube Algorithm Is About To Be VERY Different… 
September 4, 2023 by admin 


I’ve asked some of the best creators on YouTube, “How do you write such great titles?”
And they’ve all said the same thing: “I just model what works.”
So this email will show you 5 videos worth modeling to help you write great titles and save time.
Btw, if you want to save more time and see all of the titles and thumbnails from Creator Hooks in one place, check out ​Creator Hooks Pro​.
Winter Is Coming


🖼️ Thumbnail 2:
WINTER
PREDICTION

UX

Sti

This Winter Is Going To be VERY Different...
7.4M views + 1 month ago * 1.55K VPH +

Title: This Winter Is Going To be VERY Different…
Framework: This (Upcoming Event or Season) Is Going To be VERY Different…
​Hook score​: +396
Why this works:
Curiosity – This title builds curiosity two ways: 1) Talking about the future builds curiosity because it’s unknown and is an inherently curiosity-filled topic. 2) It opens a loop because you need to click to learn what’s going to be different about this winter.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience how something coming up is not going to be what they expect.
Examples of this framework in action:

This NFL Season Is Going To be VERY Different…
The 2024 Mustang Is Going To be VERY Different…

🖼️ Thumbnail 3:
The HIDDEN MESSAGE in this Season 4
Scene from the Chosen?

351K views + 1 month ago + 177 VPH + 174 VPH

Title: The HIDDEN MESSAGE in this Season 4 Scene from the Chosen?
Framework: The HIDDEN MESSAGE in (Entity)?
​Hook score​: +1176
Why this works:
Curiosity – Talking about “hidden” things is a great way to build curiosity.
Timeliness – This video was published soon after the creator of The Chosen released teaser footage previewing season 4.
How you can use this framework: Tease that your audience missed a hidden message in something.
Examples of this framework in action:

The HIDDEN MESSAGE in MrBeast’s Latest Video?
The HIDDEN MESSAGE in Kanye’s First Album?

102K views - 1 month ago - 4 VPH + 5 VPH

Title: This One-Page Productivity Hack Will Change Your Life
Framework: This (Limited) (Activity or Goal) Hack Will Change Your Life
​Hook score​: +2081
Why this works:
Desire – This creator’s audience wants productivity hacks that will change their life.
Constraint – The constraint of “one-page” enhances the desire because it seems simple. It also adds curiosity because it makes you wonder how a small thing like a “one-page” productivity hack can change your life.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience about a seemingly simple thing that can change their life.
Examples of this framework in action:

These 4-Ingredient Cookies Will Change Your Life
This 3-Minute Guitar Warmup Will Change Your Life

So Long Supplements


🖼️ Thumbnail 7:
Stop Wasting Your Money on These 7
USELESS Supplements! | Dr. Steven Gundry

1.1M views + 1 month ago - 89 VPH

Title: Stop Wasting Your Money on These 7 USELESS Supplements! | Dr. Steven Gundry
Framework: Stop Wasting Your (Resources) on These 7 USELESS (Tools)
​Hook score​: +2820
Why this works:
Negativity – Telling your audience to stop doing something they’re currently doing or think is good for them is a great way to get their attention. This also leverages loss aversion because nobody wants to waste money on useless things.
Curiosity – This title makes you want to click to learn what these 7 supplements are.
List – Lists make videos feel more tangible (you know exactly what the video will be about) and they add some curiosity.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience to stop wasting their resources on useless things.
Examples of this framework in action:

Stop Wasting Your Time on These 7 USELESS Exercises
Stop Wasting Your Money on These 7 USELESS Makeup Products

Flop of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 8:
Sisen ae LIES,

iL a
3
Trans. £2
Cholesterol 1S 40m

Shoiesterol 5S ma
al
oma carbohysy

BIGGEST Lies on Food Labels: 3 Ways You're
Being TRICKED With Misleading Food Labe...

9.3K views + 1 month ago

Title: BIGGEST Lies on Food Labels: 3 Ways You’re Being TRICKED With Misleading Food Labels | Dr. Gundry
Hook score: -94
Why this flopped: The title and thumbnail are both too complicated.
There’s so much going on that you don’t know what to focus on.
You scroll on by because it’s too much work to try to understand what the video is about.
If I were to rewrite this title, I’d probably use “3 BIGGEST Lies on Food Labels” *or* “3 Ways You’re Being TRICKED With Misleading Food Labels” (not both, like this title did).
Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
Hope you enjoyed this week’s edition and if you know a fellow creator who needs help with writing better titles, please send them to creatorhooks.com.

## Newsletter 265: The Wrong Way to Grow Your YouTube Channel

**URL:** https://creatorhooks.com/the-wrong-way-to-grow-your-youtube-channel/

```
The Wrong Way to Grow Your YouTube Channel 
August 28, 2023 by admin 


I’ve asked some of the best creators on YouTube, “How do you write such great titles?”
And they’ve all said the same thing: “I just model what works.”
So this email will show you 5 videos worth modeling to help you write great titles and save time.
Btw, if you want to save more time and see all of the titles and thumbnails from Creator Hooks in one place, check out ​Creator Hooks Pro​.
“Hip Hop Is Dead”


🖼️ Thumbnail 2:
Something weird is happening in hip-hop
1.1M views + 3 weeks ago + 663 VPH +

Title: Something weird is happening in hip-hop
Framework: Something weird is happening in (Niche)
​Hook score​: +335
Why this works:
Curiosity – This title builds curiosity two ways: 1) Talking about “weird” things builds curiosity. 2) This title opens a loop because you want to know what’s happening.
Negativity – “Dying” in the thumbnail grabs the audience’s attention.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience about something weird happening in your niche.
Examples of this framework in action:

Something weird is happening in F1
Something weird is happening in Roblox

Poutine Party


🖼️ Thumbnail 3:
The Wrong Way to Cross the Border into
Canada (NEW BRUNSWICK)

236K views + 3 weeks ago *

Title: The Wrong Way to Cross the Border into Canada (NEW BRUNSWICK)
Framework: The Wrong Way To (Nerve-Wracking Activity)
​Hook score​: +472
Why this works:
Negativity – This video is about something potentially nerve-wracking, and to make matters worse, they apparently did it the wrong way.
Curiosity – This title opens a loop and makes you wonder what happened.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience about the wrong way to do something potentially nerve-wracking.
Examples of this framework in action:

The Wrong Way To Propose To Your Girlfriend
The Wrong Way To Interview For Your Dream Job

Get Your Personal YouTube Coach


🖼️ Thumbnail 4:
Hi, I'm your personal Al coach

How about “5 Easy Tips to increase Your

YouTube Views Today”? It is more specific and er)
has a clearer call to action.

vidIQ just launched their AI Personal YouTube coach to help you grow your channel.
It can help you:

Come up with video ideas
Write better titles
Write your video script
Write your tags and description
And more

If you wanna give it a try, you can get a 30-day trial for just $1 here:
Try vidIQ for $1.
​(Affiliate link)
Inside Scoop


🖼️ Thumbnail 5:
| Worked at REI for 12 Years - Here's the REI
Gear You Should Buy!

572K views + 1 month ago « 157 VPH + 206 VPH

Title: I Worked at REI for 12 Years – Here’s the REI Gear You Should Buy!
Framework: I Worked at (Place) for (Time Frame) – Here’s the (Products) You Should Buy!
​Hook score​: +554
Why this works:
Credibility – Sharing that she worked at REI for 12 years makes you trust her more when it comes to REI gear.
Time Frame – If she just said she worked at REI, that would add some credibility, but the time frame of 12 years adds even more.
Desire – This creator’s audience loves buying gear.
How you can use this framework: Share something your audience wants to know and back it up with your credentials.
Examples of this framework in action:

I Worked at Best Buy for 5 Years – Here’s The Best Tech You Should Buy!
I Worked in Silicon Valley for 10 Years – Here’s My Best Career Advice

It’s Going Down For Real


🖼️ Thumbnail 6:
A YF Wey

a 7 Eo Socaastcacceal Mf sean saascests val
WV 2008 \

1929 9023
[13:51 ]

This Is WORSE Than the 1929 and 2008
Yield Curve Inversions

331K views + 1 month ago + 69 VPH + 69 VPH

Title: This Is WORSE Than the 1929 and 2008 Yield Curve Inversions
Framework: This Is WORSE Than the (Previous Worst Events)
​Hook score​: +570
Why this works:
Normally titles with big words like “yield curve inversions” land in the flop of the week section, but this one works for a few reasons:
1) The thumbnail does a great job of showing exactly what this video is about.
2) This title makes sense even if you take out the complicated words at the end: “This Is WORSE Than the 1929 and 2008”
Now here’s what actually got people to click:
Negativity – This creator’s audience knows how bad 1929 and 2008 were, so that fear grabs their attention and gets them to click.
Timeliness – This video is about what’s happening right now, so it grabs people’s attention.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience about how what’s happening now is worse than previous events.
Examples of this framework in action:

This Is WORSE Than Hurricane Katrina
This Is WORSE Than America’s Big Sugar Problem

Fake Games Real Fun


🖼️ Thumbnail 7:
| Bought Bootleg Nintendo Games from
Temu

1M views + 2 weeks ago + 590 VPH

Title: I Bought Bootleg Nintendo Games from Temu
Framework: I Bought Bootleg (Objects) from (Controversial Store)
​Hook score​: +740
Why this works:
Curiosity – “Bootleg” items build curiosity because you never what you’re gonna get.
Negativity – Temu has a shady reputation which adds controversy and another element of uncertainty to this video, and “bootleg” adds to the controversy, too.
How you can use this framework: Buy bootleg items from a shady place.
Examples of this framework in action:

I Bought a Knockoff Rolex from eBay
I Bought Bootleg Golf Clubs from Craigslist

Flop of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 8:
When Frank Ocean and Tyler The Creator
Unite

75K views + 4 months ago + 4 VPH

Title: When Frank Ocean and Tyler The Creator Unite
Hook score: -86
Why this flopped: There’s no emotion or intrigue here, it’s just a plain statement.
They could’ve gone with:

A positive angle: “The Magic of Frank Ocean and Tyler The Creator”
A negative angle: “The Downfall of Frank Ocean and Tyler The Creator”
A curiosity angle: “What Really Happened To Frank Ocean & Tyler The Creator”

But instead, they with with no angle and it flopped.
Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
Hope you enjoyed this week’s edition and if you know a fellow creator who needs help with writing better titles, please send them to creatorhooks.com.
– Jake
(@jthomas__ on Twitter)
P.S. Read previous editions here.
P.P.S. If you want to see all of the videos from Creator Hooks in one place, check out Creator Hooks Pro.

## Newsletter 266: The Wrong Way to Grow Your YouTube Channel

**URL:** https://creatorhooks.com/the-wrong-way-to-grow-your-youtube-channel/

```
The Wrong Way to Grow Your YouTube Channel 
August 28, 2023 by admin 


I’ve asked some of the best creators on YouTube, “How do you write such great titles?”
And they’ve all said the same thing: “I just model what works.”
So this email will show you 5 videos worth modeling to help you write great titles and save time.
Btw, if you want to save more time and see all of the titles and thumbnails from Creator Hooks in one place, check out ​Creator Hooks Pro​.
“Hip Hop Is Dead”


🖼️ Thumbnail 2:
Something weird is happening in hip-hop
1.1M views + 3 weeks ago + 663 VPH +

Title: Something weird is happening in hip-hop
Framework: Something weird is happening in (Niche)
​Hook score​: +335
Why this works:
Curiosity – This title builds curiosity two ways: 1) Talking about “weird” things builds curiosity. 2) This title opens a loop because you want to know what’s happening.
Negativity – “Dying” in the thumbnail grabs the audience’s attention.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience about something weird happening in your niche.
Examples of this framework in action:

Something weird is happening in F1
Something weird is happening in Roblox

Poutine Party


🖼️ Thumbnail 3:
The Wrong Way to Cross the Border into
Canada (NEW BRUNSWICK)

236K views + 3 weeks ago *

Title: The Wrong Way to Cross the Border into Canada (NEW BRUNSWICK)
Framework: The Wrong Way To (Nerve-Wracking Activity)
​Hook score​: +472
Why this works:
Negativity – This video is about something potentially nerve-wracking, and to make matters worse, they apparently did it the wrong way.
Curiosity – This title opens a loop and makes you wonder what happened.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience about the wrong way to do something potentially nerve-wracking.
Examples of this framework in action:

The Wrong Way To Propose To Your Girlfriend
The Wrong Way To Interview For Your Dream Job

Get Your Personal YouTube Coach


🖼️ Thumbnail 4:
Hi, I'm your personal Al coach

How about “5 Easy Tips to increase Your

YouTube Views Today”? It is more specific and er)
has a clearer call to action.

vidIQ just launched their AI Personal YouTube coach to help you grow your channel.
It can help you:

Come up with video ideas
Write better titles
Write your video script
Write your tags and description
And more

If you wanna give it a try, you can get a 30-day trial for just $1 here:
Try vidIQ for $1.
​(Affiliate link)
Inside Scoop


🖼️ Thumbnail 5:
| Worked at REI for 12 Years - Here's the REI
Gear You Should Buy!

572K views + 1 month ago « 157 VPH + 206 VPH

Title: I Worked at REI for 12 Years – Here’s the REI Gear You Should Buy!
Framework: I Worked at (Place) for (Time Frame) – Here’s the (Products) You Should Buy!
​Hook score​: +554
Why this works:
Credibility – Sharing that she worked at REI for 12 years makes you trust her more when it comes to REI gear.
Time Frame – If she just said she worked at REI, that would add some credibility, but the time frame of 12 years adds even more.
Desire – This creator’s audience loves buying gear.
How you can use this framework: Share something your audience wants to know and back it up with your credentials.
Examples of this framework in action:

I Worked at Best Buy for 5 Years – Here’s The Best Tech You Should Buy!
I Worked in Silicon Valley for 10 Years – Here’s My Best Career Advice

It’s Going Down For Real


🖼️ Thumbnail 6:
A YF Wey

a 7 Eo Socaastcacceal Mf sean saascests val
WV 2008 \

1929 9023
[13:51 ]

This Is WORSE Than the 1929 and 2008
Yield Curve Inversions

331K views + 1 month ago + 69 VPH + 69 VPH

Title: This Is WORSE Than the 1929 and 2008 Yield Curve Inversions
Framework: This Is WORSE Than the (Previous Worst Events)
​Hook score​: +570
Why this works:
Normally titles with big words like “yield curve inversions” land in the flop of the week section, but this one works for a few reasons:
1) The thumbnail does a great job of showing exactly what this video is about.
2) This title makes sense even if you take out the complicated words at the end: “This Is WORSE Than the 1929 and 2008”
Now here’s what actually got people to click:
Negativity – This creator’s audience knows how bad 1929 and 2008 were, so that fear grabs their attention and gets them to click.
Timeliness – This video is about what’s happening right now, so it grabs people’s attention.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience about how what’s happening now is worse than previous events.
Examples of this framework in action:

This Is WORSE Than Hurricane Katrina
This Is WORSE Than America’s Big Sugar Problem

Fake Games Real Fun


🖼️ Thumbnail 7:
| Bought Bootleg Nintendo Games from
Temu

1M views + 2 weeks ago + 590 VPH

Title: I Bought Bootleg Nintendo Games from Temu
Framework: I Bought Bootleg (Objects) from (Controversial Store)
​Hook score​: +740
Why this works:
Curiosity – “Bootleg” items build curiosity because you never what you’re gonna get.
Negativity – Temu has a shady reputation which adds controversy and another element of uncertainty to this video, and “bootleg” adds to the controversy, too.
How you can use this framework: Buy bootleg items from a shady place.
Examples of this framework in action:

I Bought a Knockoff Rolex from eBay
I Bought Bootleg Golf Clubs from Craigslist

Flop of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 8:
When Frank Ocean and Tyler The Creator
Unite

75K views + 4 months ago + 4 VPH

Title: When Frank Ocean and Tyler The Creator Unite
Hook score: -86
Why this flopped: There’s no emotion or intrigue here, it’s just a plain statement.
They could’ve gone with:

A positive angle: “The Magic of Frank Ocean and Tyler The Creator”
A negative angle: “The Downfall of Frank Ocean and Tyler The Creator”
A curiosity angle: “What Really Happened To Frank Ocean & Tyler The Creator”

But instead, they with with no angle and it flopped.
Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
Hope you enjoyed this week’s edition and if you know a fellow creator who needs help with writing better titles, please send them to creatorhooks.com.
– Jake
(@jthomas__ on Twitter)
P.S. Read previous editions here.
P.P.S. If you want to see all of the videos from Creator Hooks in one place, check out Creator Hooks Pro.

## Newsletter 267: From 0 to 100000 Subs with ONE Video

**URL:** https://creatorhooks.com/from-0-to-100000-subs-with-one-video/

```
From 0 to 100,000 Subs with ONE Video 
August 21, 2023 by admin 


I’ve asked some of the best creators on YouTube, “How do you write such great titles?”
And they’ve all said the same thing: “I just model what works.”
So this email will show you 5 videos worth modeling to help you write great titles and save time.
Btw, if you want to save more time and see all of the titles and thumbnails from Creator Hooks in one place, check out ​Creator Hooks Pro​.
Bird is the Word


🖼️ Thumbnail 2:
@ 40

ENGLISH

WORDS YOU'LL USE eens Ret
Pas.

40 English Words You'll Use Every Day -
Basic Vocabulary #44

A8K views + 2 weeks ago « + 24VPH

Title: 40 English Words You’ll Use Every Day – Basic Vocabulary #44
Framework: 40 (Entities) You’ll Use Every Day – (Keywords)
​Hook score​: +436
Why this works:
List – Lists make videos feel more tangible (you know exactly what the video will be about) and they add some curiosity.
Daily – Talking about things you can use every day makes a video feel more tangible and valuable.
Beginners – Making this video about “basic vocabulary” attracts beginners, which is the biggest, hungriest part of any audience.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience about a list of things beginners will use every day.
Examples of this framework in action:

5 Cleaning Products New Moms Will Use Every Day
7 Basketball Moves You’ll Use Every Game – Improve Your Basketball Game

Elon’s X


🖼️ Thumbnail 3:
HS

FIXING

What's WRONG with the Twitter x Logo !?

129K views + 2 weeks ag /PH

Title: What’s WRONG with the Twitter 𝕏 Logo ⁉️
Framework: What’s WRONG with (Trending Thing) ⁉️
​Hook score​: +484
Why this works:
Timeliness – This video was published right around the time Elon changed the name from Twitter to X and changed the logo, so everybody was talking about this.
Curiosity – This title builds curiosity by asking a question and the thumbnail builds curiosity with the red circle making you wonder what’s wrong with it.
Negativity – People are drawn to negativity and drama and critiquing a new logo of a popular company fits this perfectly.
How you can use this framework: Ask what’s wrong with something trending right now.
Examples of this framework in action:

What’s WRONG with the Barbie Movie ⁉️
What’s WRONG with Remote Work ⁉️

Get Your Personal YouTube Coach


🖼️ Thumbnail 4:
Hi, I'm your personal Al coach

How about “5 Easy Tips to increase Your

YouTube Views Today”? It is more specific and er)
has a clearer call to action.

vidIQ just launched their AI Personal YouTube coach to help you grow your channel.
It can help you:

Come up with video ideas
Write better titles
Write your video script
Write your tags and description
And more

If you wanna give it a try, you can get a 30-day trial for just $1 here:
Try vidIQ for $1.
​(Affiliate link)
Food Facts


🖼️ Thumbnail 5:
The truth about organic food - according to
science | Tim Spector

460K views + 4 weeks ago

Title: The truth about organic food – according to science | Tim Spector
Framework: The truth about (Topic) – according to (Authority) | (Authority Figure)
​Hook score​: +484
Why this works:
Curiosity – “The Truth About” makes you feel like you’re getting in on a secret and finally learning “the truth”. This phrase also opens a loop because you need to click to learn the truth.
Negativity – The title feels a little ominous and the thumbnail supports that feeling with the text admitting “I Was Wrong”.
Authority – “according to science” gives this title a little bit more credibility.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience the truth about a trending or controversial topic, according to an authority figure.
Examples of this framework in action:

The truth about Flying First Class – according to a flight attendant
The truth about Lamborghinis – according to a mechanic

Single Stream Speedrun


🖼️ Thumbnail 6:
Copper to Champion in ONE STREAM - R6
Ranked Speedrun Attempt

246K views + 1 month ago

Title: Copper to Champion in ONE STREAM – R6 Ranked Speedrun Attempt
Framework: (Beginner) to (Expert) in ONE (Activity or Unit of Time) – (Keywords)
​Hook score​: +891
Why this works:
Constraint – The challenge of going from beginner to expert level in just one stream makes for an entertaining video.
How you can use this framework: Take on the challenge of going from beginner to expert in a small unit of time.
Examples of this framework in action:

From 0 to 100,000 Subs with ONE Video – Cracking The Youtube Algorithm
How I Went From Beginner to Fluent Spanish Speaker in ONE Month

Mailbox Money


🖼️ Thumbnail 7:
How She Makes $50k a month from Mini-
Mailboxes?

348K views * 2 months ago

Title: How She Makes $50k a month from Mini-Mailboxes?
Framework: How She (Achieves Goal) from (New and Unlikely Opportunity)?
​Hook score​: +4702
Why this works:
Desire – This creator’s audience would love to make $50K a month with a business.
New Opportunity – New opportunities (like making money with mini-mailboxes) offer hope. People haven’t failed with making money from mini-mailboxes yet, so they’re excited about this new opportunity.
How you can use this framework: Share the story of how somebody achieves your audience’s dream goal with a new opportunity.
Examples of this framework in action:

How He Stopped Slicing The Golf Ball Using The Water Bottle Drill
How She Lost 20 Pounds With The “Insanity Workout”

Flop of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 8:
The truth about menopause supplements |
Dr Sarah Berry

15K views + 1 month ago

Title: The truth about menopause supplements | Dr Sarah Berry
Hook score: -90
Why this flopped: This title uses the same framework as the organic food title above, so why did it flop?
Probably because of the smaller market. Many people in this creator’s audience are interested in organic food, but I’m assuming not as many are currently interested in menopause supplements.
I’m not saying views are everything when it comes to YouTube videos, but if getting more views is your goal, it’s important to consider the size of the market.
Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
Hope you enjoyed this week’s edition and if you know a fellow creator who needs help with writing better titles, please send them to creatorhooks.com.
– Jake
(@jthomas__ on Twitter)
P.S. Read previous editions here.
P.P.S. If you want to see all of the videos from Creator Hooks in one place, check out Creator Hooks Pro.

## Newsletter 269: 5 YouTube Growth Hacks That Have Been Banned From YouTube

**URL:** https://creatorhooks.com/5-youtube-growth-hacks-that-have-been-banned-from-youtube/

```
5 YouTube Growth Hacks That Have Been Banned From YouTube 
August 20, 2023August 14, 2023 by admin 


I’ve asked some of the best creators on YouTube, “How do you write such great titles?”
And they’ve all said the same thing: “I just model what works.”
So this email will show you 5 videos worth modeling to help you write great titles and save time.
Btw, if you want to save more time and see all of the titles and thumbnails from Creator Hooks in one place, check out ​Creator Hooks Pro​.
Keepin Cool


🖼️ Thumbnail 2:
A MINI SPLIT WINDOW AC UNIT 2

Next-Gen Window AC | Efficient Cooling
Without The Annoying Noise

367K views + 2 weeks ago -

Title: Next-Gen Window AC | Efficient Cooling Without The Annoying Noise
Framework: (New Product) | (Goal) Without (Complaint)
​Hook score​: +458
Why this works:
Desire – This creator’s audience is into new products, like this next-gen window AC.
Pain Point – This title takes a big pain point of AC units (annoying noise) and says that this product will give you what you want without the thing you don’t want.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience about a new product that accomplishes a goal without their biggest pain point or annoyance.
Examples of this framework in action:

Next-Gen Camping Tent | The First Tent To Set Itself Up
Next-Gen Electric Car | Clean Energy Without Long Charging Times

The Name’s Rocket


🖼️ Thumbnail 3:
Rocket Raccoon Sculpture Timelapse -
Guardians of the Galaxy

893K views + 3m

Title: Rocket Raccoon Sculpture Timelapse – Guardians of the Galaxy
Framework: (Trending Entity) (Activity)
​Hook score​: +470
Why this works:
This is a simple example of adapting your content to what’s going on now. This video was published the day before Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 3 (a popular movie largely about Rocket Raccoon) was released.
How you can use this framework: Adapt your content to what everybody is talking about right now.
Examples of this framework in action:

I Made A Real Life Barbie House
I Tried Every Olympic Track Event — Here Are My Results

🖼️ Thumbnail 5:
Mark Zuckerberg tv)
@thezuck

There is a shortage of
software engineetgag fan

11:12 AM - Jul 2%

How to Avoid Burnout In Your Software Job
Search

65K views + 2 weeks ago

Title: How to Avoid Burnout In Your Software Job Search
Framework: How to Avoid (Pain Point) In Your (Activity)
​Hook score​: +684
Why this works:
Negativity – This is all about avoiding a pain point (in this case, burnout).
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience how they can avoid something bad happening to them on their journey.
Examples of this framework in action:

How to Avoid Drying Out Your Thanksgiving Turkey
How to Avoid Losing Money In The Stock Market

Ronaldo’s Ruses


🖼️ Thumbnail 6:
8 Football Tricks That Have Been BANNED
From Football

2.3M views + 2 months ago -

Title: 8 Football Tricks That Have Been BANNED From Football
Framework: 8 (Activity) Tricks That Have Been BANNED From (Entity)
​Hook score​: +766
Why this works:
Banned – “Banned” adds curiosity and controversy to this video, which makes it way more interesting than just regular football tricks.
List – Lists make videos feel more tangible (you know exactly what the video will be about) and they add some curiosity.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience about banned things in your niche.
Examples of this framework in action:

Healthy Supplements That Have Been BANNED From Pro Sports
8 Bar Drinks That Have Been BANNED In The US

Cricut Delights


🖼️ Thumbnail 7:
BET YA DIDN'T KNOW YOUR CRICUT COULD
DOTHIS

78K views * 1 month ago

Title: BET YA DIDN’T KNOW YOUR CRICUT COULD DO THIS 🧐
Framework: Bet Ya Didn’t Know (Tool) Could Do This 🧐
​Hook score​: +1003
Why this works:
Curiosity – This title opens a loop because it makes you wonder what your Cricut can do.
Desire – This creator’s audience loves using their Cricut.
How you can use this framework: Tell your audience they probably don’t know their favorite tool can do “this”.
Examples of this framework in action:

Bet Ya Didn’t Know ConvertKit Could Do This 🧐
You Probably Didn’t Know Zillow Could Do This 🧐

Flop of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 8:
Renting Equipment For DIY Home
Improvements] 9 Tips Every DlYer

17K views + 1 month ago

Title: Renting Equipment For DIY Home Improvements| 9 Tips Every DIYer Should Know
Hook score: -89
Why this flopped: I think this video flopped for two reasons:
1) The topic isn’t exactly what his audience watches his channel for. It seems like the videos that do best for him are small DIY house projects anybody can do (like installing an AC unit, the first video in this email), not big projects that require you to rent heavy duty equipment like what the thumbnail shows.
2) The potential audience is small. Many people have window AC units, but much fewer people are willing and able to rent heavy duty equipment for their house projects.
To avoid this mistake, know why your audience watches your channel and if your topic has a big enough potential audience.
Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
Hope you enjoyed this week’s edition and if you know a fellow creator who needs help with writing better titles, please send them to creatorhooks.com.
– Jake
(@jthomas__ on Twitter)

## Newsletter 271: Whats Working on YouTube In August to Grow Your Channel

**URL:** https://creatorhooks.com/whats-working-on-youtube-in-august-to-grow-your-channel/

```
What’s Working on YouTube In August to Grow Your Channel 
August 7, 2023 by admin 


I’ve asked some of the best creators on YouTube, “How do you write such great titles?”
And they’ve all said the same thing: “I just model what works.”
So this email will show you 5 videos worth modeling to help you write great titles and save time.
Btw, if you want to save more time and see all of the titles and thumbnails from Creator Hooks in one place, check out ​Creator Hooks Pro​.
Yikes


🖼️ Thumbnail 2:
UNFORGIVABLE Sin
N

What is the Unforgivable Sin?
350K views « 1 month ago + 65 VPH.

Title: What is the Unforgivable Sin?
Framework: What is the (Intense Negative Entity)?
​Hook score​: +300
Why this works:
Curiosity – This title builds curiosity by asking a question and making you want to know what the unforgivable sin is.
Negativity – This title and thumbnail are full of fear, which stresses the audience out, which makes them want to click.
Epic – “Unforgivable Sin” just has an epic feel to it, which makes this video more interesting.
How you can use this hook: Ask what an intense, negative thing is in your niche.
Examples of this hook in action:

What is the Unforgivable Lie?
What is the Deadliest Drug In America?

Timely Turnips


🖼️ Thumbnail 3:
= YOU STILL
HAVE
2 Time!

What Crops to Sow in June for Self-
Sufficiency

222K views + 2 months ago

Title: What Crops to Sow in June for Self-Sufficiency
Framework: What (Entity) to (Activity) in (Specific Time) for (Goal)
​Hook score​: +380
Why this works:
Desire – This creator’s audience loves crops and self-sufficiency.
Timeliness – This video was published on June 1, when people were thinking about what they could plant in June.
Curiosity – This title is a bit of a cliffhanger because it leaves you wanting to know which crops are in the video.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about things they can do this month to achieve a goal.
Examples of this hook in action:

What Stocks to Buy in August for Passive Income
What National Parks to Visit in September For Beautiful Hiking

Get Your Personal YouTube Coach


🖼️ Thumbnail 4:
Hi, I'm your personal Al coach

How about “5 Easy Tips to increase Your

YouTube Views Today”? It is more specific and er)
has a clearer call to action.

vidIQ just launched their AI Personal YouTube coach to help you grow your channel.
It can help you:

Come up with video ideas
Write better titles
Write your video script
Write your tags and description
And more

If you wanna give it a try, you can get a 30-day trial for just $1 here:
Try vidIQ for $1.
(Affiliate link)
The New “Ultimate Guide”


🖼️ Thumbnail 5:
Making A Music Video From Start to Finish
(Behind the Scenes, VFX, Color Grading &...

130K views » 1 month ago - 42 VPH

Title: Making A Music Video From Start to Finish (Behind the Scenes, VFX, Color Grading & MORE)
Framework: Making A (Entity) From Start to Finish ([Specific Details])
​Hook score​: +517
Why this works:
Desire – This creator’s audience wants to learn how to make music videos and showing them how to make one from start to finish promises a ton of value. It kinda feels like the new version of “The Ultimate Guide” content that got played out in the early days of content marketing.
Beginner – Talking about making videos from start to finish also feels very beginner-friendly, and beginners are the biggest, hungriest part of any audience.
Length – I’m not saying this is why this video did so well, but it’s interesting to note that this video is ~3 times longer than most of the other videos on this channel. It probably got a ton of watch time and gave the audience time to build a stronger bond with the creator.
How you can use this hook: Show your audience how to do something from start to finish.
Examples of this hook in action:

Getting Ready For Date Night From Start to Finish (Choosing My Outfit, Perfume & MORE)
Making A Brisket From Start to Finish (Selecting the Meat, Rub Recipe, Texas Crutch & MORE)

Six Figure Start


🖼️ Thumbnail 6:
FIRST YEAR

Six Figures My First Year As A YouTuber?
True Or False? You Decide...

313K views * 1 month ago

Title: $ix Figures My First Year As A YouTuber? True Or False? You Decide…
Framework: (Goal) My First Year As A (Position)? True Or False? You Decide…
​Hook score​: +788
Why this works:
This title breaks all the best practices, but it’s such a good idea that it still did well. Here’s why the idea is so good:
Desire – Most YouTubers would love to make six figures in their first year.
Beginners – Talking about his “first year” relates to beginners, which is the biggest, hungriest group of any audience.
Controversy – Many people think it’s impossible to earn six figures in their first year and this creator presses on that with “True Or False?” This question also builds curiosity.
Platform Fit – It’s probably worth mentioning that another reason this did so well is that it’s a YouTube video talking about how much he made on YouTube. If this video was about much he made on TikTok, it might not be so powerful.
How you can use this hook: Tell the story of how you accomplished something amazing in your first year (or other time frame or cycle).
Examples of this hook in action:

Sub 3 Hours My First Marathon? True Or False? You Decide…
I Beat The S&P By 46% In My First Year As An Investor

I’m Not Crying You’re Crying


🖼️ Thumbnail 7:
I secretly learned my mother's language

2.3M views + 3 months ago *

Title: I secretly learned my mother’s language
Framework: I secretly (Emotional Activity)
​Hook score​: +11143
Why this works:
Emotion – Speaking her mother’s native language with her hits you right in the feels because it’s so personal.
Curiosity – Learning her mother’s language “secretly” builds curiosity because people love secret things.
How you can use this hook: Secretly learn or do something powerfully emotional.
Examples of this hook in action:

I secretly learned sign language to talk with my coworker
I bought my old dog a doggy wheelchair

Flop of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 8:
A Day in the Life of Huw Richards

17K views + 2 months ago

Title: A Day in the Life of Huw Richards
Hook score: -77
Why this flopped: People watch this channel to learn something that benefits them.
The video featured from this channel earlier in this email titled, “What Crops to Sow in June for Self-Sufficiency,” is a good example of this.
But this video is creator-focused.
This is a good strategy for people like Logan Paul, Emma Chamberlain, or other entertainment creators, but not as much for educational creators.
One thing I would try is to position this video as a day in the life of what the audience’s dream is, like this:
“A Day in the Life of a Full-Time Farmer”
Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
Hope you enjoyed this week’s edition and if you know a fellow creator who needs help with writing better titles, please send them to creatorhooks.com.
– Jake
(@jthomas__ on Twitter)
P.S. Read previous editions here.
P.P.S. If you want to see all of the videos from Creator Hooks in one place, check out Creator Hooks Pro.

## Newsletter 272: Whats Working on YouTube In August to Grow Your Channel

**URL:** https://creatorhooks.com/whats-working-on-youtube-in-august-to-grow-your-channel/

```
What’s Working on YouTube In August to Grow Your Channel 
August 7, 2023 by admin 


I’ve asked some of the best creators on YouTube, “How do you write such great titles?”
And they’ve all said the same thing: “I just model what works.”
So this email will show you 5 videos worth modeling to help you write great titles and save time.
Btw, if you want to save more time and see all of the titles and thumbnails from Creator Hooks in one place, check out ​Creator Hooks Pro​.
Yikes


🖼️ Thumbnail 2:
UNFORGIVABLE Sin
N

What is the Unforgivable Sin?
350K views « 1 month ago + 65 VPH.

Title: What is the Unforgivable Sin?
Framework: What is the (Intense Negative Entity)?
​Hook score​: +300
Why this works:
Curiosity – This title builds curiosity by asking a question and making you want to know what the unforgivable sin is.
Negativity – This title and thumbnail are full of fear, which stresses the audience out, which makes them want to click.
Epic – “Unforgivable Sin” just has an epic feel to it, which makes this video more interesting.
How you can use this hook: Ask what an intense, negative thing is in your niche.
Examples of this hook in action:

What is the Unforgivable Lie?
What is the Deadliest Drug In America?

Timely Turnips


🖼️ Thumbnail 3:
= YOU STILL
HAVE
2 Time!

What Crops to Sow in June for Self-
Sufficiency

222K views + 2 months ago

Title: What Crops to Sow in June for Self-Sufficiency
Framework: What (Entity) to (Activity) in (Specific Time) for (Goal)
​Hook score​: +380
Why this works:
Desire – This creator’s audience loves crops and self-sufficiency.
Timeliness – This video was published on June 1, when people were thinking about what they could plant in June.
Curiosity – This title is a bit of a cliffhanger because it leaves you wanting to know which crops are in the video.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about things they can do this month to achieve a goal.
Examples of this hook in action:

What Stocks to Buy in August for Passive Income
What National Parks to Visit in September For Beautiful Hiking

Get Your Personal YouTube Coach


🖼️ Thumbnail 4:
Hi, I'm your personal Al coach

How about “5 Easy Tips to increase Your

YouTube Views Today”? It is more specific and er)
has a clearer call to action.

vidIQ just launched their AI Personal YouTube coach to help you grow your channel.
It can help you:

Come up with video ideas
Write better titles
Write your video script
Write your tags and description
And more

If you wanna give it a try, you can get a 30-day trial for just $1 here:
Try vidIQ for $1.
(Affiliate link)
The New “Ultimate Guide”


🖼️ Thumbnail 5:
Making A Music Video From Start to Finish
(Behind the Scenes, VFX, Color Grading &...

130K views » 1 month ago - 42 VPH

Title: Making A Music Video From Start to Finish (Behind the Scenes, VFX, Color Grading & MORE)
Framework: Making A (Entity) From Start to Finish ([Specific Details])
​Hook score​: +517
Why this works:
Desire – This creator’s audience wants to learn how to make music videos and showing them how to make one from start to finish promises a ton of value. It kinda feels like the new version of “The Ultimate Guide” content that got played out in the early days of content marketing.
Beginner – Talking about making videos from start to finish also feels very beginner-friendly, and beginners are the biggest, hungriest part of any audience.
Length – I’m not saying this is why this video did so well, but it’s interesting to note that this video is ~3 times longer than most of the other videos on this channel. It probably got a ton of watch time and gave the audience time to build a stronger bond with the creator.
How you can use this hook: Show your audience how to do something from start to finish.
Examples of this hook in action:

Getting Ready For Date Night From Start to Finish (Choosing My Outfit, Perfume & MORE)
Making A Brisket From Start to Finish (Selecting the Meat, Rub Recipe, Texas Crutch & MORE)

Six Figure Start


🖼️ Thumbnail 6:
FIRST YEAR

Six Figures My First Year As A YouTuber?
True Or False? You Decide...

313K views * 1 month ago

Title: $ix Figures My First Year As A YouTuber? True Or False? You Decide…
Framework: (Goal) My First Year As A (Position)? True Or False? You Decide…
​Hook score​: +788
Why this works:
This title breaks all the best practices, but it’s such a good idea that it still did well. Here’s why the idea is so good:
Desire – Most YouTubers would love to make six figures in their first year.
Beginners – Talking about his “first year” relates to beginners, which is the biggest, hungriest group of any audience.
Controversy – Many people think it’s impossible to earn six figures in their first year and this creator presses on that with “True Or False?” This question also builds curiosity.
Platform Fit – It’s probably worth mentioning that another reason this did so well is that it’s a YouTube video talking about how much he made on YouTube. If this video was about much he made on TikTok, it might not be so powerful.
How you can use this hook: Tell the story of how you accomplished something amazing in your first year (or other time frame or cycle).
Examples of this hook in action:

Sub 3 Hours My First Marathon? True Or False? You Decide…
I Beat The S&P By 46% In My First Year As An Investor

I’m Not Crying You’re Crying


🖼️ Thumbnail 7:
I secretly learned my mother's language

2.3M views + 3 months ago *

Title: I secretly learned my mother’s language
Framework: I secretly (Emotional Activity)
​Hook score​: +11143
Why this works:
Emotion – Speaking her mother’s native language with her hits you right in the feels because it’s so personal.
Curiosity – Learning her mother’s language “secretly” builds curiosity because people love secret things.
How you can use this hook: Secretly learn or do something powerfully emotional.
Examples of this hook in action:

I secretly learned sign language to talk with my coworker
I bought my old dog a doggy wheelchair

Flop of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 8:
A Day in the Life of Huw Richards

17K views + 2 months ago

Title: A Day in the Life of Huw Richards
Hook score: -77
Why this flopped: People watch this channel to learn something that benefits them.
The video featured from this channel earlier in this email titled, “What Crops to Sow in June for Self-Sufficiency,” is a good example of this.
But this video is creator-focused.
This is a good strategy for people like Logan Paul, Emma Chamberlain, or other entertainment creators, but not as much for educational creators.
One thing I would try is to position this video as a day in the life of what the audience’s dream is, like this:
“A Day in the Life of a Full-Time Farmer”
Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
Hope you enjoyed this week’s edition and if you know a fellow creator who needs help with writing better titles, please send them to creatorhooks.com.
– Jake
(@jthomas__ on Twitter)
P.S. Read previous editions here.
P.P.S. If you want to see all of the videos from Creator Hooks in one place, check out Creator Hooks Pro.

## Newsletter 273: 5 Small Changes To Improve Your YouTube Channel Forever

**URL:** https://creatorhooks.com/5-small-changes-to-improve-your-youtube-channel-forever/

```
5 Small Changes To Improve Your YouTube Channel Forever 
August 6, 2023July 31, 2023 by admin 


I’ve asked some of the best creators on YouTube, “How do you write such great titles?”
And they’ve all said the same thing: “I just model what works.”
So this email will show you 5 videos worth modeling to help you write great titles and save time.
Btw, if you want to save more time and see all of the titles and thumbnails from Creator Hooks in one place, check out ​Creator Hooks Pro​.
Decluttering Duties


🖼️ Thumbnail 2:
ONE-MINUTE Decluttering Habits For A
Organized & Clutter-Free Home (+15 Ideas!...

225K views * 3 weeks ago - 7 VPH

Title: ONE-MINUTE Decluttering Habits For A Organized & Clutter-Free Home (+15 Ideas!) 📦
Formula: ONE-MINUTE [Activity] Habits For A [End Result] (+15 Ideas!) [Emoji]
​Hook score​: +302
Why this works:
Desire – This creator’s audience wants an organized and clutter-free home.
Time Frame – “One-Minute” adds to the desire because the short habits seem easily doable. Plus, the time frame makes these habits feel more tangible, which makes this video more interesting.
List – Lists make videos feel more tangible (you know exactly what the video will be about) and they add some curiosity.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about the one-minute habits they can do to achieve a goal.
Examples of this hook in action:

ONE-MINUTE Exercise Habits To Lose Weight (+15 Ideas!)
ONE-MINUTE Spanish Speaking Habits To Be Fluent Fast

Livin Large


🖼️ Thumbnail 3:
8 Small Changes To Improve Your Life
Forever

203K views + 1 month ago

Title: 8 Small Changes To Improve Your Life Forever
Formula: 8 Small Changes To (Achieve Big Goal)
​Hook score​: +404
Why this works:
Desire – This creator’s audience wants to improve their life forever. Plus, the “small changes” to improve your life “forever” makes it seem like you’re getting a lot for a little.
Curiosity – There’s contrast between “small changes” and “forever”.
List – Lists make videos feel more tangible (you know exactly what the video will be about) and they add some curiosity.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about the small changes they can make for a big gain.
Examples of this hook in action:

8 Small Changes To Improve Your Marriage Forever
8 Small Changes To Improve Your Music Forever

Get Your Personal YouTube Coach


🖼️ Thumbnail 4:
Hi, I'm your personal Al coach

How about “5 Easy Tips to increase Your

YouTube Views Today”? It is more specific and er)
has a clearer call to action.

vidIQ just launched their AI Personal YouTube coach to help you grow your channel.
It can help you:

Come up with video ideas
Write better titles
Write your video script
Write your tags and description
And more

If you wanna give it a try, you can get a 30-day trial for just $1 here:
Try vidIQ for $1.
(Affiliate link)
Repulsive Rides


🖼️ Thumbnail 5:
| Shouldn't Be Allowed To Show You This.

1.2M views + 2 months ago

Title: I Shouldn’t Be Allowed To Show You This…
Formula: I Shouldn’t Be Allowed To (Activity)…
​Hook score​: +485
Why this works:
I normally don’t like this vague title style, but the thumbnail gives you enough context that the title doesn’t need to spell out what the video is about again.
The curiosity (this title promises to reveal a secret) and negativity (the disgusting car and rebellious vibe of the title) combine for successful packaging.
How you can use this hook: Hint that you’re letting your audience in on some drama.
Examples of this hook in action:

I Shouldn’t Be Allowed To Say This Out Loud…
They Shouldn’t Serve This At Restaurants…

Note: these hypothetical titles would obviously be paired with a fantastic (and context-providing) thumbnail.
Average Joe


🖼️ Thumbnail 6:
Can A Regular Guy Shoot Better Than A Navy
SEAL?

314K views + 3 weeks ago

Title: Can A Regular Guy Shoot Better Than A Navy SEAL?
Formula: Can A (Average Person) (Activity) Better Than A (Elite Person)?
​Hook score​: +564
Why this works:
Curiosity – This title builds curiosity two ways: 1) There’s contrast between “regular guy” and “Navy SEAL”. 2) It asks a question.
How you can use this hook: Ask if a regular person can do something better than a professional.
Examples of this hook in action:

Can A Regular Guy Shoot Better Than Steph Curry?
Can A Regular Guy Drive Faster Than An F1 Driver?

Price Is Right


🖼️ Thumbnail 7:
Guess The Price of These New Construction
Homes In Tampa! Which Would You...

30K views + 1 month ago

Title: Guess The Price of These New Construction Homes In Tampa! Which Would You Choose??
Formula: Guess The Price of These (Objects)! Which Would You Choose??
​Hook score​: +596
Why this works:
Curiosity – This builds curiosity by asking a question and feels kinda like trivia.
Desire – This creator’s audience is into new homes in Tampa.
How you can use this hook: Ask your audience to guess the price (or another attribute) of something.
Examples of this hook in action:

Guess The Price of These New Offshore Boats! Which Would You Choose??
Guess The Price of These Vintage Watches! Which Would You Choose??

Flop of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 8:
This Might Be The WORST Idea I've Ever
Had...

67K views « 1 month ago


Title: This Might Be The WORST Idea I’ve Ever Had…
Hook score: -82
Why this flopped: This title doesn’t give enough context for you to be interested in the video unless you’re a big fan of the creator.
If you’re going to use a vague title (like this channel did earlier in this email with the dirty car), the thumbnail needs to make it abundantly clear what your video is about.
This video has a vague title and a vague thumbnail, which is a recipe to land in the flop of the week.
It’s hard for your audience to be interested in a video where they don’t know what it’s about.
Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
Hope you enjoyed this week’s edition and if you know a fellow creator who needs help with writing better titles, please send them to creatorhooks.com.
– Jake
(@jthomas__ on Twitter)
P.S. Read previous editions here.
P.P.S. If you want to see all of the videos from Creator Hooks in one place, check out Creator Hooks Pro.
Read last week’s edition »

## Newsletter 275: 6 YouTube Tips In 6 Minutes

**URL:** https://creatorhooks.com/6-youtube-tips-in-6-minutes/

```
6 YouTube Tips In 6 Minutes 
July 24, 2023 by admin 


I’ve asked some of the best creators on YouTube, “How do you write such great titles?”
And they’ve all said the same thing: “I just model what works.”
So this email will show you 5 videos worth modeling to help you write great titles and save time.
Btw, if you want to save more time and see all of the titles and thumbnails from Creator Hooks in one place, check out ​Creator Hooks Pro​.
Baggy Is Back


🖼️ Thumbnail 2:
Why You Look BAD In Baggy/Wide Fit Pants
(16 MISTAKES)

136K views » 2 months ago » 27 VPH

Title: Why You Look BAD In Baggy/Wide Fit Pants (10 MISTAKES)
Formula: Why [Pain Point] (10 MISTAKES)
​Hook score​: +403
Why this works:
Negativity – This title addresses a pain point (looking bad in baggy pants) and mistakes around this pain point.
List – Lists make videos feel more tangible (you know exactly what the video will be about) and they add some curiosity.
How you can use this hook: Explain why your audience is experiencing a problem and the 10 mistakes they’re making.
Examples of this hook in action:

Why Nobody Subscribes To Your YouTube Channel (10 MISTAKES)
Why You’re So Poor (10 Money Mistakes)

A Key Feature


🖼️ Thumbnail 3:
.

NO USB REQUIRED??:¢:::

every MIDI keyboard should have this
feature in 2023

47K views « 2 months ago

Title: every MIDI keyboard should have this feature in 2023
Formula: every (Product) should have this feature in (Current Year)
​Hook score​: +534
Why this works:
Curiosity – This title opens a loop because it makes you wonder what “this feature” is.
Timeliness – Dropping the current year in there lets the audience know this is about new products.
Desire – Most audiences love hearing about products and tools.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about the feature every product needs right now.
Examples of this hook in action:

every Electric Car should have this feature in 2023
every Smart Watch should have this feature in 2023

Get Your Personal YouTube Coach


🖼️ Thumbnail 4:
Hi, I'm your personal Al coach

How about “5 Easy Tips to increase Your

YouTube Views Today”? It is more specific and er)
has a clearer call to action.

vidIQ just launched their AI Personal YouTube coach to help you grow your channel.
It can help you:

Come up with video ideas
Write better titles
Write your video script
Write your tags and description
And more

If you wanna give it a try, you can get a 30-day trial for just $1 here:
Try vidIQ for $1.
(Affiliate link)
Mr. Muscle


🖼️ Thumbnail 5:
The Only 10 Exercises Men Need To Build
Muscle | 4X Mr Olympia Chris Bumstead

2.7M views + 2 weeks ago + 359 VPH

Title: The Only 10 Exercises Men Need To Build Muscle | 4X Mr Olympia Chris Bumstead
Formula: The Only 10 (Things) (Specific Audience) Need To (Achieve Goal) | (Authority Figure)
​Hook score​: +1261
Why this works:
Desire – This creator’s audience wants to build muscle.
Specific Audience – Calling out men specifically makes men more likely to watch this video.
Curiosity – The constraint of “only 10” makes you wonder what these exercises are and why they’re so special.
List – Lists make videos feel more tangible (you know exactly what the video will be about) and they add some curiosity.
Authority – Dropping Chris Bumstead’s achievement of “4X Mr Olympia” adds credibility and authority to make him more believable.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about the only things they need to achieve a goal, according to an authority figure.
Examples of this hook in action:

The Only 3 Cities You Need To Visit In Italy (According To A Local)
The Only 10 Spices Amateur Chefs Need In Their Kitchen | Joël Robuchon 31 Michelin Stars

Crash Is Coming


🖼️ Thumbnail 6:
Do NOT
a House |

(You're being lied to)

Housing Market Predictions: The 2023
Housing Crash Has Just Begun (It's Now...

509K views « 10 days ago -

Title: Housing Market Predictions: The 2023 Housing Crash Has Just Begun (It’s Now Completely Illiquid)
Formula: [Niche] Predictions: The [Negative Event] Has Just Begun ([Problem])
​Hook score​: +1778
Why this works:
Negativity – This title is all about the fear of a housing market crash and the thumbnail adds to that with a warning.
Timeliness – Everybody seems to be waiting for a housing crash and putting the current year in there also grabs the audience’s attention.
Curiosity – Talking about predictions for the future builds a ton of curiosity.
How you can use this hook: Share your predictions for your niche and spice up the drama and fear.
Examples of this hook in action:

NFL Predictions: 2023-2024 Will Be The Softest Season Yet
Job Market Predictions: The 2023 Job Market Is The Most Competitive

Hiking Hints


🖼️ Thumbnail 7:
20 Thru Hiking Tips in 6 Minutes for Your
FIRST Thru Hike

193K views * 2 months ago - 56 VPH

Title: 20 Thru Hiking Tips in 6 Minutes for Your FIRST Thru Hike
Formula: 20 (Activity) Tips in 6 Minutes for Your FIRST (Activity)
​Hook score​: +2021
Why this works:
List – Lists make videos feel more tangible (you know exactly what the video will be about) and they add some curiosity.
Time Frame – The time frame of 6 minutes make this video with a lot of tips feel less intimidating.
Beginner – Making videos for beginners (in this case, for people looking for their FIRST thru hike) is a great strategy because it attracts a large, specific subset of the audience that is also willing to learn.
Desire – This creator’s audience loves thru hikes.
How you can use this hook: Give your audience 20 tips in a short amount of time to help with their first time doing something.
Examples of this hook in action:

20 Marathon Tips in 6 Minutes for Your FIRST Marathon
20 Home Buying Tips in 6 Minutes for Your FIRST House

Flop of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 8:
What Does Everyone Misunderstand About
Eugenics?

13K views + 3 weeks ago

Title: What Does Everyone Misunderstand About Eugenics?
Hook score: -85
Why this flopped: The best titles are written in simple, easy-to-read language.
Is “eugenics” a simple word? No.
Also, bold statements often work better than questions.
If I were to rewrite this title, I’d turn this into a statement with an opinion, instead of just posing a vague question about an intellectual topic.
Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
Hope you enjoyed this week’s edition and if you know a fellow creator who needs help with writing better titles, please send them to creatorhooks.com.
– Jake
(@jthomas__ on Twitter)
P.S. Read previous editions here.
P.P.S. If you want to see all of the videos from Creator Hooks in one place, check out Creator Hooks Pro.

## Newsletter 277: How To INSTANTLY Improve YouTube Titles

**URL:** https://creatorhooks.com/2846-2/

```
How To INSTANTLY Improve YouTube Titles 
July 23, 2023July 17, 2023 by admin 


I’ve asked some of the best creators on YouTube, “How do you write such great titles?”
And they’ve all said the same thing: “I just model what works.”
So this email will show you 5 videos worth modeling to help you write great titles and save time.
Btw, if you want to save more time and see all of the titles and thumbnails from Creator Hooks in one place, check out ​Creator Hooks Pro​.
I Love Love


🖼️ Thumbnail 2:
How to Attract Love Effortlessly: 4 Mindset
Shifts

161K views * 12 days ago

Title: How to Attract Love Effortlessly: 4 Mindset Shifts
Formula: How to (Achieve Goal) Effortlessly: 4 Mindset Shifts
​Hook score​: +342
Why this works:
Desire – Not only does this channel’s audience want to attract love, but “effortlessly” makes this video even more enticing and “mindset shifts” don’t sound like a lot of work.
List – Lists make videos feel more tangible (you know exactly what the video will be about) and they add some curiosity.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience how they can attract something they want effortlessly with a few mindset shifts.
Examples of this hook in action:

How to Lose Fat Effortlessly: 4 Mindset Shifts
How to Attract Clients Effortlessly: 4 Mindset Shifts

Myth Busters


🖼️ Thumbnail 3:
w

The
emotion ~
illusion ©

Lise Felaman Barret wae

The biggest myths about emotions,
debunked | Lisa Feldman Barrett

273K views * 1 month ago *

Title: The biggest myths about emotions, debunked | Lisa Feldman Barrett
Formula: The biggest myths about (Topic), debunked
​Hook score​: +493
Why this works:
Curiosity – Debunking myths is a great way to build curiosity.
How you can use this hook: Debunk the biggest myths about a complicated topic in your industry.
Examples of this hook in action:

The biggest myths about Camping, debunked
The biggest myths about San Francisco, debunked

Get Your Personal YouTube Coach


🖼️ Thumbnail 4:
Hi, I'm your personal Al coach

How about “5 Easy Tips to increase Your

YouTube Views Today”? It is more specific and er)
has a clearer call to action.

vidIQ just launched their AI Personal YouTube coach to help you grow your channel.
It can help you:

Come up with video ideas
Write better titles
Write your video script
Write your tags and description
And more

If you wanna give it a try, you can get a 30-day trial for just $1 here:
Try vidIQ for $1.
(Affiliate link)
Big Daddy Groot


🖼️ Thumbnail 5:
BABY GROOT to KING GROOT, explained in 3
minutes!

193K views - 2 months ago

Title: BABY GROOT to KING GROOT, explained in 3 minutes!
Formula: (Transformation), explained in 3 minutes!
​Hook score​: +660
Why this works:
Timeliness – This video was published 6 days after Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 3 was released, so everybody was talking about this at the time.
Time Frame – The time frame of “3 minutes” makes you feel like you’re going to understand this complex topic in a short amount of time.
How you can use this hook: Explain a transformation of something (preferably something trending) in a short amount of time.
Examples of this hook in action:

Web 1.0 to Web 3.0, explained in 3 minutes!
How to go from Obese to Jacked, explained in 3 minutes!

Controversial Titans


🖼️ Thumbnail 6:
Jordan Peterson's Thoughts on Andrew Tate

2.4M views + 1 month ago «

Title: Jordan Peterson’s Thoughts on Andrew Tate
Formula: (Controversial Figure)’s Thoughts on (Controversial Figure)
​Hook score​: +995
Why this works:
Authority – This is clash of the Controversial Titans right here. Dropping both of their names makes this video irresistible to this audience.
Drama – The expression on both of their faces promises this video will be filled with lots of drama.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience what one controversial figure thinks about another.
Examples of this hook in action:

Elon Musk’s Thoughts on Mark Zuckerberg
Ron De Santis’s Thoughts on AOC

*Mouth Starts Watering*


🖼️ Thumbnail 7:
$420 Dinner in Tokyo - Kobe Beef vs.
Matsusaka Beef vs. Kuroge Wagyu

2.4M views + 10 days ago

Title: $420 Dinner in Tokyo – Kobe Beef vs. Matsusaka Beef vs. Kuroge Wagyu
Formula: (Expensive Thing) – (Option 1) vs. (Option 2) vs. (Option 3)
​Hook score​: +1678
Why this works:
Desire – This channel’s audience loves expensive dinners and fancy steaks (can you still call them steaks at this level?).
Curiosity – This title makes you wonder what the outcome of this competition is and which beef is the best.
Authority – Although there are no “authority figures” in this title, Wagyu and Kobe are household names that grab people’s attention.
Epic – The expensive price tag, Tokyo, and the famous types of beef all make this video feel epic.
How you can use this hook: Compare high-end things in your niche against each other.
Examples of this hook in action:

$1,000 Golf Club – XXIO vs. Callaway vs. PING
$20,000 Camera Lens – Nikon vs. Sigma vs. Canon

Flop of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 8:
The First Cut is the Deepest

19K views + 1 month ago

Title: The First Cut is the Deepest
Hook score: -95
Why this flopped: This title breaks the #1 rule when it comes to writing titles:
Be clear, not clever.
This title tells you nothing about what’s in the video, so people don’t click.
To avoid making this mistake, be crystal clear about what your video is about in your title.
Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
Hope you enjoyed this week’s edition and if you know a fellow creator who needs help with writing better titles, please send them to creatorhooks.com.
– Jake
(@jthomas__ on Twitter)
P.S. Read previous editions here.
P.P.S. If you want to see all of the videos from Creator Hooks in one place, check out Creator Hooks Pro.

Thumbnail: The First Cut is the Deepest

```

```
