## Newsletter 278: How To INSTANTLY Improve YouTube Titles

**URL:** https://creatorhooks.com/2846-2/

```
How To INSTANTLY Improve YouTube Titles 
July 23, 2023July 17, 2023 by admin 


I’ve asked some of the best creators on YouTube, “How do you write such great titles?”
And they’ve all said the same thing: “I just model what works.”
So this email will show you 5 videos worth modeling to help you write great titles and save time.
Btw, if you want to save more time and see all of the titles and thumbnails from Creator Hooks in one place, check out ​Creator Hooks Pro​.
I Love Love


🖼️ Thumbnail 2:
How to Attract Love Effortlessly: 4 Mindset
Shifts

161K views * 12 days ago

Title: How to Attract Love Effortlessly: 4 Mindset Shifts
Formula: How to (Achieve Goal) Effortlessly: 4 Mindset Shifts
​Hook score​: +342
Why this works:
Desire – Not only does this channel’s audience want to attract love, but “effortlessly” makes this video even more enticing and “mindset shifts” don’t sound like a lot of work.
List – Lists make videos feel more tangible (you know exactly what the video will be about) and they add some curiosity.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience how they can attract something they want effortlessly with a few mindset shifts.
Examples of this hook in action:

How to Lose Fat Effortlessly: 4 Mindset Shifts
How to Attract Clients Effortlessly: 4 Mindset Shifts

Myth Busters


🖼️ Thumbnail 3:
w

The
emotion ~
illusion ©

Lise Felaman Barret wae

The biggest myths about emotions,
debunked | Lisa Feldman Barrett

273K views * 1 month ago *

Title: The biggest myths about emotions, debunked | Lisa Feldman Barrett
Formula: The biggest myths about (Topic), debunked
​Hook score​: +493
Why this works:
Curiosity – Debunking myths is a great way to build curiosity.
How you can use this hook: Debunk the biggest myths about a complicated topic in your industry.
Examples of this hook in action:

The biggest myths about Camping, debunked
The biggest myths about San Francisco, debunked

Get Your Personal YouTube Coach


🖼️ Thumbnail 4:
Hi, I'm your personal Al coach

How about “5 Easy Tips to increase Your

YouTube Views Today”? It is more specific and er)
has a clearer call to action.

vidIQ just launched their AI Personal YouTube coach to help you grow your channel.
It can help you:

Come up with video ideas
Write better titles
Write your video script
Write your tags and description
And more

If you wanna give it a try, you can get a 30-day trial for just $1 here:
Try vidIQ for $1.
(Affiliate link)
Big Daddy Groot


🖼️ Thumbnail 5:
BABY GROOT to KING GROOT, explained in 3
minutes!

193K views - 2 months ago

Title: BABY GROOT to KING GROOT, explained in 3 minutes!
Formula: (Transformation), explained in 3 minutes!
​Hook score​: +660
Why this works:
Timeliness – This video was published 6 days after Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 3 was released, so everybody was talking about this at the time.
Time Frame – The time frame of “3 minutes” makes you feel like you’re going to understand this complex topic in a short amount of time.
How you can use this hook: Explain a transformation of something (preferably something trending) in a short amount of time.
Examples of this hook in action:

Web 1.0 to Web 3.0, explained in 3 minutes!
How to go from Obese to Jacked, explained in 3 minutes!

Controversial Titans


🖼️ Thumbnail 6:
Jordan Peterson's Thoughts on Andrew Tate

2.4M views + 1 month ago «

Title: Jordan Peterson’s Thoughts on Andrew Tate
Formula: (Controversial Figure)’s Thoughts on (Controversial Figure)
​Hook score​: +995
Why this works:
Authority – This is clash of the Controversial Titans right here. Dropping both of their names makes this video irresistible to this audience.
Drama – The expression on both of their faces promises this video will be filled with lots of drama.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience what one controversial figure thinks about another.
Examples of this hook in action:

Elon Musk’s Thoughts on Mark Zuckerberg
Ron De Santis’s Thoughts on AOC

*Mouth Starts Watering*


🖼️ Thumbnail 7:
$420 Dinner in Tokyo - Kobe Beef vs.
Matsusaka Beef vs. Kuroge Wagyu

2.4M views + 10 days ago

Title: $420 Dinner in Tokyo – Kobe Beef vs. Matsusaka Beef vs. Kuroge Wagyu
Formula: (Expensive Thing) – (Option 1) vs. (Option 2) vs. (Option 3)
​Hook score​: +1678
Why this works:
Desire – This channel’s audience loves expensive dinners and fancy steaks (can you still call them steaks at this level?).
Curiosity – This title makes you wonder what the outcome of this competition is and which beef is the best.
Authority – Although there are no “authority figures” in this title, Wagyu and Kobe are household names that grab people’s attention.
Epic – The expensive price tag, Tokyo, and the famous types of beef all make this video feel epic.
How you can use this hook: Compare high-end things in your niche against each other.
Examples of this hook in action:

$1,000 Golf Club – XXIO vs. Callaway vs. PING
$20,000 Camera Lens – Nikon vs. Sigma vs. Canon

Flop of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 8:
The First Cut is the Deepest

19K views + 1 month ago

Title: The First Cut is the Deepest
Hook score: -95
Why this flopped: This title breaks the #1 rule when it comes to writing titles:
Be clear, not clever.
This title tells you nothing about what’s in the video, so people don’t click.
To avoid making this mistake, be crystal clear about what your video is about in your title.
Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
Hope you enjoyed this week’s edition and if you know a fellow creator who needs help with writing better titles, please send them to creatorhooks.com.
– Jake
(@jthomas__ on Twitter)
P.S. Read previous editions here.
P.P.S. If you want to see all of the videos from Creator Hooks in one place, check out Creator Hooks Pro.

## Newsletter 279: How To INSTANTLY Improve YouTube Titles

**URL:** https://creatorhooks.com/how-to-instantly-improve-youtube-titles/

```
How To INSTANTLY Improve YouTube Titles 
July 10, 2023 by admin 


I’ve asked some of the best creators on YouTube, “How do you write such great titles?”
And they’ve all said the same thing: “I just model what works.”
So this email will show you 5 videos worth modeling to help you write great titles and save time.
Btw, if you want to save more time and see all of the titles and thumbnails from Creator Hooks in one place, check out ​Creator Hooks Pro​.
Cheapies


🖼️ Thumbnail 2:
10 of the CHEAPEST PlayStation
Accessories You Should Buy!

138K views + 2 weeks ago -

Title: 10 of the CHEAPEST PlayStation Accessories You Should Buy!
Formula: 10 of the CHEAPEST (Entity) Accessories You Should Buy!
​Hook score​: +343
Why this works:
List – Lists make videos feel more tangible (you know exactly what the video will be about) and they add some curiosity.
Desire – This creator’s audience loves gaming accessories, especially cheap ones.
Extreme – These aren’t just cheap accessories, they’re the “CHEAPEST“, which builds curiosity because they’re extreme and unique.
Accessible – Everybody can benefit from cheap things, so this video has a broader audience than, say, the most expensive accessories you can buy.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about the cheapest accessories they should buy.
Examples of this hook in action:

10 of the CHEAPEST Sewing Accessories You Should Buy!
10 of the CHEAPEST Golf Accessories You Should Buy!

Ballin on a Budget


🖼️ Thumbnail 3:
Low Income
Budgeting

Boe

How | Manage My Money On A Low Income
(Budgeting + Saving)
48K views * 2 weeks ago « 34 VPH

Title: How I Manage My Money On A Low Income (Budgeting + Saving)
Formula: How I [Achieve Goal] On A [Tough, Common Condition] ([Keyword] + [Keyword])
​Hook score​: +371
Why this works:
Desire – This creator’s audience wants to know how to manage their money.
Specific Audience – By saying this is for people on a low income, this makes this video way more attractive to people on a low income (which is a large potential audience).
Negativity – This title calls out a pain point (low income) and promises to help manage it better.
Accessible – Like the video above about cheap accessories, this video appeals to a big audience because many people are on a low income.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience how to achieve a goal even if they’re in a tough situation.
Examples of this hook in action:

How I Study With A Full-Time Job (Productivity + Memory Tricks)
How I Travel On A Low Income (Flight & Hotel Hacks)

Get Your Personal YouTube Coach


🖼️ Thumbnail 4:
Hi, I'm your personal Al coach

How about “5 Easy Tips to increase Your

YouTube Views Today”? It is more specific and er)
has a clearer call to action.

vidIQ just launched their AI Personal YouTube coach to help you grow your channel.
It can help you:

Come up with video ideas
Write better titles
Write your video script
Write your tags and description
And more

If you wanna give it a try, you can get a 30-day trial for just $1 here:
Try vidIQ for $1.
(Affiliate link)
Just a small town girl


🖼️ Thumbnail 5:
| Visited the Most Remote Town in the USA
(Outside of Alaska)

3.1M views * 8 days ago + 3.3K VPH

Title: I Visited the Most Remote Town in the USA (Outside of Alaska)
Formula: I (Activity) the (Extreme Entity) in (Place)
​Hook score​: +501
Why this works:
Extreme – Doing extreme and epic things like this is more interesting than, say, “I Visited A Really Remote Town”.
Negativity – “The Most Remote Town” instills a bit of fear and heebie-jeebies, which also makes this video more interesting.
How you can use this hook: Do something epic and extreme.
Examples of this hook in action:

I Lifted the Heaviest Dumbbell In The World
I Played the Scariest Game In The World

Foot Stuff


🖼️ Thumbnail 6:
How to INSTANTLY Improve Circulation in
Your Feet and Toes

274K views + 1 month ago + 162 VPH

Title: How to INSTANTLY Improve Circulation in Your Feet and Toes
Formula: How to INSTANTLY (Achieve Goal)
​Hook score​: +522
Why this works:
Desire – Not only do many people want to improve the circulation in their feet and toes, but they also want to do it instantly.
Curiosity – “Do This” in the thumbnail is a cliffhanger that makes you want to click and find out what “this” is.
Negativity – “Bad Circulation?” in the thumbnail calls out a common pain point.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience how to instantly achieve a goal.
Examples of this hook in action:

How to INSTANTLY Quit Procrastinating
How to INSTANTLY Upgrade Your Style

It’s going down for real


🖼️ Thumbnail 7:
Al is worse
than climate

change!

EMERGENCY EPISODE: Ex-Google Officer
Finally Speaks Out On The Dangers Of Al! -...

5.4M views + 1 month ago »

Title: EMERGENCY EPISODE: Ex-Google Officer Finally Speaks Out On The Dangers Of AI! – Mo Gawdat | E252
Formula: EMERGENCY (Medium): Ex-(Company Employee) Finally Speaks Out On (Controversial and/or Trending Subject)
​Hook score​: +1013
Why this works:
Negativity – This title hypes up the drama with “EMERGENCY EPISODE” in the beginning and doubles down on it by talking about the dangers of AI. “EMERGENCY EPISODE” also adds urgency to this video to make you want to watch it now.
Authority – Since this is from an Ex-Google officer, this title has more credibility.
Timeliness – Everybody is talking about AI right now, so this video rides that wave.
How you can use this hook: Build up the drama with an “emergency” video and tell your audience about something dangerous and trending. (Bonus points if the warning comes from a reputable source.)
Examples of this hook in action:

EMERGENCY VIDEO: Ex-Instagram Officer Speaks Out On The Privacy Danger From Threads!
EMERGENCY EPISODE: Ex-NFL Coach Finally Speaks Out On The Dangers Of Concussions

Flop of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 8:
The Surprising & Unbelievable Dark Side Of
Open Relationships: Aubrey Marcus | E242

156K views » 2 months ago

Title: The Surprising & Unbelievable Dark Side Of Open Relationships: Aubrey Marcus | E242
Hook score: -80
Why this flopped: Good titles make you say, “Wait, what?!” *Clicks video to find out more*
This title makes me say, “There’s a dark side to open relationships? Duh!”
There’s no curiosity here because it’s obvious there’s a dark side to open relationships.
The best titles are non-obvious. They challenge the audience’s assumptions and show them a new truth.
To avoid making this mistake, know what your audience believes to be true and in your title and thumbnail show them they’re actually wrong.
Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
Hope you enjoyed this week’s edition and if you know a fellow creator who needs help with writing better titles, please send them to creatorhooks.com.
– Jake
(@jthomas__ on Twitter)
P.S. Read previous editions here.
P.P.S. If you want to see all of the videos from Creator Hooks in one place, check out Creator Hooks Pro.
Read last week’s edition »

## Newsletter 281: writing the perfect ONE WORD title for YouTube

**URL:** https://creatorhooks.com/writing-the-perfect-one-word-title-for-youtube/

```
writing the perfect ONE WORD title for YouTube 
July 3, 2023 by admin 


I’ve asked some of the best creators on YouTube, “How do you write such great titles?”
And they’ve all said the same thing: “I just model what works.”
So this email will show you 5 videos worth modeling to help you write great titles and save time.
Btw, if you want to save more time and see all of the titles and thumbnails from Creator Hooks in one place, check out ​Creator Hooks Pro​.
Smart Spanish


🖼️ Thumbnail 2:
Harder, = 4

j

You Should Be Learning Spanish Verbs Like
This

41K views + 1 month ago +

Title: You Should Be Learning Spanish Verbs Like This
Formula: You Should Be (Working Towards Goal) Like This
​Hook score​: +312
Why this works:
Desire – This creator’s audience wants to learn Spanish verbs, and the thumbnail adds to the desire with “study smarter not harder”.
Curiosity – This title is a cliffhanger so you have to click to find out how you should be learning Spanish verbs.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience how they should be working towards a goal.
Examples of this hook in action:

You Should Be Cooking Chicken Like This
You Should Be Planting Tomatoes Like This

One Tile One City


🖼️ Thumbnail 3:
Engineering the perfect ONE TILE city in
Cities Skylines!

2.3M views * 1 month ago

Title: Engineering the perfect ONE TILE city in Cities Skylines!
Formula: (Activity) the perfect ONE (Object) in (Entity)!
​Hook score​: +681
Why this works:
Constraint – The constraint of “one tile” adds curiosity because you want to know how he did it.
Desire – This creator’s audience wants to make (or watch this creator make) a perfect city.
How you can use this hook: Make something amazing with a constraint.
Examples of this hook in action:

Testing the perfect ONE WORD Pickup Line On Girls
Making the perfect THREE INGREDIENT Homemade Protein Bar!

Get Your Personal YouTube Coach


🖼️ Thumbnail 4:
Hi, I'm your personal Al coach

How about “5 Easy Tips to increase Your

YouTube Views Today”? It is more specific and er)
has a clearer call to action.

vidIQ just launched their AI Personal YouTube coach to help you grow your channel.
It can help you:

Come up with video ideas
Write better titles
Write your video script
Write your tags and description
And more

If you wanna give it a try, you can get a 30-day trial for just $1 here:
Try vidIQ for $1.
(Affiliate link)
Sad Smarts


🖼️ Thumbnail 5:
The Sad Story of the Smartest Man Who
Ever Lived

1.1M views + 1 month ago

Title: The Sad Story of the Smartest Man Who Ever Lived
Formula: The Sad Story of the (Extreme Entity)
​Hook score​: +1004
Why this works:
Negativity – People love drama and this title promises a lot of it.
Curiosity – This title builds curiosity two ways. 1) You would think it’s great to be the “Smartest Man Who Ever Lived”, so the fact that he has a sad story is a bit counterintuitive. 2) The fact that this is about the “Smartest Man Who Ever Lived” adds an element of uniqueness to the story.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about the dramatic story of an extreme person or thing in your niche.
Examples of this hook in action:

The Sad Story of the Tallest Man Who Ever Lived
The Scary Story of the Biggest Bank Scam In History

Duplicate Design


🖼️ Thumbnail 6:
i made the same design in every program
ever

731K views » 2 months ago » 160 VPH

Title: i made the same design in every program ever
Formula: i made the same (Object) in every (Entity) ever
​Hook score​: +1178
Why this works:
Curiosity – You want to see what happens in this experiment, so you have to click.
Credibility – There’s an element of credibility and believability to this video because it’s not just his opinion on the different design programs, he’s actually making something with them all.
How you can use this hook: Do the same thing across multiple platforms and show the audience what happened.
Examples of this hook in action:

i posted the same video on every social media platform
i made the same steak on every cooking appliance ever

What did you say?


🖼️ Thumbnail 7:
p YOU RE NOT T

Why You Struggle to ARTICULATE Your
Thoughts Clearly. 5 TRUTHS!

314K views » 3 weeks ago » 540 VPH

7 BY

Title: Why You Struggle to ARTICULATE Your Thoughts Clearly. 5 TRUTHS!
Formula: Why You Struggle to (Action). 5 TRUTHS!
​Hook score​: +2641
Why this works:
Negativity – The pain point of struggling to articulate your thoughts clearly grabs the audience’s attention and makes them want to watch.
Curiosity – In addition to this video being about a pain point, this title promises to tell you why you struggle with this pain point, so it makes you want to click and find out why.
List – This title finishes off with a list of 5 truths, which makes this video feel more tangible (you know exactly what the video will be about) and adds some curiosity.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience why they’re struggling with something.
Examples of this hook in action:

Why You Struggle to Take Beautiful Street Photography. 5 TRUTHS!
Why You Struggle to Catch Bass Consistently. 5 TRUTHS!

Flop of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 8:
The Tylenol Murders that Terrified America
12K views + 1 month ago « 2 VPH

Title: The Tylenol Murders that Terrified America
Hook score: -95
Why this flopped: I think this flopped for two reasons.
The first is that I don’t know how believable this is.
Yes, murders that terrified America make for a great story that could be successful on YouTube… but do you actually believe that there are Tylenol murders?
Tylenol feels too innocent. Nobody is here saying “I knew it! Let me check this out so I can tell my friends.”
Now if this video was about vaccine deaths that terrified America, then everybody would be saying “I knew it! Let me check this out so I can tell my friends.”
Also, this channel is about tech news and business, not medicine, so this is also about the wrong topic for this channel.
Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
Hope you enjoyed this week’s edition and if you know a fellow creator who needs help with writing better titles, please send them to creatorhooks.com.
– Jake
(@jthomas__ on Twitter)
P.S. Read previous editions here.
P.P.S. If you want to see all of the videos from Creator Hooks in one place, check out Creator Hooks Pro.

## Newsletter 283: The 1 Reason YouTubers Hit 1000000 Subscribers

**URL:** https://creatorhooks.com/the-1-reason-youtubers-hit-1000000-subscribers/

```
The #1 Reason YouTubers Hit 1,000,000 Subscribers 
June 26, 2023 by admin 


I’ve asked some of the best creators on YouTube, “How do you write such great titles?”
And they’ve all said the same thing: “I just model what works.”
So this email will show you 5 videos worth modeling to help you write great titles and save time.
Btw, if you want to save even more time and see all of the titles and thumbnails from Creator Hooks in one place, check out ​Creator Hooks Pro​.
Driving Drill


🖼️ Thumbnail 2:
The No.1 Reason | Am NOW Hitting My
Driver 300 Yards CARRY!...INCREDIBLE Drill!

160K views - 1 month ago

Title: The No.1 Reason I Am NOW Hitting My Driver 300 Yards CARRY!…INCREDIBLE Drill!
Formula: The No. 1 Reason I Am NOW (Achieving Goal)!…INCREDIBLE (Activity)!
​Hook score​: +385
Why this works:
Desire – This creator’s audience wants to hit their driver 300 yards.
Curiosity – This opens a loop because it doesn’t tell you what the “No.1 Reason” is, but makes you want to know what it is, so you have to click.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience the number 1 reason you’re achieving a goal.
Examples of this hook in action:

The No. 1 Reason I Am NOW Baking Delicious Bread Every Time!…INCREDIBLE Recipe!
The No. 1 Reason I Am NOW Catching HUGE Bass!

The 99% Trick


🖼️ Thumbnail 3:
99% of EXs WILL Come Back and CHASE you
IF you do this ONE THING | Scientific Studi...

109K views * 1 month ago + 36 VPH

Title: 99% of EXs WILL Come Back and CHASE you IF you do this ONE THING | Scientific Studies Included
Formula: 99% of (People) WILL (Positive Activity) IF you do this ONE THING
​Hook score​: +473
Why this works:
Desire – This creator’s audience wants their exs to come back and chase them.
Curiosity – This opens a loop because it doesn’t tell you what “this ONE THING” is, but makes you want to know what it is, so you have to click.
99% – The “99%” catches people’s eyes and adds a bit of curiosity.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience that what they really want will happen if they do this one thing.
Examples of this hook in action:

99% of Cars Would Be Faster If They Had This ONE Change
99% of Men Look Slimmer When They Wear This ONE Thing

Get Your Personal YouTube Coach


🖼️ Thumbnail 4:
Hi, I'm your personal Al coach

How about “5 Easy Tips to increase Your

YouTube Views Today”? It is more specific and er)
has a clearer call to action.

vidIQ just launched their AI Personal YouTube coach to help you grow your channel.
It can help you:

Come up with video ideas
Write better titles
Write your video script
Write your tags and description
And more

If you wanna give it a try, you can get a 30-day trial for just $1 here:
Try vidIQ for $1.
(Affiliate link)
Cool Quilt


🖼️ Thumbnail 5:
FREE Scrappy Quilt Pattern | a
Make 4 at a time-No Waste! fh faa

Create a Spectacular Quilt with Just Scraps
and this Free Pattern!

60K views + 2 months ago

Title: Create a Spectacular Quilt with Just Scraps and this Free Pattern!
Formula: Create a Spectacular (Outcome) with Just (Object) and this Free (Object)!
​Hook score​: +518
Why this works:
Desire – This title promises a lot for a little by saying you can get a spectacular quilt with “just scraps” and a “free pattern”.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience how they can make or do something amazing without having to spend or sacrifice a lot.
Examples of this hook in action:

Take Spectacular Photos with a $50 Camera and Natural Light!
Create Amazing Music with a Cheap Mic and this Free App!

Creepy Commissions


🖼️ Thumbnail 6:
My CREEPIEST Commission Story [Art +
Storytime]
486K views + 2 months ago » 62 VPH

Title: My CREEPIEST Commission Story [Art + Storytime]
Formula: My CREEPIEST (Niche) Story
​Hook score​: +926
Why this works:
Creepy – People just love creepy stories a lot.
How you can use this hook: Tell your creepiest story that’s relevant to your audience.
Examples of this hook in action:

My CREEPIEST Daycare Story
My CREEPIEST Uber Story

So Long Side Hustles


🖼️ Thumbnail 7:
Side
Hufstle

Want to be Rich? DON'T Start a Side Hustle.
439K views + 1 month ago » 106 VPH

Title: Want to be Rich? DON’T Start a Side Hustle.
Formula: Want to (Achieve Goal)? DON’T (Common Solution)
​Hook score​: +1040
Why this works:
Desire – This creator’s audience wants to be rich and the question in the beginning lets the audience know this video is for them.
Curiosity – A lot of people want to start side hustles because they want to be rich, so this title telling you not to start a side hustle is counterintuitive to that thought.
How you can use this hook: Call out your audience with a question and then tell them to do the opposite of what they think they should be doing.
Examples of this hook in action:

Want to be skinny? DON’T do Cardio
Want a new Job? DON’T Look on Indeed

Flop of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 8:
Scottie Scheffler Makes This Challenge Look
EASY...It's NOT!

10K views » 2 months ago

Title: Scottie Scheffler Makes This Challenge Look EASY…It’s NOT!
Hook score: -85
Why this flopped: There’s no reason for the audience to watch.
This channel is more about education than entertainment, so the audience wants to learn something from these videos.
But this title is all entertainment and doesn’t promise any benefit to the audience.
To avoid this mistake, know why your audience watches and deliver it to them in every video.
Also, if it were me, I’d test “World’s Best Golfer” in place of Scottie Scheffler’s name in the title.
I’m not really up to date with current golfers, but “World’s Best Golfer” is more broadly appealing than “Scottie Scheffler” (no offense, Scottie), and they already put his name in the thumbnail.
Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
Hope you enjoyed this week’s edition and if you know a fellow creator who needs help with writing better titles, please send them to creatorhooks.com.
– Jake
(@jthomas__ on Twitter)
P.S. Read previous editions here.
P.P.S. If you want to see all of the videos from Creator Hooks in one place, check out Creator Hooks Pro.

## Newsletter 284: The 1 Reason YouTubers Hit 1000000 Subscribers

**URL:** https://creatorhooks.com/the-1-reason-youtubers-hit-1000000-subscribers/

```
The #1 Reason YouTubers Hit 1,000,000 Subscribers 
June 26, 2023 by admin 


I’ve asked some of the best creators on YouTube, “How do you write such great titles?”
And they’ve all said the same thing: “I just model what works.”
So this email will show you 5 videos worth modeling to help you write great titles and save time.
Btw, if you want to save even more time and see all of the titles and thumbnails from Creator Hooks in one place, check out ​Creator Hooks Pro​.
Driving Drill


🖼️ Thumbnail 2:
The No.1 Reason | Am NOW Hitting My
Driver 300 Yards CARRY!...INCREDIBLE Drill!

160K views - 1 month ago

Title: The No.1 Reason I Am NOW Hitting My Driver 300 Yards CARRY!…INCREDIBLE Drill!
Formula: The No. 1 Reason I Am NOW (Achieving Goal)!…INCREDIBLE (Activity)!
​Hook score​: +385
Why this works:
Desire – This creator’s audience wants to hit their driver 300 yards.
Curiosity – This opens a loop because it doesn’t tell you what the “No.1 Reason” is, but makes you want to know what it is, so you have to click.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience the number 1 reason you’re achieving a goal.
Examples of this hook in action:

The No. 1 Reason I Am NOW Baking Delicious Bread Every Time!…INCREDIBLE Recipe!
The No. 1 Reason I Am NOW Catching HUGE Bass!

The 99% Trick


🖼️ Thumbnail 3:
99% of EXs WILL Come Back and CHASE you
IF you do this ONE THING | Scientific Studi...

109K views * 1 month ago + 36 VPH

Title: 99% of EXs WILL Come Back and CHASE you IF you do this ONE THING | Scientific Studies Included
Formula: 99% of (People) WILL (Positive Activity) IF you do this ONE THING
​Hook score​: +473
Why this works:
Desire – This creator’s audience wants their exs to come back and chase them.
Curiosity – This opens a loop because it doesn’t tell you what “this ONE THING” is, but makes you want to know what it is, so you have to click.
99% – The “99%” catches people’s eyes and adds a bit of curiosity.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience that what they really want will happen if they do this one thing.
Examples of this hook in action:

99% of Cars Would Be Faster If They Had This ONE Change
99% of Men Look Slimmer When They Wear This ONE Thing

Get Your Personal YouTube Coach


🖼️ Thumbnail 4:
Hi, I'm your personal Al coach

How about “5 Easy Tips to increase Your

YouTube Views Today”? It is more specific and er)
has a clearer call to action.

vidIQ just launched their AI Personal YouTube coach to help you grow your channel.
It can help you:

Come up with video ideas
Write better titles
Write your video script
Write your tags and description
And more

If you wanna give it a try, you can get a 30-day trial for just $1 here:
Try vidIQ for $1.
(Affiliate link)
Cool Quilt


🖼️ Thumbnail 5:
FREE Scrappy Quilt Pattern | a
Make 4 at a time-No Waste! fh faa

Create a Spectacular Quilt with Just Scraps
and this Free Pattern!

60K views + 2 months ago

Title: Create a Spectacular Quilt with Just Scraps and this Free Pattern!
Formula: Create a Spectacular (Outcome) with Just (Object) and this Free (Object)!
​Hook score​: +518
Why this works:
Desire – This title promises a lot for a little by saying you can get a spectacular quilt with “just scraps” and a “free pattern”.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience how they can make or do something amazing without having to spend or sacrifice a lot.
Examples of this hook in action:

Take Spectacular Photos with a $50 Camera and Natural Light!
Create Amazing Music with a Cheap Mic and this Free App!

Creepy Commissions


🖼️ Thumbnail 6:
My CREEPIEST Commission Story [Art +
Storytime]
486K views + 2 months ago » 62 VPH

Title: My CREEPIEST Commission Story [Art + Storytime]
Formula: My CREEPIEST (Niche) Story
​Hook score​: +926
Why this works:
Creepy – People just love creepy stories a lot.
How you can use this hook: Tell your creepiest story that’s relevant to your audience.
Examples of this hook in action:

My CREEPIEST Daycare Story
My CREEPIEST Uber Story

So Long Side Hustles


🖼️ Thumbnail 7:
Side
Hufstle

Want to be Rich? DON'T Start a Side Hustle.
439K views + 1 month ago » 106 VPH

Title: Want to be Rich? DON’T Start a Side Hustle.
Formula: Want to (Achieve Goal)? DON’T (Common Solution)
​Hook score​: +1040
Why this works:
Desire – This creator’s audience wants to be rich and the question in the beginning lets the audience know this video is for them.
Curiosity – A lot of people want to start side hustles because they want to be rich, so this title telling you not to start a side hustle is counterintuitive to that thought.
How you can use this hook: Call out your audience with a question and then tell them to do the opposite of what they think they should be doing.
Examples of this hook in action:

Want to be skinny? DON’T do Cardio
Want a new Job? DON’T Look on Indeed

Flop of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 8:
Scottie Scheffler Makes This Challenge Look
EASY...It's NOT!

10K views » 2 months ago

Title: Scottie Scheffler Makes This Challenge Look EASY…It’s NOT!
Hook score: -85
Why this flopped: There’s no reason for the audience to watch.
This channel is more about education than entertainment, so the audience wants to learn something from these videos.
But this title is all entertainment and doesn’t promise any benefit to the audience.
To avoid this mistake, know why your audience watches and deliver it to them in every video.
Also, if it were me, I’d test “World’s Best Golfer” in place of Scottie Scheffler’s name in the title.
I’m not really up to date with current golfers, but “World’s Best Golfer” is more broadly appealing than “Scottie Scheffler” (no offense, Scottie), and they already put his name in the thumbnail.
Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
Hope you enjoyed this week’s edition and if you know a fellow creator who needs help with writing better titles, please send them to creatorhooks.com.
– Jake
(@jthomas__ on Twitter)
P.S. Read previous editions here.
P.P.S. If you want to see all of the videos from Creator Hooks in one place, check out Creator Hooks Pro.

## Newsletter 285: He Posts 1 YouTube Video A Month  Makes 6 Figures

**URL:** https://creatorhooks.com/he-posts-1-youtube-video-a-month-makes-6-figures/

```
He Posts 1 YouTube Video A Month & Makes 6 Figures 
June 19, 2023 by admin 


I’ve asked some of the best creators on YouTube, “How do you write such great titles?”
And they’ve all said the same thing: “I just model what works.”
So this email will show you 5 videos worth modeling to help you write great titles and save time.
Btw, if you want to save more time and see all of the titles and thumbnails from Creator Hooks in one place, check out ​Creator Hooks Pro​.
Sunday Scaries


🖼️ Thumbnail 2:
The Scariest Day In NFL History

2.4M views + 4 weeks ago

Title: The Scariest Day In NFL History
Formula: The Scariest Day In (Entity) History
​Hook score​: +416
Why this works:
Negativity – People love clicking on titles with drama and fear and this title is full of both.
Curiosity – This builds curiosity by being extreme (Scariest Day In NFL History), and, therefore, unique.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about the scariest day in your niche’s history.
Examples of this hook in action:

The Scariest Day In American History
The Scariest Day In YouTube History

Murci Murci


🖼️ Thumbnail 3:
Mat Armstrong got SCAMMED on his
Murcielago project!

981K views + 2 weeks ago +

Title: Mat Armstrong got SCAMMED on his Murcielago project!
Formula: (Influencer) got SCAMMED on his (Object) project!
​Hook score​: +806
Why this works:
Authority – Mat Armstrong is a popular YouTuber in the car niche and this creator’s audience probably knows and likes him. Dropping his name in the title makes them more likely to click.
Negativity – People love drama and “SCAMMED” in the title and “THEY LIED…” in the thumbnail promise a lot of it.
How you can use this hook: Tell the story of how an influencer or celebrity in your niche got scammed.
Examples of this hook in action:

Jimmy Butler got CHEATED Out Of An NBA Title!
Graham Stephan got SCAMMED on his Investing project!

Get Your Personal YouTube Coach


🖼️ Thumbnail 4:
Hi, I'm your personal Al coach

How about “5 Easy Tips to increase Your

YouTube Views Today”? It is more specific and er)
has a clearer call to action.

vidIQ just launched their AI Personal YouTube coach to help you grow your channel.
It can help you:

Come up with video ideas
Write better titles
Write your video script
Write your tags and description
And more

If you wanna give it a try, you can get a 30-day trial for just $1 here:
Try vidIQ for $1.
(Affiliate link)
Mysterious Monsters


🖼️ Thumbnail 5:
Most People Don't Even Realize That This Is
In The Bible

538K views + 2 weeks ago

Title: Most People Don’t Even Realize That This Is In The Bible
Formula: Most People Don’t Even Realize That This Is In (Entity)
​Hook score​: +922
Why this works:
Curiosity – This builds curiosity three ways:
1) It opens a loop because it doesn’t tell you what “this” is.
2) It makes you feel like you’re getting in on a secret and learning the truth about what’s in the bible.
3) There’s contrast between the monster in the thumbnail and what people would expect to be in the bible.
Negativity – The scary monster does a great job at grabbing attention.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about what they don’t realize is in something of theirs.
Examples of this hook in action:

Most People Don’t Realize This Is In Instagram’s Terms of Service
Most People Don’t Even Realize That This Is In Their Garden

Livin Off The Land


🖼️ Thumbnail 6:
He Farms 35 Hours a Week By Himself and
Makes 6 Figures

1.4M views + 4 weeks ago

Title: He Farms 35 Hours a Week By Himself and Makes 6 Figures
Formula: He (Activity) (Number) Hours a Week (With Constraint) and (Achieves Goal)
​Hook score​: +1053
Why this works:
Desire – Many people in this creator’s audience would love to make 6 figures farming.
Constraint – The constraint of only 35 hours a week and farming by himself builds curiosity because it seems impossible.
Time Frame – The time frame of “35 hours a week” makes this video more tangible and, therefore, more interesting.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience the story of somebody achieving a goal even with constraints.
Examples of this hook in action:

He Codes 30 Hours a Week Without A Degree and Makes 6 Figures
She Sews 10 Hours a Week and Makes $5,000/Month

Hello Sony


🖼️ Thumbnail 7:
The Reason I'm Switching to Sony After 14
Years With Canon as a Professional...

78K views « 2 months ago

Title: The Reason I’m Switching to Sony After 14 Years With Canon as a Professional Photographer
Formula: The Reason I’m Switching to (Option) After (Time Frame) With (Option) as a (Profession)
​Hook score​: +1061
Why this works:
Curiosity – This builds curiosity two ways: 1) You want to know the reason she’s switching from Canon to Sony. 2) Change like this does a great job of starting a story and building curiosity.
Drama – There’s a bit of drama here with switching from one brand to another.
Time Frame – The time frame of 14 years shows how much experience with Canon she has, which adds to the drama of switching away from it. It also helps with this next point…
Credibility – This creator’s opinion holds more weight because she’s a professional photographer and she’s been using Canon for 14 years.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience the reason you’re switching from one option to another.
Examples of this hook in action:

The Reason I’m Switching to Brooks After 12 Years With Asics as an Olympic Runner
The Reason I’m Eating Meat Again After 5 Years As A Vegan

Flop of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 8:
Dear Earth..... You Need Hear This

14K views + 3 weeks ago

Title: Dear Earth….. You Need Hear This
Hook score: -86
Why this flopped: There’s a fine line between leaving out enough details to build curiosity and leaving out too many details and not getting your audience’s interest.
This title doesn’t have enough details.
This channel is about the bible, but there are no words in the title or images in the thumbnail to let the audience know that they would like this video.
There’s really not much at all in the thumbnail or title that lets you know what this video is about or who it’s for, so relatively few people clicked.
To avoid this mistake, make it clear in your title and thumbnail what your video is about and who it’s for.
Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
Hope you enjoyed this week’s edition and if you know a fellow creator who needs help with writing better titles, please send them to creatorhooks.com.
– Jake
(@jthomas__ on Twitter)
P.S. Read previous editions here.
P.P.S. If you want to see all of the videos from Creator Hooks in one place, check out Creator Hooks Pro.
Read last week’s edition »

## Newsletter 287: Dont Build A YouTube Channel Unless You Can Handle These 6 Things

**URL:** https://creatorhooks.com/dont-build-a-youtube-channel-unless-you-can-handle-these-6-things/

```
Don’t Build A YouTube Channel Unless You Can Handle These 6 Things 
June 12, 2023 by admin 


I’ve asked some of the best creators on YouTube, “How do you write such great titles?”
And they’ve all said the same thing: “I just model what works.”
So this email will show you 5 videos worth modeling to help you write great titles and save time.
Btw, if you want to save more time and see all of the titles and thumbnails from Creator Hooks in one place, check out ​Creator Hooks Pro​.
Copyright Sting


🖼️ Thumbnail 2:
STING'S MOST COPIED RIFF - Why it's so
HARD to get it right

1.7M views + 2 months ago

Title: STING’S MOST COPIED RIFF – Why it’s so HARD to get it right
Formula: (Entity)’s Most (Negative Action) (Object) – Why it’s so HARD to (Action)
​Hook score​: +338
Why this works:
Authority – Dropping the name of a very popular musician grabs the audience’s attention.
Negativity – “Most copied” brings a little drama to this title, and “why it’s so hard” adds a bit more negativity.
Curiosity – This title builds curiosity by not telling you what the riff is, so you have to click to find out.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about something that’s the most copied (or some other negative action).
Examples of this hook in action:

MARVEL’S MOST COPIED STORYTELLING Trick – Why it’s so HARD to get it right
Amazon’s Most Copied Conversion Tactic – Why it’s so HARD to get it right

Dolla Dolla Bill Y’all


🖼️ Thumbnail 3:
Eating For $1 A Day (Full Week)
776K views + 2 months ago » 107 VPH

Title: Eating For $1 A Day (Full Week)
Formula: [Activity] For $1 A Day (Full Week)
​Hook score​: +493
Why this works:
Curiosity – A challenge like this builds curiosity because you want to know how it worked out. Plus, the constraint of just $1 a day adds to the curiosity.
Time Frame – Adding a time frame to a challenge like this makes it more tangible (and therefore more interesting).
Relatable – Doing cheap challenges like this are more relatable than, say, “Eating for $1,000 A Day (Full Week)”.
How you can use this hook: Do something impossibly cheap for a full week.
Examples of this hook in action:

Living Off $1 A Day (Full Week)
Spending a Week In NYC For $50 A Day

Get Your Personal YouTube Coach


🖼️ Thumbnail 4:
Hi, I'm your personal Al coach

How about “5 Easy Tips to increase Your

YouTube Views Today”? It is more specific and er)
has a clearer call to action.

vidIQ just launched their AI Personal YouTube coach to help you grow your channel.
It can help you:

Come up with video ideas
Write better titles
Write your video script
Write your tags and description
And more

If you wanna give it a try, you can get a 30-day trial for just $1 here:
Try vidIQ for $1.
(Affiliate link)
Budget Boarding


🖼️ Thumbnail 5:
| Tried Buying The Cheapest House In
California

424K views + 1 month ago » 50 VPH

Title: I Tried Buying The Cheapest House In California
Formula: I Tried Buying The Cheapest (Object ) In (Place)
​Hook score​: +693
Why this works:
Curiosity – This builds curiosity because you want to know how it worked out. And taking things to the extreme (“the cheapest house”) adds to the curiosity because it’s unique.
How you can use this hook: Try to buy the cheapest thing in your niche for an interesting challenge and story.
Examples of this hook in action:

I Did a Photoshoot With The Cheapest Camera On Amazon
I Tried Booking The Cheapest Flight In America

Under Where?


🖼️ Thumbnail 6:
Em im...

Underwear SECRETS Every Woman Should
LEARN

672K views + 3 weeks ago + 3.4K VPH

Title: Underwear SECRETS Every Woman Should LEARN
Formula: (Provocative Subject) SECRETS Every (Specific Person) Should Learn
​Hook score​: +758
Why this works:
Curiosity – Talking about secrets is a great way to build curiosity.
Specific Audience – This creator calls out specifically who should watch this video with “Every Woman”, which gets more women to click.
Provocative Subject – This probably wouldn’t have performed as well as if it was about t-shirt secrets. (Although now that I think about it, maybe it would’ve done well since it’s relatable and everybody wears t-shirts? I think that would all come down to how good the thumbnail is.)
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about the secrets of a provocative subject in your niche that a specific audience should learn.
Examples of this hook in action:

Tax SECRETS Every Business Owner Should LEARN
Blackhat SEO SECRETS Every Website Owner Should Learn

Seattle Scaries


🖼️ Thumbnail 7:
Don't Move to Seattle,Washington Unless
You Can Handle These 6 Things!

9.6K views * 1 month ago -

Title: Don’t Move to Seattle,Washington Unless You Can Handle These 6 Things!
Formula: Don’t (Activity) Unless You Can Handle These 6 Things!
​Hook score​: +2157
Why this works:
Negativity – Warnings do a great job at grabbing our attention and getting us to click.
Curiosity – “These 6 Things” opens a loop because it doesn’t specify what the 6 things are, so you have to click to find out.
List – Lists make videos feel more tangible (you know exactly what the video will be about) and they add some curiosity.
How you can use this hook: Warn your audience that they shouldn’t do something unless they can handle a list of things.
Examples of this hook in action:

Don’t Take Fish Oil Unless You’re Doing These 6 Things!
Don’t Buy A Truck Unless You Can Handle These 6 Things!

Flop of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 8:
BEHIND ROCK'S MOST ICONIC GUITAR RIFF:
Deep Purple

91K views + 4 weeks ago

Title: BEHIND ROCK’S MOST ICONIC GUITAR RIFF: Deep Purple
Hook score: -87
Why this flopped: This is a similar format to the first video in this email (also from this channel): “STING’S MOST COPIED RIFF – Why it’s so HARD to get it right,” but there are 3 main differences:

Negativity often works better than positivity (“most copied” vs. “most iconic” and “why it’s so hard” vs. “behind rock’s most iconic guitar riff”).
There’s not much curiosity here. “Behind rock’s most iconic guitar riff” doesn’t really put any question in your mind, and the thumbnail and title tell you it’s about “Smoke On The Water” by Deep Purple.
I’m not the most knowledgeable when it comes to rock bands, but I know who Sting is and I’ve never heard of Deep Purple.

It might’ve been better if they wrote the title as “Behind Rock’s Most Iconic Guitar Riff” and in the thumbnail said something like “This Is Insane” or “This Is Genius!”
That would’ve added a little bit more curiosity.
They could’ve also added more curiosity or negativity, but I think that simple change would’ve done better with the current packaging idea.
And then there’s the fact that this video is half as long as the video about Sting’s most copied riff.
Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
Hope you enjoyed this week’s edition and if you know a fellow creator who needs help with writing better titles, please send them to creatorhooks.com.
– Jake
(@jthomas__ on Twitter)
P.S. Read previous editions here.
P.P.S. If you want to see all of the videos from Creator Hooks in one place, check out Creator Hooks Pro.

## Newsletter 289: I Tried MrBeasts 1000000 YouTube Strategy

**URL:** https://creatorhooks.com/i-tried-mrbeasts-1000000-youtube-strategy/

```
I Tried MrBeast’s $1,000,000 YouTube Strategy 
June 5, 2023 by admin 


I’ve asked some of the best creators on YouTube, “How do you write such great titles?”
And they’ve all said the same thing: “I just model what works.”
So this email will show you 5 videos worth modeling to help you write great titles and save time.
Btw, if you want to save more time and see all of the titles and thumbnails from Creator Hooks in one place, check out ​Creator Hooks Pro​.
Anti-Aging Is Expensive


🖼️ Thumbnail 2:
| Tried Bryan Johnsons $2,000,000 Anti-
Aging Routine

3.1M views + 1 month ago

Title: I Tried Bryan Johnsons $2,000,000 Anti-Aging Routine
Formula: I Tried (Authority Figure)’s $(Expensive) Anti-(Problem) Routine
​Hook score​: +332
Why this works:
Curiosity – This builds curiosity because it starts a story and makes you wonder how trying the routine went.
Number – Big numbers grab the audience’s attention in titles because they’re pattern interrupts (titles are typically made up of mostly words).
Epic – The big number also makes this video feel more epic.
Desire – Almost everybody wants an anti-aging solution.
Authority – Bryan Johnson is getting more and more popular for his weird anti-aging stuff, so dropping his name helped get more people interested in this video.
How you can use this hook: Try an authority figure’s epic routine to help accomplish a goal.
Examples of this hook in action:

I Tried LeBron James’ $100,000 Anti-Gravity Workout
I Tried Jeff Bezos’ $145 BILLION Morning Routine

Unlimited Pho


🖼️ Thumbnail 3:
What Can $1,000 Get in VIETNAM (World's
Cheapest Country)

773K views + 1 month ago

Title: What Can $1,000 Get in VIETNAM (World’s Cheapest Country)
Formula: What Can $1,000 Get in [Place] (World’s Cheapest [Entity])
​Hook score​: +546
Why this works:
Curiosity – This title opens a question in the audience’s mind and gets them wondering what $1,000 will get you in Vietnam.
Extreme – This video is so much more interesting because it’s about the “world’s cheapest country,” not just a pretty cheap country.
How you can use this hook: Show your audience what a certain amount of money gets you somewhere.
Examples of this hook in action:

What Can $1,000 Get in Dollar Tree (World’s Cheapest Store)
What Can $1,000 Get on eBay (World’s Biggest Auction)

Get Your Personal YouTube Coach


🖼️ Thumbnail 4:
Hi, I'm your personal Al coach

How about “5 Easy Tips to increase Your

YouTube Views Today”? It is more specific and er)
has a clearer call to action.

vidIQ just launched their AI Personal YouTube coach to help you grow your channel.
It can help you:

Come up with video ideas
Write better titles
Write your video script
Write your tags and description
And more

If you wanna give it a try, you can get a 30-day trial for just $1 here:
Try vidIQ for $1.
(Affiliate link)
Brown Ketchup?


🖼️ Thumbnail 5:
—y

This is REAL Ketchup and its Mind Blowing

783K views + 4 months ago

Title: This is REAL Ketchup and its Mind Blowing
Formula: This is REAL (Object) and its Mind Blowing
​Hook score​: +677
Why this works:
Curiosity – This video’s packaging is counterintuitive. The brown stuff is clearly not ketchup, but the thumbnail text and title make you wonder if maybe it actually is ketchup, so you have to click to find out.
Later in this email, there’s actually another video that used the exact same strategy.
How you can use this hook: Show your audience something that is not what they’re expecting.
Examples of this hook in action:

This is a REAL Camera and its Mind Blowing
This Makes $1,000,000/Year and its Cheap

End of the Road


🖼️ Thumbnail 6:
He lived (and died) through every
Backpacker's WORST Nightmare...

3M views + 3 months ago

Title: He lived (and died) through every Backpacker’s WORST Nightmare…
Formula: He lived (and died) through every [Occupation]’s WORST Nightmare…
​Hook score​: +1198
Why this works:
Negativity – This title is full of fear, which does a great job at getting people to click.
Curiosity – This title opens a loop and makes you wonder what exactly is a backpacker’s worst nightmare (and what happened to this guy?).
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience the dramatic story of how somebody in your niche lived and died through their worst nightmare.
Examples of this hook in action:

He survived every Investor’s WORST Nightmare…
She lived (and died) through every Actress’s WORST Nightmare…

Hammer Time


🖼️ Thumbnail 7:
THIS IS A HAMMER

The Hammer, Reinvented.
6.4M views + 2 weeks ago * 5.5K VPH

Title: The Hammer, Reinvented.
Formula: The (Object), Reinvented.
​Hook score​: +4740
Why this works:
Curiosity – The thumbnail is counterintuitive. It definitely doesn’t look like a hammer, but when you read the title, you wonder if maybe this is the hammer of the future.
New Opportunity – People love new things and this video is apparently about a new hammer.
How you can use this hook: Show your audience an object or tool that isn’t what they’re expecting.
Examples of this hook in action:

The Receptionist, Reinvented.
The Football, Reinvented.

Flop of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 8:
"| was So stone
hoy do | have

REBRETS IN

5 FATAL Mistakes that left this hiker
Stranded until Death (learn from them)

34K views + 2 months ago

Title: 5 FATAL Mistakes that left this hiker Stranded until Death (learn from them)
Hook score: -93
Why this flopped: Most of the videos that do well on this channel are entertaining stories:

“The Most Horrific Crime In National Park History” (329K views)
“The Strangest Death in the History of Backpacking” (492K views)
“He lived (and died) through every Backpacker’s WORST Nightmare…” (3M views)

Educational lists, like this one, don’t seem to do as well.
This channel isn’t that bad (they only have 2 content formats), but I’ve seen many creators treat their channels like a content dumping ground. (I think I got this term from ​@jamierawsthorne​.) They post podcasts, tutorials, vlogs, and whatever else they create on their channel.
If it were me, I’d double down on what’s working by creating just one content format, and stop doing everything else.
Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
Hope you enjoyed this week’s edition and if you know a fellow creator who needs help with writing better titles, please send them to creatorhooks.com.
– Jake
(@jthomas__ on Twitter)

## Newsletter 290: I Tried MrBeasts 1000000 YouTube Strategy

**URL:** https://creatorhooks.com/i-tried-mrbeasts-1000000-youtube-strategy/

```
I Tried MrBeast’s $1,000,000 YouTube Strategy 
June 5, 2023 by admin 


I’ve asked some of the best creators on YouTube, “How do you write such great titles?”
And they’ve all said the same thing: “I just model what works.”
So this email will show you 5 videos worth modeling to help you write great titles and save time.
Btw, if you want to save more time and see all of the titles and thumbnails from Creator Hooks in one place, check out ​Creator Hooks Pro​.
Anti-Aging Is Expensive


🖼️ Thumbnail 2:
| Tried Bryan Johnsons $2,000,000 Anti-
Aging Routine

3.1M views + 1 month ago

Title: I Tried Bryan Johnsons $2,000,000 Anti-Aging Routine
Formula: I Tried (Authority Figure)’s $(Expensive) Anti-(Problem) Routine
​Hook score​: +332
Why this works:
Curiosity – This builds curiosity because it starts a story and makes you wonder how trying the routine went.
Number – Big numbers grab the audience’s attention in titles because they’re pattern interrupts (titles are typically made up of mostly words).
Epic – The big number also makes this video feel more epic.
Desire – Almost everybody wants an anti-aging solution.
Authority – Bryan Johnson is getting more and more popular for his weird anti-aging stuff, so dropping his name helped get more people interested in this video.
How you can use this hook: Try an authority figure’s epic routine to help accomplish a goal.
Examples of this hook in action:

I Tried LeBron James’ $100,000 Anti-Gravity Workout
I Tried Jeff Bezos’ $145 BILLION Morning Routine

Unlimited Pho


🖼️ Thumbnail 3:
What Can $1,000 Get in VIETNAM (World's
Cheapest Country)

773K views + 1 month ago

Title: What Can $1,000 Get in VIETNAM (World’s Cheapest Country)
Formula: What Can $1,000 Get in [Place] (World’s Cheapest [Entity])
​Hook score​: +546
Why this works:
Curiosity – This title opens a question in the audience’s mind and gets them wondering what $1,000 will get you in Vietnam.
Extreme – This video is so much more interesting because it’s about the “world’s cheapest country,” not just a pretty cheap country.
How you can use this hook: Show your audience what a certain amount of money gets you somewhere.
Examples of this hook in action:

What Can $1,000 Get in Dollar Tree (World’s Cheapest Store)
What Can $1,000 Get on eBay (World’s Biggest Auction)

Get Your Personal YouTube Coach


🖼️ Thumbnail 4:
Hi, I'm your personal Al coach

How about “5 Easy Tips to increase Your

YouTube Views Today”? It is more specific and er)
has a clearer call to action.

vidIQ just launched their AI Personal YouTube coach to help you grow your channel.
It can help you:

Come up with video ideas
Write better titles
Write your video script
Write your tags and description
And more

If you wanna give it a try, you can get a 30-day trial for just $1 here:
Try vidIQ for $1.
(Affiliate link)
Brown Ketchup?


🖼️ Thumbnail 5:
—y

This is REAL Ketchup and its Mind Blowing

783K views + 4 months ago

Title: This is REAL Ketchup and its Mind Blowing
Formula: This is REAL (Object) and its Mind Blowing
​Hook score​: +677
Why this works:
Curiosity – This video’s packaging is counterintuitive. The brown stuff is clearly not ketchup, but the thumbnail text and title make you wonder if maybe it actually is ketchup, so you have to click to find out.
Later in this email, there’s actually another video that used the exact same strategy.
How you can use this hook: Show your audience something that is not what they’re expecting.
Examples of this hook in action:

This is a REAL Camera and its Mind Blowing
This Makes $1,000,000/Year and its Cheap

End of the Road


🖼️ Thumbnail 6:
He lived (and died) through every
Backpacker's WORST Nightmare...

3M views + 3 months ago

Title: He lived (and died) through every Backpacker’s WORST Nightmare…
Formula: He lived (and died) through every [Occupation]’s WORST Nightmare…
​Hook score​: +1198
Why this works:
Negativity – This title is full of fear, which does a great job at getting people to click.
Curiosity – This title opens a loop and makes you wonder what exactly is a backpacker’s worst nightmare (and what happened to this guy?).
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience the dramatic story of how somebody in your niche lived and died through their worst nightmare.
Examples of this hook in action:

He survived every Investor’s WORST Nightmare…
She lived (and died) through every Actress’s WORST Nightmare…

Hammer Time


🖼️ Thumbnail 7:
THIS IS A HAMMER

The Hammer, Reinvented.
6.4M views + 2 weeks ago * 5.5K VPH

Title: The Hammer, Reinvented.
Formula: The (Object), Reinvented.
​Hook score​: +4740
Why this works:
Curiosity – The thumbnail is counterintuitive. It definitely doesn’t look like a hammer, but when you read the title, you wonder if maybe this is the hammer of the future.
New Opportunity – People love new things and this video is apparently about a new hammer.
How you can use this hook: Show your audience an object or tool that isn’t what they’re expecting.
Examples of this hook in action:

The Receptionist, Reinvented.
The Football, Reinvented.

Flop of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 8:
"| was So stone
hoy do | have

REBRETS IN

5 FATAL Mistakes that left this hiker
Stranded until Death (learn from them)

34K views + 2 months ago

Title: 5 FATAL Mistakes that left this hiker Stranded until Death (learn from them)
Hook score: -93
Why this flopped: Most of the videos that do well on this channel are entertaining stories:

“The Most Horrific Crime In National Park History” (329K views)
“The Strangest Death in the History of Backpacking” (492K views)
“He lived (and died) through every Backpacker’s WORST Nightmare…” (3M views)

Educational lists, like this one, don’t seem to do as well.
This channel isn’t that bad (they only have 2 content formats), but I’ve seen many creators treat their channels like a content dumping ground. (I think I got this term from ​@jamierawsthorne​.) They post podcasts, tutorials, vlogs, and whatever else they create on their channel.
If it were me, I’d double down on what’s working by creating just one content format, and stop doing everything else.
Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
Hope you enjoyed this week’s edition and if you know a fellow creator who needs help with writing better titles, please send them to creatorhooks.com.
– Jake
(@jthomas__ on Twitter)
P.S. Read previous editions here.
P.P.S. If you want to see all of the videos from Creator Hooks in one place, check out Creator Hooks Pro.

## Newsletter 291: The 5 New AI Tools That Will Change YouTube Forever

**URL:** https://creatorhooks.com/the-5-new-ai-tools-that-will-change-youtube-forever/

```
The 5 New AI Tools That Will Change YouTube Forever 
May 29, 2023 by admin 


I’ve asked some of the best creators on YouTube, “How do you write such great titles?”
And they’ve all said the same thing: “I just model what works.”
So this email will show you 5 videos worth modeling to help you write great titles and save time.
Btw, if you want to save time and see all of the titles and thumbnails from Creator Hooks in one place, check out ​Creator Hooks Pro​.
Hidden Hangout


🖼️ Thumbnail 2:
| Built a Secret Gaming Room to Hide From
My Mom!

15M views + 4 months ago

Title: I Built a Secret Gaming Room to Hide From My Mom!
Formula: I Built a Secret (Activity) Room To (Action)!
​Hook score​: +487
Why this works:
Curiosity – Building a “secret” room and Matthew doing the shhh face in the thumbnail both build curiosity.
Desire – Maybe it’s just me, but I feel like this is something every kid wanted growing up.
How you can use this hook: Build a secret place to hide and do something cool.
Examples of this hook in action:

I Built a Secret Man Cave to Hide From My Kids!
I Built a Secret Music Room to Play My Guitar!

Sub 3


🖼️ Thumbnail 3:
3:59 12:48
FF

How | Went from 3:59 to 2:48 in the
MARATHON

63K views + 7 days ago

Title: How I Went from 3:59 to 2:48 in the MARATHON
Formula: How I Went from (Good) to (Great) in (Event)
​Hook score​: +588
Why this works:
Desire – Many people in this creator’s audience want to run a marathon in under 3 hours.
Progress – Telling people how you went from point A to point B starts a story and builds curiosity.
Credibility – This isn’t just an empty “how to” video, it’s about “How I”, which makes this video more believable.
How you can use this hook: Tell the story of how you went from good to great in something your audience wants to do.
Examples of this hook in action:

How I Went from Noob to Expert in Photoshop
How My Agency Went from $10,000 to $100,000/Month

Get Your Personal YouTube Coach


🖼️ Thumbnail 4:
Hi, I'm your personal Al coach

How about “5 Easy Tips to increase Your

YouTube Views Today”? It is more specific and er)
has a clearer call to action.

vidIQ just launched their AI Personal YouTube coach to help you grow your channel.
It can help you:

Come up with video ideas
Write better titles
Write your video script
Write your tags and description
And more

If you wanna give it a try, you can get a 30-day trial for just $1 here:
Try vidIQ for $1.
(Affiliate link)
Brain Training


🖼️ Thumbnail 5:
Train Your Brain To Make More Money Now |
Marisa Peer

94K views + 3 weeks ago

Title: Train Your Brain To Make More Money Now | Marisa Peer
Formula: Train Your Brain To (Achieve Goal)
​Hook score​: +644
Why this works:
Desire – This creator’s audience wants to make more money now.
Curiosity – There’s something about “train your brain” that feels like a shortcut that could actually work, but it’s mysterious enough that it builds curiosity and makes you want to click.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience how they can train their brain to achieve a goal.
Examples of this hook in action:

Train Your Brain To Be Fluent In Spanish In 2 Months
Train Your Brain To Quit Procrastinating

Travel Transformation


🖼️ Thumbnail 6:
The 5 New Commercial Aircraft That Will
Change The Way We Travel

368K views + 3 weeks ago » 283 VPH

Title: The 5 New Commercial Aircraft That Will Change The Way We Travel
Formula: The 5 New (Object) That Will Change The Way We (Activity)
​Hook score​: +1289
Why this works:
List – Lists make videos feel more tangible (you know exactly what the video will be about) and they add some curiosity.
Curiosity – “Change” builds curiosity because you know something will be different, but you don’t know what, so you have to click and find out.
“New” – People love new things. That’s it, that’s the tweet.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about new things that will change the way you currently do things.
Examples of this hook in action:

3 New Studies That Will Change The Way We Work Out
The 5 New AI Tools That Will Change Software Development Forever

Salt Slinger


🖼️ Thumbnail 7:
Mix detergent with SALT @ You will not
believe the incredible result

5.9M views + 1 month ago + 10.5K VPH

Title: Mix detergent with SALT 😱 You will not believe the incredible result
Formula: Mix (Object) with (Object) 😱 You will not believe the incredible result
​Hook score​: +1384
Why this works:
Curiosity – The title builds curiosity by opening a loop and not telling you what will happen. The thumbnail builds curiosity by showing something that’s just about to happen. Both of these make you feel the need to click and see what happens.
Broad audience – Everybody has detergent and salt in their home, so nearly anybody can enjoy this video.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience that something incredible happens when you mix two things together.
Examples of this hook in action:

Dip Oreos In Orange Juice 😱 You will not believe the incredible taste
Combine AI & Old School Businesses To Hit $10,000/Month

Flop of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 8:
NSW NERS PEED IO REMERS RST)

What Was it Like To Fly The SR-71 Blackbird

3.7K views + 1 month ago

Title: What Was it Like To Fly The SR-71 Blackbird
Hook score: -92
Why this flopped: Do you know what the SR-71 Blackbird is? Yeah, me neither.
And although this creator’s audience loves military planes and many of them might know what the SR-71 Blackbird is, it would be more powerful (and easy to read) if they titled this video something like:
“What It Was Like To Fly The Fastest Military Plane In History”
Giving things labels and taking them to the extreme is a great way to make obscure topics more exciting and easy to understand.
Oh… and when you’re making your thumbnails, make sure the text is easy to read.
Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
Hope you enjoyed this week’s edition and if you know a fellow creator who needs help with writing better titles, please send them to creatorhooks.com.
– Jake
(@jthomas__ on Twitter)
P.S. Read previous editions here.
P.P.S. If you want to see all of the videos from Creator Hooks in one place, check out Creator Hooks Pro.
Read last week’s edition »

Hook score: -92
Why this flopped: Do you know what the SR-71 Blackbird is? Yeah, me neither.
And although this creator’s audience loves military planes and many of them might know what the SR-71 Blackbird is, it would be more powerful (and easy to read) if they titled this video something like:
“What It Was Like To Fly The Fastest Military Plane In History”
Giving things labels and taking them to the extreme is a great way to make obscure topics more exciting and easy to understand.
Oh… and when you’re making your thumbnails, make sure the text is easy to read.
Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
Hope you enjoyed this week’s edition and if you know a fellow creator who needs help with writing better titles, please send them to creatorhooks.com.
– Jake
(@jthomas__ on Twitter)
P.S. Read previous editions here.
P.P.S. If you want to see all of the videos from Creator Hooks in one place, check out Creator Hooks Pro.

## Newsletter 293: 5 Reasons To Hit 100000 Subscribers As Soon As You Can

**URL:** https://creatorhooks.com/5-reasons-to-hit-100000-subscribers-as-soon-as-you-can/

```
5 Reasons To Hit 100,000 Subscribers As Soon As You Can 
May 22, 2023 by admin 


I’ve asked some of the best creators on YouTube, “How do you write such great titles?”
And they’ve all said the same thing: “I just model what works.”
So this email will show you 5 videos worth modeling to help you write great titles and save time.
P.S. If you want to save more time and see all of the titles and thumbnails from Creator Hooks in one place, check out ​Creator Hooks Pro​.
Max Blox


🖼️ Thumbnail 2:
Noob To Max Level With Dragon in One
Video! [Blox Fruits]

2.2M views + 2 months ago +

Title: Noob To Max Level With Dragon in One Video! [Blox Fruits]
Formula: (Beginner) To (Expert) With (Object) in One Video! [(Keyword)]
​Hook score​: +383
Why this works:
Curiosity – There’s contrast between “noob” and “max level” which builds curiosity (how did he do that?) and starts a story. Plus, “in One Video” adds a constraint which makes this even more interesting.
Beginners – Most gamers are noobs (everybody starts as a noob, but not everybody advances), so this attracts the biggest part of the audience.
How you can use this hook: Show your audience how you went from noob to expert in something.
Examples of this hook in action:

Beginner To Expert Seamstress in One Video! [Sewing Tutorial]
Fat To Fit in One Month!

AI Achievement


🖼️ Thumbnail 3:
How to Make $10,561 / month with Digital
Products Using Al

261K views « 1 month ago

Title: How to Make $10,561 / month with Digital Products Using AI
Formula: How to (Achieve Goal) with (Object) Using AI
​Hook score​: +498
Why this works:
Desire – This creator’s audience wants to make over $10K/month, and making it with digital products using AI makes this possibility even more exciting.
Timeliness – This title rides the wave of everybody being excited about AI.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience how to achieve a goal with AI.
Examples of this hook in action:

How to Beat a Rubik’s Cube in 3 Minutes Using AI
How to Learn Coding In One Month Using AI

Get Your Personal YouTube Coach


🖼️ Thumbnail 4:
Hi, I'm your personal Al coach

How about “5 Easy Tips to increase Your

YouTube Views Today”? It is more specific and er)
has a clearer call to action.

vidIQ just launched their AI Personal YouTube coach to help you grow your channel.
It can help you:

Come up with video ideas
Write better titles
Write your video script
Write your tags and description
And more

If you wanna give it a try, you can get a 30-day trial for just $1 here:
Try vidIQ for $1.
(Affiliate link)
Retirement Ready


🖼️ Thumbnail 5:
5 REASONS TELLING YOU

5 Reasons to Retire as Soon as You Can

295K views * 1 month ago

Title: 5 Reasons to Retire as Soon as You Can
Formula: 5 Reasons to (Action) as Soon as You Can
​Hook score​: +781
Why this works:
List – Lists make videos feel more tangible (you know exactly what the video will be about) and they add some curiosity.
Urgency – This title hints that there’s some urgency to retire, which makes you want to click more.
Desire – This creator’s audience wants to retire as soon as they can.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience the 5 reasons to do something they already want to do as soon as they can.
Examples of this hook in action:

5 Reasons to Start a YouTube Channel as Soon as You Can
5 Reasons to Sell Your Niche Website as Soon as You Can

That New New


🖼️ Thumbnail 6:
Apple's New Glasses Are Genius, Here's Why
90K views « 3 weeks ago * 2717 VPH

Title: Apple’s New Glasses Are Genius, Here’s Why
Formula: (New Product) Are Genius, Here’s Why
​Hook score​: +1600
Why this works:
Desire – This creator’s audience loves new products.
Curiosity – The “Here’s Why” opens a loop that makes you want to click and learn why these glasses are genius.
Timeliness – This is about a new product and people love new.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about how a new product is genius and explain why.
Examples of this hook in action:

Fender’s New Guitar Is Genius, Here’s Why
Nike’s New Shoes Are Genius, Here’s Why

Food 50


🖼️ Thumbnail 7:
ANTI “AGING ay

Top 8 Anti-Aging Foods To Eat After 50
937K views * 2 months ago +

Title: Top 8 Anti-Aging Foods To Eat After 50
Formula: Top 8 Anti-(Problem) (Objects) To (Action) (For Specific Audience)
​Hook score​: +3481
Why this works:
List – Lists make videos feel more tangible (you know exactly what the video will be about) and they add some curiosity.
Negativity – This video talks about aging, which is a big problem this target audience is facing.
Specific Audience – By calling out people over 50, this attracts a specific (but huge) audience, which makes them more likely to click.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about the products that can help solve a problem for a specific audience.
Examples of this hook in action:

Top 8 Anti-Inflammatory Meals To Cook For Arthritis
Top 10 Anti-Recessions Stocks To Buy In 2023

Flop of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 8:
< tch time LS Subs
4.0K +2. ok

IN 12 wanes

| got monetized in 12 weeks - HERE'S HOW |
DID IT

7.6K views * 1 month ago

Title: I got monetized in 12 weeks – HERE’S HOW I DID IT
Hook score: -91
Why this flopped: This title is good because it’s about achieving a big goal in a specific time frame, right?
Well, maybe if this was a YouTube education channel.
But it’s not.
She talks about selling on etsy, digital products, and side hustles. Her audience probably doesn’t know or care what “I got monetized” means.
This video probably would’ve done better if she spoke in terms her audience understands.
Maybe something like, “I Made $X,XXX On YouTube in 12 weeks – HERE’S HOW I DID IT.”
Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
Hope you enjoyed this week’s edition and if you know a fellow creator who needs help with writing better titles, please send them to creatorhooks.com.
– Jake
(@jthomas__ on Twitter)
P.S. Read previous editions here.
P.P.S. If you want to see all of the videos from Creator Hooks in one place, check out Creator Hooks Pro.
Read last week’s edition »

## Newsletter 295: YouTube CEO This Algorithm Shift Will Change The Entire Platform

**URL:** https://creatorhooks.com/youtube-ceo-this-algorithm-shift-will-change-the-entire-platform/

```
YouTube CEO: “This Algorithm Shift Will Change The Entire Platform” 
May 15, 2023 by admin 


I’ve asked some of the best creators on YouTube, “How do you write such great titles?”
And they’ve all said the same thing: “I just model what works.”
So this email will show you 5 videos worth modeling to help you write great titles and save time.
Btw, if you want to save more time and see all of the titles and thumbnails from Creator Hooks in one place, check out Creator Hooks Pro.
Curious Cube


🖼️ Thumbnail 2:
The Cube: Saudi Arabia's Next Megaproject
2.2M views + 2 months ago * 240 VPH

Title: The Cube: Saudi Arabia’s Next Megaproject
Formula: (Name): (Entity)’s Next (Impressive Object)
Hook score: +522
Why this works:
Epic – “Megaproject” and “Impossible Cube” along with the image of the cube in the thumbnail make this video feel epic.
Future – This is about Saudi Arabia’s next megaproject, and talking about the future builds curiosity because the future is obviously unknown.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about something epic coming in the future.
Examples of this hook in action:

The Vaporfly: Nike’s Next Super Shoe
Madame Web: Marvel’s Next Mega Franchise

Telling Triggers


🖼️ Thumbnail 3:
By FEELING / me

These Triggers Are “The Tell” That You were
Neglected and Ostracized As a Child

260K views * 1 month ago «

Title: These Triggers Are “The Tell” That You were Neglected and Ostracized As a Child
Formula: These Triggers Are “The Tell” That You Were (Negative Action)
Hook score: +634
Why this works:
Curiosity – This title opens a loop because it doesn’t specify what “these triggers” are, so you have to click and find out.
Negativity – This picks at a very intense pain point for many people, which makes them want to click and learn more about it.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about the triggers that reveal something bad happened to them.
Examples of this hook in action:

These Triggers Are “The Tell” Your YouTube Channel Is Failing
These Are The Signs Your Garden Is Dying

Get Your Personal YouTube Coach


🖼️ Thumbnail 4:
Hi, I'm your personal Al coach

How about “5 Easy Tips to increase Your

YouTube Views Today”? It is more specific and er)
has a clearer call to action.

vidIQ just launched their AI Personal YouTube coach to help you grow your channel.
It can help you:

Come up with video ideas
Write better titles
Write your video script
Write your tags and description
And more

If you wanna give it a try, you can get a 30-day trial for just $1 here:
Try vidIQ for $1.
(Affiliate link)
Free Tools


🖼️ Thumbnail 5:
12 Free Tools I'd Use to Learn Japanese (If |
Could Start Over)

142K views * 1 month ago «

Title: 12 Free Tools I’d Use to Learn Japanese (If I Could Start Over)
Formula: 12 Free Tools I’d Use to [Achieve Goal] (If I Could Start Over)
Hook score: +716
Why this works:
List – Lists make videos feel more tangible (you know exactly what the video will be about) and they add some curiosity.
Desire – This creator’s audience wants to learn Japanese and “free tools” makes it sound even better.
Beginner tips – The “If I Could Start Over” part relates to and attracts beginners, which is the biggest, hungriest part of any audience.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about the free tools you’d use to learn your subject if you could start over.
Examples of this hook in action:

12 Free Tools I’d Use to Learn Guitar (If I Could Start Over)
12 Free Tools I’d Use to Learn Graphic Design (If I Could Start Over)

Realistic Renders


🖼️ Thumbnail 6:
| Made Famous Logos ULTRA Realistic
4.5M views + 1 month ago » 388 VPH

Title: I Made Famous Logos ULTRA Realistic
Formula: I Made Famous (Objects) ULTRA (Description)
Hook score: +1020
Why this works:
Curiosity – The realistic logo on the right being blurred out and the title both do a great job at starting a story, but leaving you on a cliffhanger so you have to click and see what they look like and how he did it.
How you can use this hook: Turn something famous into an unexpected version of it.
Examples of this hook in action:

I Played Famous Guitar Solos On The Piano
I Made Famous Logos Into Desserts

End of EVs


🖼️ Thumbnail 7:
Toyota CEO: "This New Engine Will Destroy
The Entire EV Industry!"

8.6M views + 1 month ago

Title: Toyota CEO: “This New Engine Will Destroy The Entire EV Industry!”
Formula: (Authority Figure): “This New (Object) Will Destroy The Entire (Industry)!”
Hook score: +12857
Why this works:
Negativity – “Destroy the Entire EV Industry” is full of drama, which grabs people’s attention and makes them click.
Timeliness – The fact that this is about a new engine makes this feel like news and tells the audience this is relevant right now.
Authority – This uses the position of “Toyota CEO” to grab attention and build credibility.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about dramatic news an authority figure announced.
Examples of this hook in action:

Microsoft CEO: “This New Technology Will Destroy The Entire Gaming Industry!”
Elon Musk: “This New Rocket Will Destroy The Entire Space Industry!”

Flop of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 8:
Cutting vie Unie

Tren Maya: Mexico‘s Biggest Megaproject
77K views + 2 months ago » 4 VPH

Title: Tren Maya: Mexico‘s Biggest Megaproject
Hook score: -89
Why this flopped: I think this flopped because it’s a boring idea.
The Tren Maya is just a train cutting through the jungle that nobody wants (according to the video).
Compare this to the other video in this edition from this channel mentioned earlier about The Cube.
It’s a massive golden cube big enough to fit 20 Empire State Buildings inside of it.
Because this is a bleh idea, the thumbnail turned out bleh too.
The image in the Tren Maya thumbnail is a little unclear. Then, once you realize it’s just a path cut in the jungle, it’s unimpressive (and kinda sad if you’re a tree hugger like me).
To avoid this mistake, make sure you have a great topic, title and thumbnail before you spend time and money creating your video.
Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
Hope you enjoyed this week’s edition and if you know a fellow creator who needs help with writing better titles, please send them to creatorhooks.com.
– Jake
(@jthomas__ on Twitter)
P.S. Read previous editions here.
P.P.S. If you want to see all of the videos from Creator Hooks in one place, check out Creator Hooks Pro.
