How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about a 20-minute daily study plan to help them achieve their goals.
Examples of this hook in action:

MBA study plan – 20-minute daily study routine
Physics study plan – 20-minute daily physics learning routine

Motor Mirage


🖼️ Thumbnail 3:
The GENIUS Reason Why F1
Steering Looks BROKEN

1.3M views + 1 month ago » 16 VPH

Title: The GENIUS Reason Why F1 Steering Looks BROKEN
Formula: The GENIUS Reason Why (Object) Looks BROKEN
Hook score: +426
Why this works:
Curiosity – There’s contrast between “genius reason” and “looks broken”, which builds curiosity and interest.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about a genius reason something in your niche looks broken.
Examples of this hook in action:

The GENIUS Reason Why the Stock Market Looks BROKEN
The GENIUS Reason Why Picasso’s Paintings Look WEIRD

Learn the Science of Succeeding as a Creator
If you love Creator Hooks, you’ll love Creator Science – a newsletter designed to help you accelerate your journey to becoming a professional creator.
Today’s most successful creators aren’t just artists – they’re scientists. They are students of the game and are constantly running experiments to understand what kind of content is working.
Creator Science is written by my friend Jay Clouse, a full-time creator earning nearly $350,000 per year.
Each week you’ll receive:

Specific growth advice
Interviews with top creators
Insight from his own creator business

Subscribe for free at creatorscience.com.
Sewing Secrets


🖼️ Thumbnail 4:
6 SECRET SEWING TOOLS YOU
DDN TANGA YOU INIEIEIBIEDE

81K views * 3 weeks ago + 147 VPH

Title: 6 SECRET SEWING TOOLS YOU DID’NT KNOW YOU NEEDED!
Formula: 6 Secret (Industry) Tools You Didn’t Know You Needed!
Hook score: +440
Why this works:
Curiosity – This title makes you feel like you’re getting in on a secret.
List – Lists make videos feel more tangible (you know exactly what the video will be about) and they add some curiosity.
Desire – This creator’s audience loves learning about sewing tools.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about the secret sewing tools they didn’t know they needed.
Examples of this hook in action:

6 Secret Productivity Tools You Didn’t Know You Needed!
6 Secret Fishing Gadgets You Didn’t Know You Needed!

Terrible Teammates


🖼️ Thumbnail 5:
10 BIG NFL Names Whose
Teammentes Are SICK Of Thema

241K views * 2 weeks ago

Title: 10 BIG NFL Names Whose Teammates Are SICK Of Them
Formula: 10 BIG (Entity) Whose (Members) Are SICK Of Them
Hook score: +453
Why this works:
Negativity – There’s lots of drama in this title, and people love drama.
Authority – “Big NFL Names” grabs your attention more than just “NFL Names”. (Plus it builds curiosity by leaving it open-ended, so you want to know who these players are.)
List – Lists make videos feel more tangible (you know exactly what the video will be about) and they add some curiosity.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about a list of negative things that are happening in your niche.
Examples of this hook in action:

5 Austin Neighborhoods People Hate Living In
10 TikTok Trends Everybody Is Sick Of

Pencil Pursuit


🖼️ Thumbnail 6:
| Tested EVERY Colored Pencil in
ine WOR BL

930K views + 1 month ago * 170 VPH

Title: I Tested EVERY Colored Pencil in the WORLD!
Formula: I Tested EVERY (Object) in the WORLD!
Hook score: +492
Why this works:
Desire – This creator’s audience is into colored pencils.
Credibility – This title makes you feel like this video is legit, so you’re more likely to watch it.
Epic – Testing every colored pencil “in the WORLD!” makes this video feel epic.
How you can use this hook: Pick out a tool your audience loves and test every option in the world.
Examples of this hook in action:

I Tested EVERY Habit Tracker in the WORLD!
I Tested EVERY Pizza Place in NYC!

Flop of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 7:
10 Times Fans Were Caught Crying
Over Their Team in the Stands

18K views * 1 month ago

Title: 10 Times Fans Were Caught Crying Over Their Team in the Stands
Hook score: -71
Why this flopped: This title and thumbnail don’t fit with the rest on this channel and they don’t call out this channel’s core audience.
Every other video on this channel is about the NFL, and these creators make it obvious they’re about the NFL.
They have “NFL” in the title and NFL players in the thumbnails.
But not this one.
So the next time you make a video, make sure it’s about a topic your audience wants to watch on your channel, and make it obvious in the title and thumbnail that your video is about that topic.
Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
Have any questions about the hooks in this edition?
Reply to this email and let me know!
And if you know a fellow creator who needs help with writing better titles, please send them to creatorhooks.com.
Here’s to the war on boring content.
– Jake
(@jthomas__ on Twitter)
P.S. Read previous editions here.
P.P.S. If you want to see all of the videos from Creator Hooks in one place, check out Creator Hooks Pro.

## Newsletter 333: YouTube Finally Unbanned This

**URL:** https://creatorhooks.com/youtube-finally-unbanned-this/

```
YouTube Finally Unbanned This… 
January 11, 2023January 2, 2023 by admin 


I’ve asked some of the best creators on YouTube, “How do you write such great titles?”
And they’ve all said the same thing: “I just model what works.”
So this email will show you 5 videos worth modeling to help you write great titles and save time.
Btw, if you want to see all of the titles and thumbnails from Creator Hooks in one place, check out Creator Hooks Pro.
Jammin Jeans


🖼️ Thumbnail 2:
The BEST Jeans For Small Waists
and Thick Thighs!

184K views * 1 month ago +

Title: The BEST Jeans For Small Waists and Thick Thighs!
Formula: The BEST (Object) For (Specific Audience)!
Hook score: +322
Why this works:
Specific Audience – If you have a small waist and thick thighs, you feel like this video was made just for you, so you’re more likely to click.
Desire – Many people in this creator’s audience apparently have a small waist and thick thighs and they’re looking for the best jeans for them.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about the best product for a specific type of person.
Examples of this hook in action:

The BEST Investments For People In Their 20s!
The BEST Cameras For Newbie Photographers!

Pump Up The Volume


🖼️ Thumbnail 3:
VOLUMIZIING SIAR PRODUCTS =OIR
FINITE THIN erie TSTININ ING FLAIR...

14K views + 3 weeks ago » 1 VPH

Title: VOLUMIZING HAIR PRODUCTS FOR FINE THIN and THINNING HAIR *before and after*
Formula: (Products) For (Pain Point) *before and after*
Hook Score: +444
Why this works:
Pain Point – This title calls out “thin and thinning hair”, and if you’re struggling with that, you’re pretty likely to click.
Desire – This creator’s audience is into volumizing hair products.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about products that solve a specific pain point.
Examples of this hook in action:

Fat-Burning Workouts To Get Rid Of Belly Fat *before and after*
Best Fertilizer For Brown Lawns *before and after*

Write Better YouTube Titles In 1 Hour
I A/B tested 103 YouTube Titles and put all the results into an ebook.
Here’s a sample page:


🖼️ Thumbnail 4:
A: 6 Ways To Create Remarkable Photos

B: | Stopped Taking Boring Photos Once |
Learned This

A:3.10% CTR B:4.01%CTR_ Difference: 29%

Explanation: Title A is a good list title, but Title B
mentions a pain point ("Stopped Taking Boring
Photos") and builds curiosity by opening a loop
("Once | Learned This"). In this test, these two
factors helped increase the CTR.

If you want to see all 103 test results and breakdowns, get the book here.
First-Class Fancy


🖼️ Thumbnail 5:
Riding Japan's $120 Completely
Private First Class Bus | Tokyo -...

4.2M views + 2 months ago *

Title: Riding Japan’s $120 Completely Private First Class Bus | Tokyo – Osaka
Formula: (Activity) (Entity)’s (Price) (Object) | (Keyword)
Hook Score: +515
Why this works:
Desire – This creator’s audience is into private first-class travel. Plus, “$120” in the title makes it feel more tangible.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about an amazing experience you had, and make it more tangible by including a number, like the price.
Examples of this hook in action:

Riding America’s $100 Beautiful Rocky Mountain Train | Colorado, USA
Eating A $1,000 Philly Cheese Steak

Finally Free


🖼️ Thumbnail 6:
Lifesteal Finally Unbenned This...

390K views * 2 months ago + 97 VPH

Title: Lifesteal Finally Unbanned This…
Formula: (Entity) Finally (Controversial Activity) This…
Hook score: +731
Why this works:
Timely – “Finally Unbanned” makes this video feel like breaking news, and people love breaking news.
Curiosity – This title opens a loop by saying Lifesteal unbanned “this”, but not specifying what “this” is.
Negativity – The word “unbanned” feels controversial.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about something that was finally unbanned.
Examples of this hook in action:

YouTube Finally Unbanned This…
Apple Finally Unbanned This…

New Year New Channel


🖼️ Thumbnail 7:
How I'd Start a YouTube Channel in
2028

45K views * 1 month ago + 768 VPH

Title: How I’d Start a YouTube Channel in 2023
Formula: How I’d Start a (New Project) in (Upcoming Year)
Hook score: +1068
Why this works:
Timeliness – Starting a YouTube channel in 2023 is on many people’s minds right now, so this title rides that wave.
Beginners – This is about “starting” a channel, not scaling a channel. It caters to beginners, which are the biggest, hungriest subset of any audience.
Desire – This creator’s audience would love to start a YouTube channel in 2023.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience how you would start something for the upcoming year.
Examples of this hook in action:

How I’d Learn To Cook BBQ in 2023
How I’d Start an Ecommerce Store in 2023

Flop of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 8:
NTAL SCH,
“ oe ave |

4TH 7

POST GRAD
PLANS?
17:08

Dental School Q&A | Entering My 4th
Year!

6.6K views + 1 month ago

Title: Dental School Q&A | Entering My 4th Year!
Hook score: -90
Why this flopped: This title probably flopped for two reasons: its boring and off-topic.
It’s boring because there’s no emotion. She could’ve added emotion by talking about the hardest or worst thing about dental school.
And it’s off-topic because most of her videos are about clothes, not dental school.
So the next time you make a video, make sure its about a topic your audience is interested in watching on your channel and include emotion into the title.
Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
If you know a fellow creator who needs help with writing better titles, please send them to creatorhooks.com.
Here’s to the war on boring content.
– Jake
(@jthomas__ on Twitter)
P.S. Read previous editions here.
P.P.S. If you want to see all of the videos from Creator Hooks in one place, check out Creator Hooks Pro.

## Newsletter 335: 7 YouTube Predictions for 2023

**URL:** https://creatorhooks.com/7-youtube-predictions-for-2023/

```
7 YouTube Predictions for 2023 
December 26, 2022 by admin 


I’ve asked some of the best creators on YouTube, “How do you write such great titles?”
And they’ve all said the same thing: “I just model what works.”
So this email will show you 5 videos worth modeling to help you write great titles and save time.
Btw, if you want to see all of the titles and thumbnails from Creator Hooks in one place, check out Creator Hooks Pro.
2023 Changes


🖼️ Thumbnail 2:
ADVANCED SEO TIPS, TRICKS, AND...

7 SFO Predictions fer 2023 (ind
row to Preparal)

41K views * 2 weeks ago * 57 VPH

Title: 7 SEO Predictions for 2023 (and How to Prepare!)
Formula: 7 [Industry] Predictions for [Upcoming Year] (and How to Prepare!)
Hook score: +392
Why this works:
List – Lists make videos feel more tangible (you know exactly what the video will be about) and they add some curiosity.
Curiosity – Talking about the future (this was published in December 2022) builds curiosity because the future is obviously unknown, but we all want to know what will happen.
Timeliness – It’s currently the end of 2022 and everybody is thinking ahead to 2023.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about your predictions for the future.
Examples of this hook in action:

7 Real Estate Predictions for 2023 (and How to Prepare!)
7 Job Market Predictions for 2023 (and How to Prepare!)

No Excuses


🖼️ Thumbnail 3:
How to Build viuscle At Any Age (7
TIPS!)

557K views * 2 months ago «

Title: How to Build Muscle At Any Age (7 TIPS!)
Formula: How to [Achieve Goal] [Refute Objection] (7 TIPS!)
Hook score: +655
Why this works:
Desire – This creator’s audience wants to build muscle, especially as they get older.
Refute Objection – This is all about refuting the objection of, “I’m too old to build muscle.”
List – Lists make videos feel more tangible (you know exactly what the video will be about) and they add some curiosity.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience how to achieve a goal and refute their biggest excuse in the title.
Examples of this hook in action:

How to Build Wealth Even If You’re Poor (7 TIPS!)
How to Take Great Photos In Bad Lighting (7 TIPS!)

The Easiest Way To Spy On Your Competitors
Two of my favorite tools for spying on my competitors:
1) vidIQ’s Trending Tool
2) vidIQ’s Competitor Dashboard
Their trending tool lets you see what videos are getting views on a channel right now (this is an awesome alternative to just sorting a channel’s videos by most popular, which may show old videos that aren’t getting views anymore).
And their competitors feature lets you add competitors to a dashboard so you can see all of their top videos in one place, instead of taking the time to go to each channel individually.
​Download vidIQ for free here.
(Affiliate link)
Horrifying History


🖼️ Thumbnail 4:
The Creepiest Historic Hauntings In
The British Isles | Historic...

874K views * 1 month ago

Title: The Creepiest Historic Hauntings In The British Isles | Historic Hauntings | Absolute History
Formula: The Creepiest (Objects) | (Keyword)
Hook score: +903
Why this works:
Timely – This was published the day before Halloween when everybody was thinking about scary and creepy things.
Curiosity – Taking things to the extreme (“The Creepiest” ) builds curiosity because it’s unique.
Negativity – This is all about fear and scary things, which grab your attention.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about an extreme, trending thing.
Examples of this hook in action:

The Creepiest Websites You’ve Never Heard Of
The Weirdest Christmas Family Traditions I’ve Ever Heard

Curler Craziness


🖼️ Thumbnail 5:
Shark Released Their Hair Styler...
OMG!

598K views * 2 months ago

Title: Shark Released Their Hair Styler… OMG!
Formula: (Company) Released Their (Product)… OMG!
Hook score: +910
Why this works:
Curiosity – “…OMG!” opens a loop because it’s vague, but makes you want to know what she thought about the hair styler.
Timeliness – This feels like news (people love news) because it’s about a newly-released product.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about a newly-released product.
Examples of this hook in action:

YouTube Released Their “YouTube Wrapped”… OMG!
Nike Released Their New Running Shoe… OMG!

Prime Time


🖼️ Thumbnail 6:
Forget the Oven, This is How | Make
a Prime Rib Now

336K views + 7 days ago

Title: Forget the Oven, This is How I Make a Prime Rib Now
Formula: Forget (Common Tool), This is How I (Activity) Now
Hook score: +3200
Why this works:
Negativity – This title is about moving away from the old way of doing things.
Curiosity – “This is How I Make a Prime Rib Now” builds curiosity by opening a loop. It’s a cliff-hanger and makes you wonder how he makes prime rib now.
Desire – This creator’s audience would love to make prime rib that looks like the one in the thumbnail.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience how you’re moving on from an old way of doing things.
Examples of this hook in action:

Forget Meditation, This is How I’m Staying Focused Now
Forget Amazon, This is How I’m Selling Products Online Now

Flop of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 7:
How To Stay Healthy While Traveling
(7 TIPS)

12K views * 4 months ago

Title: How To Stay Healthy While Traveling (7 TIPS)
Hook score: -80
Why this flopped: I know this is a topic that’s commonly talked about in fitness, but I don’t think it’s that big of a pain point.
How much do you really care about eating healthy while you’re traveling?
I think building muscle when you’re older (see the video from this creator mentioned earlier) is a much bigger pain point.
Plus, the thumbnail doesn’t really look like it’s about fitness. It’s a girl in a bikini with the words “Travel Tips”. Looks like a travel video to me, so this creator’s core audience might just gloss over it.
And finally, since this title flopped, I might try to write it from a negative angle. Something like:

“Don’t Do This If You Want To Stay Healthy When Traveling”

Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
If you know a fellow creator who needs help with writing better titles, please send them to creatorhooks.com.
Here’s to the war on boring content.
– Jake
(@jthomas__ on Twitter)
P.S. Read previous editions here.

## Newsletter 336: 7 YouTube Predictions for 2023

**URL:** https://creatorhooks.com/7-youtube-predictions-for-2023/

```
7 YouTube Predictions for 2023 
December 26, 2022 by admin 


I’ve asked some of the best creators on YouTube, “How do you write such great titles?”
And they’ve all said the same thing: “I just model what works.”
So this email will show you 5 videos worth modeling to help you write great titles and save time.
Btw, if you want to see all of the titles and thumbnails from Creator Hooks in one place, check out Creator Hooks Pro.
2023 Changes


🖼️ Thumbnail 2:
ADVANCED SEO TIPS, TRICKS, AND...

7 SFO Predictions fer 2023 (ind
row to Preparal)

41K views * 2 weeks ago * 57 VPH

Title: 7 SEO Predictions for 2023 (and How to Prepare!)
Formula: 7 [Industry] Predictions for [Upcoming Year] (and How to Prepare!)
Hook score: +392
Why this works:
List – Lists make videos feel more tangible (you know exactly what the video will be about) and they add some curiosity.
Curiosity – Talking about the future (this was published in December 2022) builds curiosity because the future is obviously unknown, but we all want to know what will happen.
Timeliness – It’s currently the end of 2022 and everybody is thinking ahead to 2023.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about your predictions for the future.
Examples of this hook in action:

7 Real Estate Predictions for 2023 (and How to Prepare!)
7 Job Market Predictions for 2023 (and How to Prepare!)

No Excuses


🖼️ Thumbnail 3:

TIPS!)

Hook score: +655

Refute Objection – This is all about refuting the objection of, “I’m too old to build muscle.”
List – Lists make videos feel more tangible (you know exactly what the video will be about) and they add some curiosity.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience how to achieve a goal and refute their biggest excuse in the title.
Examples of this hook in action:

How to Build Wealth Even If You’re Poor (7 TIPS!)
How to Take Great Photos In Bad Lighting (7 TIPS!)

The Easiest Way To Spy On Your Competitors
Two of my favorite tools for spying on my competitors:
1) vidIQ’s Trending Tool
2) vidIQ’s Competitor Dashboard
Their trending tool lets you see what videos are getting views on a channel right now (this is an awesome alternative to just sorting a channel’s videos by most popular, which may show old videos that aren’t getting views anymore).
And their competitors feature lets you add competitors to a dashboard so you can see all of their top videos in one place, instead of taking the time to go to each channel individually.
​Download vidIQ for free here.
(Affiliate link)

## Newsletter 337: My YouTube Channel Lost Money The Truth About Being A YouTuber

**URL:** https://creatorhooks.com/my-youtube-channel-lost-money-the-truth-about-being-a-youtuber/

```
My YouTube Channel Lost Money… The Truth About Being A YouTuber 
December 24, 2022December 19, 2022 by admin 


I’ve asked some of the best creators on YouTube, “How do you write such great titles?”
And they’ve all said the same thing: “I just model what works.”
So this email will show you 5 videos worth modeling to help you write great titles and save time.
Btw, if you want to see all of the titles and thumbnails from Creator Hooks in one place, check out Creator Hooks Pro.
Planning Time


🖼️ Thumbnail 2:
OWNING a ‘gt

PLAN Q4 WITH ME | Changing my
relationship with time

58K views * 2 months ago

Title: PLAN Q4 WITH ME | Changing my relationship with time
Formula: (Timely Activity) With Me | Changing my relationship with (Entity)
Hook score: +400
Why this works:
Timeliness – This was video was published at the beginning of Q4 when everybody was thinking about planning Q4.
Desire – This creator’s audience loves planning.
How you can use this hook: Ask your audience to do something timely with you.
Examples of this hook in action:

Christmas Shop With Me | Changing my relationship with gifts
Plan 2023 With Me | Changing my business goals

Rental Reality


🖼️ Thumbnail 3:
My Airbnb Lost Money... Here's The
Truth About Being an Airbnb Host

383K views + 1 month ago + 48 VPH

Title: My Airbnb Lost Money… Here’s The Truth About Being an Airbnb Host
Formula: My (Possession) (Negative Action)… Here’s The Truth About (Activity)
Hook score: +472
Why this works:
Negativity – People love seeing negativity, plus this is a fear many people in real estate have.
Curiosity – This builds curiosity three ways: 1) It opens a loop with “The Truth About”. 2) “The Truth About” also makes you feel like you’re getting in on a secret. 3) Many people think real estate is a great business model that you can easily make money in, so this is counterintuitive to what they think.
How you can use this hook: Tell the story of how you took a loss in a great opportunity.
Examples of this hook in action:

My Marriage Lost Its Fire… Here’s The Truth About Marriage
My YouTube Channel Lost Money… Here’s The Truth About The Creator Economy

The Easiest Way To Spy On Your Competitors
Two of my favorite tools for spying on my competitors:
1) vidIQ’s Trending Tool
2) vidIQ’s Competitor Dashboard
Their trending tool lets you see what videos are getting views on a channel right now (this is an awesome alternative to just sorting a channel’s videos by most popular, which may show old videos that aren’t getting views anymore).
And their competitors feature lets you add competitors to a dashboard so you can see all of their top videos in one place, instead of taking the time to go to each channel individually.
Download vidIQ for free here.
(Affiliate link)
Quick Cars


🖼️ Thumbnail 4:
ORLD'S FASTEST
D pel

The World's Quickest Cars: Lucid Air
Sapphire v Bugatti Chiron v Tesla...

1.6M views + 3 days ago + 19.5K VPH

Title: The World’s Quickest Cars: Lucid Air Sapphire v Bugatti Chiron v Tesla Plaid – Cammisa’s Drag Race
Formula: The World’s (Extreme Objects): (Option 1) v (Option 2) v (Option 3)
Hook score: +533
Why this works:
Curiosity – Taking things to the extreme (“world’s quickest cars”) builds curiosity because it’s unique.
X vs. Y – People love competition and want to see how things compare against each other.
How you can use this hook: Do a competition to find the world’s best.
Examples of this hook in action:

The Best Careers: Doctor v Lawyer v Consultant
America’s Most Beautiful Parks: Glacier v Zion v Grand Canyon

Helpful Habits


🖼️ Thumbnail 5:
yu that h pe me

minimal %,

Japanese Minimalist'* : 6 Habits
that help me stay minimal

224K views * 1 month ago * 35 VPH

Title: Japanese Minimalist🇯🇵: 6 Habits that help me stay minimal
Formula: (Interesting Trait): 6 Habits that help me (Achieve Goal)
Hook score: +583
Why this works:
Habits – People love habits because they’re tangible and we can envision easily plugging them into our day.
List – Lists make videos feel more tangible (you know exactly what the video will be about) and they add some curiosity.
Desire – This creator’s audience wants to stay minimal.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about habits that help them achieve their goal.
Examples of this hook in action:

6% Body Fat: 6 Habits that help me stay lean
Digital Nomad: 6 Habits that help keep my life organized

No College No Problem


🖼️ Thumbnail 6:
8 Work From Home Jobs That Are
Alweys Hiring! (AM theut College)
546K views * 1 month ago

Title: 8 Work From Home Jobs That Are Always Hiring! (Without College)
Formula: 8 [Objects] That Are Always [Activity]! (Without [Objection])
Hook score: +893
Why this works:
List – Lists make videos feel more tangible (you know exactly what the video will be about) and they add some curiosity.
Desire – This creator’s audience is looking for work from home jobs.
Refute Objection – Someone might see this and think, “I can’t get a job without a college degree.” But “(Without College)” at the end gets rid of that excuse.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about a list of things that can help them achieve their goal, then get rid of their most common excuse at the end.
Examples of this hook in action:

8 Amazon Products That Always Make Sales! (No Experience Needed)
7 Home Decor Trends That Always Look Pretty! (Cheap Decorations That Look Great)

Flop of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 7:
How This Junkyard Chevy Del Ray Is
Now Worth $75k | The Appraiser

48K views * 8 days ago «

Title: How This Junkyard Chevy Del Ray Is Now Worth $75k | The Appraiser
Hook score: -90
Why this flopped: It seems like they tried to use contrast to build curiosity: “Junkyard Chevy Del Ray” vs. “Worth $75K”.
But I think that failed because it’s not abundantly clear. The thumbnail doesn’t look like a junkyard car and many people don’t know what a Chevy Del Ray is.
If I were to rewrite it, I would say, “How This Junkyard Car Is Now Worth $75,000”.
Contrast this with the title mentioned earlier about the world’s quickest car.
In just four words the idea is conveyed. You know exactly what it’s about and (if you’re a car fan) you’re excited about it.
So make your titles and thumbnails crystal clear and use simple words.
Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
If you know a fellow creator who needs help with writing better titles, please send them to creatorhooks.com.
Here’s to the war on boring content.
– Jake
(@jthomas__ on Twitter)
P.S. Read previous editions here.
P.P.S. If you want to see all of the videos from Creator Hooks in one place, check out Creator Hooks Pro.
Read last week’s edition »

2) vidIQ’s Competitor Dashboard
Their trending tool lets you see what videos are getting views on a channel right now (this is an awesome alternative to just sorting a channel’s videos by most popular, which may show old videos that aren’t getting views anymore).
And their competitors feature lets you add competitors to a dashboard so you can see all of their top videos in one place, instead of taking the time to go to each channel individually.
Download vidIQ for free here.
(Affiliate link)
Quick Cars


🖼️ Thumbnail 4:
ORLD'S FASTEST
D pel

The World's Quickest Cars: Lucid Air
Sapphire v Bugatti Chiron v Tesla...

1.6M views + 3 days ago + 19.5K VPH

Title: The World’s Quickest Cars: Lucid Air Sapphire v Bugatti Chiron v Tesla Plaid – Cammisa’s Drag Race
Formula: The World’s (Extreme Objects): (Option 1) v (Option 2) v (Option 3)
Hook score: +533
Why this works:
Curiosity – Taking things to the extreme (“world’s quickest cars”) builds curiosity because it’s unique.
X vs. Y – People love competition and want to see how things compare against each other.
How you can use this hook: Do a competition to find the world’s best.
Examples of this hook in action:

The Best Careers: Doctor v Lawyer v Consultant
America’s Most Beautiful Parks: Glacier v Zion v Grand Canyon

Helpful Habits


🖼️ Thumbnail 5:
yu that h pe me

minimal %,

Japanese Minimalist'* : 6 Habits
that help me stay minimal

224K views * 1 month ago * 35 VPH

Title: Japanese Minimalist🇯🇵: 6 Habits that help me stay minimal
Formula: (Interesting Trait): 6 Habits that help me (Achieve Goal)
Hook score: +583
Why this works:
Habits – People love habits because they’re tangible and we can envision easily plugging them into our day.
List – Lists make videos feel more tangible (you know exactly what the video will be about) and they add some curiosity.
Desire – This creator’s audience wants to stay minimal.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about habits that help them achieve their goal.
Examples of this hook in action:

6% Body Fat: 6 Habits that help me stay lean
Digital Nomad: 6 Habits that help keep my life organized

No College No Problem


🖼️ Thumbnail 6:
8 Work From Home Jobs That Are
Alweys Hiring! (AM theut College)
546K views * 1 month ago

Title: 8 Work From Home Jobs That Are Always Hiring! (Without College)
Formula: 8 [Objects] That Are Always [Activity]! (Without [Objection])
Hook score: +893
Why this works:
List – Lists make videos feel more tangible (you know exactly what the video will be about) and they add some curiosity.
Desire – This creator’s audience is looking for work from home jobs.
Refute Objection – Someone might see this and think, “I can’t get a job without a college degree.” But “(Without College)” at the end gets rid of that excuse.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about a list of things that can help them achieve their goal, then get rid of their most common excuse at the end.
Examples of this hook in action:

8 Amazon Products That Always Make Sales! (No Experience Needed)
7 Home Decor Trends That Always Look Pretty! (Cheap Decorations That Look Great)

Flop of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 7:
How This Junkyard Chevy Del Ray Is
Now Worth $75k | The Appraiser

48K views * 8 days ago «

Title: How This Junkyard Chevy Del Ray Is Now Worth $75k | The Appraiser
Hook score: -90
Why this flopped: It seems like they tried to use contrast to build curiosity: “Junkyard Chevy Del Ray” vs. “Worth $75K”.
But I think that failed because it’s not abundantly clear. The thumbnail doesn’t look like a junkyard car and many people don’t know what a Chevy Del Ray is.
If I were to rewrite it, I would say, “How This Junkyard Car Is Now Worth $75,000”.
Contrast this with the title mentioned earlier about the world’s quickest car.
In just four words the idea is conveyed. You know exactly what it’s about and (if you’re a car fan) you’re excited about it.
So make your titles and thumbnails crystal clear and use simple words.
Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
If you know a fellow creator who needs help with writing better titles, please send them to creatorhooks.com.
Here’s to the war on boring content.
– Jake
(@jthomas__ on Twitter)
P.S. Read previous editions here.
P.P.S. If you want to see all of the videos from Creator Hooks in one place, check out Creator Hooks Pro.

## Newsletter 339: 5 Things You Should NEVER Do On YouTube

**URL:** https://creatorhooks.com/5-things-you-should-never-do-on-youtube/

```
5 Things You Should NEVER Do On YouTube 
December 12, 2022 by admin 


I’ve asked some of the best creators on YouTube, “How do you write such great titles?”
And they’ve all said the same thing: “I just model what works.”
So this email will show you 5 videos worth modeling to help you write great titles and save time.
Btw, if you want to see all of the titles and thumbnails from Creator Hooks in one place, check out Creator Hooks Pro.
The Old New


🖼️ Thumbnail 2:
| Bought a Brand New 40 year
Yamaha Motorcycle

2.5M views + 1 month ago + 97 VPH

Title: I Bought a Brand New 40 year Yamaha Motorcycle
Formula: I Bought a Brand New (Old) (Object)
Hook score: +438
Why this works:
Curiosity – There’s contrast between “brand new” and “40 year”, which builds curiosity because it doesn’t make sense.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about a counterintuitive object you got.
Examples of this hook in action:

I Bought a Brand New 30-Year-Old Camera
I Bought a Brand New Vintage Conventional Fishing Reel

“Let’s Circle Back On That”


🖼️ Thumbnail 3:
5 Things You Should NEVER Tell
Coworkers

82K views * 2 weeks ago + 48 VPH

Title: 5 Things You Should NEVER Tell Coworkers
Formula: 5 Things You Should NEVER Tell (Group of People)
Hook score: +820
Why this works:
List – Lists make videos feel more tangible (you know exactly what the video will be about) and they add some curiosity.
Negativity – What you should never tell your coworkers makes for a more spicy and interesting video than one about what’s ok to tell your coworkers.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about things they should never say to a group of people important to them.
Examples of this hook in action:

5 Things You Should NEVER Tell a Job Recruiter
5 Things You Should NEVER Tell Your Husband

Every Serious YouTuber Needs YouTube Insurance


🖼️ Thumbnail 4:
YOUR ACCOUNT HAS
BEEN COMPROMISED

> Lady Hear Me Out

YouTubers are getting hacked more than you realize. Just ask Gizmochina, the British military, or the numerous YouTubers who got scammed by this shady malware that even two-step verification can’t protect you from.
So check out Notch: the first insurance for social media accounts. If your YouTube channel is hacked, you won’t be on your own, stressing about lost income and frantically trying to get help from non-existent “customer support”.
Instead, with Notch you’ll have a team in your corner helping you get your account back — and a financial safety net.
Here’s what’ll happen:

You’ll be instantly notified if your account is hacked and be connected with a Notch team member right away.
For each day your account is hacked you’ll get a daily payment based on your coverage.
A dedicated team of experts works around the clock to retrieve your account.
You’re back in business.

Pricing starts as low as $8/mo— go here to get your free quote.
(Sponsored)
Mystery Meat


🖼️ Thumbnail 5:
This Food is WORSE than Sugar on
alketo (Low Carls) Diet

617K views * 1 month ago +

Title: This Food is WORSE than Sugar on a Keto (Low Carb) Diet
Formula: This [Object] is WORSE than [Other, Seemingly Worst Object]
Hook score: +881
Why this works:
Curiosity – This title builds curiosity three ways: 1) It opens a loop by telling you this food is worse than sugar, but not telling you what it is. 2) It makes you feel like you’re getting in on a secret because you assume sugar is the worst thing for you on a keto diet. 3) It’s also counterintuitive because of this.
Negativity – Talking about foods that are bad for you gets your audience’s attention more than talking about foods that are good for you.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about something that is worse for them than what they currently think is the worst thing.
Examples of this hook in action:

This Device is WORSE than iPads for Kids
This New Workout Trend is WORSE than CrossFit For Your Joints

Hacker’s Haven


🖼️ Thumbnail 6:
DIRTY SECRETS

OF HOME CAMERAS! i r

These Home Cameras are NOT
Private!

464K views * 1 month ago

Title: These Home Cameras are NOT Private!
Formula: These (Objects) are NOT (Important Quality)!
Hook score: +1004
Why this works:
Negativity – This title picks at a very strong fear in our culture right now.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about objects that are not as safe or effective as they think they are.
Examples of this hook in action:

These Austin Neighborhoods are NOT Safe!
These “Weight Loss” Foods are NOT Healthy!

Tis’ The Season


🖼️ Thumbnail 7:
Asking Strangers Their Thoughts On
Christianity

136K views * 10 days ago + 599 VPH

Title: Asking Strangers Their Thoughts On Christianity
Formula: Asking Strangers Their Thoughts On (Subject)
Hook score: +1012
Why this works:
Curiosity – This style of video is going crazy on YouTube right now because it makes you want to know what these strangers said.
Credibility – There’s an element of credibility to this style of video because it’s not the creator’s opinion — it’s what real people think or have experienced.
How you can use this hook: Ask strangers for their thoughts on your industry or a highly-debated topic.
Examples of this hook in action:

Asking Strangers Their Favorite Travel Destination
Asking Strangers How Much Money They Have In Their Bank Accounts

Flop of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 8:
hext move —

what's up

with the hat?

4 |
managing

workload |

Answering Your Career Questions

1.8K views * 1 month ago + 7 VPH

Title: Answering Your Career Questions
Hook score: -90
Why this flopped: This title is too boring. I think it could’ve done better if the creator made it a little spicier.
Something like:

“Answering Your Embarrassing Career Questions”
“Answering Your Toughest Career Questions”

On top of the title being boring, the quotes in the thumbnail are boring, too.
“Managing workload” is pretty boring compared to something like, “Horrible Boss”.
And “what’s up with the hat?” is kinda funny, but doesn’t make me want to click as much as, say, “2023 interview tips”.
Even if she didn’t get interesting questions like this, I’d probably throw them in there because it’ll make for a better video and more clickable thumbnail and title.
Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
If you know a fellow creator who needs help with writing better titles, please send them to creatorhooks.com.
Here’s to the war on boring content.
– Jake
(@jthomas__ on Twitter)
P.S. Read previous editions here.
P.P.S. If you want to see all of the videos from Creator Hooks in one place, check out Creator Hooks Pro.

## Newsletter 341: 4 YouTube Changes That Will Have a BIG IMPACT in 2023

**URL:** https://creatorhooks.com/4-youtube-changes-that-will-have-a-big-impact-in-2023/

```
4 YouTube Changes That Will Have a BIG IMPACT in 2023 
December 7, 2022December 5, 2022 by admin 


I’ve asked some of the best creators on YouTube, “How do you write such great titles?”
And they’ve all said the same thing: “I just model what works.”
So this email will show you 5 videos worth modeling to help you write great titles and save time.
Btw, if you want to see all of the titles and thumbnails from Creator Hooks in one place, check out Creator Hooks Pro.
Recession Remedy


🖼️ Thumbnail 2:
Alex & Leila Hormozi's Billionaire
Blueprint (Recession Proof)

44K views * 1 month ago +

Title: Alex & Leila Hormozi’s Billionaire Blueprint (Recession Proof)
Formula: [Authority Figure]’s [Desirable] Blueprint ([Alleviate Paint Point])
 Hook score: +400
Why this works:
Authority – Alex & Leila Hormozi are two of the most influential people in business right now, so dropping their names gives this title credibility and name recognition.
Desire – This creator’s audience would love a billionaire blueprint.
Timeliness – Since we’re in a recession right now, including “(Recession Proof)” at the end of the title does a great job at grabbing the audience’s attention.
Refute Objection – Continuing on the point above, the audience might thinking, “This billionaire blueprint is cool and all, but we’re in a recession now, so it probably won’t work.” But including “(Recession Proof)” gets rid of that excuse.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about an authority figure’s blueprint to help them achieve their goals, and then refute an objection at the end.
Examples of this hook in action:

MrBeast’s Million-Subscriber Blueprint (Starting From Scratch)
Olympic Runner’s Sub 20 Minute 5K Blueprint (Step By Step Plan)

One-Person Wonder


🖼️ Thumbnail 3:
UNLIFTABLE
DUMBBELL

The Dumbbell Only 1 Person Could
Lift
7.1M views * 2 weeks ago * 432 VPH

Title: The Dumbbell Only 1 Person Could Lift
Formula: The (Object) Only 1 Person Could (Action)
Hook score: +435
Why this works:
Curiosity – This title sounds a little too crazy… a dumbbell only 1 person could lift? But it’s also just believable enough that you have to click to see what’s going on. Plus, it’s open ended. Who’s the 1 person? How much does the dumbbell weigh? These questions make you want to click even more.
Constraint – “Only 1 Person Could Lift” adds a constraint, which makes for a much more interesting story.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about something that only one person could do.
Examples of this hook in action:

The Puzzle Only 1 Person Could Solve
The Monument Only 1 Person Can Visit Per Year

Timeliness – Lots of people are thinking about Christmas right now and this title rides that wave.

Negativity – This title and thumbnail are all about addressing a big pain point the audience has.

Why Your Ecommerce Store Isn’t Getting Sales

4 Social Security Changes That Will

716K views * 2 weeks ago * 339 VPH

Title: 4 Social Security Changes That Will Have a BIG IMPACT in 2023
Formula: 4 (Entity) Changes That Will Have a BIG IMPACT in (Next Year)
Hook score: +1665
Why this works:
List – Lists make videos feel more tangible (you know exactly what the video will be about) and they add some curiosity.
Curiosity – This title builds curiosity two different ways: it talks about change and the future. Both are unknown and open-ended, so you have to click to learn more.
Timeliness – Since it’s near the end of the year, people are looking forward to 2023.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about changes that will have a big impact next year.

Flop of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 8:
CAN TWO
NARCISSISTS
[ BE TRULY
IN LOWE
AND HAPPY?
Are Two Narcissists actually "in
lever"

10K views + 1 month ago

Title: Are Two Narcissists actually “in love?”
Hook score: -77
Why this flopped: Who’s this video about? Narcissists. Who’s probably not watching videos about love because they think they’re the greatest? Narcissists.
This is different from the video mentioned earlier titled, “WHY THEY ARE PICKING FIGHTS WITH YOU!”
That video is about narcissists’ victims, who are hurting and probably watching more YouTube than the narcissists.
So before you make your next video, think about who’s actually watching your videos.
Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!

## Newsletter 342: 4 YouTube Changes That Will Have a BIG IMPACT in 2023

**URL:** https://creatorhooks.com/4-youtube-changes-that-will-have-a-big-impact-in-2023/

```
4 YouTube Changes That Will Have a BIG IMPACT in 2023 
December 7, 2022December 5, 2022 by admin 


I’ve asked some of the best creators on YouTube, “How do you write such great titles?”
And they’ve all said the same thing: “I just model what works.”
So this email will show you 5 videos worth modeling to help you write great titles and save time.
Btw, if you want to see all of the titles and thumbnails from Creator Hooks in one place, check out Creator Hooks Pro.
Recession Remedy


🖼️ Thumbnail 2:
Alex & Leila Hormozi's Billionaire
Blueprint (Recession Proof)

44K views * 1 month ago +

Title: Alex & Leila Hormozi’s Billionaire Blueprint (Recession Proof)
Formula: [Authority Figure]’s [Desirable] Blueprint ([Alleviate Paint Point])
 Hook score: +400
Why this works:
Authority – Alex & Leila Hormozi are two of the most influential people in business right now, so dropping their names gives this title credibility and name recognition.
Desire – This creator’s audience would love a billionaire blueprint.
Timeliness – Since we’re in a recession right now, including “(Recession Proof)” at the end of the title does a great job at grabbing the audience’s attention.
Refute Objection – Continuing on the point above, the audience might thinking, “This billionaire blueprint is cool and all, but we’re in a recession now, so it probably won’t work.” But including “(Recession Proof)” gets rid of that excuse.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about an authority figure’s blueprint to help them achieve their goals, and then refute an objection at the end.
Examples of this hook in action:

MrBeast’s Million-Subscriber Blueprint (Starting From Scratch)
Olympic Runner’s Sub 20 Minute 5K Blueprint (Step By Step Plan)

One-Person Wonder


🖼️ Thumbnail 3:
UNLIFTABLE
DUMBBELL

The Dumbbell Only 1 Person Could
Lift
7.1M views * 2 weeks ago * 432 VPH

Title: The Dumbbell Only 1 Person Could Lift
Formula: The (Object) Only 1 Person Could (Action)
Hook score: +435
Why this works:
Curiosity – This title sounds a little too crazy… a dumbbell only 1 person could lift? But it’s also just believable enough that you have to click to see what’s going on. Plus, it’s open ended. Who’s the 1 person? How much does the dumbbell weigh? These questions make you want to click even more.
Constraint – “Only 1 Person Could Lift” adds a constraint, which makes for a much more interesting story.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about something that only one person could do.
Examples of this hook in action:

The Puzzle Only 1 Person Could Solve
The Monument Only 1 Person Can Visit Per Year

So check out Notch: the first insurance for social media accounts. If your YouTube channel is hacked, you won’t be on your own, stressing about lost income and frantically trying to get help from non-existent “customer support”.
Instead, with Notch you’ll have a team in your corner helping you get your account back — and a financial safety net.
Here’s what’ll happen:

## Newsletter 343: Psychologist Explains How To Write Great YouTube Titles

**URL:** https://creatorhooks.com/psychologist-explains-how-to-write-great-youtube-titles/

```
Psychologist Explains How To Write Great YouTube Titles 
December 9, 2022November 28, 2022 by admin 


I’ve asked some of the best creators on YouTube, “How do you write such great titles?”
And they’ve all said the same thing: “I just model what works.”
So this email will show you 5 videos worth modeling to help you write great titles and save time.
Btw, if you want to see all of the titles and thumbnails from Creator Hooks in one place, check out Creator Hooks Pro.
Keepin It Cool


🖼️ Thumbnail 2:
Trein ng for Winter Bas geac zg! |
How | Sleep WARM in Freezing...

45K views * 2 weeks ago » 70 VPH

Title: Training for Winter Backpacking! | How I Sleep WARM in Freezing Temperatures with Lightweight Gear
Formula: (Activity) for (Future Event)! | How I (Achieve Goal) with (Contrasting Object)
Hook score : +338
Why this works:
Timeliness – It’s about to be winter, so this channel’s audience is thinking about winter backpacking.
Desire – This channel’s audience (and pretty much everybody) wants to sleep warm, especially when it’s cold out.
Curiosity – There’s contrast between “sleeping warm”, “freezing temperatures” and “lightweight gear”, which builds curiosity.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about how you’re preparing for the upcoming season.
Examples of this hook in action:

Preparing For 2023 Tax Season! | How I Pay $0 In Taxes On My $500,000 Income
Prepping My Ecom Store For Christmas! | How I Get A 10X ROAS On Ad Spend

Towing Tragedy


🖼️ Thumbnail 3:
UELECTRIC, TOWING DISASTER!

Towing with my Ford Lightning EV
Pickup was a TOTAL DISASTER!

2.9M views * 2 months ago

Title: Towing with my Ford Lightning EV Pickup was a TOTAL DISASTER!
Formula: (Activity) with my (New Product) was a TOTAL DISASTER!
Hook score : +367
Why this works:
Negativity – People love drama and this title about a total disaster promises the video will have lots of drama.
Timeliness – Ford Lightning EV Pickups are new and everybody is interested in what they can do.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about how it was a disaster when you used a new product.
Examples of this hook in action:

Shooting A Wedding with my Sony Alpha a7 II was a TOTAL DISASTER!
Planting Vegetables in my Vertical Garden was a TOTAL DISASTER!

😲 CREATORS: WANT MORE BRAND PARTNERSHIPS? 💸
Justin Moore, founder of Creator Wizard, has a free weekly report that’s gonna get you PAID!


🖼️ Thumbnail 4:
‘Sarah Renae Clark

is one of the newsletters I’m excited
to open. That’s a weird emotion to have in your inbox,
right?

He teaches creators how to negotiate good brand
deals, and he’s done a lot of research for you! So much

value in every email.
Kristen Bousquet
vous

Replying to

But also is the king of creator
newsletters, in my opinion.

The Creator Wizard newsletter will help you:

Find which brands & companies are sponsoring creators
Pitch more effectively
Get paid more $$$ on each partnership
Immediately get better looking and tell funnier jokes (probably)​

​​Join 8K+ creators and sign up in one click here.
(Sponsored)
Harvard Haggling


🖼️ Thumbnail 5:
Harvard negotiator explains how to
argue | Dan Shapiro

1.5M views + 1 month ago

Title: Harvard negotiator explains how to argue | Dan Shapiro
Formula: (Authority Figure) explains how to (Activity) | (Authority Figure’s Name)
Hook score : +553
Why this works:
Authority – This channel borrows the credibility from this Harvard negotiator to get people to click.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about how an expert in your field does something.
Examples of this hook in action:

Michelin Star Chef explains how to cook | Alain Ducasse
Olympic Marathon Runner explains how to run without getting tired

Controversial Contraptions


🖼️ Thumbnail 6:
4 BANNED GADGETS YOU SiMLL
CAN BUY ON AMAZCNI

4.6M views « 7 days ago * 61.4K VPH

Title: 14 BANNED GADGETS YOU STILL CAN BUY ON AMAZON
Formula: 14 Banned (Objects) You Still Can (Action)
Hook score : +592
Why this works:
List – Lists make videos feel more tangible (you know exactly what the video will be about) and they add some curiosity.
Negativity – “Banned” gadgets make this video so much cooler than just regular gadgets.
Curiosity – It’s counterintuitive that can still buy these “banned” gadgets, which builds curiosity and makes you want to click.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about banned or controversial objects they can still buy or use.
Examples of this hook in action:

14 Forbidden Museums You Still Can Visit
10 Banned iPhone Apps You Still Can Download

Boss Moves


🖼️ Thumbnail 7:
| saw my boss do these 10 things in
Exeall

1.1M views + 1 month ago

Title: I saw my boss do these 10 things in Excel!
Formula: I saw my boss do these 10 things in (Platform)!
Hook score : +2682
Why this works:
Authority – “My Boss” adds a little extra credibility here.
Curiosity – This title opens a loop (and builds curiosity) by telling you about “these 10 things in Excel” but not telling you what they are.
List – Lists make videos feel more tangible (you know exactly what the video will be about) and they add some curiosity.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about 10 things you saw an authority figure in your niche do.
Examples of this hook in action:

I saw my boss look for these 3 things on resumes
I saw MrBeast look at these 5 things in YouTube Studio!

Flop of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 8:
HERES TG @SQIrFESSIONALS
SEP CLEAN THINGS

68K views * 1 month ago +

Title: HERE’S HOW PROFESSIONALS DEEP CLEAN THINGS
Hook score: -91
Why this flopped: This channel is all about crazy gadgets and inventions (like the banned gadgets video above).
You know what it’s not about? How professionals deep clean things.
Not that that’s a bad topic, it’s just a bad topic for this channel.
So if you don’t want your video to flop, make sure that you’re choosing a topic your audience wants to watch on your channel.
Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
If you know a fellow creator who needs help with writing better titles, please send them to creatorhooks.com.
Here’s to the war on boring content.
– Jake
( @jthomas__  on Twitter)

## Newsletter 344: Psychologist Explains How To Write Great YouTube Titles

**URL:** https://creatorhooks.com/psychologist-explains-how-to-write-great-youtube-titles/

```
Psychologist Explains How To Write Great YouTube Titles 
December 9, 2022November 28, 2022 by admin 


I’ve asked some of the best creators on YouTube, “How do you write such great titles?”
And they’ve all said the same thing: “I just model what works.”
So this email will show you 5 videos worth modeling to help you write great titles and save time.
Btw, if you want to see all of the titles and thumbnails from Creator Hooks in one place, check out Creator Hooks Pro.
Keepin It Cool


🖼️ Thumbnail 2:
Trein ng for Winter Bas geac zg! |
How | Sleep WARM in Freezing...

45K views * 2 weeks ago » 70 VPH

Title: Training for Winter Backpacking! | How I Sleep WARM in Freezing Temperatures with Lightweight Gear
Formula: (Activity) for (Future Event)! | How I (Achieve Goal) with (Contrasting Object)
Hook score : +338
Why this works:
Timeliness – It’s about to be winter, so this channel’s audience is thinking about winter backpacking.
Desire – This channel’s audience (and pretty much everybody) wants to sleep warm, especially when it’s cold out.
Curiosity – There’s contrast between “sleeping warm”, “freezing temperatures” and “lightweight gear”, which builds curiosity.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about how you’re preparing for the upcoming season.
Examples of this hook in action:

Preparing For 2023 Tax Season! | How I Pay $0 In Taxes On My $500,000 Income
Prepping My Ecom Store For Christmas! | How I Get A 10X ROAS On Ad Spend

Towing Tragedy


🖼️ Thumbnail 3:
UELECTRIC, TOWING DISASTER!

Towing with my Ford Lightning EV
Pickup was a TOTAL DISASTER!

2.9M views * 2 months ago

Title: Towing with my Ford Lightning EV Pickup was a TOTAL DISASTER!
Formula: (Activity) with my (New Product) was a TOTAL DISASTER!
Hook score : +367
Why this works:
Negativity – People love drama and this title about a total disaster promises the video will have lots of drama.
Timeliness – Ford Lightning EV Pickups are new and everybody is interested in what they can do.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about how it was a disaster when you used a new product.
Examples of this hook in action:

Shooting A Wedding with my Sony Alpha a7 II was a TOTAL DISASTER!
Planting Vegetables in my Vertical Garden was a TOTAL DISASTER!

🖼️ Thumbnail 4:
‘Sarah Renae Clark

is one of the newsletters I’m excited
to open. That’s a weird emotion to have in your inbox,
right?

deals, and he’s done a lot of research for you! So much

value in every email.
Kristen Bousquet

(Sponsored)
Harvard Haggling


🖼️ Thumbnail 5:
Harvard negotiator explains how to
argue | Dan Shapiro

1.5M views + 1 month ago

Title: Harvard negotiator explains how to argue | Dan Shapiro
Formula: (Authority Figure) explains how to (Activity) | (Authority Figure’s Name)
Hook score : +553
Why this works:
Authority – This channel borrows the credibility from this Harvard negotiator to get people to click.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about how an expert in your field does something.
Examples of this hook in action:

Michelin Star Chef explains how to cook | Alain Ducasse
Olympic Marathon Runner explains how to run without getting tired

Controversial Contraptions


🖼️ Thumbnail 6:
4 BANNED GADGETS YOU SiMLL
CAN BUY ON AMAZCNI

4.6M views « 7 days ago * 61.4K VPH

Title: 14 BANNED GADGETS YOU STILL CAN BUY ON AMAZON
Formula: 14 Banned (Objects) You Still Can (Action)
Hook score : +592
Why this works:
List – Lists make videos feel more tangible (you know exactly what the video will be about) and they add some curiosity.
Negativity – “Banned” gadgets make this video so much cooler than just regular gadgets.
Curiosity – It’s counterintuitive that can still buy these “banned” gadgets, which builds curiosity and makes you want to click.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about banned or controversial objects they can still buy or use.
Examples of this hook in action:

14 Forbidden Museums You Still Can Visit
10 Banned iPhone Apps You Still Can Download

Boss Moves


🖼️ Thumbnail 7:
| saw my boss do these 10 things in
Exeall

1.1M views + 1 month ago

Title: I saw my boss do these 10 things in Excel!
Formula: I saw my boss do these 10 things in (Platform)!
Hook score : +2682
Why this works:
Authority – “My Boss” adds a little extra credibility here.
Curiosity – This title opens a loop (and builds curiosity) by telling you about “these 10 things in Excel” but not telling you what they are.
List – Lists make videos feel more tangible (you know exactly what the video will be about) and they add some curiosity.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about 10 things you saw an authority figure in your niche do.
Examples of this hook in action:

I saw my boss look for these 3 things on resumes
I saw MrBeast look at these 5 things in YouTube Studio!

Flop of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 8:
HERES TG @SQIrFESSIONALS
SEP CLEAN THINGS

68K views * 1 month ago +

Title: HERE’S HOW PROFESSIONALS DEEP CLEAN THINGS
Hook score: -91
Why this flopped: This channel is all about crazy gadgets and inventions (like the banned gadgets video above).
You know what it’s not about? How professionals deep clean things.
Not that that’s a bad topic, it’s just a bad topic for this channel.
So if you don’t want your video to flop, make sure that you’re choosing a topic your audience wants to watch on your channel.
Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
If you know a fellow creator who needs help with writing better titles, please send them to creatorhooks.com.
Here’s to the war on boring content.
– Jake
( @jthomas__  on Twitter)
P.S. Read previous editions here .
P.P.S. If you want to see all of the videos from Creator Hooks in one place, check out Creator Hooks Pro.

## Newsletter 345: 4 One-Minute Habits To Grow On YouTube

**URL:** https://creatorhooks.com/4-one-minute-habits-to-grow-on-youtube/

```
4 One-Minute Habits To Grow On YouTube 
December 9, 2022November 21, 2022 by admin 


I’ve asked some of the best creators on YouTube, “How do you write such great titles?”
And they’ve all said the same thing: “I just model what works.”
So this email will show you 5 videos worth modeling to help you write great titles and save time.
Btw, if you want to see all of the titles and thumbnails from Creator Hooks in one place, check out Creator Hooks Pro.
Fishing Frenzy


🖼️ Thumbnail 2:
Fishing the Gulf Pier When
Something Wild Happened!

674K views * 1 month ago

Title: Fishing the Gulf Pier When Something Wild Happened!
Formula: (Activity) When Something Wild Happened!
Hook score: +1036
Why this works:
Curiosity – “When Something Wild Happened” starts a story, but doesn’t finish it, so it builds a ton of curiosity.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about how you were doing something, but then something wild happened.
Examples of this hook in action:

Cooking Thanksgiving Dinner When Something Wild Happened!
Running The Thanksgiving Turkey Trot When Something Wild Happened!

Secret Stash


🖼️ Thumbnail 3:
SPECIAL™
MONTHLY

WE ag > hy

The Secret Weapon That Got Me
$17,000 in VA Disability Benefits

168K views * 3 weeks ago

Title: The Secret Weapon That Got Me $17,000 in VA Disability Benefits
Formula: The Secret Weapon That Got Me (Big Achievement)
Hook score: +1200
Why this works:
Curiosity – “The Secret Weapon” builds curiosity by making you wonder what it is.
Desire – This creator’s audience would love to get $17,000 in VA disability benefits.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about the secret weapon that got you something awesome.
Examples of this hook in action:

The Secret Weapon That Got Me $100,000 In Sales On Black Friday
The Negotiation Hack That Got Me $10,000 Off My New Home

😲 CREATORS: WANT MORE BRAND PARTNERSHIPS? 💸
Justin Moore, founder of Creator Wizard, has a free weekly report that’s gonna get you PAID!


🖼️ Thumbnail 4:
Sarah Renae Clark
@SarahRenaeClark

Replying to

is one of the newsletters I’m excited
to open. That’s a weird emotion to have in your inbox,
right?

He teaches creators how to negotiate good brand

deals, and he’s done a lot of research for you! So much
value in every email.

Kristen Bousquet
@kbousq
Replying to

But also is the king of creator
newsletters, in my opinion.

The Creator Wizard newsletter will help you:

Find which brands & companies are sponsoring creators
Pitch more effectively
Get paid more $$$ on each partnership
Immediately get better looking and tell funnier jokes (probably)​

Join 8K+ creators and sign up here.​
(Sponsored)
Vitamin Warnings


🖼️ Thumbnail 5:
SCIENTIFIC STUDIES: | ;
fae Vitamin

You're Probably Taking your Vitamin
D Wrong, and it Could be Dangerou...

358K views * 4 weeks ago

Title: You’re Probably Taking your Vitamin D Wrong, and it Could be Dangerous (& Ineffective)
Formula: You’re Probably (Activity) Wrong, and it Could be Dangerous (& [Other Unwanted Effect])
Hook score: +1352
Why this works:
Negativity – This title gets the audience’s attention by giving them a warning.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about something they’re doing wrong.
Examples of this hook in action:

You’re Probably Using Your iPhone Wrong, and it Could be Dangerous
You’re Probably Booking Flights Wrong, and it Could be Costing You Money

Bikepacking Beliefs


🖼️ Thumbnail 6:
5 MYTHS about Bicycle Traveling /
Bikepacking vs. Reality // Tips &...

86K views * 1 month ago *

Title: 5 MYTHS about Bicycle Traveling / Bikepacking vs. Reality // Tips & Tricks // Cycling the World
Formula: 5 MYTHS about (Activity) / (Activity) vs. Reality // Tips & Tricks // (Activity)
Hook score: +1555
Why this works:
List – Lists make videos feel more tangible (you know exactly what the video will be about) and they add some curiosity.
Curiosity – “Myths” add a lot of curiosity because they’re things you think are true but aren’t, so you want to know what the truth is.
*Note: I think this title is too long and if I was using this framework, I’d only model the first part.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about the myths about something in your industry.
Examples of this hook in action:

5 MYTHS about Freshwater Aquariums / Instagram Aquariums vs. Reality
5 MYTHS about Painting Your Own House

Time Tricks


🖼️ Thumbnail 7:
4 ONE-MINUTE Habits That Save
Me 20+ Hours a Week | Time...

364K views * 2 weeks ago

Title: 4 ONE-MINUTE Habits That Save Me 20+ Hours a Week | Time Management For Busy People
Formula: 4 ONE-MINUTE Habits That (Achieve Goal) | (Activity) For (Specific Audience)
​Hook score: +14160
Why this works:
List – Lists make videos feel more tangible and they add some curiosity.
Time Frame – Time frames also make videos feel more tangible and “one-minute habits” is a great example of this.
Desire – This creator’s audience loves to save time.
Specific Audience – This title calls out a specific audience with “Time Management For Busy People”.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about the one-minute habits that help them achieve their goals, and finish the title by calling out a specific group of people.
Examples of this hook in action:

4 ONE-MINUTE Eating Habits That Got Me A 6 Pack | Healthy Eating For Busy People
5 ONE-MINUTE Habits That Saved My Marriage

Flop of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 8:
How do you prove a service
connection for sinusitis?

7.6K views * 4 weeks ago

Title: How do you prove a service connection for sinusitis?
Hook score: -85
Why this flopped: A lot of people say that you should make videos about the questions you get from your audience.
This feels like that’s what happened here, and this is why I don’t love this advice.
In this case, it seems like it’s way too specific of a question to do well on YouTube because it has a small potential audience.
So here’s a question I always like to ask myself before coming up with a video idea: is there a big enough potential audience for me to meet my view goals for this video?
Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
If you know a fellow creator who needs help with writing better titles, please send them to creatorhooks.com.
Here’s to the war on boring content.
– Jake
(@jthomas__ on Twitter)
P.S. Read previous editions here.
P.P.S. If you want to see all of the videos from Creator Hooks in one place, check out  Creator Hooks Pro.

## Newsletter 347: 4 Things NOT to do on YouTube

**URL:** https://creatorhooks.com/4-things-not-to-do-on-youtube/

```
4 Things NOT to do on YouTube 
December 7, 2022November 14, 2022 by admin 


I’ve asked some of the best creators on YouTube, “How do you write such great titles?”
And they’ve all said the same thing: “I just model what works.”
So this email will show you 5 videos worth modeling to help you write great titles and save time.
Btw, if you want to see all of the titles and thumbnails from Creator Hooks in one place, check out Creator Hooks Pro.
Forever Free


🖼️ Thumbnail 2:
| Can't Believe this Waves PLUGIN is
still FREE

24K views * 2 months ago «

Title: I Can’t Believe this Waves PLUGIN is still FREE
Formula: I Can’t Believe this (Tool) is still FREE
Hook score: +300
Why this works:
Curiosity – Saying you can’t believe something is still free makes the audience wonder what it is and why it’s so good.
Desire – Everybody likes free stuff, and this creator’s audience loves great waves plugins.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about the tool that’s so good you can’t believe it’s free.
Examples of this hook in action:

I Can’t Believe this Photo Editing App is still FREE
I Can’t Believe these Quilting Templates are still FREE

Red Flag Alert


🖼️ Thumbnail 3:
7 RED FLAGS You're Dealing With
An Emotionally BROKEN MAN...

395K views * 2 months ago + 35 VPH

Title: 7 RED FLAGS You’re Dealing With An Emotionally BROKEN MAN…
Formula: 7 RED FLAGS You’re Dealing With An (Negative Entity)…
Hook score: +329
Why this works:
List – Lists make videos feel more tangible and add a little bit of curiosity.
Negativity – Red flags and the fear of dealing with “an emotionally broken man” grabs the audience’s attention.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about the red flags that reveal they’re dealing with a tough situation.
Examples of this hook in action:

7 RED FLAGS A Job Listing Isn’t What You Think…
5 RED FLAGS We’re Already In A Recession

“I Love You”


🖼️ Thumbnail 5:
4 Things NOT to Say to Your
Therapist

210K views + 11 days ago * 133 VPH

Title: 4 Things NOT to Say to Your Therapist
Formula: 4 Things NOT to Say to Your (Person)
Hook score: +583
Why this works:
List – Lists make videos feel more tangible and add a little bit of curiosity.
Negativity – We’re biologically wired to listen to warnings, and this title leverages that.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about the things they should not say to a person.
Examples of this hook in action:

4 Things NOT to Say to Your Kids
4 Things NOT to Say to Your Boss

Discontinued Delectables


🖼️ Thumbnail 6:
Bee ae err FuinTSTonES

om “PUSH

20 Greatest Discontinued Foods of
All Time

246K views * 3 weeks ago * 95 VPH

Title: 20 Greatest Discontinued Foods of All Time
Formula: 20 (Contrasting Objects) of All Time
Hook score: +702
Why this works:
Curiosity – There’s contrast between “greatest” and “discontinued” and it’s counterintuitive… why would a great food be discontinued? The contrast and counterintuitive both build curiosity.
List – Lists make videos feel more tangible and add a little bit of curiosity.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about a list of contrasting things (like greatest discontinued foods).
Examples of this hook in action:

20 Greatest Discontinued YouTube Channels of All Time
10 Greatest Forgotten Bodyweight Exercises in History

Prefab Party


🖼️ Thumbnail 7:
Aifordelole PRERAE HOMES Il Heel
No Idea Existed!

825K views * 2 months ago + 108 VPH

Title: Affordable PREFAB HOMES I Had No Idea Existed!
Formula: (Desirable Entities) I Had No Idea Existed!
Hook score: +1178
Why this works:
Curiosity – “I had no idea existed” is a fun way to build curiosity around a subject because it feels like you and the audience are getting in on a secret together.
Desire – This creator’s audience is into affordable prefab homes.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about cool things in your niche you had no idea existed.
Examples of this hook in action:

Beautiful Cities I Had No Idea Existed!
Amazing Wearable Tech I Had No Idea Existed!

Flop of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 8:
The WORST Dating Advice For
Women You SHOULD NOT Listen To!

29K views * 3 months ago

Title: The WORST Dating Advice For Women You SHOULD NOT Listen To!
Hook score: -83
Why this flopped: Normally I have a good idea for why a video flopped, but this week I don’t.
This video (and my second choice for flop of the week) seems to have a pretty good title.
So this is just a reminder that the title isn’t everything. There’s a lot that goes into how successful you are on YouTube.
My favorite bit of advice? Get 1% better with every video.
Alright, that wraps up this week’s edition of Creator Hooks!
If you know a fellow creator who needs help with writing better titles, please send them to creatorhooks.com.
Here’s to the war on boring content.
– Jake

## Newsletter 349: Youre Not Bad At YouTube How To Get Your First 1000 Subscribers

**URL:** https://creatorhooks.com/youre-not-bad-at-youtube-how-to-get-your-first-1000-subscribers/

```
You’re Not Bad At YouTube: How To Get Your First 1,000 Subscribers 
December 7, 2022November 7, 2022 by admin 


I’ve asked some of the best creators on YouTube, “How do you write such great titles?”
And they’ve all said the same thing: “I just model what works.”
So this email will show you 5 videos worth modeling to help you write great titles and save time.
Btw, if you want to see all of the titles and thumbnails from Creator Hooks in one place, check out Creator Hooks Pro.
The Purposeful Leak


🖼️ Thumbnail 2:
BIG TAX DEDUCTION
i 20:46)

LEAKED! Tax Strategist Reveals
How LLC Business Owners AVOID...

67K views * 2 months ago

Title: LEAKED! Tax Strategist Reveals How LLC Business Owners AVOID Taxes…
Formula: LEAKED! (Authority Position) Reveals How (Specific Audience) AVOID (Unwanted Action)…
Hook score: +418
Why this works:
Desire – Everybody in this creator’s audience would love to avoid taxes.
Curiosity – Starting the title with “LEAKED!” and making the thumbnail look like a clip from leaked footage is a fun way to build curiosity by making the audience feel like they’re getting in on a secret.
Negativity – This video is about avoiding a pain point — paying taxes.
Authority – This video has credibility because it’s from a Tax Strategist.
How you can use this hook: Make it feel like your secret for how to help your audience avoid doing something they don’t want to do was leaked.
Examples of this hook in action:

LEAKED! Flight Attendant Reveals How Travelers Can AVOID Paying For Checked Bags…
LEAKED! Recruiter Reveals How Job Applicants Can ALWAYS Land Interviews…

Lazy Livin


🖼️ Thumbnail 3:
» Habits aren’t
+) for everyone.

You're Not Lazy: How to Live a
Chaetically Grgenfeed Life

1.3M views + 3 months ago7184 VPH

Title: You’re Not Lazy: How to Live a Chaotically Organised Life
Formula: You’re Not (Unwanted Trait): How To (Achieve Goal)
Hook score: +424
Why this works:
Negativity – This title starts out by refuting a self-doubt.
Desire – Everyone in this creator’s audience would love to live an organised life.
Curiosity – “Chaotically Organised” are opposites, which builds curiosity.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience that they’re not a negative trait, then tell them how to achieve one of their goals.
Examples of this hook in action:

You’re Not Slow: How To Run A 5K Under 20 Minutes
You’re Not A Bad Guitar Player: How To Get Better At Guitar Fast

Check Out Creator Wizard
Wanna join 7,821 creators getting paid sponsorship opportunities from @CreatorWizard’s free weekly newsletter?
“I have made over $17,000 from brand deals I found through Justin’s newsletter. His list not only makes me money on a regular basis, but it also provides tips and information that saves me time and energy when pitching and working with brands. I cannot believe he gives all of this away for free!” Molly, 867K followers
Sign up in one click here.
(Sponsored)
Cruising Into Controversy


🖼️ Thumbnail 4:
| a mutt

This “Adult Onky” Cruise Was The
Worst Cruise I've Ever Taken | Virgi...

190K views + 13 days ago

Title: This “Adult Only” Cruise Was The Worst Cruise I’ve Ever Taken | Virgin Voyages
Formula: This “(Controversial)” (Experience) Was The Worst (Experience) I’ve Ever Taken | (Keywords)
Hook score: +662
Why this works:
Negativity – People love hearing about drama.
Extreme – The “Worst Cruise I’ve Ever Taken” makes for a much better story than just a really bad cruise.
How you can use this hook: Dig into the controversy and tell your audience about the worst experience you’ve ever had in regards to your niche.
Examples of this hook in action:

This “Invite Only” Grand Opening Was The Worst Meal I’ve Ever Had
This “Early Release” Smart Phone Is The Worst Technology I’ve Ever Used

Cake Boss Calamity


🖼️ Thumbnail 5:
Buddy Valastro Was Never The
Same After Caxe Boss

413K views * 3 weeks ago

Title: Buddy Valastro Was Never The Same After Cake Boss
Formula: (Entity) Was Never The Same After (Event)
Hook score: +1086
Why this works:
Curiosity – “Was Never The Same” opens up a loop and makes you wonder what happened to Buddy.
How you can use this hook: Tell the story of how someone or something was never the same after an event.
Examples of this hook in action:

Elon Musk Was Never The Same After Buying Twitter
Real Estate Investing Will Never Be The Same After 2022

Double Doozy


🖼️ Thumbnail 6:
STANDING

16:58

Stop Standing HERE in Doubles!
Gary yourra losing)

101K views * 2 weeks ago

Title: Stop Standing HERE in Doubles! (why you’re losing)
Formula: Stop [Common Action]! (why you’re [Unwanted Result])
Hook score: +1231
Why this works:
Curiosity – Many tennis players think it’s good to stand at the spot shown in the thumbnail, so it’s counterintuitive for the creator to say stop standing there.
Negativity – Telling your audience to stop doing something common is a great way to get their attention.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience to stop doing something common or supposedly good.
Examples of this hook in action:

Stop Counting Calories! (why you’re overweight)
Stop Paying Off Debt! (why you’re broke)

Flop of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 7:
LIES I WAS TAUGHT ABQ

5 Things Every Ex-Child Needs to
Hear

70K views * 1 month ago72 VPH

Title: 5 Things Every Ex-Child Needs to Hear
Hook score: -81
Why this flopped: I thought this title was funny and clever, but that’s not what get clicks.
Clarity beats cleverness every time, and this is a good example of that.
So the next time you’re coming up with ideas, ask yourself, is this title crystal clear?
Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
If you know a fellow creator who needs help with writing better titles, please send them to creatorhooks.com.
– Jake
(@jthomas__ on Twitter)
P.S. Read previous editions here.
P.P.S. If you want to see all of the videos from Creator Hooks in one place, check out Creator Hooks Pro.
Read last week’s edition »

Why this works:
Negativity – This title starts out by refuting a self-doubt.
Desire – Everyone in this creator’s audience would love to live an organised life.
Curiosity – “Chaotically Organised” are opposites, which builds curiosity.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience that they’re not a negative trait, then tell them how to achieve one of their goals.
Examples of this hook in action:

You’re Not Slow: How To Run A 5K Under 20 Minutes
You’re Not A Bad Guitar Player: How To Get Better At Guitar Fast

Check Out Creator Wizard
Wanna join 7,821 creators getting paid sponsorship opportunities from @CreatorWizard’s free weekly newsletter?
“I have made over $17,000 from brand deals I found through Justin’s newsletter. His list not only makes me money on a regular basis, but it also provides tips and information that saves me time and energy when pitching and working with brands. I cannot believe he gives all of this away for free!” Molly, 867K followers
Sign up in one click here.
(Sponsored)
Cruising Into Controversy


🖼️ Thumbnail 4:
| a mutt

This “Adult Onky” Cruise Was The
Worst Cruise I've Ever Taken | Virgi...

190K views + 13 days ago

Title: This “Adult Only” Cruise Was The Worst Cruise I’ve Ever Taken | Virgin Voyages
Formula: This “(Controversial)” (Experience) Was The Worst (Experience) I’ve Ever Taken | (Keywords)
Hook score: +662
Why this works:
Negativity – People love hearing about drama.
Extreme – The “Worst Cruise I’ve Ever Taken” makes for a much better story than just a really bad cruise.
How you can use this hook: Dig into the controversy and tell your audience about the worst experience you’ve ever had in regards to your niche.
Examples of this hook in action:

This “Invite Only” Grand Opening Was The Worst Meal I’ve Ever Had
This “Early Release” Smart Phone Is The Worst Technology I’ve Ever Used

Cake Boss Calamity


🖼️ Thumbnail 5:
Buddy Valastro Was Never The
Same After Caxe Boss

413K views * 3 weeks ago

Title: Buddy Valastro Was Never The Same After Cake Boss
Formula: (Entity) Was Never The Same After (Event)
Hook score: +1086
Why this works:
Curiosity – “Was Never The Same” opens up a loop and makes you wonder what happened to Buddy.
How you can use this hook: Tell the story of how someone or something was never the same after an event.
Examples of this hook in action:

Elon Musk Was Never The Same After Buying Twitter
Real Estate Investing Will Never Be The Same After 2022

Double Doozy


🖼️ Thumbnail 6:
STANDING

16:58

Stop Standing HERE in Doubles!
Gary yourra losing)

101K views * 2 weeks ago

Title: Stop Standing HERE in Doubles! (why you’re losing)
Formula: Stop [Common Action]! (why you’re [Unwanted Result])
Hook score: +1231
Why this works:
Curiosity – Many tennis players think it’s good to stand at the spot shown in the thumbnail, so it’s counterintuitive for the creator to say stop standing there.
Negativity – Telling your audience to stop doing something common is a great way to get their attention.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience to stop doing something common or supposedly good.
Examples of this hook in action:

Stop Counting Calories! (why you’re overweight)
Stop Paying Off Debt! (why you’re broke)

Flop of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 7:
LIES I WAS TAUGHT ABQ

5 Things Every Ex-Child Needs to
Hear

70K views * 1 month ago72 VPH

Title: 5 Things Every Ex-Child Needs to Hear
Hook score: -81
Why this flopped: I thought this title was funny and clever, but that’s not what get clicks.
Clarity beats cleverness every time, and this is a good example of that.
So the next time you’re coming up with ideas, ask yourself, is this title crystal clear?
Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
If you know a fellow creator who needs help with writing better titles, please send them to creatorhooks.com.
– Jake
(@jthomas__ on Twitter)
P.S. Read previous editions here.
P.P.S. If you want to see all of the videos from Creator Hooks in one place, check out Creator Hooks Pro.
