## Newsletter 351: The ONLY 3 Tools You Need to Grow on YouTube

**URL:** https://creatorhooks.com/the-only-3-tools-you-need-to-grow-on-youtube/

```
The ONLY 3 Tools You Need to Grow on YouTube 
December 7, 2022October 31, 2022 by admin 


I’ve asked some of the best creators on YouTube, “How do you write such great titles?”
And they’ve all said the same thing: “I just model what works.”
So this email will show you 5 videos worth modeling to help you write great titles and save time.
P.S. Last month I ran 103 YouTube Title A/B tests to see how the strategies I’ve shared in this newsletter work.
See what happened here.
Dog Days


🖼️ Thumbnail 2:
Worst Mistake I've
Ever Made...

Not My Best Day As A Dog
Breeder

56K views * 1 month ago2 VPH

Title: Not My Best Day As A Dog Breeder
Formula: Not My Best Day As A (Occupation)
Hook score: +350
Why this works:
Negativity – Negativity does a great job at getting people’s attention, and this title uses it in the title (“Not My Best Day”) and in the thumbnail (“Worst Mistake”).
Curiosity – This title builds curiosity by starting a story, but not finishing it. You can’t help but wonder what was her mistake and what happened after reading this title.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about “not your best day” and the worst mistake you’ve ever made.
Examples of this hook in action:

Not My Best Day As A New Dad
Not My Best Day As A YouTuber

Flexible Transformation


🖼️ Thumbnail 3:
The ONLY 3 Stretches You
Need for Better Flexibility

1.3M views + 1 month ago7.3K VPH

Title: The ONLY 3 Stretches You Need for Better Flexibility
Formula: The ONLY 3 (Exercise or Tool) You Need for (Goal)
Hook score: +610
Why this works:
Desire – This is all about a big desire for her audience.
List – Lists make videos feel more tangible and add a little bit of curiosity.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about the only 3 things they need to reach their goals.
Examples of this hook in action:

The ONLY 3 Revenue Streams You Need to Become a Millionaire
The ONLY 3 Lures You Need to Catch Big Bass This Fall

A/B Test Your Titles & Thumbnails
One of the most effective ways I’ve grown my channel (and clients’ channels) is by A/B testing thumbnails and titles.
It’s helped me increase CTR so I can get more views AND it’s helped me get better at writing titles and making thumbnails by showing me what works and what doesn’t.
I just did a big project where I ran 103 YouTube title A/B tests using a simple tool called ThumbnailTest.
Check out ThumbnailTest here.
(Affiliate Link)
Debt Doomer


🖼️ Thumbnail 4:
The FED Just Gave a Serious
Warning To All Americans...

589K views * 1 month ago3 VPH

Title: The FED Just Gave a Serious Warning To All Americans With DEBT
Formula: The (Authority) Just Gave a Serious Warning To All (Specific Audience)
Hook score: +647
Why this works:
Curiosity – This title builds curiosity by starting a story, but not finishing it. You can’t help but wonder what warning the Fed gave after reading this title.
Negativity – We’re genetically wired to listen to warnings, and this creator uses that to his advantage in this title.
Timeliness – The word “Just” makes this title feel like news, and, in general, we’re more interested in news than education.
Authority – “The FED” brings credibility to this video.
Specific Audience – If you’re an American with debt (which is a ton of people), then you’re way more likely to watch this video because it feels like this was made just for you.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about a warning that an authority in your industry just gave to a specific group of people.
Examples of this hook in action:

Google Just Gave a Serious Warning To All SEOs
Shopify Just Gave a Serious Warning To All Ecom Store Owners

Vinyl Victory


🖼️ Thumbnail 5:
MIND-BLOWING VINYL
HACKS THAT WILL CHANG...

66K views + 1 month ago23 VPH

Title: MIND-BLOWING VINYL HACKS THAT WILL CHANGE THE GAME!
Formula: Mind-Blowing (Object) Hacks That Will Change The Game!
Hook score: +670
Why this works:
Curiosity – “Mind-Blowing” hacks is a fun (although maybe extreme) way to build curiosity.
Desire – This channel’s audience would love vinyl hacks.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about the mind-blowing hacks that will change the game for them.
Examples of this hook in action:

Mind-Blowing Instagram Hacks That Will Change The Game!
Mind-Blowing Travel Hacks That Will Change Your Life!

Normies


🖼️ Thumbnail 6:
My Simple Productivity
System (for normal people)!

459K views * 2 months ago478 VPH

Title: My Simple Productivity System (for normal people)!
Formula: My Simple [Goal] System (for normal people)!
Hook score: +675
Why this works:
Desire – This creator’s audience loves simple productivity systems.
Specific Audience – By calling out normal people in the title, all us normal people feel like this video was made for us. It also kinda refutes an objection. People might think, “these hacks won’t work for me,” but by ending the title with “(for normal people)”, it gets rid of that excuse.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about a simple system you use to help them (“normal people”) achieve their goal.
Examples of this hook in action:

My Simple 6-Pack System (for normal people)!
My Simple Job Search System (for normal people)!

Flop of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 7:
What Product Marketers
ACTUALLY Do in Tech...

13K views * 4 months ago2 VPH

Title: What Product Marketers ACTUALLY Do in Tech Companies!
Hook score: -89
Why this flopped: The video above (the one about normal people) calls out a massive audience with a great benefit. That’s a good formula for a successful video.
But this title is about a much smaller audience (product marketers).
I would assume that almost everybody wants a simple productivity system, but much fewer people care about what product marketers actually do at tech companies.
Here’s a question I always ask when evaluating an idea (BEFORE making the video):
“Does this topic have a big enough audience for my view goals?”
Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
If you know a fellow creator who needs help with writing better titles, please send them to creatorhooks.com.
Here’s to the war on boring content.
– Jake
(@jthomas__ on Twitter)
P.S. Read previous editions here.
P.P.S. If you want to see all of the videos from Creator Hooks in one place, check out Creator Hooks Pro.
Read last week’s edition »

## Newsletter 353: 7 WORST Things To Do Before Publishing A YouTube Video

**URL:** https://creatorhooks.com/7-worst-things-to-do-before-publishing-a-youtube-video/

```
7 WORST Things To Do Before Publishing A YouTube Video 
December 7, 2022October 24, 2022 by admin 


I’ve asked some of the best creators on YouTube, “How do you write such great titles?”
And they’ve all said the same thing: “I just model what works.”
So this email will show you 5 videos worth modeling to help you write great titles and save time.
P.S. Last month I ran 103 YouTube Title A/B tests to see how the strategies I’ve shared in this newsletter work.
See what happened here.
Post Workout Mistakes


🖼️ Thumbnail 2:
7 WORST Things to Do After
Your Workout

320K views * 1 month ago

Title: 7 WORST Things to Do After Your Workout
Formula: 7 WORST Things to Do After (Activity)
Hook score: +326
Why this works:
Negativity – After working hard you don’t want to ruin the potential benefits from your workout, so you have to watch this video.
List – Lists make videos feel more tangible and add curiosity.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about the worst things they can do after a common activity.
Examples of this hook in action:

7 WORST Things to Do After A Job Interview
7 WORST Things to Do After A First Date

Inside Namibia


🖼️ Thumbnail 3:
Namibia: A Side Of Africa The
Media Won't Show You

674K views + 13 days ago

Title: Namibia: A Side Of Africa The Media Won’t Show You
Formula: (Topic): A Side Of (Entity) The Media Won’t Show You
Hook score: +332
Why this works:
Curiosity – This makes you feel like you’re getting in on a secret.
Negativity – This title has an “anti-establishment” or “sticking it to the man” feel to it.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about the side of a topic the establishment won’t show them.
Examples of this hook in action:

Profit: A Side of Entrepreneurship The Business Gurus Won’t Show You
Head Injuries: A Side Of Football The Media Won’t Show You

One Liner Magic


🖼️ Thumbnail 4:
Mind @
10 Blowing

Top 10 CSS One Liners That
Will Blow Your Mind

171K views * 2 weeks ago

Title: Top 10 CSS One Liners That Will Blow Your Mind
Formula: Top 10 (Simple Objects) That Will Blow Your Mind
Hook score: +469
Why this works:
List – Lists make videos feel more tangible and add curiosity.
Curiosity – There’s contrast between “one liners” (sounds simple) and “will blow your mind” (sounds incredible).
Desire – This channel’s audience would love some amazing CSS one liners.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about simple things that will blow their mind.
Examples of this hook in action:

Top 10 Bodyweight Exercises That Will Get You Ripped
10-Minute Dinners That Will Blow Your Mind

Credit Card Calamity


🖼️ Thumbnail 5:
The Video Credit Card
Companies Don't Want You ...

70K views + 1 month ago

Title: The Video Credit Card Companies Don’t Want You to See
Formula: The Video (Big Enemy) Don’t Want You to See
Hook score: +530
Why this works:
Curiosity – This builds curiosity by revealing a secret.
Negativity – Like the Namibia example above, this gives a big “anti-establishment” vibe.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about the video the “establishment” doesn’t want you to see.
Examples of this hook in action:

The Video Guitar Teachers Don’t Want You to See
The Video Apple Didn’t Want Me To Make

Frumpy & Bumpy


🖼️ Thumbnail 6:
&

... STOP LOOKING)
Di FRUMPYEZS

How To Not Look Frumpy &
Older Than You Are-Get Out...

420K views + 2 weeks ago * 7.8K VPH

Title: How To Not Look Frumpy & Older Than You Are-Get Out of That Rut!!
Formula: How To Not (Unwanted Outcome)-(Encouraging Words)!!
Hook score: +1750
Why this works:
Negativity – This video is all about avoiding an unwanted outcome.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience how to avoid an unwanted outcome.
Examples of this hook in action:

How To Not Have an Outdated & Ugly House – Simple Changes!!
How To Not Be Broke Forever – Escape the Rat Race!!

Flop of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 7:
HIS LIFE CHANGING JOURN

How a South African Who
Came From Poverty Change...

32K views » 3 months ago

Title: How a South African Who Came From Poverty Changed His Life
Hook score: -89
Why this flopped: The clearest difference between this video (32K views) and the Namibia video above (674K views) is the thumbnail.
The Namibia thumbnail is bright and you can clearly see their faces.
This thumbnail is dark and the subjects are small.
It’s also a little wordy and complicated. To make it simpler and punchier, I would’ve worded it like this: “How a Poor South African Changed His Life”.
Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
If you know a fellow creator who needs help with writing better titles, please send them to creatorhooks.com.
Here’s to the war on boring content.
– Jake
(@jthomas__ on Twitter)
P.S. Read previous editions here.
P.P.S. If you want to see all of the videos from Creator Hooks in one place, check out Creator Hooks Pro.
Read last week’s edition »

## Newsletter 354: 7 WORST Things To Do Before Publishing A YouTube Video

**URL:** https://creatorhooks.com/7-worst-things-to-do-before-publishing-a-youtube-video/

```
7 WORST Things To Do Before Publishing A YouTube Video 
December 7, 2022October 24, 2022 by admin 


I’ve asked some of the best creators on YouTube, “How do you write such great titles?”
And they’ve all said the same thing: “I just model what works.”
So this email will show you 5 videos worth modeling to help you write great titles and save time.
P.S. Last month I ran 103 YouTube Title A/B tests to see how the strategies I’ve shared in this newsletter work.
See what happened here.
Post Workout Mistakes


🖼️ Thumbnail 2:
7 WORST Things to Do After
Your Workout

320K views * 1 month ago

Title: 7 WORST Things to Do After Your Workout
Formula: 7 WORST Things to Do After (Activity)
Hook score: +326
Why this works:
Negativity – After working hard you don’t want to ruin the potential benefits from your workout, so you have to watch this video.
List – Lists make videos feel more tangible and add curiosity.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about the worst things they can do after a common activity.
Examples of this hook in action:

7 WORST Things to Do After A Job Interview
7 WORST Things to Do After A First Date

Inside Namibia


🖼️ Thumbnail 3:
Namibia: A Side Of Africa The
Media Won't Show You

674K views + 13 days ago

Title: Namibia: A Side Of Africa The Media Won’t Show You
Formula: (Topic): A Side Of (Entity) The Media Won’t Show You
Hook score: +332
Why this works:
Curiosity – This makes you feel like you’re getting in on a secret.
Negativity – This title has an “anti-establishment” or “sticking it to the man” feel to it.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about the side of a topic the establishment won’t show them.
Examples of this hook in action:

Profit: A Side of Entrepreneurship The Business Gurus Won’t Show You
Head Injuries: A Side Of Football The Media Won’t Show You

One Liner Magic


🖼️ Thumbnail 4:
Mind @
10 Blowing

Top 10 CSS One Liners That
Will Blow Your Mind

171K views * 2 weeks ago

Title: Top 10 CSS One Liners That Will Blow Your Mind
Formula: Top 10 (Simple Objects) That Will Blow Your Mind
Hook score: +469
Why this works:
List – Lists make videos feel more tangible and add curiosity.
Curiosity – There’s contrast between “one liners” (sounds simple) and “will blow your mind” (sounds incredible).
Desire – This channel’s audience would love some amazing CSS one liners.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about simple things that will blow their mind.
Examples of this hook in action:

Top 10 Bodyweight Exercises That Will Get You Ripped
10-Minute Dinners That Will Blow Your Mind

Credit Card Calamity


🖼️ Thumbnail 5:
The Video Credit Card
Companies Don't Want You ...

70K views + 1 month ago

Title: The Video Credit Card Companies Don’t Want You to See
Formula: The Video (Big Enemy) Don’t Want You to See
Hook score: +530
Why this works:
Curiosity – This builds curiosity by revealing a secret.
Negativity – Like the Namibia example above, this gives a big “anti-establishment” vibe.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about the video the “establishment” doesn’t want you to see.
Examples of this hook in action:

The Video Guitar Teachers Don’t Want You to See
The Video Apple Didn’t Want Me To Make

Frumpy & Bumpy


🖼️ Thumbnail 6:
&

... STOP LOOKING)
Di FRUMPYEZS

How To Not Look Frumpy &
Older Than You Are-Get Out...

420K views + 2 weeks ago * 7.8K VPH

Title: How To Not Look Frumpy & Older Than You Are-Get Out of That Rut!!
Formula: How To Not (Unwanted Outcome)-(Encouraging Words)!!
Hook score: +1750
Why this works:
Negativity – This video is all about avoiding an unwanted outcome.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience how to avoid an unwanted outcome.
Examples of this hook in action:

How To Not Have an Outdated & Ugly House – Simple Changes!!
How To Not Be Broke Forever – Escape the Rat Race!!

Flop of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 7:
HIS LIFE CHANGING JOURN

How a South African Who
Came From Poverty Change...

32K views » 3 months ago

Title: How a South African Who Came From Poverty Changed His Life
Hook score: -89
Why this flopped: The clearest difference between this video (32K views) and the Namibia video above (674K views) is the thumbnail.
The Namibia thumbnail is bright and you can clearly see their faces.
This thumbnail is dark and the subjects are small.
It’s also a little wordy and complicated. To make it simpler and punchier, I would’ve worded it like this: “How a Poor South African Changed His Life”.
Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
If you know a fellow creator who needs help with writing better titles, please send them to creatorhooks.com.
Here’s to the war on boring content.
– Jake
(@jthomas__ on Twitter)
P.S. Read previous editions here.
P.P.S. If you want to see all of the videos from Creator Hooks in one place, check out Creator Hooks Pro.

## Newsletter 355: 6 Ugly YouTube Truths You Need to Hear

**URL:** https://creatorhooks.com/6-ugly-youtube-truths-you-need-to-hear/

```
6 Ugly YouTube “Truths” You Need to Hear… 
December 7, 2022October 17, 2022 by admin 


I’ve asked some of the best creators on YouTube, “How do you write such great titles?”
And they’ve all said the same thing: “I just model what works.”
So this email will show you 5 videos worth modeling to help you write great titles and save time.
P.S. Last month I ran 103 YouTube Title A/B tests to see how the strategies I’ve shared in this newsletter work.
See what happened here.
Ballin on a Budget


🖼️ Thumbnail 2:
What | Spend In a Week as a
24 Year Old in NYC

647K views + 1 month ago * 62 VPH

Title: What I Spend In a Week as a 24 Year Old in NYC
Formula: What I Spend In (Time Frame) as a (Specific Person)
Hook score: +359
Why this works:
Time Frame – The time frame “in a week” makes this video more tangible (and therefore, more interesting).
Curiosity – This feels like you’re getting in on a secret. Especially because this video is about money, and money is typically a private subject.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about how much you spend in a specific time frame, or give them insight into another typically private subject.
Examples of this hook in action:

What I Spend In a Week as a Full-Time YouTuber
What I Spend In a Month as a Full-Time Digital Nomad

Retirement Woes


🖼️ Thumbnail 3:
UNCOMFORTABLE

4 iw RETIREMENT TRUTHS
Hie

6 Uncomfortable Retirement Truths

6 Uncomfortable Retirement
"Truths" You Need to Hear...

35K views * 8 days ago * 118 VPH

Title: 6 Uncomfortable Retirement “Truths” You Need to Hear…
Formula: 6 Uncomfortable (Niche or Activity) “Truths” You Need to Hear…
Hook score: +466
Why this works:
List – Lists make videos feel more tangible and add some curiosity.
Negativity – “Uncomfortable Truths” are way more interesting than, say, “Happy Truths”.
Curiosity – This title makes you wonder what these “uncomfortable truths” are, so you have to watch.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about the uncomfortable truths they need to hear.
Examples of this hook in action:

6 Uncomfortable “Truths” About Being A Mom You Need to Hear…
6 Uncomfortable 6 Pack “Truths” You Need to Hear…

Climbing Legends


🖼️ Thumbnail 4:
World's Steepest Climb Vs
World's Best Climber: Can H...

1.3M views + 2 months ago » 200 VPH

Title: World’s Steepest Climb Vs World’s Best Climber: Can He Defeat It?
Formula: World’s (Extreme Element) Vs World’s Best (Element Conqueror): Can (Person) Defeat It?
Hook score: +797
Why this works:
Extreme – Extreme things, like world’s steepest climb and best climber, make for an interesting story.
X vs. Y – Everybody loves a little competition and they have to click to see what happened.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about a big challenge that the world’s best took on.
Examples of this hook in action:

World’s Best Fisherman Vs World’s Biggest Fish: Can He Land One?
World’s Fastest Eater Vs World’s Biggest Steak: Can He Finish It?

Amazon Alternatives


🖼️ Thumbnail 5:
Where Else To Self-Publish
Books That's NOT Amazon...

58K views * 1 month ago » 37 VPH

Title: Where Else To Self-Publish Books That’s NOT Amazon KDP – Self-Publish Low Content Books
Formula: Where Else To (Activity) That’s NOT (Current Leader In Activity) – (Keywords)
Hook score: +874
Why this works:
Refute an objection – This channel’s audience might think their only option to self-publish books is Amazon KDP, so they’re interested to hear other options.
Curiosity – A simple way to build curiosity is to tell your audience about what something is not, so they have to click and find out what it is.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about a little-known alternative to solve a big problem they have.
Examples of this hook in action:

Safe Places To Invest Your Money That’s NOT The Stock Market
Where Else To Fix Your Marriage That’s NOT Marriage Counseling

Sticky Pans


🖼️ Thumbnail 6:
cronep JN
Food Sticking to Stainless
Steel Pans? 4 Common...

569K views + 2 months ago * 1.2K VPH

Title: Food Sticking to Stainless Steel Pans? 4 Common Mistakes to Avoid
Formula: (Problem)? 4 Common Mistakes To Avoid
Hook score: +18966
Why this works:
Negativity – This title starts with a pain point and then talks about mistakes to avoid.
List – Lists make videos feel more tangible and add some curiosity.
How you can use this hook: Address a big paint point for your audience, then tell them about the common mistakes to avoid.
Examples of this hook in action:

Instagram Engagement Down? 4 Common Mistakes To Avoid
Knee Pain? 4 Common Mistakes To Avoid

Flop of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 7:
Top 5 Ways To Train On Your
Commute!

51K views * 1 month ago +

Title: Top 5 Ways To Train On Your Commute!
Hook score: -78
Why this flopped: My first thought was that this flopped because it’s a narrow topic — how many people are actually riding their bike to work?
But a video titled, “What’s The Best Way To Ride To Work? | GCN Commuter Challenge,” recently did well for this channel (350K views).
My theory is that this flopped because those who are riding their bikes to work are doing it because they enjoy it, not because they’re trying to train hard on their way to work.
Whether or not that’s true, the lesson is to always strive to know your audience better and understand their goals and motives.
Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
If you know a fellow creator who needs help with writing better titles, please send them to creatorhooks.com.
– Jake
(@jthomas__ on Twitter)
P.S. Read previous editions here.
P.P.S. If you want to see all of the videos from Creator Hooks in one place, check out Creator Hooks Pro.
Read last week’s edition »

## Newsletter 357: 12 Rules To Blow Up On YouTube

**URL:** https://creatorhooks.com/12-rules-to-blow-up-on-youtube/

```
12 Rules To Blow Up On YouTube 
December 7, 2022October 10, 2022 by admin 


I’ve asked some of the best creators on YouTube, “How do you write such great titles?”
And they’ve all said the same thing: “I just model what works.”
So this email will show you 5 videos worth modeling to help you write great titles and save time.
Btw, if you want to see all of the titles and thumbnails from Creator Hooks in one place, check out Creator Hooks Pro.
Clean Houses Rule


🖼️ Thumbnail 2:
12 Rules For A Cleaner
Home! (Cleaning Motivation)

413K views + 1 month ago + 183 VPH

Title: 12 Rules For A Cleaner Home! (Cleaning Motivation)
Formula: 12 Rules For A [Ideal Possession]! ([Niche] Motivation)
Hook score: +338
Why this works:
List – Lists make a video feel more tangible and add some curiosity.
Desire – This audience would love rules for a cleaner home.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about the 12 rules to get exactly what they want.
Examples of this hook in action:

12 Rules For A Perfect Resume! (Job Search Tips)
My 12 Rules For Investing In Real Estate

Cheap Sheets


🖼️ Thumbnail 3:
24 HOURS IN THE CHEAPEST
ALL-INCLUSIVE RESORT IN...

103K views + 1 month ago

Title: 24 HOURS IN THE CHEAPEST ALL-INCLUSIVE RESORT IN MEXICO (Playa Del Carmen)
Formula: [Time Frame] In The [Extreme] [Place] ([Specific Place])
Hook score: +393
Why this works:
Time Frame – People love time frames because they make a video feel more tangible — you know exactly what to expect and that makes you more excited to click on it.
Extreme – The “Cheapest All-Inclusive” is much cooler than “A Cheap All-Inclusive”
Desire – Even though it’s the cheapest one, this video is still about an all-inclusive resort, which is something this audience loves.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about how you spent a specific time frame in an extreme place.
Examples of this hook in action:

12 Hours In The Cheapest Gym In America
30 Days With The Cheapest Smart Phone

Algorithm Adventure


🖼️ Thumbnail 4:
& YOUTUBE "ime

How The YouTube Algorithm
Works in 2022

200K views + 2 months ago

Title: How The YouTube Algorithm Works in 2022
Formula: How (Powerful Entity) Works In 2022
Hook score: +555
Why this works:
Timeliness – Since the algorithm is always changing, adding “2022” at the end of the title lets the audience know this is relevant right now.
Desire – YouTubers are obsessed with the algorithm.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience how something they’re obsessed with is working right now.
Examples of this hook in action:

How Mortgage Interest Rates Will Change In 2022
What Will Happen To Crypto In 2023

Diggin Dividends


🖼️ Thumbnail 5:
7 Dividend Stocks That Pay
Me $500+ Per Month

128K views + 2 weeks ago *

Title: 7 Dividend Stocks That Pay Me $500+ Per Month
Formula: 7 (Objects) That (Achieve Goal)
Hook score: +752
Why this works:
List – Lists make a video feel more tangible and add some curiosity.
Desire – This creator’s audience loves dividend stocks and loves to get paid every month.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about a list of things that will help them get their specific desires.
Examples of this hook in action:

7 Shopify Apps That Help Me Sell $100,000 Per Month
5 Best Lures To Catch Fall Redfish

Fix The Flex


🖼️ Thumbnail 6:
as

FIX LOWER BACK. BEFORE
IT'S IRREVERSIBLE

3.2M views + 4 months ago «

Title: FIX LOWER BACK. BEFORE IT’S IRREVERSIBLE
Formula: Fix (Problem). Before It’s Irreversible
Hook score: +1729
Why this works:
Negativity – This title is all about a pain point and a warning to go along with it.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience how to fix a pain point before it’s irreversible.
Examples of this hook in action:

Fix Your Running Form. Before You Can’t Run Anymore
Fix Your Lawn. Before It’s Irreversible

Flop of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 7:
Angel Investing, Explained
Easy!

8.7K views * 3 weeks ago :

Title: Angel Investing, Explained Easy!
Hook score: -70
Why this flopped: Some topics just have a smaller audience. Here’s a screenshot from Google Trends of Angel Investing vs. Dividend Investing (the topic of a video that crushed it for this channel recently):


🖼️ Thumbnail 8:
@ dividend investing

Search term

United States v

Interest over time

Past 12 months v

@ angel investing
Search term

Allcategories v

Web Search v

+ Add comparison

Average

Oct 10, 2021

lote

Feb 13, 2022

Jun 19, 2022

le

Knowing that angel investing isn’t exactly the most popular topic, this creator could’ve spice up the title with some curiosity or negativity.
Something like:

The Best Investment Strategy Nobody Talks About
I Wish I Knew About This Investment Strategy 5 Years Ago

Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
If you know a fellow creator who needs help with writing better titles, please send them to creatorhooks.com.
Here’s to the war on boring content.
– Jake
(@jthomas__ on Twitter)
P.S. Read previous editions here.
P.P.S. If you want to see all of the videos from Creator Hooks in one place, check out Creator Hooks Pro.

## Newsletter 359: If only I knew THIS about YouTube 5 Years Ago

**URL:** https://creatorhooks.com/if-only-i-knew-this-about-youtube-5-years-ago/

```
If only I knew THIS about YouTube 5 Years Ago 
December 7, 2022October 3, 2022 by admin 


I’ve asked some of the best creators on YouTube, “How do you write such great titles?”
And they’ve all said the same thing: “I just model what works.”
So this email will show you 5 videos worth modeling to help you write great titles and save time.
Btw, if you want to see all of the titles and thumbnails from Creator Hooks in one place, check out Creator Hooks Pro.
Trail Mix


🖼️ Thumbnail 2:
I've Heard This Is The Best
Trail In The World - SOLO...

107K views + 1 month ago «

Title: I’ve Heard This Is The Best Trail In The World – SOLO HIKING THE LAUGAVEGUR IN ICELAND
Formula: I’ve Heard This Is The Best (Entity) In The World – (Specific Activity)
Hook score: + 356
Why this works:
Desire – This creator’s audience would love to solo hike the best trail in the world.
Extreme – “Best Trail In The World” is a lot cooler than “Really Cool Trail.”
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about something you heard is the best in the world.
Examples of this hook in action:

I’ve Heard This Is The Best Sandwich In The World – EATING MCDONALD’S McRIB
NBA Scouts Say This Is The Best Prospect In The World

Rise Up


🖼️ Thumbnail 3:
The Sudden Rise of UGC

52K views * 2 weeks ago + 196 VPH

Title: The Sudden Rise of UGC
Formula: The Sudden Rise of (New Trend)
Hook score: +422
Why this works:
Timeliness – This is about a current trend and it feels like news. People love news.
New opportunity – UGC is a new opportunity for people trying to make money online (this creator’s audience). People love new opportunities because they haven’t failed at the new thing yet, so it offers hope.
Negativity – This thumbnail sparks fear in the audience.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about the sudden rise of a new trend.
Examples of this hook in action:

The Sudden Rise of Homesteading
The Sudden Rise of Alex Hormozi

Starving Regrets


🖼️ Thumbnail 4:
If Only | Knew This About
FASTING 5 Years Ago

432K views * 3 weeks ago * 382 VPH

Title: If Only I Knew This About FASTING 5 Years Ago
Formula: If Only I Knew This About (Subject) 5 Years Ago
Hook score: +540
Why this works:
Negativity – This is about a regret that this creator has.
Curiosity – “If only I knew this…” builds curiosity by opening a loop. What did he wish he knew?!
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about what you wish you knew about a subject 5 years ago.
Examples of this hook in action:

If Only I Knew This About Twitter 5 Years Ago
If Only I Knew This About Living In Las Vegas 3 Years Ago

Wake n’ Make


🖼️ Thumbnail 5:
How to Wake up Before 6am
Everyday

357K views + 2 months ago -

Title: How to Wake up Before 6am Everyday
Formula: How to (Achieve Goal) Every Day
Hook score: +595
Why this works:
Desire – The specific desire of “Wake up Before 6am” is much better than just, “Wake up Early.”
Daily – People love things they can plug into their daily routine.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about how they can achieve a goal everyday.
Examples of this hook in action:

How to Meet New People Every Day
How to Get 10,000 Steps Every Day

Broke to Ballin


🖼️ Thumbnail 6:
from BROKE to ABUNDANCE
mindset | *how this change...

98K views = 1 month ago * 8 VPH

Title: from BROKE to ABUNDANCE mindset | *how this changed my life*
Formula: from (Negative State) to (POSITIVE State) | *how this changed my life*
Hook score: +891
Why this works:
Transformation – From a bad state to a positive state promises a good story and is exactly what the audience wants.
Curiosity – This builds curiosity a few ways. First, the “how this changed my life” part adds curiosity by opening a loop. Plus “changed my life” adds curiosity because it’s vague and open to interpretation. And finally, there’s contrast between “broke” and “abundance”.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about how you went from a bad state to a positive state.
Examples of this hook in action:

from Dead Grass to a Thick Green Lawn | *how this changed my yard*
from BROKE to ABUNDANCE mindset | *how this changed my life*

Flop of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 7:
How MSG Affects the Brain
(good, bad, and ugly)

22K views + 4 weeks ago *

Title: How MSG Affects the Brain (good, bad, and ugly)
Hook score: -82
Why this flopped: This probably flopped because it doesn’t take a stance on MSG.
Looking at some of his other videos, they’ve either had an opinion (unlike this title’s “good, bad, and ugly”) or built curiosity.
The thumbnail is also bland and vague, just like the title.
Here are some other angles he could’ve taken with the title:

MSG’s Surprising Benefits for Brain Health (positive stance)
STOP Eating These Foods With MSG In Them (negative stance)
This Controversial Ingredient Can Change Your Brain (curiosity driven)

Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
And if you know a fellow creator who needs help with writing better titles, please send them to creatorhooks.com.
Here’s to the war on boring content.
– Jake
(@jthomas__ on Twitter)
P.P.S. If you want to see all of the videos from Creator Hooks in one place, check out Creator Hooks Pro.
Read last week’s edition »

🖼️ Thumbnail 5:
How to Wake up Before 6am
Everyday

357K views + 2 months ago -

Title: How to Wake up Before 6am Everyday
Formula: How to (Achieve Goal) Every Day
Hook score: +595
Why this works:
Desire – The specific desire of “Wake up Before 6am” is much better than just, “Wake up Early.”
Daily – People love things they can plug into their daily routine.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about how they can achieve a goal everyday.
Examples of this hook in action:

How to Meet New People Every Day
How to Get 10,000 Steps Every Day

Broke to Ballin


🖼️ Thumbnail 6:
from BROKE to ABUNDANCE
mindset | *how this change...

98K views = 1 month ago * 8 VPH

Title: from BROKE to ABUNDANCE mindset | *how this changed my life*
Formula: from (Negative State) to (POSITIVE State) | *how this changed my life*
Hook score: +891
Why this works:
Transformation – From a bad state to a positive state promises a good story and is exactly what the audience wants.
Curiosity – This builds curiosity a few ways. First, the “how this changed my life” part adds curiosity by opening a loop. Plus “changed my life” adds curiosity because it’s vague and open to interpretation. And finally, there’s contrast between “broke” and “abundance”.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about how you went from a bad state to a positive state.
Examples of this hook in action:

from Dead Grass to a Thick Green Lawn | *how this changed my yard*
from BROKE to ABUNDANCE mindset | *how this changed my life*

Flop of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 7:
How MSG Affects the Brain
(good, bad, and ugly)

22K views + 4 weeks ago *

Title: How MSG Affects the Brain (good, bad, and ugly)
Hook score: -82
Why this flopped: This probably flopped because it doesn’t take a stance on MSG.
Looking at some of his other videos, they’ve either had an opinion (unlike this title’s “good, bad, and ugly”) or built curiosity.
The thumbnail is also bland and vague, just like the title.
Here are some other angles he could’ve taken with the title:

MSG’s Surprising Benefits for Brain Health (positive stance)
STOP Eating These Foods With MSG In Them (negative stance)
This Controversial Ingredient Can Change Your Brain (curiosity driven)

Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
And if you know a fellow creator who needs help with writing better titles, please send them to creatorhooks.com.
Here’s to the war on boring content.
– Jake
(@jthomas__ on Twitter)
P.P.S. If you want to see all of the videos from Creator Hooks in one place, check out Creator Hooks Pro.

## Newsletter 361: My HONEST Opinion After 6 Months on YouTube

**URL:** https://creatorhooks.com/my-honest-opinion-after-6-months-on-youtube/

```
My HONEST Opinion After 6 Months on YouTube 
December 7, 2022September 26, 2022 by admin 


I’ve asked some of the best creators on YouTube, “How do you write such great titles?”
And they’ve all said the same thing: “I just model what works.”
So this email will show you 5 videos worth modeling to help you write great titles and save time.
Btw, if you want to see all of the titles and thumbnails from Creator Hooks in one place, check out Creator Hooks Pro.
Chinese Collapse


🖼️ Thumbnail 2:
it ends in

, 28 days.

Q Jennifer Zeng @ 2
-$6 Billion GONE.

The total economic death of
China (is happening NOW)

1.7M views + 1 month ago

Title: The total economic death of China (is happening NOW)
Formula: The [Negative Event] (is happening NOW)
Hook score: +383
Why this works:
Negativity – People can’t get enough of the drama and this title is full of it.
Timeliness – People also love news and “(is happening NOW)” lets the audience know, well, this is happening now.
Curiosity – This title reads like the start of a story, and you can’t help but want to know more.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about a negative event that is happening now.
Examples of this hook in action:

The Biggest Google Algorithm Shift (is happening NOW)
Florida’s Deadliest Hurricane Season (is happening NOW)

Timely Thoughts


🖼️ Thumbnail 3:
My HONEST Opinion After 1
Month in the Philippines

352K views * 1 month ago + 200 VPH

Title: My HONEST Opinion After 1 Month in the Philippines
Formula: My HONEST Opinion After (Time Frame) (Experience)
Hook score: +541
Why this works:
Time frame – This time frame gives his video credibility because he’s been there an entire month, as opposed to some people who’ve traveled there for just a few days.
Curiosity – It might just be me, but the way he uses “HONEST” makes you feel like you’re getting insider information here.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about your honest opinion after a specific time frame of doing something or being somewhere.
Examples of this hook in action:

My HONEST Opinion After 1 Month with an iPhone 14
My HONEST Opinion After 1 Month of CrossFit

T-Bone Time


🖼️ Thumbnail 4:
| SURVIVED 50 HOURS W/
LIVER KING...

6.1M views + 1 month ago + 884 VPH

Title: I SURVIVED 50 HOURS W/ LIVER KING…
Formula: I SURVIVED (Time Frame) W/ (Authority Figure)…
Hook score: +554
Why this works:
Authority – Liver King’s name and picture makes a ton of people click.
Negativity – “Survived” adds to the feeling that you’re about to watch an intense video with some tough adventures.
Time Frame – The time frame of 50 hours makes this video feel more tangible and interesting.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience how you survived with an authority figure for a specific period of time.
Examples of this hook in action:

I Survived 1 Year Working For Elon Musk…
I SURVIVED 50 HOURS IN AMERICA’S HOTTEST DESERT

Look Rich, Be Rich


🖼️ Thumbnail 5:
How to LOOK RICH and
WEALTHY! Simple tips and...

205K views + 1 month ago : 57 VPH

Title: How to LOOK RICH and WEALTHY! Simple tips and tricks to achieve that sophisticated, polished look!
Formula: How to (Achieve Goal)! Simple tips and tricks to (Achieve Specific Goal)!
Hook score: +602
Why this works:
Desire – This creator really nailed down what their audience wants. “How to LOOK RICH and WEALTHY!” is way more specific than just, “How to Look Pretty.” Plus, people love simple tips and tricks.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience how to get their very specific desire.
Examples of this hook in action:

How to Get 10,000 YouTube Subs Per Month! Simple tips to grow your YouTube channel!
How to Run a 20-Minute 5K! Simple tips and tricks to run faster!

Quittin Time


🖼️ Thumbnail 6:
— cat :
| Quit Teaching & Here's What
| Learned

104K views + 2 weeks ago » 44 VPH

Title: I Quit Teaching & Here’s What I Learned
Formula: I Quit (Activity) & Here’s What I Learned
Hook score: +702
Why this works:
Negativity – Quitting something good is a great way to get people’s attention.
Curiosity – “Here’s What I Learned” opens a loop and makes you want to find out what she learned.
Milestone – Your audience will love hearing about big milestones, like quitting a job.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about how you quit something good and what you learned from it.
Examples of this hook in action:

I Quit Journaling & Here’s What I Learned
I Ended a Toxic Relationship & Here’s What I Learned

Flop of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 7:
Your money is cheating on
you (with your bank)

93K views + 2 months ago *

Title: Your money is cheating on you (with your bank)
Hook score: -87
Why this flopped: This title is a classic example of “clarity > cleverness.”
It’s not immediately obvious what she means by, “your money is cheating on you.” And if you can’t instantly understand what a video is about, you’ll keep scrolling right past it.
This probably would’ve done better with a clearer title, like, “You’re Losing Money Every Day (thanks to your bank)”.
Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
If you know a fellow creator who needs help with writing better titles, please send them to creatorhooks.com.
Here’s to the war on boring content.
– Jake
(@jthomas__ on Twitter)
P.S. Read previous editions here.
P.P.S. If you want to see all of the videos from Creator Hooks in one place, check out Creator Hooks Pro.

My HONEST Opinion After 6 Months on YouTube

I’ve asked some of the best creators on YouTube, “How do you write such great titles?”
And they’ve all said the same thing: “I just model what works.”
So this email will show you 5 videos worth modeling to help you write great titles and save time.

Chinese Collapse

, 28 days.

Title: The total economic death of China (is happening NOW)
Formula: The [Negative Event] (is happening NOW)
Hook score: +383
Why this works:
Negativity – People can’t get enough of the drama and this title is full of it.
Timeliness – People also love news and “(is happening NOW)” lets the audience know, well, this is happening now.
Curiosity – This title reads like the start of a story, and you can’t help but want to know more.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about a negative event that is happening now.

My HONEST Opinion After 1 Month with an iPhone 14
My HONEST Opinion After 1 Month of CrossFit

🖼️ Thumbnail 4:
| SURVIVED 50 HOURS W/
LIVER KING...

6.1M views + 1 month ago + 884 VPH

Title: I SURVIVED 50 HOURS W/ LIVER KING…
Formula: I SURVIVED (Time Frame) W/ (Authority Figure)…
Hook score: +554
Why this works:
Authority – Liver King’s name and picture makes a ton of people click.
Negativity – “Survived” adds to the feeling that you’re about to watch an intense video with some tough adventures.
Time Frame – The time frame of 50 hours makes this video feel more tangible and interesting.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience how you survived with an authority figure for a specific period of time.

Title: How to LOOK RICH and WEALTHY! Simple tips and tricks to achieve that sophisticated, polished look!
Formula: How to (Achieve Goal)! Simple tips and tricks to (Achieve Specific Goal)!
Hook score: +602
Why this works:
Desire – This creator really nailed down what their audience wants. “How to LOOK RICH and WEALTHY!” is way more specific than just, “How to Look Pretty.” Plus, people love simple tips and tricks.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience how to get their very specific desire.
Examples of this hook in action:

How to Get 10,000 YouTube Subs Per Month! Simple tips to grow your YouTube channel!

Quittin Time

Hook score: +702
Why this works:
Negativity – Quitting something good is a great way to get people’s attention.

Milestone – Your audience will love hearing about big milestones, like quitting a job.

## Newsletter 363: 6 Reasons Most YouTube Channels Suck

**URL:** https://creatorhooks.com/6-reasons-most-youtube-channels-suck/

```
6 Reasons Most YouTube Channels Suck 
December 7, 2022September 19, 2022 by admin 


I’ve asked some of the best creators on YouTube, “How do you write such great titles?”
And they’ve all said the same thing: “I just model what works.”
So this email will show you 5 videos worth modeling to help you write great titles and save time.
Btw, if you want to see all of the titles and thumbnails from Creator Hooks in one place, check out Creator Hooks Pro.
Steppin Into Next Month


🖼️ Thumbnail 2:
20,000 STEPS j
3 “EVERY °Y DAY
FOR A MONTH

| Tried Getting 20,000 Steps a
Day for a Month, Here's Wha...

365K views + 2 months ago * 62 VPH

Title: I Tried Getting 20,000 Steps a Day for a Month, Here’s What Happened
Formula: I Tried (Goal) (Time Frame), Here’s What Happened
​Hook score: +317
Why this works:
Curiosity – “Here’s What Happened” opens a loop and makes you want to click to find out what happened.
Time Frame – “for a Month” makes the video feel more tangible and more relatable/achievable. You can try something for a month, right?
Daily – This video about is about getting 20,000 steps every day, and people love hearing about things they can plug into their daily routine.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about how you tried to do something every day for a month and what happened.
Examples of this hook in action:

I Visited A New State Every Day For A Month, Here’s What We Saw
I Crocheted A New Shirt Every Day For 30 Days, Here’s What Happened

17 Hour Power


🖼️ Thumbnail 3:
Fi7 HOUR =“ SINGaPorE!
M ELAYOVER, (AIRPORT)

17 hours in SINGAPORE
CHANGI AIRPORT

42K views * 1 month ago «

Title: 17 hours in SINGAPORE CHANGI AIRPORT
Formula: (Time Frame) in (Place)
​Hook score: +350
Why this works:
Time Frame – People love time frames because they make videos feel more tangible. You can envision exactly how the video will go, which makes you more likely to click. (Note: just because you can envision how the video will go doesn’t mean you can envision what will happen. There’s still curiosity there that makes you want to watch.)
These next two click triggers are theories, and I may be stretching it a bit…
Curiosity – This video is counterintuitive. Nobody willingly spends 17 hours in an airport — why would you do that? This makes people click.
Negativity – There’s fear here: nobody wants to spend 17 hours in an airport. This also makes you click.
How you can use this hook: Tell the story of how you spent a significant, specific period of time somewhere.
Examples of this hook in action:

24 Hours Banned from Instagram
3 Hours On The Worst Date Ever

Car Loan Clues


🖼️ Thumbnail 4:
The Car Loan Bubble Just
Popped (HUGE Recession...

724K views * 2 months ago + 7 VPH

Title: The Car Loan Bubble Just Popped (HUGE Recession Indicator)
Formula: [Negative Event Just Happened]([Sign Of Future Event])
​Hook score: +593
Why this works:
Timeliness – The word “just” makes this video feel like news, and people love news.
Negativity – This video is all about scary events: recessions and popping bubbles.
Curiosity – Talking about the future builds curiosity, and this title ends by talking about the future.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about a negative event that just happened and what it means for the future.
Examples of this hook in action:

Google Just Announced A New Algorithm Update (HUGE Shift For SEO)
Snook Just Started Leaving The Beaches (Fall Fishing Is Here)

Blurry Blunders


🖼️ Thumbnail 5:
6 Reasons Your Photos are
not SHARP

322K views * 2

Title: 6 Reasons Your Photos are not SHARP
Formula: 6 Reasons (Pain Point)
​Hook score: +1055
Why this works:
List – Lists make a video feel more tangible and add some curiosity.
Negativity – If you’re a photographer, you definitely don’t want your photos to look “not SHARP.” This creator does a great job at grabbing her audience’s attention by calling out a specific pain point.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about the 6 reasons they’re struggling.
Examples of this hook in action:

6 Reasons You’re Broke
6 Reasons Your YouTube Channel Sucks

Tis The Season


🖼️ Thumbnail 6:
2 Products | Sell Every
Christmas To EXPLODE My...

23K views + 2 weeks ago + 309 VPH

Title: 2 Products I Sell Every Christmas To EXPLODE My Etsy Store Sales (Holiday Print on Demand)
Formula: 2 [Activity] Every [Season] To [Achieve Goal] ([Keywords])
​Hook score: +1345
Why this works:
List – Lists make a video feel more tangible and add some curiosity.
Desire – Everyone in this creator’s audience would love to explode their Etsy store sales.
Timeliness – Christmas is just around the corner and Etsy store owners are looking to prep for it.
How you can use this hook: Give your audience tips for nailing an upcoming event.
Examples of this hook in action:

2 Habits I Start Every Holiday Season To AVOID Gaining Weight
3 Amazing Fall Festivals New Residents In LA Will Love (Things to do in Los Angeles)

Flop of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 7:
Can this machine help save
the world?

31K views + 3 weeks ago * 5 VPH

Title: Can this machine help save the world?
Hook score: -80
Why this flopped: Here are some of the other videos on this channel:

We Tried 7 Simple Exercises to Strengthen our Relationship
How to Eat Less Sugar, Realistically
I Worked Out for 700 Days, Here’s How it Changed Me

The audience is here because they want to save themselves, not save the world.
There’s nothing wrong with that, of course.
But as a creator, you need to know why your audience watches your channel and, if you want to avoid having your videos flop, make sure you’re delivering on that.
Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
Have any questions about the hooks in this edition?
Reply to this email and let me know!
And if you know a fellow creator who needs help with writing better titles, please send them to creatorhooks.com.
Here’s to the war on boring content.
– Jake
(@jthomas__ on Twitter)
P.S. Read previous editions here.
P.P.S. If you want to see all of the videos from Creator Hooks in one place, check out Creator Hooks Pro.

This NEW Approach To YouTube Will Change Your Channel Forever!My HONEST Opinion After 6 Months on YouTube

### 🖼️ Images (OCR):

**Image 1:**
URL: https://creatorhooks.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/09/Screen-Shot-2022-09-16-at-10.43.15-PM.png

## Newsletter 364: 6 Reasons Most YouTube Channels Suck

**URL:** https://creatorhooks.com/6-reasons-most-youtube-channels-suck/

```
6 Reasons Most YouTube Channels Suck 
December 7, 2022September 19, 2022 by admin 


I’ve asked some of the best creators on YouTube, “How do you write such great titles?”
And they’ve all said the same thing: “I just model what works.”
So this email will show you 5 videos worth modeling to help you write great titles and save time.
Btw, if you want to see all of the titles and thumbnails from Creator Hooks in one place, check out Creator Hooks Pro.
Steppin Into Next Month


🖼️ Thumbnail 2:
20,000 STEPS j
3 “EVERY °Y DAY
FOR A MONTH

| Tried Getting 20,000 Steps a
Day for a Month, Here's Wha...

365K views + 2 months ago * 62 VPH

Title: I Tried Getting 20,000 Steps a Day for a Month, Here’s What Happened
Formula: I Tried (Goal) (Time Frame), Here’s What Happened
​Hook score: +317
Why this works:
Curiosity – “Here’s What Happened” opens a loop and makes you want to click to find out what happened.
Time Frame – “for a Month” makes the video feel more tangible and more relatable/achievable. You can try something for a month, right?
Daily – This video about is about getting 20,000 steps every day, and people love hearing about things they can plug into their daily routine.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about how you tried to do something every day for a month and what happened.
Examples of this hook in action:

I Visited A New State Every Day For A Month, Here’s What We Saw
I Crocheted A New Shirt Every Day For 30 Days, Here’s What Happened

17 Hour Power


🖼️ Thumbnail 3:
Fi7 HOUR =“ SINGaPorE!
M ELAYOVER, (AIRPORT)

17 hours in SINGAPORE
CHANGI AIRPORT

42K views * 1 month ago «

Title: 17 hours in SINGAPORE CHANGI AIRPORT
Formula: (Time Frame) in (Place)
​Hook score: +350
Why this works:
Time Frame – People love time frames because they make videos feel more tangible. You can envision exactly how the video will go, which makes you more likely to click. (Note: just because you can envision how the video will go doesn’t mean you can envision what will happen. There’s still curiosity there that makes you want to watch.)
These next two click triggers are theories, and I may be stretching it a bit…
Curiosity – This video is counterintuitive. Nobody willingly spends 17 hours in an airport — why would you do that? This makes people click.
Negativity – There’s fear here: nobody wants to spend 17 hours in an airport. This also makes you click.
How you can use this hook: Tell the story of how you spent a significant, specific period of time somewhere.
Examples of this hook in action:

24 Hours Banned from Instagram
3 Hours On The Worst Date Ever

Car Loan Clues


🖼️ Thumbnail 4:
The Car Loan Bubble Just
Popped (HUGE Recession...

724K views * 2 months ago + 7 VPH

Title: The Car Loan Bubble Just Popped (HUGE Recession Indicator)
Formula: [Negative Event Just Happened]([Sign Of Future Event])
​Hook score: +593
Why this works:
Timeliness – The word “just” makes this video feel like news, and people love news.
Negativity – This video is all about scary events: recessions and popping bubbles.
Curiosity – Talking about the future builds curiosity, and this title ends by talking about the future.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about a negative event that just happened and what it means for the future.
Examples of this hook in action:

Google Just Announced A New Algorithm Update (HUGE Shift For SEO)
Snook Just Started Leaving The Beaches (Fall Fishing Is Here)

Blurry Blunders


🖼️ Thumbnail 5:
6 Reasons Your Photos are
not SHARP

322K views * 2

Title: 6 Reasons Your Photos are not SHARP
Formula: 6 Reasons (Pain Point)
​Hook score: +1055
Why this works:
List – Lists make a video feel more tangible and add some curiosity.
Negativity – If you’re a photographer, you definitely don’t want your photos to look “not SHARP.” This creator does a great job at grabbing her audience’s attention by calling out a specific pain point.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about the 6 reasons they’re struggling.
Examples of this hook in action:

6 Reasons You’re Broke
6 Reasons Your YouTube Channel Sucks

Tis The Season


🖼️ Thumbnail 6:
2 Products | Sell Every
Christmas To EXPLODE My...

23K views + 2 weeks ago + 309 VPH

Title: 2 Products I Sell Every Christmas To EXPLODE My Etsy Store Sales (Holiday Print on Demand)
Formula: 2 [Activity] Every [Season] To [Achieve Goal] ([Keywords])
​Hook score: +1345
Why this works:
List – Lists make a video feel more tangible and add some curiosity.
Desire – Everyone in this creator’s audience would love to explode their Etsy store sales.
Timeliness – Christmas is just around the corner and Etsy store owners are looking to prep for it.
How you can use this hook: Give your audience tips for nailing an upcoming event.
Examples of this hook in action:

2 Habits I Start Every Holiday Season To AVOID Gaining Weight
3 Amazing Fall Festivals New Residents In LA Will Love (Things to do in Los Angeles)

Flop of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 7:
Can this machine help save
the world?

31K views + 3 weeks ago * 5 VPH

Title: Can this machine help save the world?
Hook score: -80
Why this flopped: Here are some of the other videos on this channel:

We Tried 7 Simple Exercises to Strengthen our Relationship
How to Eat Less Sugar, Realistically
I Worked Out for 700 Days, Here’s How it Changed Me

The audience is here because they want to save themselves, not save the world.
There’s nothing wrong with that, of course.
But as a creator, you need to know why your audience watches your channel and, if you want to avoid having your videos flop, make sure you’re delivering on that.
Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
Have any questions about the hooks in this edition?
Reply to this email and let me know!
And if you know a fellow creator who needs help with writing better titles, please send them to creatorhooks.com.
Here’s to the war on boring content.
– Jake
(@jthomas__ on Twitter)
P.S. Read previous editions here.
P.P.S. If you want to see all of the videos from Creator Hooks in one place, check out Creator Hooks Pro.

## Newsletter 365: This NEW Approach To YouTube Will Change Your Channel Forever

**URL:** https://creatorhooks.com/this-new-approach-to-youtube-will-change-your-channel-forever/

```
This NEW Approach To YouTube Will Change Your Channel Forever! 
January 4, 2023September 12, 2022 by admin 


I’ve asked some of the best creators on YouTube, “How do you write such great titles?”
And they’ve all said the same thing: “I just model what works.”
So this email will show you 5 videos worth modeling to help you write great titles and save time.
Btw, if you want to see all of the titles and thumbnails from Creator Hooks in one place, check out Creator Hooks Pro.
Mr. Fixer Upper


🖼️ Thumbnail 2:
This Fixes EVERYTHING!

1.1M views + 2 months ago » 320 VPH

Title: This Fixes EVERYTHING!
Formula: This (Activity) Fixes EVERYTHING!
Hook score: +310
Why this works:
Curiosity – This title builds curiosity by not telling you exactly what it fixes. This opens a loop, which builds curiosity and makes you want to click and find out.
Desire – This creator’s audience wants to be more mobile and probably has injuries they’d like to be fixed. The title and thumbnail cater to those desires.
Thumbnail – Since the title is a bit vague, a lot of the success of the video can be attributed to the thumbnail. The shirtless hairy man with his legs spread is a little awkward and very bold, which catches your attention. But he’s also in an intriguing position that works well with the title.
How you can use this hook: You could just use this very same title with a bold thumbnail related to you niche, or you could talk about an activity that fixes everything.
Examples of this hook in action:

This Farming Hack Fixes EVERYTHING!
This iPhone Trick Fixes EVERYTHING!

Hot Hot Habits


🖼️ Thumbnail 3:
3 Feminine Habits Men Find
WILDLY Attractive (Make Hi...

236K views * 3 weeks ago + 453 VPH

Title: 3 Feminine Habits Men Find WILDLY Attractive (Make His Heart RACE with Naturally Seductive Behavior)
Formula: 3 [Contrasting] Habits [Achieve Goal] ([Achieve Goal])
Hook score: +637
Why this works:
Curiosity – There’s contrast between “feminine” and “men” which builds curiosity and makes you do a double take.
List – Lists make videos more tangible and add some curiosity.
Desire – Women would love to know what these habits are that men find attractive.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about 3 habits that can help them achieve their goals. But the real magic here is giving some contrast to those habits to build curiosity.
Examples of this hook in action:

3 Eating Habits To Starve Fat (Get Shredded Fast By Eating)
3 Spending Habits That’ll Save You Money

Blazing Bus


🖼️ Thumbnail 4:
Van Camping in a Heat Wave
111°F

191K views * 1 month ago + 130 VPH

Title: Van Camping in a Heat Wave 111°F
Formula: (Activity) in (Extreme Environment)
Hook score: +1123
Why this works:
Extreme – 111°F increases the stakes in this videos, which makes for a more interesting story.
Negativity – People love watching videos about scary things, like camping in a dangerous heatwave.
How you can use this hook: Do an activity in an extreme environment and let your audience live vicariously through you.
Examples of this hook in action:

Getting a Job in a Recession
Becoming a Crypto Millionaire during the Crypto Crash

HODL


🖼️ Thumbnail 5:
| Bought 96 Stocks, Then Did
Nothing for 816 Days...

78K views + 1 month ago + 9 VPH

Title: I Bought 96 Stocks, Then Did Nothing for 816 Days…
Formula: I (Activity), Then (Activity) for (Time Frame)…
Hook score: +1083
Why this works:
Curiosity – This title opens a loop because you want to know what happens after the 816 days. Also, the thumbnail opens a loop, too, by saying, “I Didn’t Expect This…” but not specifying what “this” is.
Time Frame – Time frames make videos feel more tangible, which makes them more interesting.
How you can use this hook: Tell the story of how you took action, then took subsequent action for a time frame.
Examples of this hook in action:

I Bought This New Fishing Lure, Then Fished for 3 Hours…
I Bought This Mystery Meat, Then Smoked It for 12 Hours…

that new new


🖼️ Thumbnail 6:
TRY THIS
FROM TODAY!
a

This NEW Approach to
Photography Will Change...

299K views * 1 month ago +

Title: This NEW Approach to Photography Will Change Your Career FOREVER!
Formula: This NEW Approach to (Activity) Will Change Your (Possession) FOREVER!
Hook score: +2718
Why this works:
New opportunity – People love new opportunities because they offer hope.
Curiosity – “Change your career forever” is open-ended, so it opens a loop and builds curiosity.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about a new approach that will change their lives forever.
Examples of this hook in action:

This NEW Approach to Real Estate Will Change Your Life FOREVER!
This NEW Approach to YouTube Will Change Your Channel FOREVER!

Flop of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 7:
We ALL SUCK as
BEGINNERS... (The Reality ...

2.8K views * 1 month ago =

Title: We ALL SUCK as BEGINNERS… (The Reality of Becoming a Professional Photographer)
Hook score: -94
Why this flopped: This title used two of my favorite click triggers: negativity and calling out beginners.
So why did it only get 2.8K views, while her video about a new approach to photography got 299K views?
Keywords.
But not in the way most people think about keywords…
“We All Suck As Beginners” is too vague. It needs a keyword to let the audience (not the algorithm) know what her video is about.
It could probably be better worded as “Why We All Suck As Beginner Photographers.”
To add to this confusion, she’s holding a guitar and a camera in her hand. Which one is this video about?
The guitar is obviously much bigger than the camera, so at first glance you might think this video is about sucking as a beginner guitar player, which is not what her channel is about.
To avoid this mistake, be crystal clear on what your video is about and who it’s for, and make sure both of those align with your channel’s audience.
Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
Have any questions about the hooks in this edition?
Reply to this email and let me know!
And if you know a fellow creator who needs help with writing better titles, please send them to creatorhooks.com.
Here’s to the war on boring content.
– Jake
(@jthomas__ on Twitter)
P.S. Read previous editions here.
P.P.S. If you want to see all of the videos from Creator Hooks in one place, check out Creator Hooks Pro.

## Newsletter 367: 3 Bad Habits Every YouTuber Should Give Up

**URL:** https://creatorhooks.com/3-bad-habits-every-youtuber-should-give-up/

```
3 Bad Habits Every YouTuber Should Give Up 
December 9, 2022September 5, 2022 by admin 


I’ve asked some of the best creators on YouTube, “How do you write such great titles?”
And they’ve all said the same thing: “I just model what works.”
So this email will show you 5 videos worth modeling to help you write great titles and save time.
Btw, if you want to see all of the titles and thumbnails from Creator Hooks in one place, check out Creator Hooks Pro.
Tiny Camera Big Changes


🖼️ Thumbnail 2:
This TINY Camera Accessory
Changes EVERYTHING!

242K views * 2 months ago = 7 VPH

Title: This TINY Camera Accessory Changes EVERYTHING!
Formula: This TINY (Object) Changes EVERYTHING!
Hook score: +302
Why this works:
Curiosity – There’s contrast between “tiny object” and “changes everything”, and “Changes EVERYTHING” kinda opens a loop. You wonder, does it really change everything? How?
Extreme – “Changes EVERYTHING” is much more interesting than “changes a lot”
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about the tiny object that changes everything.
Examples of this hook in action:

This TINY Tax Loop Changes EVERYTHING!
This TINY Thumbnail Tweak Changes EVERYTHING!

No Haus


🖼️ Thumbnail 3:
Why Germans Don't Buy
Houses | Feli from Germany

619K views * 2 months ago * 60 VPH

Title: Why Germans Don’t Buy Houses | Feli from Germany
Formula: Why (Group) Don’t (Action)
Hook score: +359
Why this works:
Curiosity – Buying a house is a normal, innocent behavior, so it doesn’t make sense why an entire group of people wouldn’t buy a house.
How you can use this hook: Tell the story of why a group in your niche doesn’t do something seemingly normal or innocent.
Examples of this hook in action:

Why Chefs Don’t Use This Common Ingredient
Why Real Estate Owners Don’t Like Single Family Homes

Kick The Habit


🖼️ Thumbnail 4:
3 Bad Habits Every Poor Man
Should Give Up

65K views * 2 weeks ago * 48 VPH

Title: 3 Bad Habits Every Poor Man Should Give Up
Formula: 3 Bad Habits Every (Specific Type of Person) Should Give Up
Hook score: +531
Why this works:
List – Lists make a video feel more tangible and add some curiosity.
Negativity – There’s a little bit of fear here that makes you want to click and find out if you have these bad habits.
Specific Audience – By calling out poor men specifically in the title, it makes poor men more likely to click.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about the 3 bad habits a specific group of them should give up.
Examples of this hook in action:

3 Bad Habits Every “Influencer” Should Give Up
3 Bad Habits Every Runner Should Give Up

Here Comes 911


🖼️ Thumbnail 5:
Engineer Builds GREATEST
Ambulance Conversion EVER!

204K views * 1 month ago

Title: Engineer Builds GREATEST Ambulance Conversion EVER!
Formula: (Position) Builds GREATEST (Object)
Hook score: +547
Why this works:
Extreme – Here’s another example of how taking it to the extreme makes for a fun video.
How you can use this hook: This is a simple one — tell the story of how somebody built or did the greatest thing ever.
Examples of this hook in action:

Guitar Teacher Builds GREATEST Instrument EVER!
13-Year-Old Builds FASTEST RC Car EVER!

Nomad Life


🖼️ Thumbnail 6:
Stn Bo on Borus
mess 13:54

Best 7 Work From Home
Companies Currently Hiring ...

33 $31/HR

644K views + 1 month ago °

Title: Best 7 Work From Home Companies Currently Hiring | Remote Jobs Worldwide
Formula: Best 7 (Entities) Currently (Action) | (Keywords)
Hook score: +757
Why this works:
List – Lists make a video feel more tangible and add some curiosity.
Urgency – Since this video is about companies currently hiring, there’s a bit of urgency to click now.
Desire – This creator’s audience is looking for jobs at remote companies.
How you can use this hook: Give your audience a list of entities that can help them achieve their goals.
Examples of this hook in action:

7 Best Countries To Work Remotely From In 2023 | Digital Nomad Life
Best 7 Vegetables I’m Currently Growing | Fall Vegetables

Flop of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 7:
Hilarious Fails & Bloopers
from "How German Sounds...

20K views * 3 months ago

Title: Hilarious Fails & Bloopers from “How German Sounds Compared To Other Languages” 😂
Hook score: -92
Why this flopped: This video probably flopped for a few reasons.
First, this doesn’t really fit with the other videos on this channel. The other videos are all about differences between America and Germany or her experiences as a German in America.
Second, you kinda have to have seen “How German Sounds Compared To Other Languages” to really appreciate this. This narrows down the total potential audience.
Third, all of the thumbnails have her face on it, and many have the American or German flag. This doesn’t have either of the flags and it kinda has her face on it… but the mustache and wig make her less recognizable.
Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
Have any questions about the hooks in this edition?
Reply to this email and let me know!
And if you know a fellow creator who needs help with writing better titles, please send them to creatorhooks.com.
Here’s to the war on boring content.
– Jake
(@jthomas__ on Twitter)
P.S. Read previous editions here.
P.P.S. If you want to see all of the videos from Creator Hooks in one place, check out Creator Hooks Pro.

## Newsletter 369: 6 Big Decisions YouTubers Almost Always Regret

**URL:** https://creatorhooks.com/6-big-decisions-youtubers-almost-always-regret/

```
6 Big Decisions YouTubers (Almost) Always Regret 
December 8, 2022August 29, 2022 by admin 


I’ve asked some of the best creators on YouTube, “How do you write such great titles?”
And they’ve all said the same thing: “I just model what works.”
So this email will show you 5 videos worth modeling to help you write great titles and save time.
Btw, if you want to see all of the titles and thumbnails from Creator Hooks in one place, check out Creator Hooks Pro.
Daily Dumbbells


🖼️ Thumbnail 2:
DO THIS
EVERY DAY

6 Things | Do Every Day to
Stay in Shape

331K views + 1 month ago + 128 VPH


Title: 6 Things I Do Every Day to Stay in Shape
Formula: 6 Things I Do Every Day to (Achieve Goal)
​Hook score: +367
Why this works:
List – Lists make videos feel more tangible and they add some curiosity.
Daily Tips – People like things you can do every day because they’re easy to plug into your routine. You don’t have to worry about when or how often to do them.
Desire – Everyone in this audience wants tips to stay in shape.
How you can use this hook: Give your audience a list of things you do every day to achieve the goal they want to achieve.
Examples of this hook in action:

6 Things I Do Every Day to Be A Great Mom
6 Things I Do Every Day to Grow My YouTube Channel

Retiree Regrets


🖼️ Thumbnail 3:
6 BIG Purchases Retirees
(Almost) Always Regret!

501K views + 13 days ago + 612 VPH

Title: 6 BIG Purchases Retirees (Almost) Always Regret!
Formula: 6 BIG [Activities] [Specific Audience] (Almost) Always Regret!
​Hook score: +770
Why this works:
List – Lists make videos feel more tangible and they add some curiosity.
Negativity – Our culture is obsessed with regrets, so that word catches people’s attention and gets them to click.
Specific Audience – By calling out retirees specifically in the title, retirees are way more likely to click.
How you can use this hook: Call out a specific segment of your audience and tell them about the decisions or activities they’re likely to regret.
Examples of this hook in action:

6 Vegetables Gardeners (Almost) Always Regret Planting!
6 BIG Decisions Married Men (Almost) Always Regret!

That’s So Fetch


🖼️ Thumbnail 4:
Raised Garden Beds Are So
Yesterday!

647K views * 4 weeks ago * 1K VPH

Title: Raised Garden Beds Are So Yesterday!
Formula: (Trend) Are So Yesterday!
​Hook score: +1012
Why this works:
Negativity – If you currently have a raised garden bed, you probably have a little FOMO because you’re doing something that’s no longer the best.
Curiosity – If raised garden beds are so yesterday, what’s the best trend to follow today?
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about the trends or tools that are so yesterday.
Examples of this hook in action:

Instagram Hashtags Are So Yesterday!
Keto Diet Is So Yesterday!

Pizza Time


🖼️ Thumbnail 5:
a

Eating The World's Best Pizza
in ITALY

483K views * 2 months ago + 43 VPH

Title: Eating The World’s Best Pizza in ITALY
Formula: (Activity) The World’s Best (Object) in (Place)
​Hook score: +1061
Why this works:
Epic – “The world’s best pizza” is so much cooler than “really good pizza”.
Desire – The title and thumbnail are simple, but work great together to get you hungry and wanting to see more of this pizza.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about something you did that took it to the extreme. Think of things that are the best, hardest, fastest, slowest, longest, tallest, loudest, etc.
Examples of this hook in action:

Running America’s Hardest 5K in Colorado
I Tested The World’s Fastest Computer in Japan

Tiny Home, Big Views


🖼️ Thumbnail 6:
Building My Dream Tiny
Home For Under $8000

3.9M views + 2 months ago «

Title: Building My Dream Tiny Home For Under $8000
Formula: Building My (Ultimate Goal) For/With (Constraint)
​Hook score: +14338 (no, this isn’t a typo, this channel’s recent videos averaged 27K views before this monster)
Why this works:
Desire – This creator’s audience would love to build their dream tiny home.
Constraint – The fact that he built his dream tiny home for such a small amount of money makes for a great and intriguing story.
How you can use this hook: Do something epic with a constraint.
Examples of this hook in action:

Building My Dream Photography Studio For Under $1,000
Building My Dream Aquarium For Under $300

Flop of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 7:
Easy Tips for Conquering
Your Fear of Flying

14K views + 4 months ago +

Title: Easy Tips for Conquering Your Fear of Flying
Hook score: -89
Why this flopped: I think this video flopped for a few reasons.
First, this channel is about going on crazy travel adventures across the world.
Tips for conquering your fear of flying doesn’t really fit with what her audience is looking for from her channel.
Second, how many people who love traveling across the world also have a fear of flying?
According to the low amount of views this video got compared to the rest on her channel, it seems like not that many.
And third, most of her thumbnails are of beautiful, brightly-colored destinations, not dark plane seats.
This video may have served a specific purpose and helped 14,000 people in her audience, which is great.
But if you’re looking to maximize views, be sure to pick a topic that a large segment of your audience wants to hear about.
Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
If you know a fellow creator who needs help with writing better titles, please send them to creatorhooks.com.
Here’s to the war on boring content.
– Jake
(@jthomas__ on Twitter)
P.S. Read previous editions here.
P.P.S. If you want to see all of the videos from Creator Hooks in one place, check out Creator Hooks Pro.
Read last week’s edition »

Why this works:
Epic – “The world’s best pizza” is so much cooler than “really good pizza”.
Desire – The title and thumbnail are simple, but work great together to get you hungry and wanting to see more of this pizza.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about something you did that took it to the extreme. Think of things that are the best, hardest, fastest, slowest, longest, tallest, loudest, etc.
Examples of this hook in action:

Running America’s Hardest 5K in Colorado
I Tested The World’s Fastest Computer in Japan

Tiny Home, Big Views


🖼️ Thumbnail 6:
Building My Dream Tiny
Home For Under $8000

3.9M views + 2 months ago «

Title: Building My Dream Tiny Home For Under $8000
Formula: Building My (Ultimate Goal) For/With (Constraint)
​Hook score: +14338 (no, this isn’t a typo, this channel’s recent videos averaged 27K views before this monster)
Why this works:
Desire – This creator’s audience would love to build their dream tiny home.
Constraint – The fact that he built his dream tiny home for such a small amount of money makes for a great and intriguing story.
How you can use this hook: Do something epic with a constraint.
Examples of this hook in action:

Building My Dream Photography Studio For Under $1,000
Building My Dream Aquarium For Under $300

Flop of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 7:
Easy Tips for Conquering
Your Fear of Flying

14K views + 4 months ago +

Title: Easy Tips for Conquering Your Fear of Flying
Hook score: -89
Why this flopped: I think this video flopped for a few reasons.
First, this channel is about going on crazy travel adventures across the world.
Tips for conquering your fear of flying doesn’t really fit with what her audience is looking for from her channel.
Second, how many people who love traveling across the world also have a fear of flying?
According to the low amount of views this video got compared to the rest on her channel, it seems like not that many.
And third, most of her thumbnails are of beautiful, brightly-colored destinations, not dark plane seats.
This video may have served a specific purpose and helped 14,000 people in her audience, which is great.
But if you’re looking to maximize views, be sure to pick a topic that a large segment of your audience wants to hear about.
Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
If you know a fellow creator who needs help with writing better titles, please send them to creatorhooks.com.
Here’s to the war on boring content.
– Jake
(@jthomas__ on Twitter)
P.S. Read previous editions here.
P.P.S. If you want to see all of the videos from Creator Hooks in one place, check out Creator Hooks Pro.
