## Newsletter 371: 7 Things Pro YouTubers Do That You Probably Dont

**URL:** https://creatorhooks.com/7-things-pro-youtubers-do-that-you-probably-dont/

```
7 Things Pro YouTubers Do That You Probably Don’t 
December 8, 2022August 22, 2022 by admin 


I’ve asked some of the best creators on YouTube, “How do you write such great titles?”
And they’ve all said the same thing: “I just model what works.”
So this email will show you 5 videos worth modeling to help you write great titles and save time.
Btw, if you want to see all of the titles and thumbnails from Creator Hooks in one place, check out Creator Hooks Pro.
Just One Thing…


🖼️ Thumbnail 2:
Tmonth

$22 Ultra Revits4:21}

Galaxy $22 ULTRA Review |
The Best Phone EXCEPT... (...

915K views * 4 months ago * 233 VPH

Title: Galaxy S22 ULTRA Review | The Best Phone EXCEPT… (1 month later)
Formula: (Product) Review | The Best (Product Category) EXCEPT… (1 month later)
​Hook score: +514
Why this works:
Curiosity – “EXCEPT” opens a loop and makes you wonder what the exception is.
Negativity – Her face in the thumbnail and the “EXCEPT…” make you feel like there’s something bad about the phone.
Time Frame – Trying the phone for 1 month gives more credibility for this video.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about a tool that is the best, except for one little thing.
Examples of this hook in action:

Slam Shady Review | The Best Paddletail EXCEPT… (5 Trips later)
Bench Press: The Best Chest Exercise EXCEPT… (3 Month Test)

Scenic Views


🖼️ Thumbnail 3:
82hrs on Amtrak's MOST
SCENIC TRAIN! - The...

1M views * 2 months ago +

Title: 82hrs on Amtrak’s MOST SCENIC TRAIN! – The California Zephyr
Formula: (Time) on (Entity)’s Most (Adjective) (Object) – (Object Name)
​Hook score: +909
Why this works:
Extreme – “Most Scenic Train” is much more interesting than “One of Amtrak’s Most Scenic Trains.”
Time Frame – The “82hrs” makes this video feel more tangible and gives it credibility because it’s not just a walkthrough tour — they spent a long time on the train.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience how you spent a specific amount of time in or at an extreme place.
Examples of this hook in action:

24hrs on Reddit Home Page – Our Business Exploded
3-Day Shoot In America’s Prettiest National Park – Photographing Zion

That’s One Way To Look At It…


🖼️ Thumbnail 4:
Top 10 Poorest Counties in
the US. (Poverty=Cheap Re...

535K views * 2 weeks ago * 9175 VPH

Title: Top 10 Poorest Counties in the US. (Poverty=Cheap Real Estate)
Formula: Top 10 [Negative Trait] in [Area or Category] ([Negative Trait]=[Benefit To Audience])
​Hook score: +912
Why this works:
List – Lists make videos feel tangible and add some curiosity.
Negativity – People are drawn to negative lists, like this one. When you look at this channel you see some of their highest-viewed videos are negative lists, like “10 Countries People Hate To Visit” and “Nevada’s Worst Rural Towns”.
Desire – At the very end they slip in a benefit for real estate folks: “Poverty=Cheap Real Estate”
How you can use this hook: Make a list of negative things in your industry, but include a little benefit for the audience at the end.
Examples of this hook in action:

Top 10 Hardest Jobs in America (Hard Jobs=Tough Employees)
10 Most Competitive Ecom Niches (Competitive=Lots Of Potential Income)

Plant Daddy


🖼️ Thumbnail 5:
@ «
WV

7 Things Plant Experts Do
That You Probably Don't

131K views : 2 weeks ago * 459 VPH

Title: 7 Things Plant Experts Do That You Probably Don’t
Formula: 7 Things (Niche) Experts Do That You Probably Don’t
​Hook score: +3589
Why this works:
List – Lists make videos feel tangible and add some curiosity.
Authority – “Plant Experts” gives this video credibility.
Curiosity – This feels like you’re getting some insider information, which builds curiosity.
Negativity – “That You Probably Don’t” adds some FOMO to this title.
How you can use this hook: Make a list of things experts in your industry do that your audience doesn’t.
Examples of this hook in action:

7 Things Professional YouTubers Do That You Probably Don’t
7 Things Real Estate Experts Do That You Probably Don’t

So It Begins


🖼️ Thumbnail 6:
Index Fund Investing Could
Change Forever

115K views * 3 weeks ago * 307 VPH

Title: Index Fund Investing Could Change Forever
Formula: (Industry) Could Change Forever
​Hook score: +3412
Why this works:
Curiosity – “Could Change Forever” builds curiosity two ways. First, it opens a loop and makes you wonder, “What will change? How will it change?” And second, it’s about the future, and people are inherently curious about the future.
Negativity – The thumbnail is full of fear — the word, “worried,” and the creator with a worried expression — both of which help to grab your attention.
How you can use this hook: This is simple: tell your audience that your industry could change forever.
Examples of this hook in action:

Online Dating Could Change Forever
Public Schools Could Change Forever

Flop of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 7:
Unboxing My New Monitor! |
Apple Studio Display First...

30K views + 2 months ago

Title: Unboxing My New Monitor! | Apple Studio Display First Impressions
Hook score: -89
Why this flopped: Most of the videos from this channel are about new laptops and phones.
New laptops and phones are sexy.
Are new monitors sexy? I don’t think so.
Plus, almost everybody has a laptop and phone. Not everybody has a monitor.
Lesson: if you want your videos to get lots of views, pick a sexy topic that has a big potential audience.
Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
If you know a fellow creator who needs help with writing better titles, please send them to creatorhooks.com.
– Jake
P.S. You can see past newsletter editions here.
P.P.S. If you want to see all of the thumbnails and titles from Creator Hooks in one place, check out Creator Hooks Pro.

535K views * 2 weeks ago * 9175 VPH

Title: Top 10 Poorest Counties in the US. (Poverty=Cheap Real Estate)
Formula: Top 10 [Negative Trait] in [Area or Category] ([Negative Trait]=[Benefit To Audience])
​Hook score: +912

Negativity – People are drawn to negative lists, like this one. When you look at this channel you see some of their highest-viewed videos are negative lists, like “10 Countries People Hate To Visit” and “Nevada’s Worst Rural Towns”.

## Newsletter 373: Why The YouTube Algorithm Doesnt Like You

**URL:** https://creatorhooks.com/why-the-youtube-algorithm-doesnt-like-you/

```
Why The YouTube Algorithm Doesn’t Like You 
December 8, 2022August 15, 2022 by admin 


I’ve asked some of the best creators on YouTube, “How do you write such great titles?”
And they’ve all said the same thing: “I just model what works.”
So this email will show you 5 videos worth modeling to help you write great titles and save time.
Btw, if you want to see all of the titles and thumbnails from Creator Hooks in one place, check out Creator Hooks Pro.
Dark Spot Delete


🖼️ Thumbnail 2:
eo rs rN
am ,
How to remove dark spots,

dark patches and...

57K views * 8 days ago + 124 VPH

Title: How to remove dark spots, dark patches and hyperpigmentation on your face and body!
Formula: How to remove (Unwanted Trait or Possession)
Hook score: +314
Why this works:
Negativity – This video is all about removing unwanted traits. Pressing on these pain points gets the audience’s attention and makes them click.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience how they can remove their pain points or unwanted traits.
Examples of this hook in action:

How to remove brown spots from your lawn
How to remove the awkwardness on a first date

Money Meltdown


🖼️ Thumbnail 3:
39)
What a Recession Could
Mean For The Commercial...

9.9K views * 1 month ago

Title: What a Recession Could Mean For The Commercial Real Estate Market
Formula: What a (Negative Event) Could Mean For The (Industry)
Hook score: +380
Why this works:
Curiosity – This title builds curiosity two ways. First, it opens a loop by not answering what a recession could mean in the title, so you have to click and find out. And second, it’s talking about the future. The future is obviously unknown, so it builds curiosity.
Negativity – Negative events, like recessions, get a lot of attention and clicks.
Timeliness – Everybody is talking about a recession right now and this creator uses that to his advantage by riding that wave.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about what a big, negative event could mean for them.
Examples of this hook in action:

What a Recession Could Mean For Teachers
What Global Warming Could Mean For Coastal Cities

Study Secrets


🖼️ Thumbnail 4:
my secret
ormula

"=f"

Wb.
How | Routinely Study With a
Full Time Job when I'm TIRED

270K views + 1 month ago : 44 VPH

Title: How I Routinely Study With a Full Time Job when I’m TIRED
Formula: How I (Activity) With (Constraints)
Hook score: +397
Why this works:
Constraint – Studying with the constraint of a full time job makes this video way more interesting. It introduces conflict, and conflict makes for a good story. Plus, many people in this creator’s audience are struggling with trying to study with a full time job, so they relate to this title.
Refute objection – “With a full time job” and “when I’m tired” are both objections that people may have when they’re trying to study. When you get rid of people’s excuses in your title, they have no excuse to not watch your video.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience how you do something that they want to do, even with all of their potential excuses.
Examples of this hook in action:

How I Consistently Work Out With a Full Time Job & Kids
How He Invests In His 401K Every Month With a Low-Paying Job

Holey Cart


🖼️ Thumbnail 5:
Why Shopping Carts Have
Holes + 15 Things You Neve...

321K views * 12 days ago ° 7.

Title: Why Shopping Carts Have Holes + 15 Things You Never Thought About
Formula: Why (Common Item) Has (Trait) + 15 Things You Never Thought About
Hook score: +642
Why this works:
Curiosity – This title makes you feel like you’re getting in on a weird secret, and the “things you never thought about” enhances that feeling.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about why something is the way it is in your industry.
Examples of this hook in action:

Why Tomatoes Don’t Grow In The Fall + 15 Things you Never Thought About
Why Bitcoin Took So Long To Become Mainstream

Mandalorian Jesus


🖼️ Thumbnail 6:
How a Church Uses
Mandalorian Tech | RED...

139K views + 2 weeks ago » 19 VPH

Title: How a Church Uses Mandalorian Tech | RED KOMODO and Unreal Engine Setup
Formula: How (Entity) uses (Cool, Futuristic, and/or Trending Tool) | (Keywords)
Hook score: +772
Why this works:
Curiosity – The contrast between “church” (which feels non-techie) and “Mandalorian tech” (which feels futuristic) builds a ton of curiosity.
Authority – “Mandalorian Tech” is an amazing way to describe tech. And it’s authoritative because, well, we’re talking about The Mandalorian.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about how a person or organization uses a new tool, and relate that tool to something futuristic or an expert (or both).
Examples of this hook in action:

How a Real Estate Company uses Wakanda Tech | Due Diligence Secrets
How a Faceless Channel used AI To Get 1,000,000 Subscribers

Flop of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 7:
A Productive Day in the Life
at Cajun Koi Academy...

9K views * 3 weeks ago

Title: A Productive Day in the Life at Cajun Koi Academy (Kuma
Hook score: -89
Why this flopped: This title feels too self-promotional.
It feels like it’s all about the creator and his academy, rather than about the audience.
I’m sure it made lots of money, but it might’ve been better framed as something like, “A Productive Day in the Life of a Study Expert” (his channel is about studying).
Framing the video this way makes it feel like he’s aligning with the audience rather than promoting his academy.
Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
Have any questions about the hooks in this edition?
Reply to this email and let me know!
And if you know a fellow creator who needs help with writing better titles, please send them to creatorhooks.com.
– Jake
P.S. Follow me on Twitter for more title tips @jthomas__​
P.P.S. If you want to see all of the videos from Creator Hooks in one place, check out Creator Hooks Pro.
Read last week’s edition »

dark patches and...

57K views * 8 days ago + 124 VPH

Mean For The Commercial...

## Newsletter 374: Why The YouTube Algorithm Doesnt Like You

**URL:** https://creatorhooks.com/why-the-youtube-algorithm-doesnt-like-you/

```
Why The YouTube Algorithm Doesn’t Like You 
December 8, 2022August 15, 2022 by admin 


I’ve asked some of the best creators on YouTube, “How do you write such great titles?”
And they’ve all said the same thing: “I just model what works.”
So this email will show you 5 videos worth modeling to help you write great titles and save time.
Btw, if you want to see all of the titles and thumbnails from Creator Hooks in one place, check out Creator Hooks Pro.
Dark Spot Delete


🖼️ Thumbnail 2:
eo rs rN
am ,
How to remove dark spots,

dark patches and...

57K views * 8 days ago + 124 VPH

Title: How to remove dark spots, dark patches and hyperpigmentation on your face and body!
Formula: How to remove (Unwanted Trait or Possession)
Hook score: +314
Why this works:
Negativity – This video is all about removing unwanted traits. Pressing on these pain points gets the audience’s attention and makes them click.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience how they can remove their pain points or unwanted traits.
Examples of this hook in action:

How to remove brown spots from your lawn
How to remove the awkwardness on a first date

Money Meltdown


🖼️ Thumbnail 3:
39)
What a Recession Could
Mean For The Commercial...

9.9K views * 1 month ago

Title: What a Recession Could Mean For The Commercial Real Estate Market
Formula: What a (Negative Event) Could Mean For The (Industry)
Hook score: +380
Why this works:
Curiosity – This title builds curiosity two ways. First, it opens a loop by not answering what a recession could mean in the title, so you have to click and find out. And second, it’s talking about the future. The future is obviously unknown, so it builds curiosity.
Negativity – Negative events, like recessions, get a lot of attention and clicks.
Timeliness – Everybody is talking about a recession right now and this creator uses that to his advantage by riding that wave.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about what a big, negative event could mean for them.
Examples of this hook in action:

What a Recession Could Mean For Teachers
What Global Warming Could Mean For Coastal Cities

Study Secrets


🖼️ Thumbnail 4:
my secret
ormula

"=f"

Wb.
How | Routinely Study With a
Full Time Job when I'm TIRED

270K views + 1 month ago : 44 VPH

Title: How I Routinely Study With a Full Time Job when I’m TIRED
Formula: How I (Activity) With (Constraints)
Hook score: +397
Why this works:
Constraint – Studying with the constraint of a full time job makes this video way more interesting. It introduces conflict, and conflict makes for a good story. Plus, many people in this creator’s audience are struggling with trying to study with a full time job, so they relate to this title.
Refute objection – “With a full time job” and “when I’m tired” are both objections that people may have when they’re trying to study. When you get rid of people’s excuses in your title, they have no excuse to not watch your video.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience how you do something that they want to do, even with all of their potential excuses.
Examples of this hook in action:

How I Consistently Work Out With a Full Time Job & Kids
How He Invests In His 401K Every Month With a Low-Paying Job

Holey Cart


🖼️ Thumbnail 5:
Why Shopping Carts Have
Holes + 15 Things You Neve...

321K views * 12 days ago ° 7.

Title: Why Shopping Carts Have Holes + 15 Things You Never Thought About
Formula: Why (Common Item) Has (Trait) + 15 Things You Never Thought About
Hook score: +642
Why this works:
Curiosity – This title makes you feel like you’re getting in on a weird secret, and the “things you never thought about” enhances that feeling.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about why something is the way it is in your industry.
Examples of this hook in action:

Why Tomatoes Don’t Grow In The Fall + 15 Things you Never Thought About
Why Bitcoin Took So Long To Become Mainstream

Mandalorian Jesus


🖼️ Thumbnail 6:
How a Church Uses
Mandalorian Tech | RED...

139K views + 2 weeks ago » 19 VPH

Title: How a Church Uses Mandalorian Tech | RED KOMODO and Unreal Engine Setup
Formula: How (Entity) uses (Cool, Futuristic, and/or Trending Tool) | (Keywords)
Hook score: +772
Why this works:
Curiosity – The contrast between “church” (which feels non-techie) and “Mandalorian tech” (which feels futuristic) builds a ton of curiosity.
Authority – “Mandalorian Tech” is an amazing way to describe tech. And it’s authoritative because, well, we’re talking about The Mandalorian.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about how a person or organization uses a new tool, and relate that tool to something futuristic or an expert (or both).
Examples of this hook in action:

How a Real Estate Company uses Wakanda Tech | Due Diligence Secrets
How a Faceless Channel used AI To Get 1,000,000 Subscribers

Flop of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 7:
A Productive Day in the Life
at Cajun Koi Academy...

9K views * 3 weeks ago

Title: A Productive Day in the Life at Cajun Koi Academy (Kuma
Hook score: -89
Why this flopped: This title feels too self-promotional.
It feels like it’s all about the creator and his academy, rather than about the audience.
I’m sure it made lots of money, but it might’ve been better framed as something like, “A Productive Day in the Life of a Study Expert” (his channel is about studying).
Framing the video this way makes it feel like he’s aligning with the audience rather than promoting his academy.
Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
Have any questions about the hooks in this edition?
Reply to this email and let me know!
And if you know a fellow creator who needs help with writing better titles, please send them to creatorhooks.com.
– Jake
P.S. Follow me on Twitter for more title tips @jthomas__​
P.P.S. If you want to see all of the videos from Creator Hooks in one place, check out Creator Hooks Pro.

## Newsletter 375: Do NOT Ask People To Subscribe on YouTube Heres why

**URL:** https://creatorhooks.com/do-not-ask-people-to-subscribe-on-youtube-heres-why/

```
Do NOT Ask People To Subscribe on YouTube! (Here’s why) 
December 8, 2022August 8, 2022 by admin 


I’ve asked some of the best creators on YouTube, “How do you write such great titles?”
And they’ve all said the same thing: “I just model what works.”
So this email will show you 5 videos worth modeling to help you write great titles and save time.
Btw, if you want to see all of the titles and thumbnails from Creator Hooks in one place, check out Creator Hooks Pro.
Every Day I’m Hustlin


🖼️ Thumbnail 2:
We Ran Every Day for 30
Days, Here's What Happened

613K views + 8 days ago

Title: We Ran Every Day for 30 Days, Here’s What Happened
Formula: We (Activity) Every Day for (Number) Days, Here’s What Happened
Hook score:  +519
Why this works:
Curiosity – This builds curiosity by opening a loop with “here’s what happened,” and not telling you what happened.
Time Frame – Challenges with a time frame are more interesting and make the video feel more tangible.
Daily – People love to watch videos about things to do every day.
How you can use this hook: Do a 30-day daily challenge, then tell your audience what happened.
Examples of this hook in action:

We Did Date Night Every Night for 30 Days, Here’s What Happened
I Sent 100 Cold Emails Every Day for 30 Days, Here’s What Happened

Why Not?


🖼️ Thumbnail 3:
Do NOT Shut Down Your
Computer! (here's why)

1.2M views + 1 month ago «

Title: Do NOT Shut Down Your Computer! (here’s why)
Formula: Do NOT [Innocent, Common Action] (here’s why)
Hook score: +916
Why this works:
Negativity – This title is a warning for you to not shut down your computer.
Curiosity – Shutting down your computer feels like an innocent thing to do, so this title builds curiosity because it doesn’t really make sense. It’s counterintuitive.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience to not do something that seems innocent, harmless, or is even a best practice.
Examples of this hook in action:

Do NOT Sell Your Bitcoin Right Now (here’s why)
Do NOT Ask People To Subscribe (here’s why)

Living Illegal


🖼️ Thumbnail 4:
German Neighborhoods are
ILLEGAL IN AMERICA |...

467K views - 2 weeks ago - 322 VPH

Title: German Neighborhoods are ILLEGAL IN AMERICA | Zoning & NIMBY-ism
Formula: (Entity) are ILLEGAL IN (Place) | (Keywords)
Hook score: +993
Why this works:
Negativity – “Illegal” catches your eye and makes this video more interesting.
Curiosity – Like the title above, this builds curiosity because it’s a drama-filled title about a seemingly innocent thing.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about something seemingly innocent that’s actually illegal.
Examples of this hook in action:

Boats are ILLEGAL In This Water | No Boating Zone Lagoon
Airbnbs are ILLEGAL IN THIS STATE | Short-Term Rental Mistakes

Anti Packing List


🖼️ Thumbnail 5:
THINGS | NEVER TRAVEL WITH

to pack Instead)

Things | Never Pack Travel
2022

144K views « 3 months ago «

Title: Things I Never Pack Travel 2022
Formula: (Objects) I Never (Action) (Current Year)
Hook score: +1870
Why this works:
Negativity – This is about things she never packs, not things she packs. We’re drawn to warnings like this.
Timeliness – Slapping the current year on the end of the title is a great way to tell your audience that this video is relevant right now.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about the items you never use.
Examples of this hook in action:

Banks I’d Never Use in 2022
Foods I Never Eat in 2022

Digital Dollars


🖼️ Thumbnail 6:
12 digital products that can
make AT LEAST $10k/month

64K views + 1 month ago

Title: 12 digital products that can make AT LEAST $10k/month
Formula: 12 (Objects) that can (Achieve Goal)
Hook score: +2030
Why this works:
Desire – Everybody in this creator’s audience would love to have a product that earns $10K/month.
List – Lists build curiosity and make a video feel more tangible.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about a list of things that can help them achieve their goals.
Examples of this hook in action:

10 Cameras that can take BEAUTIFUL Outdoor Photos
12 Job Boards that may have your DREAM JOB

Flop of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 7:
My Studio Tour 2022! Where
we film & tools we use

27K views + 1 month ago + 4 VPH

Title: My Studio Tour 2022! Where we film & tools we use
Hook score: -89
Why this flopped: This creator’s audience watches her channel for Excel and technology tips that they can use, not for a tour of the studio where she films.
Plus, most of her thumbnails have her face nice and big on the thumbnail, not a zoomed out shot of her on a couch where you can’t really tell if it’s her or not.
Both the topic and thumbnail style were new. If you’re going to branch out on one of these, at least use what’s proven for the other.
Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
Have any questions about the hooks in this edition?
Reply to this email and let me know!
And if you know a fellow creator who needs help with writing better titles, please send them to creatorhooks.com.
– Jake
P.S. Follow me on Twitter for more title tips @jthomas__​
P.P.S. If you want to see all of the videos from Creator Hooks in one place, check out Creator Hooks Pro.
Read last week’s edition »

## Newsletter 377: 10 Simple Habits To Grow Your YouTube Channel

**URL:** https://creatorhooks.com/10-simple-habits-to-grow-your-youtube-channel/

```
10 Simple Habits To Grow Your YouTube Channel 
December 8, 2022August 1, 2022 by admin 


I’ve asked some of the best creators on YouTube, “How do you write such great titles?”
And they’ve all said the same thing: “I just model what works.”
So this email will show you 5 videos worth modeling to help you write great titles and save time.
Btw, if you want to see all 300+ titles and thumbnails from Creator Hooks in one place, check out Creator Hooks Pro.
No Debt Free For Me


🖼️ Thumbnail 2:
STOP Paying Off Debt! |
Unpopular Money Opinions...

16K views + 1 month ago -

Title: STOP Paying Off Debt! | Unpopular Money Opinions 2022
Formula: STOP (Positive Action) | Unpopular (Industry) Opinions 2022
Hook score: +320
Why this works:
Negativity – This video about stopping doing something beneficial.
Curiosity – Stopping paying off debt is counterintuitive, so it builds lots of curiosity.
Timeliness – This creator lets you know this video is relevant right now by using the current year at the end of the title.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience to stop doing something that is commonly considered a best practice, but that you know is actually not an optimal decision for them.
Examples of this hook in action:

STOP Doing Situps | Unpopular Fitness Opinions 2022
STOP Using Apple’s Email App | Unpopular Tech Opinions 2022

Beary Long


🖼️ Thumbnail 3:
How Long Are We Going To
Be In A Bear Market?

170K views + 3 weeks ago + 21 VPH

Title: How Long Are We Going To Be In A Bear Market?
Formula: How Long (Will Bad Thing Last)?
Hook score: +361
Why this works:
Curiosity – This title builds curiosity by asking a question.
Negativity – This title uses negativity because it’s talking about a negative subject — bear markets.
Timeliness – Since we’re currently in a bear market, this title joins the conversation in everyone’s mind.
How you can use this hook: Ask your audience how long something bad that they’re currently going through will last.
Examples of this hook in action:

How Long Will Gas Be Over $5?
How Long Do Beginner Guitar Players Suck?

Keep It Simple


🖼️ Thumbnail 4:
10 Simple Exercises To
Improve Your Running

163K views - 2 weeks ago -

Title: 10 Simple Exercises To Improve Your Running
Formula: 10 Simple (Activities) To (Achieve Goal)
Hook score: +388
Why this works:
List – Lists add curiosity and make videos feel more tangible.
Desire – Every runner wants to improve their running, and they’d also love to do it with simple exercises.
Time Frame – The thumbnail includes a short, 10-minute time frame, which makes improving your running feel more achievable.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about simple activities they can do to improve what they’re working on.
Examples of this hook in action:

10 Simple YouTube Tips To Reach 100,000 Subscribers
10 Simple Lunches For Back To School Time

I’m Feeling Seen


🖼️ Thumbnail 5:
ORGANIZED PEOPLE
DON 1DOMHIS

5 THINGS ORGANIZED
PEOPLE DO (That You Dont...

542K views + 1 month ago + 208 VPH

Title: 5 THINGS ORGANIZED PEOPLE DO (That You Don’t 😱)
Formula: 5 Things (Desired Trait) People Do (That You Don’t 😱)
Hook score: +410
Why this works:
Authority – Organized people have credibility here and this creator’s audience would love to be more like them.
List – Lists add curiosity and make videos feel more tangible.
Negativity – Finishing this title with “(That You Don’t)” gives you a little fomo.
Curiosity – The thumbnail builds curiosity by opening up a loop.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about things that people they want to be like do.
Examples of this hook in action:

5 Things Rich House Flippers Do (That You Don’t)
5 Things Happy People Do (That You Don’t 😔)

R-E-S-P-E-C-T


🖼️ Thumbnail 6:
MAKE
PEOPLE
ALWAYS

your

How to Instantly Make
Anyone Respect You

115K views - 3 weeks ago -

Title: How to Instantly Make Anyone Respect You
Formula: How to Instantly (Achieve Goal)
Hook score: +727
Why this works:
Desire – Not only is this video about something this audience wants (respect), but it also promises to help them get it instantly.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about how to achieve their goals instantly.
Examples of this hook in action:

How to Instantly Have A Happier Marriage
How to Instantly Get Better At Journaling

Flop of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 7:
How This Pro Runner
Handles Race Anxiety

10K views + 2 months ago «

Title: How This Pro Runner Handles Race Anxiety
Hook score: -75
Why this flopped: This title has some good things going for it — authority and a pain point. So why did it flop?
I think it’s because the pain point is only shared by a few people. Or maybe most people experience it so few times that by the time they see this video on their home page, they don’t care or have forgotten about it.
Contrast this title with something like, “How This Pro Runner Handles Getting Tired While Running.”
Everyone experiences this pain point, and they experience it every time they go for a run, so it’d probably get more views.
The next time you make a video about a pain point, make sure that there’s enough demand for the video.
Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
Have any questions about the hooks in this edition?
Reply to this email and let me know!
And if you know a fellow creator who needs help with writing better titles, please send them to creatorhooks.com.
Here’s to the war on boring content.
– Jake
P.S. Follow me on Twitter for more title tips @jthomas__​
P.P.S. If you want to see all of the videos from Creator Hooks in one place, check out Creator Hooks Pro.

## Newsletter 378: 10 Simple Habits To Grow Your YouTube Channel

**URL:** https://creatorhooks.com/10-simple-habits-to-grow-your-youtube-channel/

```
10 Simple Habits To Grow Your YouTube Channel 
December 8, 2022August 1, 2022 by admin 


I’ve asked some of the best creators on YouTube, “How do you write such great titles?”
And they’ve all said the same thing: “I just model what works.”
So this email will show you 5 videos worth modeling to help you write great titles and save time.
Btw, if you want to see all 300+ titles and thumbnails from Creator Hooks in one place, check out Creator Hooks Pro.
No Debt Free For Me


🖼️ Thumbnail 2:
STOP Paying Off Debt! |
Unpopular Money Opinions...

16K views + 1 month ago -

Title: STOP Paying Off Debt! | Unpopular Money Opinions 2022
Formula: STOP (Positive Action) | Unpopular (Industry) Opinions 2022
Hook score: +320
Why this works:
Negativity – This video about stopping doing something beneficial.
Curiosity – Stopping paying off debt is counterintuitive, so it builds lots of curiosity.
Timeliness – This creator lets you know this video is relevant right now by using the current year at the end of the title.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience to stop doing something that is commonly considered a best practice, but that you know is actually not an optimal decision for them.
Examples of this hook in action:

STOP Doing Situps | Unpopular Fitness Opinions 2022
STOP Using Apple’s Email App | Unpopular Tech Opinions 2022

Beary Long


🖼️ Thumbnail 3:
How Long Are We Going To
Be In A Bear Market?

170K views + 3 weeks ago + 21 VPH

Title: How Long Are We Going To Be In A Bear Market?
Formula: How Long (Will Bad Thing Last)?
Hook score: +361
Why this works:
Curiosity – This title builds curiosity by asking a question.
Negativity – This title uses negativity because it’s talking about a negative subject — bear markets.
Timeliness – Since we’re currently in a bear market, this title joins the conversation in everyone’s mind.
How you can use this hook: Ask your audience how long something bad that they’re currently going through will last.
Examples of this hook in action:

How Long Will Gas Be Over $5?
How Long Do Beginner Guitar Players Suck?

Keep It Simple


🖼️ Thumbnail 4:
10 Simple Exercises To
Improve Your Running

163K views - 2 weeks ago -

Title: 10 Simple Exercises To Improve Your Running
Formula: 10 Simple (Activities) To (Achieve Goal)
Hook score: +388
Why this works:
List – Lists add curiosity and make videos feel more tangible.
Desire – Every runner wants to improve their running, and they’d also love to do it with simple exercises.
Time Frame – The thumbnail includes a short, 10-minute time frame, which makes improving your running feel more achievable.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about simple activities they can do to improve what they’re working on.
Examples of this hook in action:

10 Simple YouTube Tips To Reach 100,000 Subscribers
10 Simple Lunches For Back To School Time

I’m Feeling Seen


🖼️ Thumbnail 5:
ORGANIZED PEOPLE
DON 1DOMHIS

5 THINGS ORGANIZED
PEOPLE DO (That You Dont...

542K views + 1 month ago + 208 VPH

Title: 5 THINGS ORGANIZED PEOPLE DO (That You Don’t 😱)
Formula: 5 Things (Desired Trait) People Do (That You Don’t 😱)
Hook score: +410
Why this works:
Authority – Organized people have credibility here and this creator’s audience would love to be more like them.
List – Lists add curiosity and make videos feel more tangible.
Negativity – Finishing this title with “(That You Don’t)” gives you a little fomo.
Curiosity – The thumbnail builds curiosity by opening up a loop.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about things that people they want to be like do.
Examples of this hook in action:

5 Things Rich House Flippers Do (That You Don’t)
5 Things Happy People Do (That You Don’t 😔)

R-E-S-P-E-C-T


🖼️ Thumbnail 6:
MAKE
PEOPLE
ALWAYS

your

How to Instantly Make
Anyone Respect You

115K views - 3 weeks ago -

Title: How to Instantly Make Anyone Respect You
Formula: How to Instantly (Achieve Goal)
Hook score: +727
Why this works:
Desire – Not only is this video about something this audience wants (respect), but it also promises to help them get it instantly.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about how to achieve their goals instantly.
Examples of this hook in action:

How to Instantly Have A Happier Marriage
How to Instantly Get Better At Journaling

Flop of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 7:
How This Pro Runner
Handles Race Anxiety

10K views + 2 months ago «

Title: How This Pro Runner Handles Race Anxiety
Hook score: -75
Why this flopped: This title has some good things going for it — authority and a pain point. So why did it flop?
I think it’s because the pain point is only shared by a few people. Or maybe most people experience it so few times that by the time they see this video on their home page, they don’t care or have forgotten about it.
Contrast this title with something like, “How This Pro Runner Handles Getting Tired While Running.”
Everyone experiences this pain point, and they experience it every time they go for a run, so it’d probably get more views.
The next time you make a video about a pain point, make sure that there’s enough demand for the video.
Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
Have any questions about the hooks in this edition?
Reply to this email and let me know!
And if you know a fellow creator who needs help with writing better titles, please send them to creatorhooks.com.
Here’s to the war on boring content.
– Jake
P.S. Follow me on Twitter for more title tips @jthomas__​
P.P.S. If you want to see all of the videos from Creator Hooks in one place, check out Creator Hooks Pro.

## Newsletter 379: the EASIEST way to get 100000 subscribers on YouTube

**URL:** https://creatorhooks.com/the-easiest-way-to-get-100000-subscribers-on-youtube/

```
the EASIEST way to get 100,000 subscribers on YouTube 
December 8, 2022July 25, 2022 by admin 


I’ve asked some of the best creators on YouTube, “How do you write such great titles?”
And they’ve all said the same thing: “I just model what works.”
So this email will show you 5 videos worth modeling to help you write great titles and save time.
Btw, if you want to see all of the titles and thumbnails from Creator Hooks in one place, check out Creator Hooks Pro.
I’m a Survivor


🖼️ Thumbnail 2:
72 HOUR SURVIVAL (no food,
no water, on an island)

3.6M views « 2 months ago +

Title: 72 HOUR SURVIVAL (no food, no water, on an island)
Formula: 72 HOUR [Achievement] (no [Amenities])
Hook score: +305
Why this works:
Constraints – Constraints like “no food, no water, on an island” build a lot of curiosity and conflict, which both make for an interesting video.
Time Frame – “72 Hour Survival” lets you know exactly what this video is about, which makes you more likely to click. If it was just “SURVIVAL (no food, no water, on an island)” it would be a little more vague and less interesting.
Negativity – This video addresses a common fear people have. People like to watch videos about things they fear and live vicariously through YouTubers.
How you can use this hook: Take on a scary challenge with several constraints and a time frame.
Examples of this hook in action:

72 HOUR Hackathon (launching an app from scratch)
Surviving a 24 Hour Ultra Marathon (100 miles, no rest)

Gear Heads


🖼️ Thumbnail 3:
Most Underrated Pieces of
Backpacking Equipment...

65K views * 1 month ago +

Title: Most Underrated Pieces of Backpacking Equipment: Inexpensive Gear That Makes a Difference
Formula: Most Underrated (Tools): (Counterintuitive Adjective)(Tools) That Make a Difference
Hook score: +340
Why this works:
Curiosity – There’s contrast (which adds curiosity) between “inexpensive gear” that “makes a difference.” And “underrated” adds to the curiosity, too.
Desire – This creator’s audience loves good gear.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about the most underrated pieces of equipment in your industry.
Examples of this hook in action:

Most Underrated Smart Home Gadgets: Small Tools That Make a Big Difference
Most Underrated Shopify Apps: Cheap Apps That Make Big Money

Style Searching


🖼️ Thumbnail 4:
a wardrobe
you actually like?

this is the EASIEST way to
find your style (no shopping...

233K views + 1 month ago « 103 VPH

Title: this is the EASIEST way to find your style (no shopping required!)
Formula: this is the EASIEST way to [achieve goal] (no [Bothersome Activity] required!)
Hook score: +358
Why this works:
Desire – This creator’s audience would love to find their style and do it the easiest way.
Refute an Objection – The audience might be thinking they don’t have time or money to go shopping, but this title gets rid of that excuse.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about the easiest way to achieve their goals without one of their big excuses.
Examples of this hook in action:

this is the EASIEST way to find a great boyfriend (no online dating required!)
the EASIEST way to grow your own vegetables (no garden required!)

Daily Duties


🖼️ Thumbnail 5:
3-MUST DO-Daily Strength
Exercises For Seniors

216K views * 1 month ago -

Title: 3-MUST DO-Daily Strength Exercises For Seniors
Formula: 3 Must Do Daily (Activities) For (Specific Group)
Hook score: +705
Why this works:
List – Lists build curiosity and make a video feel more tangible.
Desire – This creator’s audience is looking for strength exercises to help them stay active.
Daily Tips – People seem to really like exercises or habits you can plug into your daily routine.
Specific Audience – If you’re a senior and you see this title, you feel like this video was made specifically for you, so you’re more likely to click.
How you can use this hook: Give your audience a list of daily activities to help them achieve their goal, and call them out by group name in the title.
Examples of this hook in action:

3 Must Do Daily Habits For YouTubers
3 Must Do Daily Exercises For Beginner Piano Players

Daily Dollars


🖼️ Thumbnail 6:
15 Websites That Will Pay
You DAILY Within 24 Hours...

483K views + 2 months ago

Title: 15 Websites That Will Pay You DAILY Within 24 Hours (Easy Work At Home Jobs)
Formula: 15 [Tools] That Will [Help Achieve Goal] DAILY Within [Time Frame] (Easy [Action])
Hook score: +710
Why this works:
Desire – This creator’s audience would love to make money daily and get an easy work at home job.
List – Lists build curiosity and make a video feel more tangible.
Time Frame – The time frame in this title refutes an objection. Many websites take a while to pay you, but this promises to tell you about websites that pay within 24 hours.
Daily Tips – Another title with a daily benefit here.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about 15 tools to help them achieve their goals fast.
Examples of this hook in action:

10 Instagram Apps That Will Get You 100 Followers DAILY in just 24 Hours (Best Instagram Tools)
5 Savings Accounts That Pay Interest MONTHLY (Best Personal Bank Accounts)

Flop of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 7:
A Simple Day In My Life
How I Build an Outfit, Life...

21K views « 2 months ago «

Title: A Simple Day In My Life 🌱 How I Build an Outfit, Life Updates, What’s New In My Wardrobe ✨
Hook score: -76
Why this flopped: This title is all about the creator and has four different subjects, so it’s not as punchy.
Plus, it’s about a simple day in her life — there’s no conflict, which is what makes a good story.
Contrast this title with some of the others on her channel:

“If I was starting my wardrobe from scratch, these are the pieces I’d buy (minimalist wardrobe)”
“What I learned by getting rid of 60% of my clothes 👗”
“7 things I cut from my budget that I don’t miss at all (where I spend & where I save)”

These are more focused, have more emotion, and talk about pain points or desires the audience has.
Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
Have any questions about the hooks in this edition?
Reply to this email and let me know!
And if you know a fellow creator who needs help with writing better titles, please forward this email to them and send them to creatorhooks.com.
Here’s to the war on boring content.
– Jake
P.S. Follow me on Twitter for more title tips @jthomas__​
P.P.S. If you want to see all of the videos from Creator Hooks in one place, check out Creator Hooks Pro.
Read last week’s edition »

## Newsletter 381: How does the YouTube Algorithm know what makes a good video

**URL:** https://creatorhooks.com/creator-hooks-62/

```
How does the YouTube Algorithm know what makes a good video?￼ 
December 8, 2022July 18, 2022 by admin 


I’ve asked some of the best creators on YouTube, “How do you write such great titles?”
And they’ve all said the same thing: “I just model what works.”
So this email will show you 5 videos worth modeling to help you write great titles and save time.
Btw, if you want to see all of the titles and thumbnails from Creator Hooks, check out Creator Hooks Pro.
Protein Party


🖼️ Thumbnail 2:
Protein is great for weight
loss... but you're doing it...

146K views + 2 months ago «

Title: Protein is great for weight loss… but you’re doing it wrong
Formula: (Popular Tool) Is Great For (Goal)… But You’re Doing It Wrong
Hook score*: +365
Why this works:
Curiosity – This opens a loop because when I hear I’m doing protein wrong I assume I’ll lean how to do it right in the video, so I need to watch and close that loop.
Desire – This creator’s audience wants to lose weight.
Negativity – “doing it wrong” feels like a warning here.
How you can use this hook: Tell you audience that something they commonly use is great to help reach their goals, but they’re doing it wrong.
Examples of this hook in action:

Rental Properties are great for passive income… but you’re doing it wrong
Apple Watch is my favorite smart watch… but you’re using it wrong

A Great Hook


🖼️ Thumbnail 3:
START Fishing A KEITECH
Like This (Don't FALL Behind)

98K views 1 month ago -

Title: START Fishing A KEITECH Like This (Don’t FALL Behind)
Formula: START [Action] Like This (Don’t FALL Behind)
Hook score: +377
Why this works:
Curiosity – “Like This” opens a loop and makes you wonder how to fish a keitech.
Negativity – The “don’t fall behind” part at the end adds a warning.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience how to use a tool, but then warn them to not fall behind.
Examples of this hook in action:

START Making TikToks Like This (Don’t FALL Behind)
START Doing Date Night Like This (Don’t Have A BORING Relationship)

Permitting Profits


🖼️ Thumbnail 4:
Just Permitted 56 Acres to
make $50,000/month

69K views - 8 days ago - 432 VPH

Title: Just Permitted 56 Acres to make $50,000/month
Formula: Just (Achieved Big Goal or Started Inspiring Project)
Hook score: +411
Why this works:
Desire – This creator’s audience would love to make $50,000/month.
Timeliness – Starting out with “Just” makes you feel like this video is about a new thing that just happened. It’s kinda mixing news and education together.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about a big project you started or a big goal you reached.
Examples of this hook in action:

Just Bought A 50 Acre Self-Sustaining Farm
Just Hit 10,000,000 Views On A Brand New Channel

Pa-Pow-Pow


🖼️ Thumbnail 5:
WHERE
DOES THE

Where does the POWER in
the GOLF SWING Come...

54K views - 3 weeks ago +

Title: Where does the POWER in the GOLF SWING Come From???
Formula: Where does the (Desired Trait) Come From??
Hook score: +469
Why this works:
Curiosity – The title builds curiosity by asking a question. This audience may assume the answer is legs, but the thumbnail adds to the curiosity by showing a guy sitting down and not using his legs.
Desire – Most golfers would love to have more power in their swing.
How you can use this hook: Ask a big question everyone in your audience is already asking.
Examples of this hook in action:

What makes your lawn GREENER?
How do you STOP your legs from burning during a run???

Lit AF


🖼️ Thumbnail 6:
BROKE AF? Start w/ Zero
Make $250 a day online (DO...

64K views « 1 month ago - 9 VPH

Title: BROKE AF? Start w/ Zero Make $250 a day online (DO THIS)
Formula: [Pain Point]? Start [From Beginning] [Achieve Big Goal] (DO THIS)
Hook score: +533
Why this works:
Curiosity – This title starts by asking a question to build curiosity, then finishes by opening a loop to build even more.
Negativity – “Broke AF” is a big pain point many people in this creator’s audience have.
Desire – This creator’s audience would love to make $250 a day online.
Beginner Tips – If you’re a beginner starting from zero, you feel like this video was made for you.
How you can use this hook: Call out a pain point, then show your audience how to achieve a specific goal.
Examples of this hook in action:

TIRED AF? Keep Your Home Clean as a New Mom (DO THIS)
BUSY AF? Start w/ a Blank Page and Plan Your Whole Week (DO THIS)

Flop of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 7:
DON'T Fish Like Everyone
Else (Fish The EXTREMES)

11K views - 1 month ago -

Title: DON’T Fish Like Everyone Else (Fish The EXTREMES)
Hook score: -67
Why this flopped: This title was good in that it’s got a warning and kinda opens a loop.
But I think it flopped because it doesn’t speak to any real fear or desire. The audience doesn’t fear fishing like everyone else — they fear not catching fish.
And they don’t care about fishing the extremes — they want to catch fish.
Don’t try to be too clever with your titles and always think about what your audience wants.
Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
Have any questions about the hooks in this edition?
Reply to this email and let me know!
And if you know a fellow creator who needs help with writing better titles, please send them to creatorhooks.com.
Here’s to the war on boring content.
– Jake
P.S. If you want to see all of the videos from every Creator Hooks edition in one place, check out creatorhookspro.com.
Read last week’s edition »

## Newsletter 383: Asking 1000000-Subscriber YouTubers How To Be Successful

**URL:** https://creatorhooks.com/asking-1000000-subscriber-youtubers-how-to-be-successful/

```
Asking 1,000,000-Subscriber YouTubers How To Be Successful 
January 4, 2023July 11, 2022 by admin 


I’ve asked some of the best creators on YouTube, “How do you write such great titles?”
And they’ve all said the same thing: “I just model what works.”
So this email has 5 videos worth modeling to help you write great titles and save time on research.
Btw, if you want to see all of the titles and thumbnails from Creator Hooks in one place, check out Creator Hooks Pro.
Housing Happenings


🖼️ Thumbnail 2:
Something Huge is Occurring
in the Housing Market

119K views - 1 month ago -

Title: Something Huge is Occurring in the Housing Market
Formula: Something Huge is Occurring in the (Industry)
Hook score: +360
Why this works:
Curiosity – This title opens a loop and makes you wonder, “What is happening?” You’ve gotta click to find out.
Timeliness – There’s lots of shifting and and changes going on in the housing market right now, and this video makes you feel like you’re getting new news.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience that something huge is occurring in your industry.
Examples of this hook in action:

Something Huge is Occurring in the Job Market
Something Huge is Coming in the Crypto Industry

Kitty Cat Conundrum


🖼️ Thumbnail 3:
21 Ways You Are Hurting
Your Cat Without Realizing

969K views + 1 month ago -

VPH

Title: 21 Ways You Are Hurting Your Cat Without Realizing
Formula: 21 Ways You Are (Negative Action) Without Realizing
Hook score: +566
Why this works:
List – Lists make a video more tangible (you know exactly what the video is about) and build curiosity.
Negativity – Nobody wants to accidentally hurt their cat, so they watch this video to avoid doing that.
Curiosity – Lists about negative things build lots of curiosity because you ask yourself, “Am I doing any of these?” and you’ve gotta watch to find out.
How you can use this hook: List out ways your audience is doing something they really don’t want to do without realizing it.
Examples of this hook in action:

15 Ways You Are Hurting Your Credit Score Without Realizing
10 Ways You Are Killing Your YouTube Channel Without Realizing

Bezos Blunders


🖼️ Thumbnail 4:
WARNING: Bad News For
Amazon Sellers

27K views + 1 month ago +

Title: WARNING: Bad News For Amazon Sellers
Formula: WARNING: Bad News For (Your Audience)
Hook score: +944
Why this works:
Negativity – Warnings work really well to get our attention and get us to click.
Authority – Jeff Bezos’ shiny bald head is easily recognizable and quickly gets the audience’s attention.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about bad news for them and slap a big ol’ WARNING in all caps in front.
Examples of this hook in action:

WARNING: Bad News For Florida Home Owners
WARNING: Bad News For Home School Moms

Ask & You Shall Receive


🖼️ Thumbnail 5:
UN Geary

Kein YOUNG Millionaires
How to be Successful

329K views + 1 month ago « 989 VPH

Title: Asking YOUNG Millionaires How to be Successful
Formula: Asking (Who Your Audience Wants To Be Like) How to (Achieve Goal)
Hook score: +1430
Why this works:
Desire – Everyone in this creator’s audience wants to know how to be successful
Authority – This creator is asking authority figures (young millionaires) how they did it.
Curiosity – You really want to see what these young millionaires say, so you’ve gotta watch this video.
How you can use this hook: Ask people who’ve made it in your industry how they did it.
Examples of this hook in action:

Asking Fitness Models How To Get Shredded
Asking Fishing Guides How To Catch More Fish

Stinky Tricks


🖼️ Thumbnail 6:
THIS Simple Trick Makes
Your Bathroom & Toilet...

376K views - 3 weeks ago *

Title: THIS Simple Trick Makes Your Bathroom & Toilet SMELL AMAZING!
Formula: THIS Simple Trick (Achieves Goal)
Hook score: +1634
Why this works:
Desire – This creator’s audience (and probably — hopefully — most people) would like to have their bathroom and toilet smell good. This title also promises a lot (an amazing-smelling bathroom) for a little (a simple trick).
Curiosity – This title opens a loop by telling you there’s a simple trick to make your bathroom and toilet smell amazing, but not what that simple trick is.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about a simple trick that helps them get what they want.
Examples of this hook in action:

THIS Simple Trick Doubles Your iPhone Battery Life
THIS Simple Trick Makes Your Lawn Greener In One Week

Flop of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 7:
How to Cut and Dice a
MANGO! @ (real simple an...

2.4K views + 2 weeks ago -

Title: How to Cut and Dice a MANGO! 🥭 (real simple and fast life hacks)
Hook score: -78
Why this flopped: The videos on this channel are mostly about cleaning and house hacks, not so much cooking and kitchen hacks.
Cutting and dicing a mango may be a great topic, but it doesn’t seem like it’s a great topic for this channel.
So when picking your next video topic, make sure you’re giving your audience what they really want to hear from YOU.
Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
Have any questions about the hooks in this edition?
Reply to this email and let me know!
And if you know a fellow creator who needs help with writing better titles, please send them to creatorhooks.com.
Here’s to the war on boring content.
– Jake
P.S. Follow me on Twitter for more title tips @jthomas__
P.P.S. If you want to see all of the titles and thumbnails from Creator Hooks in one place, check out Creator Hooks Pro.

329K views + 1 month ago « 989 VPH

Title: Asking YOUNG Millionaires How to be Successful
Formula: Asking (Who Your Audience Wants To Be Like) How to (Achieve Goal)
Hook score: +1430
Why this works:
Desire – Everyone in this creator’s audience wants to know how to be successful
Authority – This creator is asking authority figures (young millionaires) how they did it.
Curiosity – You really want to see what these young millionaires say, so you’ve gotta watch this video.
How you can use this hook: Ask people who’ve made it in your industry how they did it.
Examples of this hook in action:

Asking Fitness Models How To Get Shredded
Asking Fishing Guides How To Catch More Fish

Stinky Tricks


🖼️ Thumbnail 6:
THIS Simple Trick Makes
Your Bathroom & Toilet...

376K views - 3 weeks ago *

Title: THIS Simple Trick Makes Your Bathroom & Toilet SMELL AMAZING!
Formula: THIS Simple Trick (Achieves Goal)
Hook score: +1634
Why this works:
Desire – This creator’s audience (and probably — hopefully — most people) would like to have their bathroom and toilet smell good. This title also promises a lot (an amazing-smelling bathroom) for a little (a simple trick).
Curiosity – This title opens a loop by telling you there’s a simple trick to make your bathroom and toilet smell amazing, but not what that simple trick is.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about a simple trick that helps them get what they want.
Examples of this hook in action:

THIS Simple Trick Doubles Your iPhone Battery Life
THIS Simple Trick Makes Your Lawn Greener In One Week

Flop of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 7:
How to Cut and Dice a
MANGO! @ (real simple an...

2.4K views + 2 weeks ago -

Title: How to Cut and Dice a MANGO! 🥭 (real simple and fast life hacks)
Hook score: -78
Why this flopped: The videos on this channel are mostly about cleaning and house hacks, not so much cooking and kitchen hacks.
Cutting and dicing a mango may be a great topic, but it doesn’t seem like it’s a great topic for this channel.
So when picking your next video topic, make sure you’re giving your audience what they really want to hear from YOU.
Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
Have any questions about the hooks in this edition?
Reply to this email and let me know!
And if you know a fellow creator who needs help with writing better titles, please send them to creatorhooks.com.
Here’s to the war on boring content.
– Jake
P.S. Follow me on Twitter for more title tips @jthomas__

## Newsletter 385: 10 YouTube Tips That Changed My Life

**URL:** https://creatorhooks.com/10-youtube-tips-that-changed-my-life/

```
10 YouTube Tips That Changed My Life 
December 8, 2022July 4, 2022 by admin 


I’ve asked some of the best creators on YouTube, “How do you write such great titles?”
And they’ve all said the same thing: “I just model what works.”
So this email has 5 videos worth modeling to help you write great titles and save time on research.
Btw, if you want to see all of the titles and thumbnails from Creator Hooks in one place, check out Creator Hooks Pro.
Stuck In A Pickle


🖼️ Thumbnail 2:
3 Common Mistakes Made
by Recreational Pickleball...

188K views - 5 months ago

Title: 3 Common Mistakes Made by Recreational Pickleball Players (& most players 4.0 and below)
Formula: 3 Common Mistakes Made by [Specific Subset of Audience] (& most [General Audience])
Hook score: +341
Why this works:
List – Lists make it easy to understand what the video is about and builds curiosity.
Negativity – Nobody wants to make mistakes, so they watch this video to avoid them. Plus, the thumbnail includes a warning.
Curiosity – The thumbnail builds curiosity by opening a loop.
Specific Audience – If you’re a recreational pickleball player, you feel like this video was made just for you. This makes you more likely to watch it.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about the 3 common mistakes they’re likely to make, and encourage them to click by specifically calling out who the video is for.
Examples of this hook in action:

3 Common Mistakes Made by Beginner Guitar Players
3 Common Mistakes Made by Backyard Gardeners

Sir Mix A Lot


🖼️ Thumbnail 3:
10 Mixing Tips That Changed
My Life

370K views - 1 month ago + 73 VPH

Title: 10 Mixing Tips That Changed My Life
Formula: 10 (Industry) Tips That Changed My Life
Hook score: +445
Why this works:
List – Lists make it easy to understand what the video is about and builds curiosity.
Desire – “Changed my life” is a strong desire that many people have.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about the industry-specific tips that changed your life.
Examples of this hook in action:

10 Money-Making Tips That Changed My Life
10 Baking Tips That Changed My Life

The Perfect Foundation


🖼️ Thumbnail 4:
HOWTO
APPLY
7 FOUNDATION

PERFECT

HOW TO APPLY
FOUNDATION FOR...

148K views - 1 month ago

Title: HOW TO APPLY FOUNDATION FOR BEGINNERS | NINA UBHI
Formula: How To (Common Task) For Beginners
Hook score: +643
Why this works:
Beginner Tips – Beginners are the biggest, hungriest subset of any audience.
Desire – A big portion of this creator’s audience wants to learn how to apply foundation perfectly.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience how to do a basic skill that many of them want to do, and let beginners know this video was made specifically for them.
Examples of this hook in action:

How To Invest In Crypto For Beginners
How To Water Your Lawn For Beginners

Excelent Video


🖼️ Thumbnail 5:
Ino longer VLOOKUP(), | use
this other function instead...

266K views « 2 months ago

Title: I no longer VLOOKUP(), I use this other function instead…
Formula: I no longer (Common Action), I use this other (Action) instead…
Hook score: +886
Why this works:
Negativity – This video is about stopping using a common function.
Curiosity – Since VLOOKUP is a common and useful function, it’s counterintuitive to stop using it. Plus, this title opens up a loop at the end by not telling you what the other function is.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about a common and useful tool you no longer use, and hint that you’ve found a better tool.
Examples of this hook in action:

I no longer use an iPhone, I use this phone instead…
I no longer bow hunt, I use this instead…

MindBlown


🖼️ Thumbnail 6:
8 Mind-Blowing Websites
You Probably Didn't Know...

1.6M views + 2 months ago

Title: 8 Mind-Blowing Websites You Probably Didn’t Know Existed
Formula: 8 Mind-Blowing (Tools) You Probably Didn’t Know Existed
Hook score: +6956
Why this works:
List – Have I told you lists make it easy to understand what the video is about and build curiosity?
Curiosity – Describing these websites as “mind-blowing” and that “you probably didn’t know existed” builds curiosity.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about the mind-blowing tools they didn’t know existed.
Examples of this hook in action:

8 Mind-Blowing National Parks You Probably Didn’t Know Existed
8 Mind-Blowing YouTube Tools You Probably Didn’t Know Existed

Flop of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 7:
MY GO TO BEAUTY TOOLS
AND MONTHLY SKINCARE...

7.6K views + 1 month ago +

Title: MY GO TO BEAUTY TOOLS AND MONTHLY SKINCARE REGIME 2022 | NINA UBHI
Hook score: -78
Why this flopped: I think this video flopped because of the word “and”.
Most successful videos are about a single topic, but this video is about two topics.
In your titles, you want to make a big promise and deliver on it.
When you start including multiple topics in your title you confuse your audience.
And if you confuse you lose.
Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
Have any questions about the hooks in this edition?
Reply to this email and let me know!
And if you know a fellow creator who needs help with writing better titles, please forward this email to them and send them to creatorhooks.com.
Here’s to the war on boring content.
 – Jake
P.S. Follow me on Twitter for more title tips @jthomas__​
P.P.S. If you want to see all of the videos from Creator Hooks in one place, check out Creator Hooks Pro.

## Newsletter 387: YouTube Titles 101  Everything You Need To Know

**URL:** https://creatorhooks.com/youtube-titles-101-everything-you-need-to-know/

```
YouTube Titles 101 – Everything You Need To Know 
December 8, 2022June 27, 2022 by admin 


I’ve asked some of the best creators on YouTube, “How do you write such great titles?”
And they’ve all said the same thing: “I just model what works.”
So here are 5 videos worth modeling to help you write great titles and save time:
P.S. If you want to see all of the titles and thumbnails from Creator Hooks in one place, check out Creator Hooks Pro.
Red Rover


🖼️ Thumbnail 2:
COLOR GRADING 101

er
Color Grading 101 -

Everything You Need to Know

209K views + 1 month ago + 52 VPH

Title: Color Grading 101 – Everything You Need to Know
Formula: (Subject) 101 – Everything You Need to Know
Hook score: +307
Why this works:
Beginner Tips – This video caters to beginners wanting to learn how to color grade. Beginners are the biggest and hungriest subset of any audience, so making videos for them can get tons of views.
How you can use this hook: Give your audience a 101 lesson on a popular subject and tell them everything they need to know.
Examples of this hook in action:

Multi-Family Real Estate 101 – Everything You Need To Know
Ecommerce 101 – Everything You Need To Know

Triplet Peaks


🖼️ Thumbnail 3:
Will The Housing Market
Crash By The End of The...

118K views - 1 month ago + 36 VPH

Title: Will The Housing Market Crash By The End of The Year? – What banks don’t want you to know
Formula: Will (Bad Event Happen) By the End Of The Year? – What (Authority) don’t want you to know
Hook score: +447
Why this works:
Curiosity – This title starts off building curiosity with a question, and then at the end opens a loop with the phrase, “What banks don’t want you to know.”
Negativity – This video is about the housing market crash and about what banks don’t want you to know… lots of fear and conspiracy in there.
Timeliness – This video does particularly well because the housing market crash is a hot topic right now.
Time Frame – By limiting the scope of this video to just the end of the year, it makes it more tangible and even gives some urgency for you to watch it.
Authority – Banks are an authority in this space, and this title makes them an enemy, which makes you want to watch it even more.
How you can use this hook: Ask the question that everybody in your industry is asking themselves right now, but put a time limit on it. Then, finish up with a conspiracy-laden open loop.
Examples of this hook in action:

Will TikTok Implode By the End Of The Year? – What China doesn’t want you to know
Will the Job Market Crash By the End Of The Year? – What Hiring Managers don’t want you to know

Light Work


🖼️ Thumbnail 4:
Unlocking Lightroom's
Darkest Editing Secret!

157K views « 4 weeks ago

Title: Unlocking Lightroom’s Darkest Editing Secret!
Formula: Unlocking (Tool)’s Darkest (Action) Secret!
Hook score: +523
Why this works:
Curiosity – Unlocking a secret builds a ton of curiosity, plus there’s contrast between Lightroom and darkest secret.
Negativity – “Lightroom’s Darkest Secret” has a lot of drama and conspiracy vibes.
Beginner tips – The thumbnail mentions beginners.
How you can use this hook: Unlock the darkest secret of one of the tools that your audience uses.
Examples of this hook in action:

Unlocking Journaling’s Darkest Productivity Secret!
Unlock Your Squat’s Greatest Strength Gains!

7th Heaven


🖼️ Thumbnail 5:
a oe

Ts (wats sreno

MY 7-FIGURE BLOG

$$$

Everything | Spend Money On
For My 7-Figure Blog |...

101K views + 3 weeks ago + 189 VPH

Title: Everything I Spend Money On For My 7-Figure Blog | Blogging Expenses
Formula: Everything I Spend Money On For My (Impressive Project) | (Keyword)
Hook score: +863
Why this works:
Curiosity – You feel like you’re getting in on a secret because you’re learning about where she spends her money, which can be a taboo subject.
Desire – This creator’s audience would love to have a 7-figure blog.
How you can use this hook: Pull back the curtain and show your audience how you or someone else spend money on their end goal.
Examples of this hook in action:

Everything I Spend Money On For My 30’ Fishing Boat | Boating Expenses
How Much Money I Spend As A Mom of 7 | Family Money Secrets

Game Changer


🖼️ Thumbnail 6:
Voice Typing Changes
Everything - So much more...

567K views « 2 months ago

Title: Voice Typing Changes Everything – So much more than Dictation!
Formula: (New Technology) Changes Everything – So much more than (Old Technology)!
Hook score: +1181
Why this works:
Curiosity – “Changes everything” opens a loop and makes you wonder how does it change everything and what changes.
New Opportunity – New technology is exciting because it promises something different. We don’t have any previous bad experiences with it, so there’s a lot of hope.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about a new technology or opportunity that changes everything and is better than the current tech.
Examples of this hook in action:

Automated Investing Changes Everything – So much more than Gambling in the Stock Market!
AI Thumbnails Change Everything – So much better than Photoshop!

Flop of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 7:
BREAKING. DOWN.

JUINADING FAI 13:02

Breaking Down the Story
Structure of Jurassic Park

20K views - 4 weeks ago -

Title: Breaking Down the Story Structure of Jurassic Park
Hook score: -77
Why this flopped: The videos that perform best on this channel are about visual effects:

Color Grading 101 – Everything You Need to Know
We Recreated a Shot from Obi-Wan Kenobi
Destroyed City Effect From Doctor Strange in the Multiverse of Madness

But this video is about storytelling.
It’s good to try new things on your channel, but know that when you do, the video may flop.
Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
Have any questions about the hooks in this edition?
Reply to this email and let me know!
And if you know a fellow creator who needs help with writing better titles, please forward this email to them and send them to creatorhooks.com.
Here’s to the war on boring content.
– Jake
P.S. Follow me on Twitter for more title tips @jthomas__​
P.P.S. If you want to see all of the videos from Creator Hooks in one place, check out Creator Hooks Pro.

## Newsletter 388: YouTube Titles 101  Everything You Need To Know

**URL:** https://creatorhooks.com/youtube-titles-101-everything-you-need-to-know/

```
YouTube Titles 101 – Everything You Need To Know 
December 8, 2022June 27, 2022 by admin 


I’ve asked some of the best creators on YouTube, “How do you write such great titles?”
And they’ve all said the same thing: “I just model what works.”
So here are 5 videos worth modeling to help you write great titles and save time:
P.S. If you want to see all of the titles and thumbnails from Creator Hooks in one place, check out Creator Hooks Pro.
Red Rover


🖼️ Thumbnail 2:
COLOR GRADING 101

er
Color Grading 101 -

Everything You Need to Know

209K views + 1 month ago + 52 VPH

Title: Color Grading 101 – Everything You Need to Know
Formula: (Subject) 101 – Everything You Need to Know
Hook score: +307
Why this works:
Beginner Tips – This video caters to beginners wanting to learn how to color grade. Beginners are the biggest and hungriest subset of any audience, so making videos for them can get tons of views.
How you can use this hook: Give your audience a 101 lesson on a popular subject and tell them everything they need to know.
Examples of this hook in action:

Multi-Family Real Estate 101 – Everything You Need To Know
Ecommerce 101 – Everything You Need To Know

Triplet Peaks


🖼️ Thumbnail 3:
Will The Housing Market
Crash By The End of The...

118K views - 1 month ago + 36 VPH

Title: Will The Housing Market Crash By The End of The Year? – What banks don’t want you to know
Formula: Will (Bad Event Happen) By the End Of The Year? – What (Authority) don’t want you to know
Hook score: +447
Why this works:
Curiosity – This title starts off building curiosity with a question, and then at the end opens a loop with the phrase, “What banks don’t want you to know.”
Negativity – This video is about the housing market crash and about what banks don’t want you to know… lots of fear and conspiracy in there.
Timeliness – This video does particularly well because the housing market crash is a hot topic right now.
Time Frame – By limiting the scope of this video to just the end of the year, it makes it more tangible and even gives some urgency for you to watch it.
Authority – Banks are an authority in this space, and this title makes them an enemy, which makes you want to watch it even more.
How you can use this hook: Ask the question that everybody in your industry is asking themselves right now, but put a time limit on it. Then, finish up with a conspiracy-laden open loop.
Examples of this hook in action:

Will TikTok Implode By the End Of The Year? – What China doesn’t want you to know
Will the Job Market Crash By the End Of The Year? – What Hiring Managers don’t want you to know

Light Work


🖼️ Thumbnail 4:
Unlocking Lightroom's
Darkest Editing Secret!

157K views « 4 weeks ago

Title: Unlocking Lightroom’s Darkest Editing Secret!
Formula: Unlocking (Tool)’s Darkest (Action) Secret!
Hook score: +523
Why this works:
Curiosity – Unlocking a secret builds a ton of curiosity, plus there’s contrast between Lightroom and darkest secret.
Negativity – “Lightroom’s Darkest Secret” has a lot of drama and conspiracy vibes.
Beginner tips – The thumbnail mentions beginners.
How you can use this hook: Unlock the darkest secret of one of the tools that your audience uses.
Examples of this hook in action:

Unlocking Journaling’s Darkest Productivity Secret!
Unlock Your Squat’s Greatest Strength Gains!

7th Heaven


🖼️ Thumbnail 5:
a oe

Ts (wats sreno

MY 7-FIGURE BLOG

$$$

Everything | Spend Money On
For My 7-Figure Blog |...

101K views + 3 weeks ago + 189 VPH

Title: Everything I Spend Money On For My 7-Figure Blog | Blogging Expenses
Formula: Everything I Spend Money On For My (Impressive Project) | (Keyword)
Hook score: +863
Why this works:
Curiosity – You feel like you’re getting in on a secret because you’re learning about where she spends her money, which can be a taboo subject.
Desire – This creator’s audience would love to have a 7-figure blog.
How you can use this hook: Pull back the curtain and show your audience how you or someone else spend money on their end goal.
Examples of this hook in action:

Everything I Spend Money On For My 30’ Fishing Boat | Boating Expenses
How Much Money I Spend As A Mom of 7 | Family Money Secrets

Game Changer


🖼️ Thumbnail 6:
Voice Typing Changes
Everything - So much more...

567K views « 2 months ago

Title: Voice Typing Changes Everything – So much more than Dictation!
Formula: (New Technology) Changes Everything – So much more than (Old Technology)!
Hook score: +1181
Why this works:
Curiosity – “Changes everything” opens a loop and makes you wonder how does it change everything and what changes.
New Opportunity – New technology is exciting because it promises something different. We don’t have any previous bad experiences with it, so there’s a lot of hope.
How you can use this hook: Tell your audience about a new technology or opportunity that changes everything and is better than the current tech.
Examples of this hook in action:

Automated Investing Changes Everything – So much more than Gambling in the Stock Market!
AI Thumbnails Change Everything – So much better than Photoshop!

Flop of the Week


🖼️ Thumbnail 7:
BREAKING. DOWN.

JUINADING FAI 13:02

Breaking Down the Story
Structure of Jurassic Park

20K views - 4 weeks ago -

Title: Breaking Down the Story Structure of Jurassic Park
Hook score: -77
Why this flopped: The videos that perform best on this channel are about visual effects:

Color Grading 101 – Everything You Need to Know
We Recreated a Shot from Obi-Wan Kenobi
Destroyed City Effect From Doctor Strange in the Multiverse of Madness

But this video is about storytelling.
It’s good to try new things on your channel, but know that when you do, the video may flop.
Alright, that wraps up this week’s Creator Hooks!
Have any questions about the hooks in this edition?
Reply to this email and let me know!
And if you know a fellow creator who needs help with writing better titles, please forward this email to them and send them to creatorhooks.com.
Here’s to the war on boring content.
– Jake
P.S. Follow me on Twitter for more title tips @jthomas__​
P.P.S. If you want to see all of the videos from Creator Hooks in one place, check out Creator Hooks Pro.
